  preference_status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text reason explaining the current status of the preference record.']
  preference_type varchar [note: 'Category of preference (e.g., marketing, transactional, service, research).']
  preferred_communication_channel varchar [note: 'Consumer\'s preferred primary channel for outreach.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'Consumer\'s preferred language for communications.']
  privacy_policy_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the privacy policy under which consent was obtained.']
  push_opt_in boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the consumer has opted in to receive push notifications.']
  sms_opt_in boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the consumer has opted in to receive SMS messages.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the preference record.']
  third_party_sharing_opt_in boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the consumer consents to share data with third parties.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the preference record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for preference_center. Referenced by preference_center_id.'
}

Table consumer.data_subject_request {
  data_subject_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for data_subject_request']
  original_data_subject_request_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on data_subject_request (original_data_subject_request_id)']
  shopper_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the consumer (person, household, or loyalty member) who submitted the request.']
  data_category_requested varchar [note: 'Specific categories of personal data the consumer is requesting access to, delete, or correct. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: personal|financial|health|location|behavioral|communication — promote to reference product]']
  data_retention_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the retained data is scheduled for deletion.']
  data_retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days personal data will be retained after request fulfillment, per policy.']
  is_sensitive_data boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the request involves special category (sensitive) personal data.']
  is_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the consumer\'s identity has been successfully verified.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code indicating the regulatory jurisdiction governing the request.']
  legal_basis varchar [note: 'Legal justification for processing the request under privacy law.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes added by compliance or support staff regarding the request.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the request after processing.']
  outcome_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for the request outcome (e.g., legal exemption, data not found).']
  privacy_policy_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the privacy policy in effect at request time.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the request record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the request record.']
  request_channel varchar [note: 'Origin channel through which the request was received.']
  request_deadline_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Regulatory deadline by which the request must be addressed (e.g., 30‑day window).']
  request_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the request becomes effective for processing.']
  request_fulfilled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the request was fully satisfied or closed.']
  request_received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the request was initially recorded.']
  request_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system where the request originated (e.g., CRM, web portal).']
  request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the request.']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Category of data subject request (e.g., access, deletion, correction, data portability, processing restriction).']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify the consumer\'s identity.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for data_subject_request. Referenced by data_subject_request_id.'
}

Table consumer.panel {
  panel_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for panel']
  loyalty_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the loyalty program the consumer belongs to.']
  parent_panel_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on panel (parent_panel_id)']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the consumer\'s street address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the consumer\'s street address (optional).']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the consumer\'s residence.']
  cltv decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total revenue expected from the consumer over the relationship.']
  consent_status varchar [note: 'Consumer\'s consent status for marketing communications.']
  consumer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the consumer within the panel.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the consumer\'s primary residence.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the panel record was first created.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'System of record or source from which the consumer data originated.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Consumer\'s birth date.']
  death_date date [note: 'Date on which the consumer passed away, if applicable.']
  do_not_call boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the consumer is on a do‑not‑call list.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for consumer communications.']
  ethnicity varchar [note: 'Self‑identified ethnicity of the consumer. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: asian|black|hispanic|white|other|prefer_not_to_say — promote to reference product]']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Given name of the consumer.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Complete legal name of the consumer.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Self‑identified gender of the consumer.']
  household_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking the consumer to a household grouping.']
  household_income decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated annual household income of the consumer.']
  is_deceased boolean [note: 'True if the consumer is recorded as deceased.']
  language_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred language for communications.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Family name of the consumer.']
  last_purchase_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent purchase made by the consumer.']
  loyalty_tier varchar [note: 'Current tier level of the consumer within the loyalty program.']
  marital_status varchar [note: 'Current marital status of the consumer.']
  marketing_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the consumer has opted in to receive marketing communications.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Latest NPS rating provided by the consumer (0‑10).']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the consumer.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code of the consumer\'s address.']
  preferred_channel varchar [note: 'Consumer\'s preferred sales or service channel.']
  primary_contact_method varchar [note: 'Preferred channel for contacting the consumer.']
  privacy_opt_in_date date [note: 'Date when the consumer gave privacy consent.']
  purchase_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct purchase transactions recorded for the consumer.']
  record_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that supplied this panel record.']
  segment varchar [note: 'Marketing segment classification for the consumer. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: value_seeker|brand_loyalist|price_sensitive|premium|occasional|new_customer — promote to reference product]']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province of the consumer\'s residence.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the consumer record.']
  total_purchase_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative monetary value of all purchases made by the consumer.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the panel record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for panel. Referenced by panel_id.'
}

Table consumer.loyalty_tier {
  loyalty_tier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for loyalty_tier']
  next_loyalty_tier_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on loyalty_tier (next_loyalty_tier_id)']
  benefits_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the benefits associated with the tier.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the tier record was first created in the system.']
  discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard discount percentage applied to purchases for members in this tier.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the tier becomes valid for members.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the tier expires or is superseded; null for open‑ended.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Detailed rules that define how members qualify for the tier beyond points/spend thresholds.']
  exclusive_offers boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tier grants access to exclusive promotions.']
  expiration_policy varchar [note: 'Policy governing how tier status expires or rolls over.']
  free_shipping boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tier includes complimentary shipping.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tier is the default assignment for new members.']
  max_points_per_year varchar [note: 'Cap on the number of points a member can earn in a calendar year while in this tier.']
  points_multiplier decimal(5,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to earned points for members in this tier.']
  priority_support boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members receive priority customer support.']
  promotional_code varchar [note: 'Optional code used in marketing campaigns to reference the tier.']
  required_points varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty points a member must accumulate to qualify for this tier.']
  required_spend_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary spend threshold (in USD) required to reach the tier.']
  reward_points_per_dollar decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of loyalty points earned for each dollar spent by members in this tier.']
  spend_multiplier decimal(5,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to spend calculations for tier‑based rewards.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the tier.']
  tier_code varchar [note: 'External code used to reference the loyalty tier in marketing and systems.']
  tier_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the loyalty tier (e.g., Gold, Platinum).']
  tier_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric rank used to order tiers from lowest to highest.']
  tier_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the tier based on the underlying qualification model.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the tier record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for loyalty_tier. Referenced by tier_id.'
}

Table consumer.survey {
  survey_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for survey']
  revised_survey_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on survey (revised_survey_id)']
  compliance_ccpa boolean [note: 'True if the survey complies with CCPA requirements.']
  compliance_gdpr boolean [note: 'True if the survey complies with GDPR requirements.']
  consent_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether respondent consent is mandatory before participation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the survey record was first created.']
  data_privacy_level varchar [note: 'Classification of survey data handling requirements.']
  delivery_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the survey is distributed to respondents.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the survey purpose, scope and content.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the survey becomes active and available to respondents.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the survey is retired or no longer available (nullable for open‑ended surveys).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the survey is no longer accepted.']
  is_anonymous boolean [note: 'Specifies if responses are collected without identifying the respondent.']
  is_multilingual boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the survey is offered in multiple languages.']
  language varchar [note: 'Primary language in which the survey is delivered.']
  last_published_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the survey was most recently made live.']
  length_minutes varchar [note: 'Estimated time required for a respondent to complete the survey.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the survey.']
  owner_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact for the survey.']
  owner_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary contact for the survey.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Internal department responsible for the survey design and execution.']
  question_count varchar [note: 'Total count of distinct questions in the survey.']
  response_rate_actual decimal(5,2) [note: 'Observed percentage of respondents who completed the survey.']
  response_rate_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned percentage of invited respondents to complete the survey.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the survey.']
  survey_category varchar [note: 'Business category of the survey for reporting and analytics.']
  survey_logo_url varchar [note: 'Link to the logo image displayed on the survey landing page.']
  survey_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the survey based on the respondent group.']
  survey_url varchar [note: 'Web address where respondents can access the survey.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Intended respondent segment for the survey.']
  title varchar [note: 'Human‑readable title of the survey presented to respondents.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the survey record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the survey definition.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for survey. Referenced by survey_id.'
}

Table customer.channel_classification {
  channel_classification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the channel classification record. Primary key.']
  parent_channel_classification_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent channel classification in a hierarchical taxonomy, enabling multi-level channel rollups. Null for top-level classifications.']
  active_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the channel classification. Active classifications are used for account assignment and reporting; inactive classifications are retained for historical reference.']
  acv_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether accounts in this channel are eligible for ACV (All Commodity Volume) measurement and reporting. True if channel participates in syndicated retail measurement services.']
  applicable_region varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code or regional code indicating the geographic scope where this channel classification applies (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX, EUR).']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the trade channel. Used as a business key for integration and reporting.']
  channel_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the channel classification, including business characteristics, typical account types, and operational considerations.']
  channel_format varchar [note: 'Store or outlet format descriptor providing additional context on the physical or digital format (e.g., superstore, neighborhood market, pure-play online, omnichannel).']
  channel_name varchar [note: 'Full business name of the trade channel (e.g., Grocery Retail, Mass Merchandise, E-Commerce, Quick Service Restaurant).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this channel classification record was first created in the system.']
  distribution_model varchar [note: 'Primary distribution model used for this channel: DSD (Direct Store Delivery), warehouse delivery, hybrid, drop ship, or VMI (Vendor Managed Inventory).']
  edi_capable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether accounts in this channel typically support EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) for order processing, invoicing, and inventory management.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this channel classification was retired or deprecated. Null for currently active classifications.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this channel classification became effective and available for use in account classification and reporting.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this channel classification record was last updated.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard lead time in days from order placement to delivery for accounts in this channel.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Market positioning segment typically served by this channel (premium, mainstream, value, discount).']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Typical MOQ (Minimum Order Quantity) requirement for accounts in this channel, expressed in cases or units.']
  order_frequency varchar [note: 'Typical order frequency pattern for accounts in this channel (daily, weekly, biweekly, monthly, on-demand).']
  otif_target_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target OTIF (On Time In Full) service level percentage for accounts in this channel, typically defined in SLA (Service Level Agreement) terms.']
  payment_terms_standard varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms typically negotiated with accounts in this channel (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30).']
  planogram_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether accounts in this channel typically require POG (Planogram) compliance for shelf placement and merchandising.']
  pos_data_available_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether POS (Point of Sale) data is typically available from accounts in this channel for demand sensing and analytics.']
  pricing_strategy varchar [note: 'Typical pricing strategy employed by accounts in this channel: EDLP (Everyday Low Price), Hi-Lo (High-Low promotional pricing), hybrid, or dynamic pricing.']
  primary_tier varchar [note: 'Top-level classification of the trade channel. Primary tier segments the market into major channel categories.']
  return_policy varchar [note: 'Standard return and unsaleables policy applicable to accounts in this channel (e.g., full credit, partial credit, no returns).']
  secondary_tier varchar [note: 'Sub-classification within the primary tier providing granular channel segmentation (e.g., grocery, mass, club, drug, convenience, e-commerce, QSR - Quick Service Restaurant, institutional, cash and carry, broker). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: grocery|mass|club|drug|convenience|ecommerce|qsr|institutional|cash_and_carry|broker — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Numeric value controlling the display sequence of channel classifications in reports and user interfaces.']
  tdp_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether accounts in this channel are eligible for TDP (Total Distribution Points) calculation. True if channel is included in weighted distribution metrics.']
  tertiary_tier varchar [note: 'Optional third-level classification for highly specialized channel segments or sub-formats within secondary tier (e.g., natural/organic grocery, dollar stores, online marketplace).']
  trade_promotion_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether accounts in this channel are eligible for trade promotion programs, TPM (Trade Promotion Management), and promotional funding.']

  Note: 'Reference taxonomy defining the trade channel hierarchy used to classify trade accounts and retail stores. Captures channel code, channel name, channel tier (primary: retail, foodservice, wholesale, DTC; secondary: grocery, mass, club, drug, convenience, e-commerce, QSR, institutional), channel description, applicable region, and active status. Used as a standardized classification across sales, promotion, and distribution domains.'
}

Table supply.demand_plan {
  demand_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the demand plan record. Primary key representing a specific version-point in the S&OP/IBP cycle for a SKU/location/channel/planning period combination.']
  channel_classification_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales channel (retail, e-commerce, wholesale, DTC) for which demand is planned.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who approved this demand plan version in the S&OP governance workflow.']
  esg_commitment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.esg_commitment. Business justification: Demand planning aligns forecasts with ESG commitments; linking plan to commitment enables KPI tracking.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Integrated Demand Planning uses brand forecasts; brand_id links demand plan to specific brand for Brand Demand Forecast report.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Business process: New Product Demand Planning ties each demand_plan to the originating R&D project, enabling planners to align forecasts with product development timelines.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product SKU for which demand is being planned.']
  sop_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the S&OP/IBP planning cycle during which this demand plan version was created.']
  supply_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution location, warehouse, or market geography for which demand is planned.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.trade_account. Business justification: REQUIRED: Account‑level demand forecast used in S&OP planning; planners need trade_account context for each demand_plan.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the demand plan version within the S&OP governance workflow.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this demand plan version was formally approved and locked for supply planning execution.']
  commercial_overlay_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Demand adjustment quantity applied by field sales teams based on customer intelligence, market conditions, and sales pipeline visibility.']
  confidence_level varchar [note: 'Planner-assigned confidence rating in the demand forecast based on data quality, market visibility, and forecast stability.']
  consensus_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Agreed demand forecast quantity representing the single demand signal passed to supply planning after S&OP consensus process. This is the authoritative demand plan.']
  created_by_persona varchar [note: 'Role or persona of the user who created this demand plan version, used for accountability and collaboration tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this demand plan version record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code used for any monetary values associated with this demand plan (e.g., revenue forecasts).']
  demand_pattern_type varchar [note: 'Classification of historical demand behavior pattern used to select appropriate statistical forecasting algorithm.']
  demand_sensing_signal varchar [note: 'Short-term demand trend indicator derived from real-time POS data, shipment data, and market signals to enable rapid forecast adjustments.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this demand plan version becomes active and is used for supply planning and ATP calculations.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this demand plan version remains active. Null indicates the version is currently active with no planned expiration.']
  forecast_accuracy_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Historical forecast accuracy metric for this SKU/location/channel combination, used for forecast quality benchmarking and continuous improvement.']
  forecast_bias_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Systematic tendency to over-forecast or under-forecast, calculated as average percentage deviation from actual demand.']
  is_consensus_version boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this version represents the official consensus demand plan approved through the S&OP process and used for supply planning.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this demand plan version was last updated or adjusted.']
  lifecycle_stage varchar [note: 'Product lifecycle stage classification used to apply appropriate forecasting models and demand shaping strategies.']
  marketing_event_uplift_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Incremental demand quantity expected from planned marketing campaigns, promotions, and brand activation events.']
  npd_launch_volume_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Forecasted demand quantity for new product launches or product line extensions during the planning period.']
  planner_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text comments and annotations from demand planners explaining assumptions, adjustments, risks, or special considerations for this forecast.']
  planning_bucket varchar [note: 'Time granularity of the demand plan (daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly) used for aggregation and reporting.']
  planning_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the planning period (week or month) for which demand is forecasted.']
  planning_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the planning period (week or month) for which demand is forecasted.']
  promotional_overlay_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Demand uplift quantity attributed to trade promotions, price discounts, and retail execution activities.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the primary risk factor affecting demand plan reliability and supply fulfillment capability.']
  risk_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator that this demand plan carries elevated supply risk due to capacity constraints, material shortages, or demand volatility.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source system from which this demand plan was extracted (e.g., SAP IBP, TradeEdge, Nielsen IQ integration).']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'Original unique identifier of this demand plan record in the source system, used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  statistical_baseline_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Unconstrained demand forecast quantity generated by statistical forecasting algorithms before any commercial overlays or adjustments.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which demand quantities are expressed (each, case, pallet, kilogram, liter, etc.). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PL|KG|LB|L|GAL — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  variance_to_baseline_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Difference between consensus quantity and statistical baseline quantity, indicating the net impact of all commercial adjustments and overlays.']
  version_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the demand plan version (e.g., \'Statistical Baseline Q1 2024\', \'Consensus Final March 2024\').']
  version_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number within the planning cycle, used to track iteration history and version progression.']
  version_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the demand plan version indicating its stage in the S&OP process. Statistical baseline represents unconstrained forecast; consensus represents agreed demand signal passed to supply planning.']

  Note: 'Core master entity representing the demand plan across all versions, stages, and consensus outcomes within the S&OP/IBP cycle for each SKU/location/channel/planning period combination. Each record represents a specific version-point: captures version name, version type (statistical baseline/field sales overlay/marketing-adjusted/consensus/final approved), planning cycle reference, created-by persona, approval status, effective date range, planning bucket (weekly/monthly), statistical baseline quantity, commercial overlay, marketing event uplift, new product launch volume, consensus quantity, variance to statistical baseline, channel disaggregation, promotional overlays, and lifecycle stage. The consensus version represents the single agreed demand signal passed to supply planning. Enables forecast accuracy benchmarking across versions. Sourced from SAP IBP Demand Planning module.'
}

Table supply.plan {
  plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the supply plan record. Primary key for the supply plan entity.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Production scheduling must align with marketing campaign launches; campaign_id enables Campaign‑Supply Execution dashboard.']
  carbon_offset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.carbon_offset. Business justification: Supply planning incorporates carbon offset purchases to meet carbon‑reduction goals; offset ID must be stored on each plan.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: CARRIER_ASSIGNMENT: Supply plan assigns a primary carrier for the planned shipments, required for cost and SLA calculations.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost allocation of each supply plan to a finance cost center for budgeting and variance analysis; planners routinely charge production plans to cost centers.']
  demand_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.demand_plan. Business justification: A supply plan is derived from a specific demand plan; linking plan to demand_plan captures the parent‑child relationship and enables traceability of plan origins.']
  distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution center or warehouse location for which this supply plan applies.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supply planner or demand planner responsible for this supply plan.']
  lane_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.lane. Business justification: TRANSPORT_PLANNING: Supply plan defines the transportation lane for moving goods; planners need lane_id to generate accurate movement schedules.']
  manufacturing_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant or production facility where the SKU will be produced.']
  planning_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.planning_run. Business justification: A supply plan is generated as part of a planning run; adding planning_run_id creates the proper parent link and allows removal of the redundant planning_run_code attribute.']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promotion_event. Business justification: REQUIRED: S&OP supply planning must align production/replenishment with upcoming promotion events to meet forecasted uplift.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Business process: Supply Planning for a new product requires linking each supply_plan to the rd_project that defines the product, ensuring capacity and material plans reflect R&D intent.']
  regulatory_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.registration. Business justification: Supply planning must avoid producing or allocating unregistered SKUs; registration link validates plan feasibility.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific SKU for which this supply plan is created.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the external supplier if the supply source is external procurement or contract manufacturing.']
  supply_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the source location (plant or DC) if the supply source is a transfer from another internal location.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.trade_account. Business justification: REQUIRED: Store‑specific supply plan ties replenishment to the owning trade_account; essential for execution and KPI reporting.']
  atp_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of the SKU that is available to promise to customers for delivery during the planning period, based on projected supply and committed demand.']
  capacity_constraint_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supply plan is constrained by production or distribution capacity limitations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this supply plan record was first created in the system.']
  ctp_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of the SKU that can be promised to customers considering both current inventory and planned production capacity.']
  demand_forecast_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The forecasted demand quantity for this SKU at this location during the planning period, used as input to the supply planning process.']
  execution_date date [note: 'The date when this supply plan was executed or committed, triggering downstream production orders or purchase requisitions.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this supply plan record was last updated or modified.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'The number of days required from order release to receipt, including production time, procurement time, or transfer time.']
  material_constraint_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supply plan is constrained by raw material or component availability.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments from the supply planner regarding assumptions, constraints, or special considerations for this supply plan.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of the supply plan record indicating its approval and execution state.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the supply plan indicating whether it is constrained by capacity and material availability, unconstrained (infinite capacity), a scenario analysis, or a simulation.']
  planned_order_release_date date [note: 'The date when the production order or purchase order should be released to meet the planned receipt date, accounting for lead times.']
  planned_production_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of the SKU planned to be produced at the specified plant during the planning period.']
  planned_receipt_date date [note: 'The date when the planned supply quantity is expected to be received or available at the destination location.']
  planned_replenishment_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of the SKU planned to be replenished or transferred to the distribution center during the planning period.']
  planning_bucket varchar [note: 'The time granularity or bucket size used for this supply plan (daily, weekly, monthly, or quarterly aggregation).']
  planning_group varchar [note: 'The organizational planning group or team responsible for managing supply planning for this SKU or location combination.']
  planning_horizon_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the planning horizon covered by this supply plan record.']
  planning_horizon_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the planning horizon covered by this supply plan record.']
  projected_inventory_balance decimal(18,3) [note: 'The projected on-hand inventory balance at the end of the planning period after accounting for planned supply and demand.']
  safety_stock_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The target safety stock quantity maintained as a buffer against demand variability and supply uncertainty for this SKU at this location.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the source system from which this supply plan record originated (e.g., SAP IBP, Oracle Cloud Supply Chain).']
  supply_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'A calculated risk score indicating the likelihood of supply disruption or shortage for this SKU at this location, based on supplier reliability, lead time variability, and demand volatility.']
  supply_source varchar [note: 'The source from which the supply will be obtained: internal production, external procurement from suppliers, transfer from another location, or contract manufacturing.']
  transportation_constraint_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supply plan is constrained by transportation or logistics capacity limitations.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the planned quantities (e.g., cases, pallets, kilograms, liters).']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the supply plan, enabling tracking of plan iterations and scenario comparisons (e.g., baseline, consensus, committed).']

  Note: 'Core master entity representing the constrained and unconstrained supply plan for each SKU/plant/DC combination. Captures planned production quantities, planned replenishment orders, supply source, planning horizon, constraint flags, and IBP supply planning run metadata. Sourced from SAP IBP Supply Planning module.'
}

Table supply.forecast_version {
  forecast_version_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the forecast version record. Primary key.']
  baseline_version_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent or baseline forecast version from which this version was derived (e.g., statistical baseline that was then adjusted). Nullable for original baseline versions.']
  planning_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Sales and Operations Planning (S&OP) or Integrated Business Planning (IBP) cycle to which this forecast version belongs.']
  primary_forecast_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user or persona who created this forecast version (e.g., demand planner, sales analyst, system batch job).']
  sop_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.sop_cycle. Business justification: forecast_version needs to be linked to its planning cycle; added new FK sop_cycle_id referencing sop_cycle.sop_cycle_id. Existing planning_cycle_id column renamed to sop_cycle_id, so removed planning_']
  tertiary_forecast_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified this forecast version record.']
  aggregation_level varchar [note: 'The level of aggregation at which this forecast version is maintained: Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) level, product family, brand, category, sales channel, customer account, geographic region, or global total. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sku|product_family|brand|category|channel|customer|region|global — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  approval_notes varchar [note: 'Comments or notes provided by the approver during the approval process, documenting any conditions, concerns, or guidance related to this forecast version. Nullable if not yet approved or no notes provided.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this forecast version was approved. Nullable if not yet approved.']
  archived_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this forecast version was archived and moved out of active planning use. Nullable if not yet archived.']
  bias_percentage decimal(6,2) [note: 'Forecast bias percentage indicating systematic over-forecasting (positive bias) or under-forecasting (negative bias). A value near zero indicates an unbiased forecast. Nullable if not yet calculated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this forecast version record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for any monetary values associated with this forecast version (e.g., revenue forecasts). Nullable if forecast is purely volume-based.']
  demand_sensing_applied boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether demand sensing algorithms (short-term forecast adjustments based on real-time Point of Sale (POS) data, shipment data, or other leading indicators) were applied to this forecast version.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this forecast version becomes effective and is used for operational planning, Distribution Requirements Planning (DRP), and Available to Promise (ATP) calculations.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'The date until which this forecast version remains effective. Nullable for open-ended versions that remain active until superseded.']
  forecast_accuracy_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated forecast accuracy percentage for this version, measured as the percentage of forecasted demand that matched actual demand. Used for benchmarking version performance and continuous improvement. Nullable if accuracy has not yet been calculated.']
  forecast_source_system varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the source system or application that generated or contributed to this forecast version (e.g., \'SAP IBP\', \'TradeEdge\', \'Excel Upload\', \'Salesforce CG Cloud\').']
  is_active_version boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this forecast version is currently active and being used for planning and execution. Only one version per planning cycle should typically be active at a time.']
  is_consensus_version boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this version represents the consensus forecast agreed upon by cross-functional stakeholders (sales, marketing, supply chain, finance) during the S&OP process.']
  is_final_approved_version boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this version is the final approved forecast that will be used for operational execution, Material Requirements Planning (MRP), and Distribution Requirements Planning (DRP).']
  mean_absolute_percentage_error decimal(5,2) [note: 'Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) metric for this forecast version, measuring the average magnitude of forecast errors as a percentage. Lower values indicate better forecast accuracy. Nullable if not yet calculated.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this forecast version record was last modified.']
  new_product_introduction_included boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether New Product Development (NPD) or New Product Introduction (NPI) launches were incorporated into this forecast version.']
  planning_horizon_end_date date [note: 'The last date of the planning period covered by this forecast version (e.g., end of the fiscal quarter or rolling 18-month horizon).']
  planning_horizon_start_date date [note: 'The first date of the planning period covered by this forecast version (e.g., start of the fiscal quarter or month).']
  promotional_events_included boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether trade promotions, marketing campaigns, and promotional events were factored into this forecast version.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or reason provided when this forecast version was rejected during the approval process. Nullable if version was not rejected.']
  statistical_model_applied varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the statistical forecasting model or algorithm applied to generate or adjust this forecast version (e.g., \'Exponential Smoothing\', \'ARIMA\', \'Machine Learning Ensemble\', \'Holt-Winters\'). Nullable if no statistical model was used.']
  time_bucket varchar [note: 'The time granularity or bucket size used for this forecast version: daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, or annual periods.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure in which forecast quantities are expressed (e.g., \'EA\' for each, \'CS\' for case, \'KG\' for kilogram, \'L\' for liter). Aligns with GS1 standards.']
  version_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code or identifier for the forecast version used in system integrations and reporting (e.g., \'STAT_BASE\', \'CONS_V1\', \'FINAL_APR\').']
  version_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description or notes about this forecast version, including assumptions, adjustments made, special considerations, or rationale for key changes.']
  version_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name assigned to this forecast version (e.g., \'Statistical Baseline Q1 2024\', \'Consensus Forecast March\', \'Final Approved Version\').']
  version_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number indicating the iteration or revision order of this forecast version within the planning cycle (e.g., 1 for first draft, 2 for first revision, 3 for consensus).']
  version_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the forecast version: draft (under construction), in review (submitted for approval), approved (validated and accepted), rejected (not accepted), archived (historical record), or active (currently in use for planning).']
  version_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the forecast version indicating its role in the planning process: statistical baseline (system-generated), field sales overlay (sales team input), marketing adjusted (marketing promotions incorporated), consensus (collaborative agreement), final approved (executive sign-off), or simulation (what-if scenario).']

  Note: 'Tracks each named version or snapshot of the demand forecast within the IBP/S&OP cycle (e.g., statistical baseline, field sales overlay, marketing-adjusted, consensus, final approved). Captures version name, version type, planning cycle reference, created-by persona, approval status, and effective date range. Enables forecast accuracy benchmarking across versions.'
}

Table supply.forecast_accuracy {
  forecast_accuracy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the forecast accuracy measurement record. Primary key for this transactional measurement entity.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the demand planner responsible for the forecast for this SKU/location combination. Enables accountability tracking and planner performance analysis.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the SKU for which forecast accuracy is being measured. Links to the product master data.']
  supply_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the planning location (distribution center, plant, or region) for which forecast accuracy is measured.']
  absolute_percent_error decimal(7,4) [note: 'Absolute percentage error calculated as |Actual - Forecast| / Actual. Used as input for Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) aggregation.']
  accuracy_status varchar [note: 'Status indicating whether the measured forecast accuracy met, exceeded, or fell short of the target. Used for exception-based management and continuous improvement prioritization.']
  accuracy_target_percent decimal(7,4) [note: 'The target forecast accuracy percentage established for this SKU/location/period combination. Used for performance benchmarking and KPI tracking.']
  actual_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The actual demand quantity realized during the planning period. Sourced from shipments, sales orders, or Point of Sale (POS) data depending on the measurement level.']
  bias_percent decimal(7,4) [note: 'Forecast bias expressed as a percentage: (Forecast - Actual) / Actual. Positive bias indicates systematic over-forecasting; negative bias indicates under-forecasting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this forecast accuracy record was first created in the system. Part of standard audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Quality score (0.0 to 1.0) representing the completeness and reliability of the input data used for accuracy calculation. Lower scores indicate missing or questionable data that may compromise measurement validity.']
  demand_sensing_applied_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether demand sensing corrections were applied to the statistical forecast for this SKU/location/period. True if near-term demand signals were incorporated.']
  demand_volatility_coefficient decimal(7,4) [note: 'Coefficient of variation representing demand volatility for the SKU/location combination. Calculated as standard deviation divided by mean demand. Higher values indicate more volatile demand patterns and typically correlate with lower forecast accuracy.']
  forecast_error decimal(18,3) [note: 'The absolute difference between forecast quantity and actual quantity (Actual - Forecast). Positive values indicate under-forecast; negative values indicate over-forecast.']
  forecast_model_type varchar [note: 'The type of forecasting model used to generate the original forecast. Identifies whether the forecast was produced by statistical algorithms, machine learning models, consensus planning, manual planner input, or a hybrid approach.']
  forecast_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The forecasted demand quantity for the SKU/location/period as predicted by the statistical model or demand planning system at the time of forecast creation.']
  forecast_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the forecast used for accuracy measurement. Enables tracking of accuracy across multiple forecast iterations and scenario planning versions within the Integrated Business Planning (IBP) cycle.']
  mape decimal(7,4) [note: 'Mean Absolute Percentage Error for the SKU/location/period. Primary forecast accuracy metric expressed as a percentage. Lower values indicate better accuracy.']
  measurement_cycle varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Sales and Operations Planning (S&OP) or Integrated Business Planning (IBP) cycle during which this accuracy measurement was performed. Typically formatted as YYYY-MM or cycle name.']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the forecast accuracy measurement was calculated and recorded. Represents the business event time of the accuracy calculation cycle close.']
  new_product_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this SKU was classified as a new product during the forecast period. New Product Development (NPD) items typically have limited historical data and lower forecast accuracy.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing contextual information about the accuracy measurement, including explanations for significant variances, data quality issues, or special circumstances affecting the period.']
  out_of_stock_days varchar [note: 'Number of days during the planning period when the SKU was out of stock at the location. Out of Stock (OOS) events distort accuracy measurement as actual demand is constrained by availability.']
  percent decimal(7,4) [note: 'Forecast accuracy percentage calculated as 100% - MAPE. Represents the complement of error, where higher values indicate better accuracy. Commonly used for executive reporting.']
  planning_level varchar [note: 'The hierarchical level at which forecast accuracy is being measured. Supports accuracy tracking at SKU/location, product family, brand, category, or total company aggregation levels.']
  planning_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the planning period for which forecast accuracy is being measured.']
  planning_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the planning period for which forecast accuracy is being measured. Typically aligned with S&OP cycle boundaries.']
  product_lifecycle_stage varchar [note: 'The lifecycle stage of the SKU at the time of forecast measurement. Accuracy expectations and benchmarks vary by lifecycle stage, with new product introductions (NPD) typically having lower accuracy than mature products.']
  promotional_period_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the planning period included promotional activity for this SKU/location. Promotional periods typically exhibit different accuracy patterns than baseline demand periods.']
  sensed_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The demand-sensed quantity after incorporating near-term signals such as Point of Sale (POS) data, syndicated market data from Nielsen IQ, or secondary sales from TradeEdge. Null if demand sensing was not applied.']
  sensing_confidence_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Confidence score (0.0 to 1.0) representing the reliability of the demand sensing signal. Higher scores indicate stronger signal quality and greater confidence in the sensed adjustment.']
  sensing_horizon_days varchar [note: 'The number of days into the future for which demand sensing was applied. Typically ranges from 7 to 90 days depending on product velocity and signal availability.']
  sensing_signal_source varchar [note: 'The primary data source used for demand sensing correction. Identifies whether the signal came from Point of Sale (POS) data, Nielsen IQ syndicated feeds, TradeEdge secondary sales, shipment actuals, order backlog, promotional lift models, or manual planner override. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pos_data|nielsen_iq|trade_edge|shipment_data|order_backlog|promotional_lift|manual_override — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sensing_uplift_percent decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage uplift or downlift applied by demand sensing relative to the statistical forecast. Calculated as (Sensed Quantity - Forecast Quantity) / Forecast Quantity. Positive values indicate uplift; negative values indicate downlift.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure in which forecast and actual quantities are expressed (e.g., cases, eaches, kilograms, liters). Must be consistent across forecast and actual for valid accuracy calculation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this forecast accuracy record was last modified. Supports change tracking and audit requirements.']
  wmape decimal(7,4) [note: 'Weighted Mean Absolute Percentage Error, calculated as sum of absolute errors divided by sum of actuals. Preferred metric for aggregated accuracy measurement across product hierarchies.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing forecast accuracy measurements and demand sensing corrections for each SKU/location/planning period. Stores accuracy metrics (MAPE, WMAPE, bias, FA%, forecast error), actuals vs forecast volumes, measurement period, benchmark targets, and near-term demand sensing signals (POS data, syndicated feeds from Nielsen IQ, TradeEdge secondary sales). Captures signal source, sensing horizon, sensed quantity, confidence score, and uplift/downlift versus statistical forecast. Supports both cycle-close accuracy measurement and short-horizon demand correction within SAP IBP. Enables continuous improvement of statistical models and demand sensing algorithms.'
}

Table supply.inventory_policy {
  inventory_policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inventory policy record. Primary key for this entity.']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Inventory policies are driven by specific compliance obligations (e.g., temperature control); linking ties policy to obligation.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Inventory policies are owned by specific cost centers; linking enables policy‑related budgeting and compliance audits.']
  policy_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Inventory Policy Governance requires a policy owner employee to approve and maintain each policy.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Business need: Inventory policies for launch items are derived from the associated R&D project, allowing policy parameters to be set per project.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) for which this inventory policy is defined.']
  supply_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution facility, warehouse, or storage location node in the supply network where this policy applies.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when this inventory policy was formally approved and authorized for use in supply planning and execution.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow status indicating whether the policy has been reviewed and approved by authorized supply planning personnel.']
  approved_by_name varchar [note: 'Name of the supply planning manager or director who approved this inventory policy for operational use.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inventory policy record was first created in the system.']
  customer_otif_commitment_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Specific OTIF performance level committed to a customer or channel, which may differ from internal targets and may be contractually binding.']
  cycle_stock_target_units decimal(18,3) [note: 'Target cycle stock quantity representing the average inventory held to meet expected demand between replenishments, excluding safety stock.']
  demand_variability_coefficient decimal(10,4) [note: 'Statistical measure of demand variability (coefficient of variation or standard deviation) used in safety stock calculations.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this inventory policy expires or is superseded. Null indicates an open-ended policy.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this inventory policy becomes active and applicable for planning and execution.']
  fill_rate_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage of customer demand that should be fulfilled from available inventory without backorders or stockouts.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inventory policy record was last updated or modified.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when this inventory policy was last reviewed and validated by the supply planning team.']
  lead_time_variability_days decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard deviation or variance of replenishment lead time in days, used to account for supply uncertainty in safety stock calculations.']
  maximum_stock_level_units decimal(18,3) [note: 'Maximum inventory level allowed at this location for this SKU, used to prevent overstocking and manage warehouse capacity constraints.']
  measurement_window_days varchar [note: 'Time period in days over which service level performance is measured and evaluated against targets (e.g., rolling 30 days, 90 days, quarterly).']
  minimum_stock_level_units decimal(18,3) [note: 'Minimum inventory level that should be maintained at this location for this SKU, typically aligned with safety stock or regulatory requirements.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next review and update of this inventory policy, ensuring policies remain aligned with business conditions.']
  on_time_delivery_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage of orders that should be delivered by the customer-requested or committed delivery date.']
  otif_composite_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite OTIF (On Time In Full) target percentage representing orders delivered both on time and in full quantity, a critical customer service metric.']
  penalty_clause_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether failure to meet the defined service level targets will result in financial penalties, chargebacks, or other contractual consequences.']
  policy_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the inventory policy, used for external reference and reporting.']
  policy_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing special considerations, exceptions, seasonal adjustments, or business context relevant to this inventory policy.']
  policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inventory policy indicating its approval and operational state. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|active|suspended|expired|archived — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reorder_point_units decimal(18,3) [note: 'Inventory level at which a replenishment order should be triggered, calculated as lead time demand plus safety stock.']
  replenishment_method varchar [note: 'The inventory replenishment strategy applied: MRP (Material Requirements Planning), DRP (Distribution Requirements Planning), VMI (Vendor Managed Inventory), JIT (Just In Time), Kanban, or Fixed Order Quantity.']
  retailer_mandated_target_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service level targets are mandated by a retail customer as part of their supplier requirements or vendor compliance program.']
  review_cycle_days varchar [note: 'Frequency in days at which inventory levels are reviewed and replenishment decisions are made for this SKU-location combination.']
  safety_stock_calculated_units decimal(18,3) [note: 'System-calculated safety stock quantity based on demand variability, lead time variability, and service level inputs before manual approval or adjustment.']
  safety_stock_calculation_method varchar [note: 'The methodology used to calculate safety stock: fixed value, forecast error-based, demand variability, lead time variability, combined variability, or service level-driven calculation.']
  safety_stock_days_of_supply decimal(10,2) [note: 'Safety stock expressed as the number of days of average demand coverage, providing a time-based view of inventory buffer.']
  safety_stock_target_units decimal(18,3) [note: 'Target safety stock quantity in base units of measure, representing the buffer inventory to protect against demand and supply variability.']
  service_level_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target service level percentage (e.g., 95%, 98%, 99.5%) representing the desired probability of not stocking out during the replenishment lead time.']

  Note: 'Master entity defining inventory policy parameters, safety stock calculations, service level commitments, and OTIF targets for each SKU/location node in the supply network. Captures safety stock target and calculated quantities (days of supply, units, calculation method, demand variability, lead time variability, service level input, calculated vs approved SS units, effective date, review status), reorder point, min/max levels, cycle stock target, replenishment method (MRP/DRP/VMI), review cycle, service level targets (fill rate %, on-time delivery %, OTIF composite %), customer/channel-level OTIF commitments, retailer-mandated target flags, penalty clause indicators, and measurement windows. Owned by supply planning; consumed by distribution and manufacturing execution.'
}

Table supply.safety_stock {
  safety_stock_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the safety stock calculation record. Primary key for the safety stock entity.']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Safety‑stock calculations must respect compliance constraints such as shelf‑life limits; link provides required obligation data.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the demand planner or supply planner responsible for reviewing and approving this safety stock calculation. Links to workforce or user master data.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Business process: Safety stock calculations for a new SKU reference the originating rd_project to capture launch‑specific risk and service‑level targets.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific product SKU for which safety stock is calculated. Links to the product master data.']
  supply_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the storage location, distribution center, or facility where the safety stock applies. Links to distribution facility master data.']
  abc_classification varchar [note: 'The ABC classification of the SKU based on revenue contribution or volume. A items are high-value requiring tight control; B items are moderate; C items are low-value. Influences service level targets and safety stock investment decisions.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the safety stock calculation was approved by the planner. Marks the transition from calculated to approved state in the workflow.']
  approved_safety_stock_units decimal(15,3) [note: 'The final approved safety stock quantity in base units of measure after planner review and adjustment. This is the target value used for inventory policy execution and Available to Promise (ATP) calculations.']
  average_daily_demand_units decimal(15,3) [note: 'The mean daily demand quantity in base units of measure. Used as input to safety stock calculations and represents expected consumption rate.']
  average_lead_time_days decimal(8,2) [note: 'The mean replenishment lead time in days from order placement to receipt. Used in safety stock calculations to determine the exposure period for demand and supply variability.']
  calculated_safety_stock_units decimal(15,3) [note: 'The system-calculated safety stock quantity in base units of measure. This is the raw output from the calculation engine before any manual adjustments or approvals.']
  calculation_method varchar [note: 'The methodology used to calculate the safety stock quantity. Statistical methods use demand and lead time variability; days-of-supply uses fixed coverage targets; manual override represents planner judgment; service level and lead time methods focus on specific risk factors; hybrid combines multiple approaches.']
  calculation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the safety stock calculation was executed by the planning system. Provides audit trail for calculation runs and version control.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this safety stock record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for record lifecycle tracking.']
  days_of_supply_target decimal(6,2) [note: 'The target safety stock coverage expressed in days of average demand. Used when calculation method is days-of-supply based. Represents the buffer duration to protect against stockouts.']
  demand_classification varchar [note: 'The demand pattern category for this SKU-location. Steady indicates consistent demand; seasonal shows periodic peaks; erratic has high variability; lumpy has infrequent large orders; intermittent has sporadic demand; obsolete indicates phase-out. Influences calculation method selection.']
  demand_variability_coefficient decimal(10,4) [note: 'Statistical measure of demand volatility expressed as coefficient of variation or standard deviation. Used in statistical safety stock calculations to quantify demand uncertainty.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date when this safety stock target becomes active and is used for inventory policy execution, Distribution Requirements Planning (DRP), and Available to Promise (ATP) calculations.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date when this safety stock target expires and is replaced by a new calculation. Used to manage the lifecycle of safety stock policies and trigger recalculation cycles.']
  forecast_accuracy_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The historical forecast accuracy for this SKU-location combination expressed as a percentage. Used to adjust safety stock calculations based on forecasting reliability. Lower accuracy typically requires higher safety stock.']
  holding_cost_per_unit decimal(15,4) [note: 'The annual cost to hold one unit of inventory including warehousing, insurance, obsolescence, and capital cost. Used in inventory optimization to balance service level against carrying cost. Expressed in local currency.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this safety stock record is currently active and being used for inventory policy execution. True means active; False means superseded or inactive.']
  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the safety stock record was last reviewed by a planner, regardless of whether changes were made. Used to track planner engagement and ensure regular review cycles.']
  lead_time_variability_days decimal(8,2) [note: 'Measure of supply lead time uncertainty expressed in days. Represents the standard deviation or range of lead time fluctuation used in safety stock calculations.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The minimum order quantity constraint from the supplier or manufacturing process. Safety stock calculations must consider MOQ to ensure replenishment orders are feasible.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this safety stock record was last modified. Updated whenever any field changes. Supports change tracking and audit requirements.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next safety stock review cycle. Used in Sales and Operations Planning (S&OP) and Integrated Business Planning (IBP) processes to ensure regular policy updates.']
  order_multiple decimal(15,3) [note: 'The order quantity increment or lot size multiple required by supplier or production constraints. Safety stock and reorder quantities must align with this multiple.']
  override_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation provided by the planner when manually overriding the calculated safety stock. Documents the business context and rationale for the adjustment.']
  override_reason_code varchar [note: 'The business reason code when approved safety stock differs from calculated safety stock. Captures planner judgment factors such as promotional events, seasonal peaks, supply chain risks, new product introductions, product phase-outs, or quality concerns.']
  planning_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the planning period for which this safety stock calculation is effective. Defines the validity window for the safety stock target.']
  planning_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the planning period for which this safety stock calculation is effective. Used in Sales and Operations Planning (S&OP) and Integrated Business Planning (IBP) cycles.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'The approval workflow status of the safety stock calculation. Pending review indicates awaiting planner action; approved means active for execution; rejected requires recalculation; under revision is being adjusted; expired indicates the planning period has passed.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'The shelf life or expiration period of the product in days. Critical for Fast-Moving Consumer Goods (FMCG) and Consumer Packaged Goods (CPG) safety stock decisions to prevent obsolescence. Influences maximum safety stock levels and First Expired First Out (FEFO) policies.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The system of record that generated or last updated this safety stock calculation. SAP IBP for integrated business planning, SAP S/4HANA for ERP-based calculations, Oracle Cloud SCM for Oracle supply chain, Blue Yonder for advanced optimization, TradeEdge for demand sensing, or Manual for planner-entered values.']
  stockout_cost_per_unit decimal(15,4) [note: 'The estimated cost of a stockout per unit including lost sales, expediting costs, customer dissatisfaction, and brand impact. Used in service level optimization to quantify the cost of Out of Stock (OOS) events. Expressed in local currency.']
  supply_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'A quantitative risk score assessing supply chain vulnerability for this SKU-location. Higher scores indicate greater risk from supplier reliability, geopolitical factors, transportation disruptions, or single-source dependencies. Used to adjust safety stock buffers.']
  target_service_level_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The desired in-stock probability or fill rate target expressed as a percentage (e.g., 95.00, 98.50). Drives the safety stock buffer size to achieve the specified On Shelf Availability (OSA) or Service Level Agreement (SLA).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for safety stock quantities. EA=Each, CS=Case, PL=Pallet, KG=Kilogram, LB=Pound, L=Liter, GAL=Gallon. Must align with SKU base unit of measure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PL|KG|LB|L|GAL — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  xyz_classification varchar [note: 'The XYZ classification based on demand variability. X items have low variability (predictable); Y items have moderate variability; Z items have high variability (unpredictable). Used alongside ABC to determine safety stock strategy.']
  z_score decimal(6,4) [note: 'The standard normal distribution value corresponding to the target service level. Used in statistical safety stock formulas to translate service level percentage into buffer quantity.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the calculated and approved safety stock quantity for each SKU/location/time-period combination. Stores calculation method (statistical, days-of-supply, manual override), demand variability, lead time variability, service level input, calculated SS units, approved SS units, effective date, and review status. Distinct from inventory_policy (policy target) as this captures the actual calculated output.'
}

Table supply.supply_replenishment_order {
  supply_replenishment_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the supply replenishment order record. Primary key for this entity.']
  carrier_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier_contract. Business justification: CONTRACT_COMPLIANCE: Each replenishment order is governed by a carrier contract; needed for rate and penalty enforcement.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the logistics carrier or transportation provider assigned to execute this replenishment shipment.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Replenishment orders incur expenses that must be tracked against the responsible cost center for expense reporting and internal charge‑backs.']
  destination_location_supply_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supply network node to which inventory will be delivered (distribution center, warehouse, or retail location).']
  drp_run_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the DRP execution run that generated this replenishment order, used for traceability and audit.']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: Regulatory traceability requires linking each replenishment order to its inspection lot for recall and safety reporting; experts expect this FK for product safety audits.']
  lane_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.lane. Business justification: ORDER_EXECUTION: Replenishment order execution follows a specific lane; lane_id links order to transportation routing.']
  logistics_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical shipment record associated with this replenishment order once released for execution.']
  primary_supply_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user or automated process that created this replenishment order record.']
  primary_supply_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supply network node from which inventory will be shipped (plant, distribution center, or warehouse).']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promotion_event. Business justification: REQUIRED: Replenishment orders are often generated to satisfy demand generated by a specific promotion event.']
  regulatory_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.registration. Business justification: Regulatory compliance verification before order release ensures SKU has active registration; essential for legal shipment.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product SKU being replenished through this order.']
  actual_receipt_date date [note: 'Actual date the goods were received at the destination location.']
  actual_ship_date date [note: 'Actual date the shipment departed from the source location.']
  available_to_promise_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'ATP quantity at the source location at the time the replenishment order was created, used to validate supply availability.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description explaining why the replenishment order was cancelled, if applicable.']
  confirmed_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of the SKU confirmed for shipment by the source location, may differ from requested quantity due to supply constraints.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this replenishment order record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this order.']
  drp_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the DRP execution run that generated this order was executed.']
  forecast_demand_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Forecasted demand quantity at the destination location that drove the replenishment calculation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this replenishment order record was last modified in the system.']
  order_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cost associated with this replenishment order, including product cost, transportation cost, and handling fees.']
  order_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or special instructions related to this replenishment order, such as handling requirements or routing constraints.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current execution status of the replenishment order in the supply chain workflow.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the replenishment order lifecycle stage: planned (system-generated recommendation), firmed (manually confirmed but not released), or released (committed for execution).']
  planned_receipt_date date [note: 'Target date for goods receipt at the destination location, accounting for transit lead time.']
  planned_ship_date date [note: 'Target date for shipment departure from the source location as calculated by the DRP engine.']
  priority_code varchar [note: 'Priority classification for order fulfillment, typically driven by stock-out risk, customer service level, or strategic importance.']
  received_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Actual quantity of the SKU received at the destination location, captured at goods receipt.']
  replenishment_order_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique order number for the replenishment order, used for tracking and reference across systems and communications.']
  requested_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of the SKU requested for replenishment as calculated by the DRP engine or demand planner.']
  safety_stock_trigger_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this replenishment order was triggered by inventory falling below safety stock threshold at the destination location.']
  shipped_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Actual quantity of the SKU shipped from the source location, captured at dispatch.']
  transit_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Planned number of days for goods to travel from source to destination location.']
  transportation_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation planned or used for this replenishment shipment.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for all quantity fields in this order (EA=Each, CS=Case, PL=Pallet, KG=Kilogram, LB=Pound, L=Liter, GAL=Gallon). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PL|EA|KG|LB|L|GAL — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Transactional record representing a planned or released replenishment order generated by DRP execution for moving inventory between supply network nodes (plant-to-DC, DC-to-DC). Captures order type (planned/firmed/released), source location, destination location, SKU, requested quantity, confirmed quantity, planned ship date, planned receipt date, and DRP run reference. Distinct from purchase orders (owned by procurement) and production orders (owned by manufacturing).'
}

Table supply.network_node {
  network_node_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for network_node']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Operating costs of each network node (warehouse, DC) are allocated to a cost center for overhead accounting and performance tracking.']
  distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_facility. Business justification: Required for Logistics Network Planning report to map each supply network node to its physical distribution center, enabling accurate cross‑dock and VMI planning.']
