  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this demand event record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for all monetary amounts in this record.']
  discontinuation_reason varchar [note: 'For product discontinuation events, the business rationale for discontinuing the SKU (e.g., low sales, regulatory, reformulation, portfolio rationalization).']
  event_code varchar [note: 'Business-assigned unique code for the demand event, used for external reference and cross-system tracking.']
  event_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the demand event, including business rationale, scope, and expected market impact.']
  event_end_date date [note: 'The date when the demand event ceases to impact demand. Null for indefinite events such as permanent price changes or discontinuations.']
  event_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the demand event for business user identification and reporting.']
  event_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for planner comments, assumptions, risks, or additional context about the demand event.']
  event_start_date date [note: 'The date when the demand event begins to impact demand. For promotions, this is the promotion start date. For launches, this is the market availability date.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the demand event. Controls whether the event is included in demand planning calculations.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the demand event indicating the nature of the business activity that creates demand variance. Determines which event-specific attributes are populated.']
  initial_stocking_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'For new product launches, the initial pipeline fill quantity required to stock distribution centers and retail shelves before consumer demand begins.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this demand event record was most recently updated.']
  last_order_date date [note: 'For product discontinuation events, the final date on which orders for the SKU will be accepted.']
  last_ship_date date [note: 'For product discontinuation events, the final date on which the SKU will be shipped to customers.']
  launch_channel_scope varchar [note: 'For new product launches, describes which distribution channels and customer segments are included in the launch (e.g., DTC-only, mass retail, specialty, omnichannel).']
  market_geography_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code or market region code where the demand event applies. For new product launches, indicates launch market scope.']
  planning_run_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the demand planning run or Sales and Operations Planning (S&OP) cycle in which this event was incorporated into the forecast.']
  price_change_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'For price change events, the absolute change in unit price. Positive for price increases, negative for price decreases.']
  price_change_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'For price change events, the percentage change in unit price relative to the previous price.']
  price_elasticity_coefficient decimal(6,4) [note: 'For price change events, the estimated price elasticity of demand used to calculate volume impact. Negative values indicate normal goods (price up, demand down).']
  promotion_funding_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'For trade promotion events, the total budget allocated to fund the promotion, including trade spend, discounts, and allowances.']
  promotion_type varchar [note: 'For trade promotion events, the specific promotion mechanism (e.g., temporary price reduction, buy-one-get-one, display allowance, feature ad).']
  ramp_up_weeks varchar [note: 'For new product launches, the number of weeks over which demand is expected to ramp from zero to steady-state volume.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this demand event was created or synchronized.']
  source_system_event_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this demand event in the source system, used for traceability and reconciliation.']
  supply_readiness_status varchar [note: 'For new product launches, indicates whether manufacturing, procurement, and supply chain are ready to support the launch volume. Critical gate for launch approval.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for volume impact quantities. Aligns with SKU base unit of measure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PL|KG|LB|L|GAL — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  volume_impact_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage change in demand relative to the statistical baseline forecast. Positive for uplift, negative for downlift.']
  volume_impact_units decimal(18,3) [note: 'Absolute incremental or decremental demand volume attributed to this event, expressed in base units. Positive for uplift events, negative for downlift events.']

  Note: 'Master entity capturing planned business events that create demand uplift or downlift adjustments to the statistical forecast. Includes trade promotions, new product launches (with ramp-up volume curves, cannibalization impacts, and supply readiness status), product discontinuations, price changes, seasonal events, and market expansion activities. Stores event type, affected SKUs, affected customers/channels, volume impact (units and percentage), event start/end date, confidence level, initial stocking quantity (for launches), launch market/channel scope, and source system reference. Provides the causal layer that explains forecast adjustments beyond statistical extrapolation and bridges R&D/product NPD pipeline with supply chain readiness planning.'
}

Table supply.otif_target {
  otif_target_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the OTIF service level target record.']
  supply_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.network_node. Business justification: otif_target is defined per supply node; added new FK supply_network_node_id referencing network_node.supply_network_node_id.']
  target_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: OTIF Performance Ownership assigns a responsible employee for each OTIF target in the OTIF Management Dashboard.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific customer account for which this OTIF target is defined. Null if target applies to a customer segment or channel rather than individual customer.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this OTIF target was formally approved and authorized for use in supply planning and performance measurement.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for this OTIF target definition, indicating whether it has been reviewed and approved by authorized stakeholders.']
  approved_by_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person who approved this OTIF target definition, typically a supply chain director or customer service leader.']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Distribution channel classification for which this OTIF target is defined (retail, wholesale, direct-to-consumer, e-commerce, foodservice, export).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this OTIF target record was first created in the system.']
  customer_segment_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the customer segment (e.g., national retailers, regional chains, independent stores) to which this OTIF target applies.']
  delivery_tolerance_hours varchar [note: 'Acceptable time window variance (in hours) before or after the scheduled delivery time within which delivery is still considered on-time.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this OTIF target expires or is superseded by a new target definition. Null indicates an open-ended commitment.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this OTIF target becomes effective and is used for performance measurement and compliance evaluation.']
  escalation_threshold_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Performance threshold below which OTIF failures trigger escalation to senior management or customer service recovery actions.']
  in_full_fill_rate_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage for in-full order fulfillment, representing the proportion of orders that must be delivered with 100% of requested quantity.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this OTIF target record, used for change tracking and audit purposes.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent review of this OTIF target definition to assess continued relevance and appropriateness.']
  measurement_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which OTIF performance against this target is measured and reported to stakeholders.']
  measurement_window_days varchar [note: 'Number of days over which OTIF performance is measured and evaluated against the target (e.g., rolling 30 days, quarterly 90 days).']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this OTIF target to ensure alignment with business strategy and customer expectations.']
  on_time_delivery_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage for on-time delivery performance, representing the proportion of orders that must be delivered within the agreed delivery window.']
  otif_composite_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite OTIF target percentage representing orders that must meet both on-time AND in-full criteria simultaneously. This is the primary service level commitment metric.']
  penalty_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether financial penalties or chargebacks apply when OTIF performance falls below the target threshold.']
  penalty_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage rate applied to calculate financial penalties when OTIF targets are not met, typically applied to order value or invoice amount.']
  planning_group varchar [note: 'Supply planning team or organizational unit responsible for achieving this OTIF target and managing associated supply plans.']
  priority_tier varchar [note: 'Priority classification for this OTIF target, indicating the strategic importance and resource allocation priority (tier 1 = highest priority customers).']
  product_category_code varchar [note: 'Product category or hierarchy level to which this OTIF target applies, enabling category-specific service level commitments.']
  quantity_tolerance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Acceptable variance percentage for order quantity fulfillment. Orders within this tolerance are considered in-full (e.g., 98% fulfillment may be acceptable).']
  retailer_mandated_target_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this OTIF target is mandated by the retailer/customer as part of their vendor compliance program versus internally set by the manufacturer.']
  sla_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the formal SLA document or contract section that defines this OTIF commitment.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system from which this OTIF target record originated, typically SAP IBP or customer SLA management system.']
  target_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the OTIF target definition, used for external reference and reporting.']
  target_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the OTIF target configuration, typically indicating the customer segment or channel it applies to.']
  target_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context, special conditions, or business rationale for this OTIF target definition.']
  target_owner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the business owner or supply chain manager accountable for achieving this OTIF target.']
  target_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the OTIF target definition (active, inactive, pending approval, expired, suspended).']

  Note: 'Master entity defining On-Time-In-Full (OTIF) service level targets by customer, channel, and product category. Captures on-time delivery target (%), in-full fill rate target (%), OTIF composite target (%), measurement window, penalty clause flag, retailer-mandated target flag, and effective date range. Provides the service level commitment baseline for supply planning and distribution execution.'
}

Table supply.atp_rule {
  atp_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for atp_rule']
  fallback_atp_rule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on atp_rule (fallback_atp_rule_id)']
  action_expression varchar [note: 'Expression defining the allocation or availability action taken when the condition is met.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate inventory when the rule is applied.']
  condition_expression varchar [note: 'Logical expression that must be satisfied for the rule to fire.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the ATP rule.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the ATP rule record was first created.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the rule\'s purpose and logic.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the rule becomes valid.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the rule is no longer applicable (null if open‑ended).']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this rule is the default fallback rule.']
  max_allocation_qty decimal(12,2) [note: 'Upper limit on quantity that can be allocated by this rule.']
  min_allocation_qty decimal(12,2) [note: 'Lower limit on quantity that must be allocated when the rule fires.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority determining rule evaluation order; lower numbers indicate higher precedence.']
  rule_category varchar [note: 'Supply‑chain domain where the rule is applied (e.g., order level, line level, inventory level).']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code used to reference the ATP rule in downstream systems.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the ATP rule.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Category of the ATP rule defining its logical purpose.']
  safety_stock_factor decimal(5,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to safety stock calculations within the rule.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rule.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the ATP rule record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for atp_rule. Referenced by atp_rule_id.'
}

Table supply.planning_period {
  planning_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for planning_period']
  previous_planning_period_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on planning_period (previous_planning_period_id)']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the planning period record was created.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the planning period purpose.']
  duration_days varchar [note: 'Number of days covered by the planning period.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the period becomes effective for planning purposes.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the period ceases to be effective (nullable for open‑ended).']
  end_date date [note: 'Last calendar day of the planning period.']
  is_historical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the period is historical (true) or future (false).']
  period_code varchar [note: 'External code used in planning systems to reference the period.']
  period_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the planning period (e.g., FY2024 Q1).']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Category of the period (fiscal, calendar, rolling, forecast).']
  planning_horizon varchar [note: 'Planning horizon classification for the period.']
  start_date date [note: 'First calendar day of the planning period.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the period.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'Time zone applicable to the period timestamps.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the planning period record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for planning_period. Referenced by planning_period_id.'
}

Table supply.planning_run {
  planning_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for planning_run']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that initiated the planning run.']
  previous_planning_run_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on planning_run (previous_planning_run_id)']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the planning run record was first created in the system.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the input data used for the planning run.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time of the run measured in minutes.']
  error_message varchar [note: 'Error details captured when the run fails or encounters issues.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations captured by the planner.']
  outcome_metric varchar [note: 'Key quantitative metric produced by the run (e.g., total forecast error, service level).']
  planning_algorithm varchar [note: 'Name or code of the algorithm/model used to generate the plan.']
  planning_horizon_end date [note: 'Last date of the planning horizon covered by the run.']
  planning_horizon_start date [note: 'First date of the planning horizon covered by the run.']
  result_summary varchar [note: 'High‑level textual summary of the planning outcomes.']
  run_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the planning run execution finished.']
  run_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name given to the planning run for easy identification.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'External reference number or code used by business users to identify the run.']
  run_priority varchar [note: 'Business‑assigned priority level influencing scheduling and resource allocation.']
  run_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the planning run execution began.']
  run_type varchar [note: 'Category of planning activity performed in the run (e.g., demand, supply, inventory, S&OP, optimization).']
  scenario_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the scenario (e.g., baseline, best‑case, worst‑case) used for this run.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the planning run.']
  success_flag boolean [note: 'True if the run completed without errors; otherwise False.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the planning run record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the run, useful for tracking iterative improvements.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for planning_run. Referenced by planning_run_id.'
}

Table supply.drp_run {
  drp_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for drp_run']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the planner (person or team) responsible for initiating the DRP run.']
  last_successful_run_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the most recent successful DRP run preceding this one.']
  previous_drp_run_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on drp_run (previous_drp_run_id)']
  algorithm_version varchar [note: 'Version of the DRP algorithm or model applied during the run.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by the planner or system about the DRP run.']
  compliance_check_passed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the run passed all configured compliance validations.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person or service that created the DRP run record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the DRP run record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary values in this DRP run.']
  data_snapshot_date date [note: 'Date of the master data snapshot (inventory, demand) used as input for the run.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the DRP run execution finished or was terminated.']
  forecast_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the demand forecast used for this DRP run.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the DRP run was triggered automatically (true) or manually (false).']
  planning_horizon_days varchar [note: 'Number of days covered by the DRP planning horizon.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the DRP run.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO region code indicating the geographic scope of the DRP run.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated risk score for the supply plan generated by this run (0‑100 scale).']
  run_code varchar [note: 'External code used to reference the DRP run in reports and interfaces.']
  run_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total execution time of the DRP run measured in seconds.']
  run_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the purpose or scenario of the DRP run.']
  run_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the DRP run execution mode.']
  scenario_name varchar [note: 'Name of the planning scenario (e.g., "Base", "Optimistic", "Pessimistic").']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that generated the DRP run (e.g., SAP IBP).']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the DRP run execution began.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the DRP run.']
  total_actual_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate cost realized after execution of the supply plan (if available).']
  total_planned_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate cost forecasted for the supply plan generated by this DRP run.']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person or service that last updated the DRP run record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the DRP run record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for drp_run. Referenced by drp_run_id.'
}

Table sales.trade_account {
  trade_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the B2B trade account. Primary key for the trade account master record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost allocation report requires each customer linked to a cost center for internal expense tracking and profitability analysis.']
  distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_facility. Business justification: Logistics planning assigns each trade account a primary distribution center for order fulfillment and inventory allocation.']
  esg_commitment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.esg_commitment. Business justification: Retailer ESG reporting requires each trade account to be linked to its ESG commitments for sustainability scorecards and supplier contracts.']
  inventory_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_storage_location. Business justification: VMI replenishment planning requires each trade account to have a designated warehouse (storage location); the default_storage_location_id FK enables the VMI allocation report.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability analysis assigns each customer to a profit center to aggregate revenue and margin in financial reporting.']
  account_close_date date [note: 'The date when this trade account was permanently closed or terminated, if applicable.']
  account_open_date date [note: 'The date when this trade account was first established and approved for business transactions.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of the trade account: active (operational), inactive (dormant), suspended (temporarily blocked), pending approval (new account under review), credit hold (payment issues), or closed (permanently terminated).']
  account_tier varchar [note: 'Strategic tier classification based on account size, coverage, and business importance: Tier 1 (national key accounts), Tier 2 (regional chains), Tier 3 (local/metro accounts), Tier 4 (independent operators).']
  account_type varchar [note: 'The business model classification of the trade partner: retailer (sells to end consumers), distributor (logistics and fulfillment), wholesaler (bulk reseller), foodservice operator (restaurants/catering), or broker (sales intermediary).']
  acv_total decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total All Commodity Volume representing the annual dollar sales volume across all product categories for this retail account, used for market coverage analysis.']
  credit_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum outstanding accounts receivable balance allowed for this trade account before credit hold is applied.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'The credit risk rating assigned to this trade account by internal credit management or external credit bureau (e.g., AAA, AA, A, BBB, BB, B, CCC, D).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The 3-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all financial transactions with this trade account (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR, MXN).']
  dba_name varchar [note: 'The trade name or DBA name under which the account operates in the market, if different from legal entity name.']
  dsd_delivery_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this trade account receives direct store delivery (DSD) shipments bypassing the retailers distribution center.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'The 9-digit DUNS number assigned by Dun & Bradstreet for unique identification of the business entity.']
  edi_capable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this trade account has EDI connectivity enabled for electronic exchange of purchase orders, invoices, and advance ship notices.']
  gln varchar [note: 'The 13-digit GS1 Global Location Number uniquely identifying the legal entity and physical location of the trade partner.']
  headquarters_address_line1 varchar [note: 'The first line of the street address for the trade account headquarters or primary business location.']
  headquarters_address_line2 varchar [note: 'The second line of the street address (suite, floor, building) for the trade account headquarters.']
  headquarters_city varchar [note: 'The city name of the trade account headquarters location.']
  headquarters_country_code varchar [note: 'The 3-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the trade account headquarters location (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX, GBR, DEU).']
  headquarters_postal_code varchar [note: 'The postal code or ZIP code of the trade account headquarters location.']
  headquarters_state_province varchar [note: 'The state, province, or administrative region of the trade account headquarters location.']
  last_order_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent purchase order received from this trade account.']
  legal_entity_name varchar [note: 'The official registered legal name of the trade partner organization as it appears on legal documents and contracts.']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'The minimum order quantity (in units or cases) required for orders placed by this trade account.']
  otif_sla_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The contractual OTIF service level agreement target percentage for order fulfillment performance (e.g., 95.00 represents 95% of orders delivered on time and in full).']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The primary payment instrument used by this account: ACH (automated clearing house), wire transfer, check, EDI payment (electronic funds transfer via EDI 820), or credit card.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'The standard payment terms code governing invoice payment due dates and early payment discounts (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30).']
  previous_status varchar [note: 'The account status immediately prior to the most recent status change, maintained for audit trail and lifecycle analysis.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'The business email address of the primary contact for order management, invoicing, and account communications.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'The full name of the primary business contact or account manager at the trade partner organization.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'The business phone number of the primary contact, including country code and extension if applicable.']
  primary_language_code varchar [note: 'The 2-letter ISO 639-1 language code for the preferred communication language with this trade account (e.g., en, es, fr, de).']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this trade account master record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this trade account master record was most recently modified.']
  status_change_reason varchar [note: 'The business reason or justification for the most recent account status change (e.g., payment default, business closure, credit improvement, contract renewal).']
  status_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the most recent account status change occurred.']
  status_changed_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID or name of the person who executed the most recent account status change, for audit and accountability purposes.']
  tax_exemption_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this trade account holds a valid tax exemption certificate for sales tax or VAT purposes.']
  tax_number varchar [note: 'The government-issued tax identification number for this legal entity (e.g., EIN in USA, VAT number in EU, GST number in other jurisdictions).']
  tdp_count varchar [note: 'The total number of distribution points (store locations or outlets) operated by this trade account where products can be sold.']
  trade_channel varchar [note: 'The primary retail or distribution channel through which this trade account operates: grocery (supermarkets), mass (mass merchandisers), club (warehouse clubs), drug (pharmacy chains), convenience stores, foodservice (restaurants/institutions), or e-commerce (online retailers). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: grocery|mass|club|drug|convenience|foodservice|e-commerce — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  vmi_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this trade account participates in a VMI program where the supplier manages inventory replenishment based on POS or warehouse data.']

  Note: 'SSOT master record for all B2B trade customers including retailers, distributors, wholesalers, and foodservice operators. Captures authoritative account identity: legal entity name, trade channel classification (grocery, mass, club, drug, foodservice, e-commerce), account tier, credit rating, payment terms, currency, tax identifiers, DUNS number, GLN (GS1 Global Location Number), primary language, account status, lifecycle dates, and full status change audit history (previous status, new status, change reason, change timestamp, changed-by user). This is the golden record for all trade partner identity — every other customer domain entity references this product via FK.'
}

Table sales.account_contact {
  account_contact_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the account contact record. Primary key.']
  reports_to_contact_id varchar [note: 'Reference to another account contact record representing this contact\'s direct manager or supervisor within the trade account organization. Enables organizational hierarchy mapping.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent trade account (retailer, distributor, wholesaler, or foodservice operator) to which this contact belongs.']
  assistant_name varchar [note: 'The name of the contact\'s administrative assistant or executive assistant, if applicable. Used for scheduling and communication routing.']
  assistant_phone varchar [note: 'The phone number of the contact\'s administrative assistant. Used for scheduling and communication routing.']
  contact_notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for capturing additional notes, preferences, or context about the contact. Used by sales representatives to document relationship insights.']
  contact_role varchar [note: 'The contact\'s role in the buying decision process. Distinguishes between primary decision-makers, influencers, and administrative contacts. Critical for sales force automation and account planning.']
  contact_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contact record. Active contacts are currently employed and engaged with the account. Inactive or terminated contacts are retained for historical reference.']
  contact_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contact\'s primary functional role at the trade account. Determines which business processes and communications this contact participates in.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this contact record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  department varchar [note: 'The organizational department or functional area within the trade account where this contact works. Examples include Procurement, Logistics, Finance, Marketing.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when this contact\'s role at the trade account ended, if applicable. Null for currently active contacts. Used for historical tracking and relationship continuity.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when this contact became active in their current role at the trade account. Used for tracking tenure and relationship history.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary business email address for the contact. Used for commercial communications, order confirmations, and business correspondence.']
  fax_number varchar [note: 'Fax number for the contact, if applicable. Still used in some trade channels for purchase orders and formal documentation.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'The given name of the individual contact at the trade account.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'The complete formatted name of the contact, typically concatenated as first name and last name. Used for display and reporting purposes.']
  is_decision_maker boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this contact has purchasing authority and decision-making power for the account. True if decision maker, False otherwise.']
  is_primary_contact boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this contact is the primary point of contact for the trade account. True if primary, False otherwise.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'The official job title or position held by the contact at the trade account organization. Examples include Senior Buyer, Category Manager, Supply Chain Director.']
  language_preference varchar [note: 'The contact\'s preferred language for business communications, represented as a 3-letter ISO 639-2 language code. Examples: ENG (English), SPA (Spanish), FRA (French).']
  last_contact_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent interaction or communication with this contact. Used to track engagement frequency and identify dormant relationships.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this contact record was most recently updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'The family name or surname of the individual contact at the trade account.']
  linkedin_profile_url varchar [note: 'The URL of the contact\'s LinkedIn professional profile. Used for social selling and relationship intelligence.']
  mobile_number varchar [note: 'Mobile or cellular phone number for the contact. Used for urgent communications and field-based retail execution activities.']
  next_follow_up_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next planned follow-up or interaction with this contact. Supports proactive account management and sales cadence planning.']
  opt_in_email boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the contact has opted in to receive commercial email communications. True if opted in, False if opted out. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance.']
  opt_in_phone boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the contact has opted in to receive commercial phone communications. True if opted in, False if opted out. Required for telemarketing compliance.']
  opt_in_sms boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the contact has opted in to receive commercial SMS or text message communications. True if opted in, False if opted out.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary business telephone number for the contact. Includes country code, area code, and local number.']
  preferred_communication_channel varchar [note: 'The contact\'s preferred method for receiving business communications. Supports personalized engagement strategies in SFA and retail execution workflows.']
  salesforce_user_code varchar [note: 'The Salesforce user ID of the sales representative or account manager responsible for this contact. Links to the internal sales team member managing the relationship.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the operational system from which this contact record originated. Examples include Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud, SAP S/4HANA SD, or manual data entry.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for this contact in the source operational system. Used for data reconciliation and integration traceability.']
  territory_code varchar [note: 'The sales territory or region code to which this contact and their account are assigned. Used for sales force automation and territory management.']

  Note: 'Individual contacts associated with a trade account, including buyers, category managers, logistics coordinators, and finance contacts at the retailer or distributor. Captures full name, job title, department, contact role (primary buyer, logistics, finance, marketing), phone, email, preferred communication channel, active status, and opt-in/opt-out flags for commercial communications. Supports SFA and retail execution workflows in Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud.'
}

Table sales.account_address {
  account_address_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the account address record. Primary key.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trade account (retailer, distributor, wholesaler, or foodservice customer) that this address belongs to.']
  account_address_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the address record. Active addresses are available for transactions; inactive addresses are retained for historical reporting; pending addresses await validation; closed addresses are permanently retired.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line including street number, street name, and building identifier. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for suite, floor, apartment, or unit number. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  address_line_3 varchar [note: 'Additional address line for complex addresses, building names, or delivery instructions. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  address_name varchar [note: 'Business name or label for this address location (e.g., \'Main Distribution Center\', \'Regional Office - Northeast\', \'Store #1234\').']
  address_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the address purpose: headquarters (HQ), billing address, ship-to location, sold-to location, returns processing center, or warehouse/distribution center. Supports Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) ship-to/bill-to mapping and Direct Store Delivery (DSD) route planning.']
  address_validation_date date [note: 'Date when the address was last validated against postal authority databases. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  address_validation_status varchar [note: 'Status of address verification against postal authority databases. Validated addresses improve delivery success rates and reduce logistics costs.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality name for the address location. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX, GBR, DEU).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this address record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Audit trail field for data governance and compliance.']
  delivery_instructions varchar [note: 'Special instructions for delivery personnel including receiving hours, dock locations, access codes, contact requirements, and handling instructions. Critical for On Time In Full (OTIF) performance.']
  dock_door_number varchar [note: 'Specific dock door or bay number for deliveries at warehouse or distribution center locations. Used by carriers and Direct Store Delivery (DSD) drivers.']
  dsd_route_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this location is eligible for Direct Store Delivery (DSD) routing (True) or requires warehouse distribution (False). Critical for route planning and logistics optimization.']
  edi_location_qualifier varchar [note: 'EDI X12 location qualifier code used in Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) transactions to identify the address role (e.g., ST for ship-to, BT for bill-to, SF for ship-from). Supports automated order processing and Advanced Shipping Notice (ASN) generation.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this address was deactivated or replaced. Null indicates the address is currently active. Format: yyyy-MM-dd. Supports address history tracking and audit compliance.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this address became active and valid for use in transactions. Format: yyyy-MM-dd. Supports address history tracking and audit compliance.']
  gln varchar [note: 'GS1 Global Location Number uniquely identifying this physical location in the global supply chain. 13-digit numeric identifier used for EDI transactions and supply chain visibility.']
  inactivation_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for address inactivation (e.g., location closed, address changed, account terminated, consolidation). Used for audit trails and business intelligence.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this address record was last updated. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Audit trail field for data governance and compliance.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate in decimal degrees for geospatial analysis, route optimization, and Direct Store Delivery (DSD) planning. Range: -90.0000000 to +90.0000000.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate in decimal degrees for geospatial analysis, route optimization, and Direct Store Delivery (DSD) planning. Range: -180.0000000 to +180.0000000.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal code, ZIP code, or postcode for mail delivery routing. Format varies by country (e.g., 5-digit ZIP in USA, alphanumeric in Canada/UK). Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  primary_address_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the primary address for the account (True) or a secondary/alternate address (False). Used for default shipping and billing operations.']
  receiving_hours_end varchar [note: 'End time for receiving deliveries at this location in 24-hour format (HH:mm). Used for delivery scheduling and On Time In Full (OTIF) compliance.']
  receiving_hours_start varchar [note: 'Start time for receiving deliveries at this location in 24-hour format (HH:mm). Used for delivery scheduling and On Time In Full (OTIF) compliance.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record that originated this address (e.g., SAP S/4HANA SD, Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud, Oracle Cloud ERP). Used for data lineage and troubleshooting.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this address in the source system of record. Used for data reconciliation and bidirectional synchronization.']
  standardized_address_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the address has been standardized to postal authority format (True) or remains in original input format (False).']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region code or name. Uses standard postal abbreviations (e.g., CA, NY, ON, QC). Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Tax jurisdiction identifier for sales tax, value-added tax (VAT), or goods and services tax (GST) calculation. Used in invoice generation and financial reporting.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for this location (e.g., America/New_York, America/Chicago, Europe/London). Used for delivery scheduling, service level agreement (SLA) tracking, and On Time In Full (OTIF) calculations.']

  Note: 'Physical and mailing addresses associated with a trade account, including headquarters, billing address, ship-to locations, and sold-to locations. Captures address type (HQ, billing, ship-to, sold-to, returns), street, city, state/province, postal code, country, GS1 GLN for the location, geo-coordinates, time zone, and address validation status. Supports EDI ship-to/bill-to mapping and DSD route planning.'
}

Table sales.account_hierarchy {
  account_hierarchy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the account hierarchy relationship record. Primary key.']
  child_account_trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the child account in the hierarchy. References the lower-level organizational entity (e.g., store, banner, chain).']
  primary_trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent account in the hierarchy. References the higher-level organizational entity (e.g., banner, chain, parent corporation).']
  acv_rollup_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether All Commodity Volume (ACV) metrics should be rolled up from child to parent account. True means include in ACV aggregation; false means exclude. Critical for market coverage and distribution analysis.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval status of the hierarchical relationship change. Draft indicates the relationship is being defined; pending approval indicates the relationship is awaiting management review; approved indicates the relationship has been validated; rejected indicates the relationship was not approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the business user who approved the hierarchical relationship. Used for audit trail and governance purposes.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the hierarchical relationship was approved. Used for audit trail and compliance reporting.']
  consolidation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether financial and operational metrics from the child account should be consolidated into the parent account for reporting purposes. True means consolidate; false means report separately. Critical for All Commodity Volume (ACV), Total Distribution Points (TDP), trade spend, and sales performance roll-ups.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the account hierarchy record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the hierarchical relationship ends or is no longer valid. Null indicates an open-ended relationship. Critical for historical analysis and point-in-time reporting.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the hierarchical relationship becomes effective. Used for temporal tracking of organizational changes, mergers, acquisitions, and restructuring events.']
  hierarchy_depth varchar [note: 'Numeric depth of the child account within the hierarchy tree, with 1 representing the top level (parent corporation) and increasing numbers representing deeper levels (chain, banner, store). Used for recursive queries and hierarchy traversal.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Level of the child account within the organizational hierarchy. Store represents individual retail locations; banner represents a retail brand or fascia; chain represents a retail chain or group; parent corporation represents the ultimate parent entity; division represents a business division; region represents a geographic region.']
  hierarchy_path varchar [note: 'Materialized path representing the full lineage from root to child account, typically formatted as slash-separated account identifiers (e.g., /1/23/456/7890). Enables efficient ancestor and descendant queries for Sales and Operations Planning (S&OP) and Trade Promotion Management (TPM) reporting.']
  hierarchy_type varchar [note: 'Type of organizational hierarchy relationship. Commercial hierarchies support sales and trade promotion roll-ups; logistics hierarchies support distribution and delivery planning; financial hierarchies support invoicing and payment consolidation; reporting hierarchies support management reporting structures; legal hierarchies represent corporate ownership; operational hierarchies support day-to-day execution.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the account hierarchy record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the hierarchical relationship. May include business context, special handling instructions, or historical information about organizational changes.']
  ownership_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of ownership or control the parent account has over the child account. Used for proportional consolidation in financial and operational reporting. Range 0.00 to 100.00.']
  primary_hierarchy_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the primary hierarchical relationship for the child account when multiple hierarchy types exist. True means this is the default hierarchy for reporting and analytics; false means this is a secondary or alternate hierarchy.']
  relationship_code varchar [note: 'Business code or identifier representing the nature of the parent-child relationship. May include codes for wholly-owned subsidiary, franchise, joint venture, affiliate, or other relationship types specific to the consumer goods trade channel.']
  relationship_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or description of the hierarchical relationship. Provides business context for the parent-child linkage (e.g., Regional Banner Group, Corporate Ownership, Distribution Network).']
  relationship_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the hierarchical relationship. Active indicates the relationship is currently in effect; inactive indicates the relationship has ended; pending indicates the relationship is awaiting activation; suspended indicates the relationship is temporarily paused; terminated indicates the relationship has been permanently ended.']
  rollup_weight decimal(10,4) [note: 'Weighting factor applied when rolling up metrics from child to parent account. Used for proportional allocation of All Commodity Volume (ACV), Total Distribution Points (TDP), trade spend, and sales performance when a child account participates in multiple hierarchies or has split ownership.']
  sales_rollup_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether sales performance metrics should be rolled up from child to parent account. True means include in sales aggregation; false means exclude. Essential for Sales and Operations Planning (S&OP) and Integrated Business Planning (IBP).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name or code of the source system that provided the hierarchical relationship data. May reference Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud, SAP S/4HANA SD (Sales and Distribution), Oracle Cloud ERP, or other master data management systems.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the hierarchical relationship record in the source system. Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and bidirectional synchronization.']
  tdp_rollup_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Total Distribution Points (TDP) metrics should be rolled up from child to parent account. True means include in TDP aggregation; false means exclude. Essential for numeric distribution and weighted distribution reporting.']
  trade_spend_rollup_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether trade promotion spending should be rolled up from child to parent account. True means include in trade spend aggregation; false means exclude. Critical for Trade Promotion Management (TPM) and Trade Promotion Optimization (TPO) reporting.']

  Note: 'Defines the parent-child organizational hierarchy of trade accounts, enabling roll-up of ACV, TDP, trade spend, and sales performance from store level to banner, banner to chain, and chain to parent corporation. Captures parent account, child account, hierarchy level (store, banner, chain, parent corporation), effective date range, hierarchy type (commercial, logistics, financial), and consolidation flag. Critical for S&OP and TPM reporting.'
}

Table sales.retail_store {
  retail_store_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the retail store location. Primary key.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Store‑level budgeting and expense tracking use a cost center per retail store for internal cost accounting.']
  distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_facility. Business justification: Store replenishment process requires an assigned distribution center to schedule deliveries and calculate OTIF performance.']
  edi_trading_partner_id varchar [note: 'EDI trading partner identifier used for electronic order transmission, invoicing, and Advanced Shipping Notices (ASN).']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Store profitability reporting aggregates sales and costs by profit center, requiring a link from store to profit_center.']
  store_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Required for Store Manager Assignment report linking each store to the responsible employee manager.']
  supplier_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_site. Business justification: Store‑level VMI or direct replenishment ties a retail store to the exact supplier site that ships inventory, needed for logistics and OTIF tracking.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent trade account, banner, or retail chain that owns or operates this store.']
  acv_weight decimal(10,4) [note: 'All Commodity Volume (ACV) weighting factor representing this stores share of total market volume, used for distribution and market share calculations.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the retail store location.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information such as suite, building, or unit number.']
  banner_name varchar [note: 'The retail banner or brand under which this store operates (e.g., Walmart Supercenter, Target, Kroger).']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the retail store is located.']
  close_date date [note: 'Date when the store permanently closed or is scheduled to close. Null if store is active.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the store is located.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this retail store record was first created in the system.']
  dsd_route_code varchar [note: 'Route code or identifier for Direct Store Delivery (DSD) logistics and delivery scheduling.']
  gln varchar [note: 'GS1 Global Location Number uniquely identifying this physical store location for Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) and supply chain transactions.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this retail store record was last updated or modified.']
  last_visit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent retail execution visit or sales call to this store location.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the store location for mapping and route optimization.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the store location for mapping and route optimization.']
  next_scheduled_visit_date date [note: 'Date of the next planned retail execution visit or sales call to this store location.']
  open_date date [note: 'Date when the store first opened for business operations.']
  operating_hours varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for the store (e.g., Mon-Fri 8am-10pm, Sat-Sun 9am-9pm) used for retail execution visit planning.']
  osa_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target On Shelf Availability (OSA) percentage for this store indicating expected in-stock performance for key Stock Keeping Units (SKUs).']
  otif_sla_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target On Time In Full (OTIF) service level percentage agreed with this store for delivery performance measurement.']
  planogram_zone varchar [note: 'Planogram zone or cluster assignment indicating which shelf layout and assortment plan applies to this store.']
  pos_system_type varchar [note: 'Type or vendor of the Point of Sale (POS) system used at this store for transaction processing and sales data capture.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the store location used for logistics and Direct Store Delivery (DSD) routing.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region where the store is located.']
  store_format varchar [note: 'The retail format classification of the store indicating size, assortment, and operational model. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hypermarket|supermarket|convenience|club|drug|dollar|specialty|e-commerce_fulfillment_center — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  store_name varchar [note: 'The official name or designation of the retail store location.']
  store_number varchar [note: 'The retailer-assigned unique store number or code used for operational identification and Direct Store Delivery (DSD) routing.']
  store_phone varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the retail store location.']
  store_size_sq_ft varchar [note: 'Total retail selling space of the store measured in square feet, used for All Commodity Volume (ACV) and Total Distribution Points (TDP) calculations.']
  store_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the retail store location.']
  store_tier varchar [note: 'Strategic tier classification (e.g., Tier 1, Tier 2) indicating store importance based on volume, profitability, or strategic value for prioritized retail execution.']
  tdp_weight decimal(10,4) [note: 'Total Distribution Points (TDP) weighting factor representing this stores contribution to numeric distribution metrics.']
  trading_area varchar [note: 'Geographic trading area or market designation (e.g., metro area, region) used for sales territory and distribution planning.']
  vmi_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this store participates in a Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) program where the manufacturer manages replenishment.']

  Note: 'Individual physical retail store or outlet location associated with a trade account banner or chain. Captures store number, store name, banner, format (hypermarket, supermarket, convenience, club, drug, dollar, e-commerce fulfillment center), store size (sq ft), trading area, store open/close dates, store manager, operating hours, and planogram zone assignment. Used for retail execution, OSA tracking, and DSD route management.'
}

Table sales.edi_trading_partner {
  edi_trading_partner_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the EDI trading partner configuration record. Primary key.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trade customer account (retailer, distributor, wholesaler, or foodservice operator) that this EDI configuration serves.']
  acknowledgment_sla_hours varchar [note: 'The contractual turnaround time (in hours) for Consumer Goods to send functional acknowledgments (997) or business acknowledgments (855) back to the trading partner after receiving an EDI transaction.']
  as2_identifier varchar [note: 'The AS2 identifier (AS2-From or AS2-To) used in AS2 message headers when AS2 protocol is in use.']
  communication_protocol varchar [note: 'The communication protocol used to transmit EDI documents between Consumer Goods and the trading partner (e.g., AS2 for secure internet-based exchange, SFTP for file transfer, VAN for value-added network).']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'The email address of the primary EDI contact at the trading partner for operational communication and issue escalation.']
  contact_name varchar [note: 'The name of the primary EDI coordinator or technical contact at the trading partner organization responsible for EDI operations and issue resolution.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'The phone number of the primary EDI contact at the trading partner for urgent operational issues.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this EDI trading partner configuration record was first created in the system.']
  digital_signature_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether EDI transmissions must include digital signatures for non-repudiation (common in AS2 protocol).']
  edi_go_live_date date [note: 'The date when EDI transactions with this trading partner went live in production.']
  edi_partner_code varchar [note: 'The unique EDI partner identifier assigned to the trading partner, used in EDI envelope headers (ISA/GS segments) to route transactions.']
  edi_standard varchar [note: 'The EDI messaging standard used for communication with this trading partner (e.g., ANSI X12 for North America, UN/EDIFACT for international).']
  edi_trading_partner_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the EDI trading partner configuration (active for live production, testing for certification phase, inactive for discontinued partners, suspended for temporary holds).']
  edi_version varchar [note: 'The specific version of the EDI standard in use (e.g., 004010, 005010 for ANSI X12; D96A, D01B for EDIFACT).']
  encryption_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether EDI transmissions with this trading partner must be encrypted (e.g., AS2 with encryption, SFTP with SSH).']
  environment_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this EDI configuration is for production transactions, testing/certification, or development purposes.']
  gs_qualifier varchar [note: 'GS application sender/receiver code qualifier that identifies the type of identification being used in the GS segment.']
  gs_receiver_code varchar [note: 'The application receiver code used in the GS03 segment, identifying the trading partner as the receiver at the functional group level.']
  gs_sender_code varchar [note: 'The application sender code used in the GS02 segment, identifying Consumer Goods as the sender at the functional group level.']
  isa_qualifier varchar [note: 'ISA interchange ID qualifier code that identifies the type of identification being used in the ISA segment (e.g., 01=DUNS, 14=DUNS+4, ZZ=Mutually Defined). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 01|14|20|27|28|29|30|33|ZZ — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  isa_receiver_code varchar [note: 'The receiver identification code used in the ISA08 segment of outbound EDI transactions, representing the trading partner receiving the data.']
  isa_sender_code varchar [note: 'The sender identification code used in the ISA06 segment of outbound EDI transactions from Consumer Goods to the trading partner.']
  mdn_requested boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Consumer Goods requests a Message Disposition Notification (MDN) receipt from the trading partner when using AS2 protocol.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing special configuration requirements, partner-specific business rules, or operational considerations for this EDI relationship.']
  processing_sla_hours varchar [note: 'The contractual turnaround time (in hours) for Consumer Goods to process inbound EDI transactions (e.g., convert 850 PO into sales order) after receipt.']
  sftp_directory_inbound varchar [note: 'The directory path on the SFTP server where Consumer Goods retrieves inbound EDI files from the trading partner.']
  sftp_directory_outbound varchar [note: 'The directory path on the SFTP server where Consumer Goods deposits outbound EDI files for the trading partner to retrieve.']
  sftp_host varchar [note: 'The SFTP server hostname or IP address used for file-based EDI exchange when SFTP protocol is in use.']
  sftp_port varchar [note: 'The TCP port number used for SFTP connections (typically 22).']
  sftp_username varchar [note: 'The username credential used to authenticate to the trading partner SFTP server.']
  transaction_set_810_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Consumer Goods sends 810 Invoice transaction sets to the trading partner for billing.']
  transaction_set_846_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether inventory position data is exchanged via 846 Inventory Inquiry/Advice transaction sets for Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) programs.']
  transaction_set_850_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the trading partner sends 850 Purchase Order transaction sets to Consumer Goods.']
  transaction_set_852_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the trading partner sends 852 Product Activity Data (POS sales data) to Consumer Goods for demand sensing and replenishment.']
  transaction_set_855_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Consumer Goods sends 855 Purchase Order Acknowledgment transaction sets back to the trading partner.']
  transaction_set_856_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Consumer Goods sends 856 Advanced Ship Notice transaction sets to notify the trading partner of shipments.']
  transaction_set_867_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the trading partner sends 867 Product Transfer and Resale Report transaction sets for secondary sales visibility (distributor to retailer).']
  transaction_set_997_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether 997 Functional Acknowledgment transaction sets are exchanged to confirm receipt and syntactic validation of EDI documents.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this EDI trading partner configuration record was last modified.']
  van_mailbox_code varchar [note: 'The mailbox identifier assigned by the VAN provider for routing EDI documents to and from the trading partner.']
  van_provider varchar [note: 'The name of the Value-Added Network provider facilitating EDI exchange (e.g., SPS Commerce, TrueCommerce, Cleo) when VAN protocol is in use.']

  Note: 'EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) configuration and trading partner profile for a trade account. Captures EDI partner ID, ISA/GS qualifiers, EDI standard (ANSI X12, EDIFACT), supported transaction sets (850 PO, 855 PO Ack, 856 ASN, 810 Invoice, 852 POS Data, 867 Product Transfer), communication protocol (AS2, SFTP, VAN), test vs. production flag, EDI go-live date, and SLA for acknowledgment turnaround. Owned by the customer domain as part of trade account configuration.'
}

Table sales.customer_vmi_agreement {
  customer_vmi_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the VMI agreement record. Primary key.']
  primary_customer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturer employee (demand planner or supply planner) responsible for managing this VMI agreement and executing replenishment decisions.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: VMI program execution requires linking each customer VMI agreement to the supplying vendor for inventory replenishment planning.']
  tertiary_customer_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user identifier of the person who last modified this VMI agreement record. Supports audit and accountability.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trade account (retailer, distributor, wholesaler, or foodservice operator) participating in this VMI agreement.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the VMI agreement, used in communications with the trade partner and in SAP IBP or APS systems.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the VMI agreement: draft (being negotiated), pending approval (awaiting sign-off), active (in force), suspended (temporarily paused), terminated (ended early), or expired (reached end date).']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the VMI agreement automatically renews at the end of the term (True) or requires explicit renegotiation (False).']
  contract_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or file path to the signed legal contract document stored in the document management system (e.g., Veeva Vault, SharePoint).']
  covered_sku_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct SKUs included in the VMI agreement scope. Used for agreement complexity assessment and system configuration.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this VMI agreement record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record lifecycle.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this VMI agreement (penalties, inventory valuation, etc.).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the VMI agreement expires or terminates. Nullable for open-ended agreements with auto-renewal clauses.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the VMI agreement becomes binding and replenishment responsibilities transfer to the manufacturer.']
  forecast_sharing_cadence varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the trade account shares demand forecasts, POS data, or promotional plans with the manufacturer to support VMI planning.']
  inventory_ownership_model varchar [note: 'Defines who owns the inventory at the trade account location: customer-owned (traditional), supplier-owned consignment (ownership transfers at sale), shared risk, or pay-on-scan (ownership transfers at POS scan).']
  inventory_valuation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method used to value inventory under this VMI agreement: FIFO (First In First Out), LIFO (Last In First Out), weighted average, or standard cost.']
  inventory_visibility_method varchar [note: 'Technical method by which the manufacturer receives inventory position data from the trade account: EDI 846 (inventory inquiry/advice), API integration, web portal access, manual reports, WMS integration, or none.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this VMI agreement record was last updated. Tracks changes to terms, status, or configuration.']
  last_performance_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal performance review meeting or report for this VMI agreement. Used to track review compliance.']
  max_inventory_weeks_of_supply decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum inventory target expressed as weeks of supply. Replenishment orders are sized to bring inventory to this level without exceeding it.']
  min_inventory_weeks_of_supply decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum inventory target expressed as weeks of supply (demand coverage). Triggers replenishment when inventory falls below this threshold.']
  next_performance_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal performance review meeting or report. Drives calendar reminders and preparation workflows.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special terms, exceptions, or operational instructions specific to this VMI agreement.']
  order_transmission_method varchar [note: 'Method by which replenishment orders are transmitted to the trade account or their systems: EDI 850 (purchase order), API, web portal, email, fax, or manual entry.']
  penalty_amount_per_incident decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed financial penalty amount charged per SLA violation incident (e.g., per OOS event, per late delivery). Expressed in the agreement currency.']
  penalty_clause_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the VMI agreement includes financial penalties or chargebacks for SLA violations (e.g., missed OTIF targets, excess OOS incidents).']
  performance_review_frequency varchar [note: 'Cadence at which the manufacturer and trade account conduct formal performance reviews of VMI metrics (fill rate, OTIF, inventory turns, OOS incidents).']
  pos_data_sharing_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the trade account shares real-time or near-real-time POS (point of sale) data with the manufacturer to improve demand sensing accuracy.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required before the end date to trigger renewal or termination discussions. Typically 30, 60, or 90 days.']
  replenishment_frequency varchar [note: 'Cadence at which the manufacturer reviews inventory levels and triggers replenishment orders: daily, twice weekly, weekly, bi-weekly, monthly, on-demand (event-driven), or continuous (real-time). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: daily|twice_weekly|weekly|bi_weekly|monthly|on_demand|continuous — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  replenishment_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Committed lead time in days from replenishment order creation to delivery at the trade account location. Used for min/max inventory calculations.']
  safety_stock_weeks decimal(5,2) [note: 'Buffer inventory expressed as weeks of supply to protect against demand variability and supply disruptions. Included in min inventory calculations.']
  sku_scope_type varchar [note: 'Defines the breadth of SKU coverage in the VMI agreement: all SKUs, specific product category, specific brand, explicit SKU list, or custom selection criteria.']
  sla_target_fill_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contractual target for order fill rate (percentage of ordered quantity delivered) that the manufacturer commits to achieve under this VMI agreement. Typically 95-99%.']
  sla_target_oos_incidents_per_month varchar [note: 'Maximum allowable number of out-of-stock incidents per month at the trade account location under this VMI agreement. Exceeding this triggers penalty or review clauses.']
  sla_target_otif_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contractual target for OTIF (On Time In Full) delivery performance that the manufacturer commits to achieve. Measures both timeliness and completeness of deliveries.']
  trade_partner_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary trade account contact for VMI communications, forecast sharing, and issue escalation.']
  trade_partner_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary contact person at the trade account responsible for VMI coordination and issue resolution.']
  trade_partner_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary trade account contact for urgent VMI issues and escalations.']
  vmi_program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the VMI program model: full VMI (supplier owns inventory decisions), CMI/Co-Managed Inventory (collaborative), consignment (supplier owns inventory until sale), supplier-managed replenishment, continuous replenishment, or vendor-owned inventory.']

  Note: 'Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) agreement between the CPG manufacturer and a trade account. Captures VMI program type (full VMI, CMI/Co-Managed Inventory, consignment), covered SKU scope, min/max inventory targets by location, replenishment frequency, lead time commitments, forecast sharing cadence, VMI start/end dates, responsible demand planner, and performance SLA thresholds (fill rate %, OTIF %, weeks of supply targets). Links to trade_account and drives automated replenishment triggers in SAP IBP or similar APS systems.'
}

Table sales.account_sla {
  account_sla_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the account service level agreement record. Primary key.']
  account_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Needed for SLA ownership tracking; SLA records must reference the employee managing the account.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trade account (retailer, distributor, wholesaler, or foodservice customer) to which this SLA applies.']
  account_sla_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the SLA. Draft indicates pending approval; active indicates in force; suspended indicates temporarily paused; expired indicates end date reached; terminated indicates early cancellation; under review indicates renegotiation in progress.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who approved this SLA. Typically a commercial director or senior account executive. Nullable for draft or unapproved SLAs.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SLA was formally approved and moved to active status. Nullable for draft or unapproved SLAs.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SLA automatically renews at the end of the effective period (True) or requires explicit renegotiation (False).']
  contract_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the master commercial contract or trading agreement under which this SLA is governed. Links SLA to broader contractual framework.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SLA record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record inception.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for penalty and incentive amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date when the SLA terms become binding and performance measurement begins.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date when the SLA terms expire or are scheduled to end. Nullable for open-ended agreements subject to termination notice.']
  escalation_procedure varchar [note: 'Description of the process and contacts for escalating SLA breaches or disputes. Includes escalation levels, response times, and resolution authority.']
  exclusion_conditions varchar [note: 'Description of circumstances under which SLA performance measurement is suspended or penalties waived (e.g., force majeure, natural disasters, customer-caused delays, system outages).']
  incentive_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary value of incentive per occurrence or per measurement period when SLA target is exceeded. Nullable if incentive is non-monetary or variable.']
  incentive_terms varchar [note: 'Description of rewards or bonuses provided when performance exceeds the target. May include volume bonuses, preferred status, or co-investment in joint initiatives.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this SLA record. Audit trail for accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SLA record was last updated. Audit trail for change tracking.']
  measurement_period varchar [note: 'Time window over which SLA performance is calculated and reported. Rolling periods provide continuous assessment; fixed periods align with calendar or fiscal cycles. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: daily|weekly|monthly|quarterly|annual|rolling_30_days|rolling_90_days — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  minimum_performance_threshold decimal(10,4) [note: 'Lowest acceptable performance level before penalties or escalation are triggered. Typically set below the target to allow for minor variance.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal SLA review meeting or renegotiation session.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special terms, historical amendments, or operational guidance related to this SLA.']
  owning_team varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the internal commercial, sales, or customer service team responsible for managing this SLA and the account relationship.']
  penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary value of penalty per occurrence or per measurement period when SLA is breached. Nullable if penalty is non-monetary or variable.']
  penalty_terms varchar [note: 'Description of financial or commercial penalties applied when performance falls below the minimum threshold. May include rebates, credits, discounts, or service recovery actions.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Cadence at which SLA performance reports are generated and shared with the account. On-demand indicates ad-hoc reporting upon request.
