  trade_promotion_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trade promotion or promotional agreement that the deduction claim is associated with, if applicable.']
  aging_days varchar [note: 'The number of days the deduction has been open since the deduction date, used for aging analysis and collection prioritization.']
  approved_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount approved by the business for write-off or credit after review and validation of the deduction claim.']
  claimed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total amount claimed or deducted by the customer or distributor from the invoice payment.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'The legal entity or company code in the ERP system that owns the accounts receivable and deduction record.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'The cost center responsible for absorbing the deduction expense, used for internal cost allocation and management reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the deduction record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the deduction amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  customer_reference_number varchar [note: 'The reference number or claim number provided by the customer or distributor for tracking the deduction on their side.']
  deduction_date date [note: 'The date on which the deduction was raised or recorded by the customer or distributor.']
  deduction_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the deduction, often provided by the customer or generated by the accounts receivable system.']
  deduction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the deduction: open, under review, approved, rejected, written off, or partially approved.']
  deduction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the deduction reason: trade promotion claim, pricing dispute, shortage claim, quality claim, freight allowance, or damaged goods claim.']
  disputed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the claimed amount that is under dispute and has not been approved or rejected.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'The distribution channel through which the sale and subsequent deduction occurred (e.g., retail, wholesale, DTC, e-commerce).']
  division varchar [note: 'The product division or business unit associated with the deduction, aligned with SAP SD organizational structure.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the deduction has been escalated to senior management or a specialized resolution team due to complexity or high value.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified the deduction record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the deduction record was last modified or updated.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'The reference number of the customer payment or remittance advice where the deduction was taken.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'The date on which the deduction was resolved (approved, rejected, or written off).']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes and comments captured by the analyst during the investigation and resolution process, including rationale for approval or rejection.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation or categorization of the underlying root cause identified during deduction investigation (e.g., system pricing error, promotional terms misalignment, delivery shortage).']
  sales_organization varchar [note: 'The sales organization code responsible for the transaction and deduction, aligned with SAP SD organizational structure.']
  sap_fi_document_number varchar [note: 'The SAP FI document number generated when the deduction is posted to the general ledger for financial accounting.']
  supporting_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to supporting documentation provided by the customer or generated during investigation (e.g., proof of delivery, promotional agreement, quality report).']
  valid_claim_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the deduction claim was determined to be valid based on investigation and supporting documentation.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or system identifier of the person or process that created the deduction record.']

  Note: 'Short-payment or deduction record raised by a retail account or distributor against an invoice, representing disputed amounts, unauthorized deductions, or trade promotion claims. Captures deduction number, originating invoice reference, deduction date, deduction type (trade promotion/pricing dispute/shortage claim/quality claim/freight allowance), claimed amount, approved amount, status (open/under review/approved/rejected/written off), resolution date, and responsible analyst. Critical for accounts receivable management and trade spend reconciliation.'
}

Table sales.sales_rebate_agreement {
  sales_rebate_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the sales rebate agreement record. Primary key.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Rebate agreements need an employee approver to satisfy financial compliance and audit requirements.']
  hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_hierarchy. Business justification: Rebate agreements are scoped to product hierarchies to calculate rebate amounts per product family; needed for rebate settlement reports.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Rebate agreements are negotiated per brand; brand‑specific rebate tracking is needed for financial reporting.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retail account or distributor participating in this rebate agreement.']
  accrual_method varchar [note: 'Method used to accrue rebate liabilities: at invoice creation, upon payment receipt, at shipment, or periodic batch calculation.']
  accrued_rebate_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total rebate amount accrued to date under this agreement but not yet settled or paid.']
  agreement_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name or title of the rebate agreement for business reference and reporting.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the rebate agreement, typically generated by SAP SD rebate processing module.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow status of the rebate agreement before it becomes active.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the business user who approved this rebate agreement.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the rebate agreement was approved for activation.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rebate agreement automatically renews at the end of its term.']
  calculation_basis varchar [note: 'The metric used to calculate rebate eligibility and amounts: revenue value, unit volume, gross sales, or net sales.']
  contract_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal contract document or attachment that governs this rebate agreement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this rebate agreement record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this rebate agreement.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'SAP distribution channel code indicating the route-to-market for this rebate agreement (e.g., retail, wholesale, DTC).']
  division varchar [note: 'SAP division code representing the product line or business unit for this rebate agreement.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the rebate agreement expires or terminates. Nullable for open-ended agreements.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the rebate agreement becomes active and eligible for accrual calculations.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic regions, countries, or sales territories where this rebate agreement applies.']
  last_settlement_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent rebate settlement payment made under this agreement.']
  maximum_rebate_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cap on the total rebate amount that can be earned under this agreement during its term. Nullable if no cap applies.']
  minimum_qualification_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum purchase amount or volume required for the customer to qualify for any rebate under this agreement.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this rebate agreement record was last modified or updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional comments, special terms, or business context related to this rebate agreement.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which rebate amounts are paid to the customer: credit memo, check, electronic funds transfer, or offset against receivables.']
  product_scope varchar [note: 'Description or reference to the product categories, SKUs, or product hierarchy levels included in this rebate agreement.']
  rebate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rebate agreement based on the performance criteria: volume-based, growth-based, new product introduction, category performance, product mix, or customer loyalty.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days prior to expiration that renewal or termination notice must be provided.']
  retroactive_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether rebate rate increases apply retroactively to all prior purchases when a higher tier is reached.']
  sales_organization varchar [note: 'SAP sales organization code responsible for managing this rebate agreement.']
  sales_rebate_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rebate agreement indicating its operational state.']
  sap_rebate_agreement_number varchar [note: 'Native SAP SD rebate agreement document number from the source ERP system.']
  settled_rebate_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total rebate amount that has been settled and paid to the customer under this agreement.']
  settlement_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which rebate payments are calculated and settled with the customer.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason or explanation for early termination of the rebate agreement, if applicable.']
  termination_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the rebate agreement was terminated before its scheduled end date.']
  tier_structure_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rebate agreement uses a tiered threshold structure with escalating rebate rates.']

  Note: 'Volume-based or performance-based rebate agreement with a retail account or distributor defining the conditions under which financial rebates are earned and paid. Captures agreement number, customer reference, rebate type (volume/growth/new product/category performance), calculation basis (revenue/units), threshold tiers, rebate percentage or fixed amount per tier, accrual method, settlement frequency (monthly/quarterly/annual), effective dates, and approval status. Manages trade spend accruals and settlement in SAP SD rebate processing.'
}

Table sales.sales_dsd_route {
  sales_dsd_route_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the DSD route record. Primary key for the sales_dsd_route product.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: DSD routes are assigned to carriers for transport; linking enables carrier SLA tracking and cost management.']
  distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse or distribution center from which this DSD route originates for inventory loading and dispatch.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the DSD driver or sales representative assigned to execute deliveries on this route.']
  network_lane_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.network_lane. Business justification: DSD Route Planning tracks which network lane (origin‑destination, carrier, lead time) a route follows; lane attributes are needed for cost and service calculations.']
  sales_rep_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.rep. Business justification: Assigning a sales rep to a DSD route clarifies responsibility for route execution.']
  actual_distance_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual distance traveled in kilometers during a specific delivery execution on this route.']
  actual_duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual time in hours taken to complete a specific delivery execution on this route, including all stops and travel time.']
  actual_stop_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of retail store stops completed during a specific delivery execution on this route.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this DSD route record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the settlement amount and financial transactions on this route.']
  delivery_confirmation_status varchar [note: 'Status of delivery confirmation for a specific route execution, indicating whether deliveries were accepted by store receivers.']
  delivery_day_of_week varchar [note: 'Scheduled day of the week when deliveries are executed on this route. Multiple days may be represented in separate route records or via custom schedule.']
  delivery_execution_date date [note: 'Actual date when a specific delivery execution event occurred on this route. Captures the real-world delivery event timestamp for execution tracking.']
  delivery_frequency varchar [note: 'Standard frequency pattern for deliveries on this DSD route, defining how often stores on the route receive deliveries.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'Distribution channel code indicating the route of goods to market for this DSD route (e.g., retail, food service).']
  division varchar [note: 'Product division or business unit code associated with this DSD route, defining the product scope served.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this DSD route configuration is discontinued or replaced. Null for open-ended routes.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this DSD route configuration becomes active and operational for delivery execution.']
  estimated_distance_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total estimated distance in kilometers for completing all stops on this DSD route.']
  estimated_duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total estimated time in hours required to complete all deliveries on this DSD route, including travel and stop time.']
  exception_count varchar [note: 'Number of delivery exceptions encountered during route execution (e.g., store closed, access denied, damaged goods, shortages).']
  exception_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes describing delivery exceptions, issues, or special circumstances encountered during route execution.']
  geographic_territory varchar [note: 'Geographic territory or region code that this DSD route covers, used for sales territory management and planning.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that last modified this DSD route record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this DSD route record was last modified or updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'General notes or comments about the DSD route configuration, special instructions, or operational considerations.']
  otif_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the route execution met OTIF service level requirements for all stops on the route.']
  planned_stop_count varchar [note: 'Number of retail store stops planned on this DSD route during a standard delivery cycle.']
  receiver_signature_captured_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether store receiver signatures were captured electronically during delivery execution for proof of delivery.']
  route_end_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled end time for route execution, typically when the driver returns to the warehouse. Format HH:mm.']
  route_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or description of the DSD route, typically including geographic or customer cluster identifiers.']
  route_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the DSD route. Externally-known route code used in field operations and driver assignments.']
  route_optimization_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated score representing the efficiency of the route configuration based on distance, time, and stop sequence optimization.']
  route_start_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled start time for route execution, typically when the driver departs from the warehouse. Format HH:mm.']
  route_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the DSD route indicating whether it is operational, planned, or discontinued.']
  route_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the DSD route based on its operational purpose and frequency pattern.']
  sales_organization varchar [note: 'Sales organization code responsible for managing this DSD route within the enterprise structure.']
  sap_sd_route_number varchar [note: 'Route number reference in SAP S/4HANA SD module for integration and cross-system reconciliation.']
  settlement_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total settlement amount for the route execution, representing the net financial value of delivered goods minus returns and adjustments.']
  total_cases_delivered varchar [note: 'Total number of cases delivered across all stops during a specific route execution.']
  total_quantity_delivered decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total quantity of products delivered across all stops during a specific route execution, measured in base unit of measure.']
  total_quantity_returned decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total quantity of products returned (empties, unsold, damaged) collected during a specific route execution, measured in base unit of measure.']
  vehicle_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the delivery vehicle assigned to this DSD route for transportation and delivery execution.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that created this DSD route record.']

  Note: 'Direct Store Delivery (DSD) route master and delivery execution record. Defines the sequence of retail store stops, delivery frequency, and assigned DSD driver/vehicle for direct delivery operations. Also captures actual delivery execution events including delivery date, SKUs/quantities delivered, quantities returned (empties/unsold), delivery confirmation status, store receiver signature, exceptions, and settlement amounts. Supports DSD channel order management, field execution planning, and delivery settlement.'
}

Table sales.sales_dsd_delivery {
  sales_dsd_delivery_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the DSD delivery record. Primary key for the sales_dsd_delivery product.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Each DSD delivery is performed by a carrier; linking enables carrier performance and cost allocation reporting.']
  distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse or distribution center from which the delivery was dispatched. Links to warehouse master for inventory and logistics tracking.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the DSD driver who executed the delivery. Links to workforce or driver master for performance tracking.']
  order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: DSD delivery records should be tied to the originating sales order for fulfillment tracking.']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retail store where the delivery was made. Links to the retail store master for account and location details.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.route. Business justification: Delivery follows a defined route; the FK supports route efficiency analysis and exception handling.']
  sales_dsd_route_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the DSD route this delivery is part of. Links to the route master for planning and optimization.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trade account associated with this delivery. Links to the customer trade account for billing and settlement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this delivery record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and record lifecycle tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this delivery record. Ensures consistent financial reporting across regions.']
  delivery_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross monetary value of products delivered in this transaction before adjustments. Base amount for settlement calculation.']
  delivery_confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the delivery was confirmed and signed off by the store receiver. Marks the completion of the delivery transaction.']
  delivery_date date [note: 'The date on which the delivery was executed at the retail store. Principal business event timestamp for DSD execution.']
  delivery_exception_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any exceptions or issues occurred during this delivery. True if exceptions were recorded, false for clean deliveries.']
  delivery_notes varchar [note: 'General free-text notes about the delivery. Captured by the driver for operational context, special instructions followed, or store feedback.']
  delivery_number varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the DSD delivery. Human-readable reference number used in operational systems and driver documentation.']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the DSD delivery in its lifecycle. Indicates whether the delivery was successfully completed, partially delivered, rejected by the store, cancelled, or still pending.']
  delivery_time timestamp [note: 'The precise timestamp when the delivery was completed at the store. Used for route optimization and On Time In Full (OTIF) performance measurement.']
  delivery_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the delivery based on its business purpose. Regular deliveries are routine replenishment, emergency deliveries address out-of-stock situations, promotional deliveries support trade promotions, and new store deliveries are initial stock placements.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total discount applied to this delivery. Includes trade promotions, volume discounts, and pricing agreement adjustments.']
  distribution_channel_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the distribution channel for this delivery. DSD is a distinct channel from warehouse distribution and e-commerce.']
  division_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the product division or business unit for this delivery. Used for divisional P&L and performance reporting.']
  exception_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes describing delivery exceptions, issues, or special circumstances. Captured by the driver for operational follow-up and dispute resolution.']
  exception_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the primary reason for delivery exception. Used for root cause analysis and process improvement.']
  gps_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'GPS latitude coordinate captured at the time of delivery. Used for route verification, geofencing compliance, and delivery proof.']
  gps_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'GPS longitude coordinate captured at the time of delivery. Used for route verification, geofencing compliance, and delivery proof.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this delivery record was last modified. Audit field for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  net_settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final net amount to be settled for this delivery after all adjustments. Calculated as delivery_amount minus return_amount minus discount_amount plus tax_amount.']
  otif_status varchar [note: 'Classification of delivery performance against On Time In Full (OTIF) service level agreement. Measures whether the delivery was completed on schedule and with complete quantities.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method of payment collected or arranged for this delivery. Cash and check are collected on delivery, credit and EDI are settled through accounts receivable, account indicates billing to trade account.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for the payment transaction associated with this delivery. Links to payment or invoice records for reconciliation.']
  return_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of products returned during this delivery. Deducted from the delivery amount in settlement.']
  sales_organization_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the sales organization responsible for this delivery. Used for organizational reporting and performance tracking.']
  sap_sd_delivery_number varchar [note: 'SAP SD system delivery document number for integration and traceability. Links this DSD delivery to the source ERP transaction.']
  scheduled_delivery_time timestamp [note: 'The planned delivery time as scheduled in the route plan. Used to measure delivery punctuality and OTIF compliance.']
  signature_captured_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a digital or physical signature was captured from the store receiver as proof of delivery. True if signature was obtained, false otherwise.']
  signature_image_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the stored signature image file or document. Links to document management system for proof of delivery retrieval.']
  store_receiver_name varchar [note: 'Name of the store employee who received and accepted the delivery. Captured for proof of delivery and accountability.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax charged on this delivery. Calculated based on jurisdiction tax rates and product tax classifications.']
  total_cases_delivered decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total number of cases delivered to the store across all SKUs in this delivery. Aggregate quantity for operational reporting.']
  total_cases_returned decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total number of cases returned from the store during this delivery. Includes empties, unsold products, damaged goods, and expired items.']
  total_units_delivered decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total number of individual units (eaches) delivered to the store across all SKUs in this delivery. Used for inventory accuracy and settlement.']
  total_units_returned decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total number of individual units returned from the store during this delivery. Used for reverse logistics and inventory reconciliation.']
  vehicle_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the vehicle used for this delivery. Links to fleet or vehicle master for asset tracking and maintenance.']

  Note: 'Direct Store Delivery (DSD) execution record capturing the actual delivery of products to a retail store by a DSD driver. Records delivery date, route reference, store/account, driver reference, vehicle reference, SKUs delivered, quantities delivered, quantities returned (empties/unsold), delivery confirmation status, store receiver signature captured flag, delivery exception notes, and settlement amount. Operational record for DSD channel order fulfillment and settlement.'
}

Table sales.account_onboarding {
  account_onboarding_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each account onboarding record.']
  account_assortment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the first product assortment agreement created during onboarding.']
  account_pricing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the first pricing agreement established during onboarding.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal employee responsible for managing the onboarding process.']
  resubmitted_account_onboarding_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on account_onboarding (resubmitted_account_onboarding_id)']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.trade_account. Business justification: Onboarding records belong to a specific trade account; linking enables traceability of the onboarding lifecycle to the account.']
  actual_activation_date date [note: 'Date the trade account was officially activated and began transacting.']
  applicant_duns_number varchar [note: 'Dun & Bradstreet unique identifier for the applicant organization.']
  applicant_legal_entity varchar [note: 'Legal name of the organization applying for a trade account.']
  assortment_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Status of the product assortment agreement with the trade account.']
  compliance_checklist_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all required compliance documents have been submitted and approved.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the onboarding record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  credit_application_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the credit application submitted during onboarding.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code used for all monetary amounts in the onboarding agreement.']
  dsd_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account uses Direct Store Delivery logistics.']
  edi_setup_milestone_date date [note: 'Date the Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) configuration was completed for the account.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the onboarding agreement becomes effective (often same as target_go_live_date).']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date the onboarding agreement ends or is superseded; null if open‑ended.']
  kyb_status varchar [note: 'Current status of KYB verification for the applicant.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current status of KYC verification for the applicant.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the onboarding record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations recorded by the onboarding team.']
  onboarding_request_date date [note: 'Date the onboarding request was submitted by the prospective trade account.']
  onboarding_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage of the onboarding lifecycle.']
  onboarding_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the onboarding effort (e.g., brand‑new account, transfer from another system, reinstatement after suspension).']
  overall_status varchar [note: 'High‑level status indicating whether onboarding is ongoing, finished, or halted.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms code (e.g., NET30, NET45) agreed for the account.']
  pricing_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Status of the pricing agreement (price list, discounts) for the trade account.']
  required_documents_completed_flag boolean [note: 'True when all mandatory legal and compliance documents have been received and validated.']
  risk_assessment_level varchar [note: 'Risk tier derived from the risk assessment score.']
  risk_assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score (0‑100) representing the overall risk of onboarding this account.']
  sla_target_osa_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target On‑Shelf‑Availability service level agreement percentage for the account.']
  sla_target_otif_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target On‑Time‑In‑Full service level agreement percentage for the account.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Salesforce) that supplied the onboarding data.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the onboarding request in the source system.']
  target_go_live_date date [note: 'Planned date for the trade account to become active in the system.']
  vmi_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account participates in a VMI program.']

  Note: 'Tracks the end-to-end onboarding lifecycle for new trade accounts from initial application through full activation. Captures onboarding request date, applicant legal entity, KYC/KYB verification status, credit application status, compliance document checklist completion, EDI setup milestone, initial assortment agreement status, pricing agreement status, assigned onboarding coordinator, target go-live date, actual activation date, and onboarding stage (application, verification, credit approval, setup, testing, active). Enables CPG manufacturers to manage the multi-week new account setup process and measure time-to-trade for new retail and distributor partners.'
}

Table sales.sales_vmi_agreement {
  sales_vmi_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the vmi_agreement association']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the supplier']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the trade account']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the VMI agreement']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the VMI agreement expires or is terminated']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the VMI agreement becomes effective']
  replenishment_model varchar [note: 'Model used for inventory replenishment under the agreement (e.g., Fixed, Variable, Consignment)']
  sales_vmi_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the agreement (e.g., Active, Inactive, Terminated)']

  Note: 'Represents a VMI agreement between a trade account and a supplier, capturing the contractual terms and effective period for each partnership.. Existence Justification: Trade accounts can partner with multiple suppliers under VMI programs, and each supplier can serve multiple trade accounts. The VMI agreement is an operational entity that is created, updated, and terminated by business users and carries its own attributes such as agreement number, effective dates, status, and replenishment model.'
}

Table sales.call {
  call_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the sales_call association']
  sales_rep_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales rep']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade account']
  activities_completed varchar [note: 'Activities performed during the call']
  call_date date [note: 'Date of the sales call']
  call_status varchar [note: 'Status of the call (e.g., completed, cancelled)']
  call_type varchar [note: 'Type of the call (e.g., visit, phone, virtual)']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Duration of the call in minutes']
  objective varchar [note: 'Objective of the call (e.g., product demo, order taking)']

  Note: 'Represents an interaction (call/visit) between a trade account and a sales representative. Captures details of each sales call such as date, type, status, objectives, activities performed, and duration.. Existence Justification: In the consumer goods business, a trade account can be visited or contacted by multiple sales representatives (field, inside, DSD) and each sales rep serves many trade accounts across territories. The interaction is recorded as a sales call with its own attributes (date, type, status, objectives, activities, duration), which are managed by users for performance reporting and territory management.'
}

Table sales.compliance_assignment {
  compliance_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the compliance_assignment association']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the compliance_obligation master record']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the trade_account master record']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the obligation for this account (e.g., pending, compliant, non‑compliant)']
  compliance_type varchar [note: 'Type of compliance activity for this account (e.g., registration, reporting)']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date the license/permit expires for the account']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the license/permit was issued to the account']
  license_number varchar [note: 'License or permit number issued to the account for this obligation']

  Note: 'This association captures the operational relationship between a trade_account and a compliance_obligation. It records which obligations each account must meet and stores compliance-specific details that belong to the link, such as status, license numbers, and relevant dates.. Existence Justification: Each B2B trade account must satisfy a set of regulatory compliance obligations, and each regulatory obligation applies to many trade accounts. The compliance team actively creates, updates, and deletes records that link a trade_account to a compliance_obligation, tracking status, dates, and license information for each link.'
}

Table sales.account_team {
  account_team_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for account_team']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who leads the team.']
  parent_account_team_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on account_team (parent_account_team_id)']

  average_deal_size decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average monetary value of deals closed by the team.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance standing of the team with internal policies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the team record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary fields.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Classification level for the team record data.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the team\'s purpose and scope.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the team became operational.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the team ceased operation (null if still active).']
  external_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the team in an external CRM or ERP system.']
  is_cross_functional boolean [note: 'True if the team spans multiple functional areas.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this team is the default assignment for new accounts.']
  is_virtual boolean [note: 'True if the team operates without a physical office.']
  last_activity_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent sales activity logged for the team.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last updated the team record.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent performance review for the team.']
  last_sync_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent synchronization with external systems.']
  manager_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the team manager.']
  manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the manager responsible for the team.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes about the team.']
  offboarding_date date [note: 'Date the team was officially retired or dissolved.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date the team was formally onboarded into the organization.']
  performance_rating varchar [note: 'Internal performance rating for the team.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address for the team\'s primary point of contact.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for the team\'s primary point of contact.']
  quota_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Annual sales quota assigned to the team, in local currency.']
  region varchar [note: 'Primary geographic region the team serves.']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the team is formally reviewed.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Primary sales channel the team operates in.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the team.']
  team_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code used in external systems for the team.']
  team_color varchar [note: 'Hex code or name used for UI representation of the team.']
  team_logo_url varchar [note: 'Web URL pointing to the team\'s logo image.']
  team_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the sales account team.']
  team_size varchar [note: 'Number of members assigned to the team.']
  team_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the team by scope or specialty.']
  total_accounts varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct customer accounts ever managed by the team.']
  total_sales_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cumulative sales revenue generated by the team.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the team record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the team record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for account_team. Referenced by account_team_id.'
}

Table quality.inspection_plan {
  inspection_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inspection plan. Primary key for the inspection plan master data entity.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Inspection Plan Approval Process requires linking the approving employee to ensure audit trail and compliance reporting.']
  manufacturing_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant or facility where this inspection plan is applicable. Inspection plans may be plant-specific due to local equipment and procedures.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Brand Quality Assurance Plan links inspection plans to specific brand for compliance reporting and marketing claim validation.']
  responsible_lab_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the quality control laboratory or testing facility responsible for executing this inspection plan.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material (raw material, packaging component, intermediate, or finished good) that this inspection plan applies to.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this inspection plan was formally approved for use. Part of the plan\'s audit trail and regulatory documentation.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether inspection results under this plan require formal approval by a quality manager or authorized person before batch release. Typically true for GMP-critical inspections.']
  aql_level decimal(5,2) [note: 'Acceptable Quality Level expressed as a percentage. Maximum percent defective that is considered acceptable for the inspection plan. Used in acceptance sampling decisions.']
  certificate_of_analysis_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal Certificate of Analysis document must be generated upon successful completion of inspections under this plan. Typically required for finished goods and customer-facing materials.']
  change_control_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the change control request or document that authorized the creation or last modification of this inspection plan. Required for GMP compliance and audit trail.']
  characteristic_count varchar [note: 'Number of quality characteristics (test parameters) defined in this inspection plan. Each characteristic has its own specification limits.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inspection plan record was first created in the source system. Part of the audit trail.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which this inspection plan is no longer valid. Nullable for open-ended plans. Used for version control and regulatory compliance.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this inspection plan becomes valid and applicable for quality inspections. Part of the plan\'s effective date range.']
  gmp_critical_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inspection plan covers GMP-critical quality attributes that directly impact product safety, efficacy, or regulatory compliance. GMP-critical inspections require enhanced documentation and cannot be skipped.']
  inspection_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Expected number of calendar days required to complete all inspections and testing defined in this plan. Used for production scheduling and inventory planning.']
  inspection_method varchar [note: 'Primary method used to perform the inspection: manual measurement, automated testing, laboratory analysis, visual examination, destructive testing, or non-destructive testing.']
  inspection_severity varchar [note: 'Severity level of inspection based on supplier quality history and risk assessment. Tightened inspection applies stricter acceptance criteria.']
  inspection_stage varchar [note: 'Production or supply chain stage at which this inspection is triggered. Aligns with GMP critical control points.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Type of quality inspection this plan governs: incoming (supplier materials), in-process (production intermediates), final (finished goods), stability (shelf-life studies), release (batch release), or periodic (routine surveillance).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this inspection plan record. Used for change tracking and data synchronization.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the material subject to inspection. Determines applicable GMP requirements and inspection rigor.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this inspection plan. GMP requires regular review of quality procedures to ensure continued appropriateness.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the inspection plan, used across quality management systems and documentation. Externally visible code for referencing the plan in quality specifications and batch records.']
  plan_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the inspection plan including scope, objectives, and special instructions for quality control personnel.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the inspection plan describing its purpose and scope.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inspection plan. Only active plans are used for inspection lot creation.']
  regulatory_requirement varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory requirement, standard, or guideline that mandates this inspection (e.g., FDA 21 CFR Part 211, EU GMP Annex 1, ISO 22716). Critical for compliance audits.']
  responsible_inspector_role varchar [note: 'Job role or qualification level required for personnel authorized to perform inspections under this plan (e.g., QC Technician, QC Analyst, QC Manager). Ensures competency requirements are met.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision number of this inspection plan. Incremented each time the plan is updated. Part of change control and audit trail.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of sample units to be drawn per inspection lot. Determined by statistical sampling plan and AQL requirements.']
  sample_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the sample (e.g., pieces, kg, liters, containers). Defines what constitutes one sample unit.']
  sampling_procedure_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the sampling procedure to be followed: sample size, sampling frequency, sampling method (random, systematic, stratified). References approved sampling SOP.']
  skip_lot_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether skip-lot inspection (reduced frequency based on consistent quality history) is permitted for this plan. Not allowed for GMP-critical inspections.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system from which this inspection plan record originated (e.g., SAP QM, Veeva Vault). Used for data lineage and troubleshooting.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this inspection plan in the source operational system. Enables traceability back to the system of record.']
  specification_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the quality specification document that this inspection plan implements. Ensures traceability to approved specifications.']
  test_equipment_required varchar [note: 'List or description of test equipment, instruments, or laboratory apparatus required to execute this inspection plan. Ensures resource availability.']

  Note: 'Master data defining QC inspection plans and quality specifications for raw materials, in-process intermediates, packaging, and finished goods. Captures sampling procedures, inspection characteristics with specification limits (USL/LSL/target), acceptable quality levels (AQL), test methods, units of measure, effective date ranges, and GMP-mandated control points. Serves as the single authoritative source for what to test, how to sample, and what limits apply — governing all inspection lot execution and feeding CoA generation. Aligned with SAP QM inspection planning.'
}

Table quality.inspection_lot {
  inspection_lot_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inspection lot. Primary key for the inspection lot entity representing a discrete QC inspection event triggered by production batch, goods receipt, or periodic review.']
  batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.batch_record. Business justification: Required for traceability: each inspection lot must be linked to the specific manufacturing batch record that produced the lot, enabling batch-level quality release decisions.']
  carbon_emission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.carbon_emission. Business justification: Needed for integrated quality‑environmental reporting: associate each inspected lot with its carbon emission record to calculate emissions per lot.']
  inbound_receipt_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.inbound_receipt_line. Business justification: Required for traceability: inspection lot must reference the inbound receipt line that supplied the lot, enabling quality inspection reports to be linked to receipt data for OTIF and compliance audits']
  inspection_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the inspection plan template applied to this lot. Defines the characteristics to be inspected, sampling procedures, and acceptance criteria.']
  inspector_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the quality inspector or technician assigned to perform the inspection activities for this lot.']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: Regulatory traceability report requires linking each inspection lot to its originating lot batch for audit trails.']
  primary_inspection_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the quality manager or authorized person who made the final usage decision for this inspection lot.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Required for traceability: link each inspection lot to the purchase order that supplied the material, enabling regulatory compliance and release decisions.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material (raw material, semi-finished good, or finished good) being inspected in this lot.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier or vendor for goods receipt inspection lots. Used for supplier quality performance tracking.']
  tertiary_inspection_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who last modified the inspection lot record. Maintains accountability for changes to quality data.']
  certificate_of_analysis_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the Certificate of Analysis issued for this inspection lot. Links to formal quality documentation in Veeva Vault.']
  coa_issue_date date [note: 'Date when the Certificate of Analysis was formally issued for this inspection lot.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inspection lot record was first created in the quality management system. Audit trail field.']
  decision_date date [note: 'Date when the formal usage decision was made and recorded in the quality management system.']
  decision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the usage decision was recorded. Used for audit trail and compliance reporting.']
  defect_count varchar [note: 'Total number of defects or non-conformances identified during inspection. Used for quality metrics and trend analysis.']
  disposition_outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome status of the lot after disposition decision and stock posting. Confirms the actual inventory movement executed.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration or best-before date assigned to the batch based on stability study results and shelf-life specifications.']
  gmp_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the inspection lot was produced and inspected in compliance with Good Manufacturing Practice regulations.']
  inspection_end_date date [note: 'Date when all inspection and testing activities were completed for this lot.']
  inspection_location varchar [note: 'Physical location or quality lab where the inspection was performed. May be different from the plant code for centralized testing facilities.']
  inspection_lot_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the inspection lot. Externally visible alphanumeric code used in SAP QM and quality documentation for traceability and audit purposes.']
  inspection_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for inspector comments, observations, or special conditions noted during the inspection process.']
  inspection_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the inspection lot. Determines the sequence and urgency of inspection execution in the quality lab.']
  inspection_start_date date [note: 'Date when the physical inspection and testing activities began for this lot.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inspection lot. Tracks progression from creation through inspection execution to final disposition decision.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inspection lot based on the triggering event. Determines the inspection plan template and sampling strategy applied.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inspection lot record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change for audit and compliance purposes.']
  lot_origin_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the originating transaction (production order number, purchase order number, delivery number, etc.) that triggered this inspection lot.']
  lot_origin_type varchar [note: 'Source document type that triggered the creation of this inspection lot. Links the quality inspection to the originating business transaction.']
  lot_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total quantity of material in the inspection lot. Represents the population from which samples are drawn for inspection.']
  lot_quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the lot quantity (each, kilogram, liter, meter, etc.). Aligns with material master UOM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|KG|L|M|M2|M3|BOX|CASE — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  material_number varchar [note: 'Business material code (SKU) of the product being inspected. Used for cross-reference and reporting.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant or facility code where the inspection is performed. Aligns with SAP plant master data.']
  regulatory_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lot is under regulatory hold pending additional review or approval from regulatory affairs or quality assurance.']
  regulatory_hold_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the lot is under regulatory hold. Documents the specific compliance or quality concern requiring additional review.']
  retest_date date [note: 'Date when the material must be re-inspected to confirm continued compliance with quality specifications. Applicable for raw materials and intermediates.']
  sample_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of material drawn as a sample for inspection testing. Determined by the inspection plan sampling procedure.']
  sample_quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the sample quantity drawn for testing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|KG|L|M|M2|M3|BOX|CASE — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  stock_posting_instruction varchar [note: 'Instruction for inventory movement based on the usage decision. Determines the target stock type for material posting in warehouse management.']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'Warehouse storage location code where the inspected material is held during quality inspection.']
  usage_decision varchar [note: 'Final disposition decision for the inspected lot. Determines whether material can be released for use, must be reworked, or should be rejected.']
  usage_decision_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the usage decision. Used for automated stock posting and reporting in SAP QM.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a QC inspection lot triggered by a production batch, goods receipt, or periodic review. Tracks lot origin (batch, purchase order, production order), inspection type, assigned inspection plan, lot quantity, inspection status, and complete disposition lifecycle including formal usage decision (accept/reject/restricted use/rework), decision maker, decision date, decision code, stock posting instruction, regulatory hold flag, and disposition outcome. Core operational entity in SAP QM representing the end-to-end inspection-to-disposition workflow.'
}

Table quality.inspection_result {
  inspection_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inspection result record. Primary key for granular QC (Quality Control) measurement and test result records captured at the characteristic level within an inspection lot.']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent inspection lot under which this characteristic-level result was captured. Links to the inspection lot entity that groups multiple inspection results for a batch or production run.']
  inspection_result_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.specification. Business justification: Link inspection_result to its governing specification for easy access to spec limits and versioning.']
  primary_inspection_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the quality inspector or technician who performed the measurement. Required for GMP audit trails and accountability.']
  sample_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the physical sample from which this measurement was taken. Enables traceability back to the specific sample unit within the inspection lot.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval status of this inspection result by a quality supervisor or manager. Pending indicates awaiting review; approved indicates result accepted; rejected indicates result invalidated and retest required.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this inspection result was approved. Required for GMP audit trails and batch release decision traceability.']
  coa_inclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this result should be included in the Certificate of Analysis (COA) document for the batch. True indicates inclusion; false indicates internal-only result.']
  control_chart_rule_violated varchar [note: 'Description of the specific SPC control chart rule that was violated (e.g., point beyond control limit, 7 consecutive points above centerline, 2 of 3 points beyond 2-sigma). Populated when control_chart_violation_flag is true.']
  control_chart_violation_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this measurement violates SPC (Statistical Process Control) control chart rules (e.g., out of control limits, run rules, trend rules). True indicates a violation requiring investigation.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source system from which this inspection result was captured (e.g., SAP QM, LIMS, Veeva Vault, manual entry). Used for data lineage and audit purposes.']
  defect_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code classifying the type of defect or non-conformance observed when result_status is fail. Used for defect trending and root cause analysis.']
  defect_severity varchar [note: 'Classification of the severity of the non-conformance. Critical defects pose safety or regulatory risk; major defects affect functionality; minor defects are cosmetic or non-functional.']
  deviation_code varchar [note: 'Reference to an approved deviation or waiver document that authorizes acceptance of a non-conforming result. Populated when result_status is waived.']
  environmental_condition_humidity decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative humidity (percentage) at the time of measurement. Critical for tests sensitive to moisture levels.']
  environmental_condition_temperature decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ambient temperature (in degrees Celsius) at the time of measurement. Critical for tests sensitive to environmental conditions.']
  inspection_characteristic_code varchar [note: 'Unique code identifying the specific quality characteristic being measured (e.g., pH level, viscosity, weight, color, microbial count). Corresponds to the master inspection characteristic definition.']
  inspection_characteristic_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the quality characteristic being tested (e.g., Product pH, Net Weight, Microbial Load, Color Consistency).']
  inspection_method_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the standardized test method or procedure used to perform this measurement (e.g., ASTM method, USP method, internal SOP number).']
  inspection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this specific characteristic measurement was performed. Critical for time-sensitive tests and GMP audit trails.']
  instrument_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the measurement instrument or equipment used to capture this result (e.g., balance ID, pH meter ID, spectrophotometer ID). Required for GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice) audit trails and instrument calibration traceability.']
  laboratory_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the laboratory or testing facility where this measurement was performed. May be an internal QC lab or an external contract lab.']
  lower_specification_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The minimum acceptable value for this characteristic as defined in the product specification or quality standard. Values below this threshold indicate non-conformance.']
  measured_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The actual quantitative measurement or test result value recorded during inspection. For qualitative characteristics, this may be encoded numerically or stored as a code.']
  measurement_uncertainty decimal(18,6) [note: 'Quantified estimate of the uncertainty or error margin associated with the measured value. Required for ISO 17025 compliance and advanced SPC (Statistical Process Control) analysis.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this inspection result record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data governance.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this inspection result record was last modified. Audit trail field for data governance and change tracking.']
  regulatory_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this result must be reported to regulatory authorities (e.g., FDA, EPA) due to non-conformance or safety concerns. True indicates reportable event.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or observations recorded by the inspector regarding this measurement. May include notes on sample condition, procedural deviations, or contextual information.']
  result_status varchar [note: 'Pass/fail status of this individual characteristic measurement. Pass indicates the measured value is within specification limits; fail indicates non-conformance; conditional indicates acceptance with restrictions; pending indicates awaiting review; waived indicates non-conformance accepted by authorized deviation.']
  retest_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this result is a retest of a previously failed or inconclusive measurement. True indicates this is a retest; false indicates initial test.']
  retest_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for retesting this characteristic. Applicable only when retest_indicator is true.']
  sample_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of this sample within the inspection lot (e.g., 1, 2, 3). Used to track sampling order and support statistical sampling plans.']
  target_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The ideal or nominal value for this characteristic. Used for process capability analysis and SPC (Statistical Process Control) charting.']
  test_batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch or run number of the test reagents or consumables used during this measurement. Required for traceability in regulated environments.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the measured value is expressed (e.g., grams, milliliters, pH units, CFU/g for colony forming units per gram, percentage).']
  upper_specification_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The maximum acceptable value for this characteristic as defined in the product specification or quality standard. Values above this threshold indicate non-conformance.']

  Note: 'Granular QC measurement and test result records captured at the characteristic level within an inspection lot. Stores measured values, upper/lower specification limits, pass/fail status, instrument used, inspector ID, and timestamp. Supports SPC (Statistical Process Control) and GMP audit trails per ISO 9001 and GMP requirements.'
}

Table quality.usage_decision {
  usage_decision_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the quality control usage decision record. Primary key.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the quality manager or authorized approver who provided final approval for the usage decision, if required. Null if no approval was needed.']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the CAPA record initiated as a result of this usage decision, if applicable. Null if no CAPA was required.']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the inspection lot for which this usage decision was made. Links to the QC inspection lot that was evaluated.']
  primary_usage_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the quality control inspector or authorized personnel who made the usage decision. Required for accountability and audit trails.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material or product that was inspected and for which the usage decision applies.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or vendor who provided the material, if applicable. Used for supplier quality management and vendor scorecarding.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date when the usage decision was formally approved by authorized quality management personnel. Null if no approval was required.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this usage decision requires additional management or quality assurance approval before batch release. True=Approval required, False=No additional approval needed.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'The production batch or lot number to which this usage decision applies. Critical for traceability and recall management.']
  capa_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this usage decision triggers a CAPA process due to significant non-conformance. True=CAPA required, False=No CAPA needed. Links to continuous improvement.']
  certificate_of_analysis_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the Certificate of Analysis document that supports this usage decision. Links to detailed test results and specifications.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional free-text comments or notes related to the usage decision. May include special handling instructions, escalation notes, or contextual information.']
  decision_code varchar [note: 'The formal quality control usage decision code. A=Accept (Unrestricted Use), R=Reject, Q=Quality Hold, S=Restricted Use (Sample/Scrap), X=Rework Required. Drives downstream inventory disposition.']
  decision_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the usage decision was formally made and recorded. Critical for compliance timelines and batch release tracking.']
  decision_maker_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the quality control inspector or authorized personnel who made the usage decision. Captured for audit and traceability purposes.']
  decision_text varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description or rationale for the usage decision. Captures the quality assessor\'s reasoning and any special conditions or instructions.']
  decision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the usage decision was recorded in the system. Provides audit trail precision for regulatory compliance.']
  defect_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the primary defect or non-conformance that led to rejection or restricted use decision. Used for root cause analysis and trend reporting.']
  defect_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the defect, non-conformance, or quality issue identified during inspection. Supports CAPA and continuous improvement processes.']
  disposition_date date [note: 'The date when the material disposition action was completed (e.g., stock posted to unrestricted, blocked, or scrapped). Null if disposition is still pending.']
  disposition_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the material disposition action following the usage decision. pending=Decision made but stock not yet moved, completed=Stock posting completed, cancelled=Decision reversed or voided.']
  gmp_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the batch meets Good Manufacturing Practice requirements. True=GMP compliant, False=Non-compliant. Required for pharmaceutical and cosmetic products.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'The type of quality inspection that led to this usage decision. goods_receipt=incoming material inspection, in_process=during production, final=finished goods, stability=shelf-life testing, complaint=customer complaint investigation.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'The manufacturing plant or facility code where the inspection and usage decision were performed. Critical for multi-site quality management.']
  quantity_accepted decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of material accepted for unrestricted use based on the usage decision. Measured in the material\'s base unit of measure.']
  quantity_rejected decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of material rejected and not suitable for use. Measured in the material\'s base unit of measure. May trigger disposal or return to supplier.']
  quantity_rework decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of material designated for rework or reprocessing to meet quality standards. Measured in the material\'s base unit of measure.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this usage decision record was first created in the lakehouse. Part of the audit trail for data governance.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this usage decision record was last updated in the lakehouse. Tracks data freshness and change history.']
  regulatory_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material is under regulatory hold and cannot be released for distribution. True=Hold in effect, False=No regulatory hold. Critical for FDA and EPA compliance.']
  regulatory_hold_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the material is under regulatory hold. May reference specific test failures, compliance issues, or pending regulatory approvals.']
  sampling_procedure varchar [note: 'The sampling method or procedure used during the inspection (e.g., random sampling, statistical sampling, 100% inspection). Ensures inspection validity.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system from which this usage decision record originated (e.g., SAP QM, Veeva Vault, Siemens Opcenter MES). Used for data lineage and integration tracking.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this usage decision record in the source operational system. Enables traceability back to the system of record.']
  stock_posting_instruction varchar [note: 'Instruction for how the inspected material should be posted to inventory. Determines the stock type and availability for use: unrestricted (available for sale/use), blocked (not available), quality_inspection (under evaluation), scrap (to be disposed), sample (for testing), rework (to be reprocessed).']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'The warehouse storage location code where the inspected material is physically located. Used for inventory disposition and stock movement.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the quantities in this usage decision (e.g., EA=Each, KG=Kilogram, L=Liter, CS=Case). Aligns with material master UOM.']

  Note: 'Records the formal QC usage decision made at the conclusion of an inspection lot — accept, reject, restricted use, or rework. Captures decision code, decision maker, decision date, stock posting instruction, and regulatory hold flag. Drives downstream batch release and inventory disposition in SAP QM/MM.'
}

Table quality.nonconformance {
  nonconformance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the nonconformance record. Primary key for the nonconformance data product.']
  action_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_action. Business justification: Nonconformance findings can lead to regulatory enforcement actions; linking supports audit trails.']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the CAPA record created to address the root cause of this nonconformance. Links to capa_record for tracking corrective and preventive actions.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost of quality report allocates nonconformance financial impact to responsible cost center for internal cost tracking.']
  inbound_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.inbound_receipt. Business justification: Required for receipt quality control: nonconformance must reference the inbound receipt that triggered the issue, enabling tracking of receipt-level defects and corrective actions.']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: Non‑conformance investigations must reference the specific lot batch to drive root‑cause analysis and recall actions.']
  nonconformance_closed_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the quality professional who closed the nonconformance record after verification. Links to workforce/employee master data.']
  nonconformance_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the quality professional or team member assigned to investigate and resolve the nonconformance. Links to workforce/employee master data.']
  product_complaint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the customer complaint record that triggered this nonconformance, when applicable. Links to consumer complaint tracking system.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Non‑conformance events are tied to the SKU that caused the issue, enabling targeted CAPA and impact analysis.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the supplier associated with this nonconformance, applicable when the issue originates from supplier-provided materials or components. Links to supplier master data.']
  transport_exception_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.transport_exception. Business justification: Captures transport‑related nonconformance incidents, supporting root‑cause analysis and corrective action planning.']
  affected_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of product units or material affected by the nonconformance. Used for containment scope and financial impact assessment.']
  affected_quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the affected quantity: EA (each/units), KG (kilograms), L (liters), M (meters), BOX (boxes), PALLET (pallets).']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the nonconformance was formally closed after verification of corrective actions and effectiveness checks. Null if still open.']
  containment_action varchar [note: 'Immediate containment actions taken to prevent further occurrence or distribution of nonconforming product. Describes short-term corrective measures implemented before root cause analysis.']
  containment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when containment actions were completed and verified effective. Critical for regulatory reporting and OTIF compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this nonconformance record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer. Audit trail for data governance.']
  defect_classification varchar [note: 'Technical classification of the defect type: appearance (cosmetic defect), functionality (performance issue), safety (consumer safety risk), labeling (incorrect or missing label information), packaging (packaging integrity issue), contamination (foreign material or microbial contamination), specification_failure (out-of-specification test result), or process_deviation (manufacturing process non-conformance). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: appearance|functionality|safety|labeling|packaging|contamination|specification_failure|process_deviation — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  defect_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the nonconformance, including observed symptoms, affected characteristics, and initial assessment. Captures the what, where, and when of the quality event.']
  detected_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the nonconformance was first detected or observed. Represents the real-world event time of quality issue identification.']
  detection_source varchar [note: 'Origin point where the nonconformance was first identified: QC inspection (quality control laboratory testing), manufacturing line (in-process detection), supplier receipt (incoming material inspection), customer complaint (consumer-reported issue), regulatory audit (FDA or regulatory body inspection), internal audit (GMP self-audit), or stability study (long-term product stability testing). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: qc_inspection|manufacturing_line|supplier_receipt|customer_complaint|regulatory_audit|internal_audit|stability_study — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  disposition_approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the quality authority who approved the disposition decision. Links to workforce/employee master data for audit trail and GMP compliance.']
  disposition_approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the disposition decision was formally approved by quality authority. Critical for GMP audit trail and batch release decisions.']
  disposition_decision varchar [note: 'Final disposition decision for the nonconforming product or material: use_as_is (approved for use despite nonconformance), rework (reprocess to meet specifications), scrap (destroy), return_to_supplier (send back to vendor), downgrade (sell as lower grade), or quarantine (hold pending further investigation).']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the quality event: complaint (customer-reported issue), defect (internal manufacturing defect), deviation (process deviation from standard), improvement request (continuous improvement opportunity), supplier issue (supplier quality problem), or audit finding (GMP audit observation).']
  financial_impact_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated or actual financial impact of the nonconformance, including costs of rework, scrap, containment, investigation, customer credits, and potential recall costs. Expressed in base currency.']
  financial_impact_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the financial impact amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  nonconformance_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the nonconformance record: open (newly created), in_investigation (root cause analysis underway), containment_active (immediate containment actions in progress), capa_assigned (corrective and preventive action assigned), closed (resolved and verified), or cancelled (invalidated).']
  notification_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the quality notification or nonconformance event. Corresponds to SAP QM notification number or Veeva Vault quality event identifier.']
