  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority for resolution based on operational impact, customer visibility, and regulatory risk. Urgent: immediate action required; High: expedited resolution needed; Medium: standard timeline; Low: routine handling.']
  regulatory_report_date date [note: 'Date when the nonconformance was reported to regulatory authorities, if applicable. Null if not reportable or not yet reported.']
  regulatory_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this nonconformance requires reporting to regulatory authorities (FDA, EPA, CPSC) based on severity, product category, and regulatory requirements. True indicates mandatory regulatory notification.']
  reported_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the nonconformance was formally logged into the quality management system (SAP QM or Veeva Vault). May differ from detection timestamp due to reporting lag.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Functional department responsible for investigating and resolving the nonconformance (e.g., Quality Assurance, Manufacturing, Supply Chain, R&D).']
  responsible_plant_code varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant or facility code where the nonconformance originated or is being managed. Links to distribution_facility for organizational accountability.']
  root_cause_analysis varchar [note: 'Detailed findings from root cause investigation, including methodology used (5-Why, Fishbone, Fault Tree Analysis) and identified root causes. Supports CAPA development.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the quality event based on impact to product safety, efficacy, and regulatory compliance. Critical: immediate risk to consumer safety or regulatory violation; Major: significant quality impact requiring containment; Minor: low impact quality issue.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system of record for this nonconformance: SAP_QM (SAP Quality Management module), VEEVA_VAULT (Veeva Vault Quality), or MANUAL_ENTRY (direct data entry).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this nonconformance record in the source system (SAP QM notification number or Veeva Vault quality event ID). Enables traceability back to operational system.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this nonconformance record was last modified in the lakehouse silver layer. Audit trail for data governance and change tracking.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify effectiveness of corrective actions and confirm the nonconformance has been resolved (e.g., re-inspection, re-testing, process validation, trend analysis).']

  Note: 'Unified quality event record for non-conformances, deviations, quality notifications, and improvement requests identified during manufacturing, QC inspection, supplier receipt, or customer complaint escalation. Captures event type (complaint, defect, deviation, improvement request), defect description, defect classification, affected batch/SKU, detection source, severity, priority, containment actions, task assignments, responsible plant, and linkage to CAPA. Serves as the single routing point for all quality events across SAP QM notifications and Veeva Vault workflows. Aligns with ISO 9001:2015 clause 10.2 and GMP deviation reporting requirements.'
}

Table quality.capa {
  capa_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the CAPA record. Primary key.']
  capa_closed_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who formally closed the CAPA record.']
  capa_created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who created the CAPA record in the system.']
  capa_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or team member assigned as the primary owner responsible for executing and closing the CAPA.']
  capa_last_modified_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who last modified the CAPA record.']
  capa_verified_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who performed the effectiveness verification.']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.compliance_obligation. Business justification: CAPA actions are often initiated to meet specific compliance obligations; linking ties corrective actions to the obligation.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: CAPA cost impact must be charged to a cost center for budgeting and expense analysis.']
  esg_commitment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.esg_commitment. Business justification: Required for ESG goal tracking: each CAPA is linked to the specific ESG commitment it addresses, enabling ESG performance reporting.']
  manufacturing_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing facility, warehouse, or site where the CAPA issue originated or is being addressed.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: CAPA Impact Report tracks corrective actions affecting brand reputation, used by marketing for risk communication and messaging.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: CAPA often stems from findings in an R&D project; linking provides root‑cause context and drives corrective actions.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product affected by or related to this CAPA. Links to the product master data.']
  supplier_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_contract. Business justification: CAPA actions often stem from supplier contract obligations; linking ensures contract compliance tracking and auditability of corrective measures.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier associated with the CAPA, if the issue originated from supplier-provided materials or services.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'The actual date when all CAPA actions were completed and verified, prior to formal closure.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing batch or lot number associated with the CAPA, if applicable. Used to trace quality issues to specific production runs.']
  capa_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the nonconformance, audit finding, complaint, or stability failure that triggered the CAPA.']
  capa_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the CAPA record, typically formatted as CAPA-NNNNNN. Used for external communication and audit trails.']
  capa_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the CAPA record, tracking progression from initiation through closure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|investigation|action_plan|implementation|verification|closed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  capa_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the CAPA as corrective (addressing existing nonconformance), preventive (preventing potential nonconformance), or combined (both corrective and preventive actions).']
  closure_comments varchar [note: 'Final comments or notes recorded at the time of CAPA closure, summarizing outcomes and lessons learned.']
  closure_date date [note: 'The date when the CAPA was formally closed after successful completion and verification of all actions.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the corrective action(s) taken to address the existing nonconformance and eliminate its cause.']
  cost_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated or actual financial cost impact of the CAPA issue, including costs of investigation, rework, scrap, recall, or other remediation activities.']
  cost_impact_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost impact amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the CAPA record was first created in the quality management system.']
  customer_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the CAPA issue had a direct impact on customers, such as product quality defects, delivery delays, or safety concerns.']
  effectiveness_verification_method varchar [note: 'The method or approach used to verify that the corrective and preventive actions were effective in resolving the issue and preventing recurrence (e.g., follow-up audit, process monitoring, trend analysis).']
  effectiveness_verification_result varchar [note: 'The outcome of the effectiveness verification, indicating whether the CAPA actions successfully resolved the issue and prevented recurrence.']
  gmp_deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the CAPA issue involved a deviation from Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) standards.']
  initiated_date date [note: 'The date when the CAPA record was formally initiated and opened in the quality management system.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the CAPA record was last updated or modified.']
  preventive_action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the preventive action(s) implemented to prevent recurrence of the issue or occurrence of similar issues.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the CAPA for resource allocation and scheduling purposes.']
  process_area varchar [note: 'The specific process, department, or functional area where the issue occurred (e.g., Manufacturing, Packaging, Quality Control, Supply Chain).']
  regulatory_report_date date [note: 'The date when the CAPA issue was reported to the relevant regulatory authority, if applicable.']
  regulatory_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this CAPA issue is required to be reported to regulatory authorities such as FDA, EPA, or other governing bodies.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'The department or functional area responsible for implementing the CAPA actions.']
  root_cause_analysis_method varchar [note: 'The methodology used to identify the root cause of the issue, such as 5 Whys, Fishbone Diagram, Fault Tree Analysis, Pareto Analysis, or Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA).']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the identified root cause(s) of the nonconformance or issue.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the CAPA issue based on impact to product quality, safety, regulatory compliance, or business operations.']
  source_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference identifier of the originating event (e.g., nonconformance ID, audit finding ID, complaint ID) that triggered this CAPA.']
  source_type varchar [note: 'The origin or trigger event that initiated the CAPA, such as nonconformance, audit finding, customer complaint, supplier quality issue, stability study failure, or regulatory inspection finding.']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'The planned or target date by which all CAPA actions should be completed and verified.']
  title varchar [note: 'Brief descriptive title summarizing the CAPA issue or focus area.']
  verification_date date [note: 'The date when the effectiveness verification was completed.']

  Note: 'Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) records initiated in response to nonconformances, audit findings, complaints, or stability failures. Tracks root cause analysis method, corrective actions, preventive actions, responsible owner, target completion dates, effectiveness verification, and closure status. Managed in Veeva Vault QMS.'
}

Table quality.batch_release {
  batch_release_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the batch release decision record. Primary key for the batch release entity.']
  batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.batch_record. Business justification: Batch release decisions are based on the manufacturing batch record; linking ensures release status reflects the underlying production data and supports regulatory audit trails.']
  batch_release_certificate_of_analysis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.certificate_of_analysis. Business justification: Link batch_release to the Certificate of Analysis that documents the released batch, removing redundant reference number.']
  distribution_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_shipment. Business justification: Required for shipment planning: batch release must reference the distribution shipment that will carry the released batch, ensuring only released batches are shipped and supporting release status dash']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the Qualified Person who authorized the batch release. QP sign-off is a regulatory requirement in EU GMP and similar frameworks.']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: Batch release decisions rely on the exact lot batch record to confirm quality release status and regulatory compliance.']
  modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the batch release record. Required for audit trail and 21 CFR Part 11 compliance.']
  prototype_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.prototype. Business justification: Prototype batch releases are recorded against the specific prototype record to track release decisions for pilot production.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Batch release reports require linking each release to the specific SKU for traceability, regulatory filing, and inventory reconciliation.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the complete audit trail log for all actions and approvals related to this batch release. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 compliance.']
  batch_size_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total quantity of finished goods produced in this batch. Used for yield calculation and inventory planning.']
  batch_size_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the batch size quantity (e.g., kilograms, liters, units). Aligns with GS1 standards for trade item measurement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: kg|lbs|liters|gallons|units|cases|pallets — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  capa_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a CAPA investigation is required based on deviations or non-conformances identified during batch production or testing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch release record was first created in the system. Part of audit trail for regulatory compliance.']
  deviation_count varchar [note: 'Number of manufacturing or quality deviations recorded during the production and testing of this batch. Used for quality trend analysis.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the batch should not be sold or used. Calculated from production date plus shelf life. Critical for FEFO inventory management and regulatory compliance.']
  gmp_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the batch was manufactured in full compliance with GMP requirements. False indicates deviation or non-conformance.']
  hold_initiated_date date [note: 'Date when the regulatory or business hold was placed on the batch. Nullable if no hold exists.']
  hold_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the batch is on hold, if applicable. Includes regulatory citation, investigation reference, or customer complaint number.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch release record was last updated. Tracks changes to release status, hold status, or other critical fields.']
  manufacturing_site_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the manufacturing facility or plant where the batch was produced. Used for site-level quality tracking and regulatory reporting.']
  product_registration_number varchar [note: 'Regulatory registration or approval number for the product in the target market. Required for FDA NDC, EU product registration, or equivalent.']
  production_date date [note: 'Date when the batch manufacturing was completed. Critical for shelf-life calculation and FEFO inventory management.']
  qc_inspection_status varchar [note: 'Overall status of QC inspection and testing for this batch. Summarizes results from multiple QC test records.']
  qp_signature_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the Qualified Person electronically signed the batch release decision. Part of 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature compliance.']
  qualified_person_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the Qualified Person who signed off on the batch release. Required for regulatory audit trail.']
  regulatory_hold_status varchar [note: 'Indicates if the batch is under any regulatory or business hold that prevents distribution. Holds may be imposed by FDA, EPA, internal quality, customer complaint, or recall investigation.']
  regulatory_market varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory market or jurisdiction for which this batch was released. Different markets may have different release requirements and QP sign-off rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|MEX|GBR|DEU|FRA|ITA|ESP|CHN|JPN|AUS|BRA|IND — 13 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  rejected_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of the batch that was rejected and marked for destruction or rework. Part of quality yield tracking.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date when the batch was officially released for distribution and sale. Marks the point at which product becomes available to promise (ATP).']
  release_decision varchar [note: 'Final disposition decision for the batch: released for distribution, rejected for destruction, quarantine for further testing, conditional release with restrictions, pending review, or on hold pending investigation. Critical GMP control point.']
  release_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and comments from the Qualified Person regarding the release decision. May include justification for conditional release or special handling instructions.']
  release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the batch release decision was recorded in the system. Used for audit trail and regulatory compliance.']
  released_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of the batch that was approved for release and distribution. May be less than batch size if partial rejection occurred.']
  retest_date date [note: 'Date when the batch must be retested to confirm continued quality and stability. Common for raw materials and intermediates.']
  sap_qm_inspection_lot varchar [note: 'SAP QM inspection lot number associated with this batch release. Links to SAP S/4HANA QM module for detailed inspection results and usage decisions.']
  stability_study_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number linking to the stability study protocol and results that support the assigned shelf life and expiry date for this batch.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier in Veeva Vault system linking to the official batch release record and supporting quality documents. Integrates with Veeva Vault QMS.']

  Note: 'Formal batch release decision records authorizing finished goods batches for distribution and sale. Captures batch number, product SKU, release decision (released, rejected, quarantine), QP (Qualified Person) sign-off, release date, certificate of analysis reference, and regulatory hold status. Critical GMP control point per FDA 21 CFR and EU GMP.'
}

Table quality.certificate_of_analysis {
  certificate_of_analysis_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the certificate of analysis record. Primary key.']
  certificate_of_analysis_laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.laboratory. Business justification: Link certificate_of_analysis to the laboratory that issued it, removing redundant name/code fields.']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the lot or batch for which this certificate of analysis was issued. Links to the specific production batch of finished goods or raw materials.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: COA must reference the supplier of the batch for traceability and regulatory compliance; supplier_name is replaced by FK.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the certificate was officially approved by the authorized signatory. Represents the moment of quality release decision.']
  authorized_signatory_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the quality assurance professional authorized to approve and sign this certificate. Must have appropriate delegation of authority.']
  authorized_signatory_title varchar [note: 'Job title or role of the authorized signatory (e.g., QA Manager, Quality Director, Laboratory Supervisor).']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'The manufacturing batch or lot number for the material being certified. Critical for traceability and recall management.']
  batch_size decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total quantity produced in the manufacturing batch. Used to assess sample representativeness and for statistical quality control.']
  batch_size_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the batch size. Typically matches the material\'s production UOM.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique certificate number assigned to this CoA document. Used for customer communication and regulatory submissions.']
  certificate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the certificate based on the material type being certified. Determines applicable test parameters and regulatory requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this certificate record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record lifecycle.']
  customer_facing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certificate is intended for external distribution to trade customers or is for internal use only. True if customer-facing.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the digitally signed PDF or scanned image of the certificate stored in Veeva Vault or document management system.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date after which the material should not be used or sold. Based on stability studies and regulatory requirements.']
  gmp_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the batch and testing process were conducted under GMP conditions. True if GMP-compliant.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date on which this certificate of analysis was officially issued and authorized for distribution to customers or regulatory bodies.']
  manufacturing_date date [note: 'The date on which the batch was manufactured or produced. Used for shelf-life calculations and FEFO inventory management.']
  manufacturing_site_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the manufacturing facility or plant where the batch was produced. Critical for site-specific compliance and traceability.']
  manufacturing_site_name varchar [note: 'Full name and location of the manufacturing site. Supports multi-site operations and regulatory site registration requirements.']
  material_code varchar [note: 'The product or material code (SKU, item number, or raw material identifier) for which this certificate was issued. Links to master product or material data.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Full textual description of the material or product covered by this certificate. Includes product name, variant, and key characteristics.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this certificate record was last modified. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.']
  overall_result_status varchar [note: 'Aggregate pass/fail status for all test parameters on this certificate. Determines batch release decision and customer distribution eligibility.']
  quantity_tested decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of material sampled and tested for this certificate. Expressed in the material\'s base unit of measure.']
  quantity_tested_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity tested (e.g., kilograms, liters, units). Aligns with material master UOM.']
  regulatory_submission_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certificate is required for regulatory submissions or compliance filings with FDA, EPA, or other governing bodies. True if required for submission.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, observations, or special conditions related to the testing or batch. May include deviations or special handling instructions.']
  retest_date date [note: 'The date by which the material should be retested to confirm continued conformance to specifications. Applicable primarily to raw materials.']
  specification_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the product specification or quality standard used for testing. Ensures traceability to the correct specification limits.']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Recommended storage conditions for the material (e.g., store in cool dry place, refrigerate at 2-8°C, protect from light). Based on stability data.']
  supplier_lot_number varchar [note: 'The lot or batch number assigned by the supplier for raw materials or packaging materials. Used for supplier quality traceability.']
  test_date date [note: 'The date on which quality control testing was performed for this certificate. May differ from manufacturing date for retained samples.']
  test_method_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the analytical test methods used (e.g., USP, ASTM, internal method codes). May be a comma-separated list for multiple methods.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Unique document identifier in Veeva Vault system where the certificate is stored. Enables integration with regulatory document management.']

  Note: 'Certificate of Analysis (CoA) documents issued per finished goods or raw material batch. Stores test parameters, specification limits, actual results, pass/fail status, issuing lab, authorized signatory, issue date, and customer-facing distribution flag. Supports trade customer requirements and regulatory submissions. Managed in Veeva Vault.'
}

Table quality.quality_stability_study {
  quality_stability_study_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the stability study record. Primary key.']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: Stability studies are performed on a defined lot batch; linking enables shelf‑life reporting and regulatory submissions.']
  quality_stability_study_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.specification. Business justification: Link quality_stability_study to the specification it validates, eliminating the free‑text reference column.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product or formulation being tested in this stability study.']
  approved_shelf_life_months varchar [note: 'Regulatory-approved or final determined shelf life in months based on stability study results. May differ from proposed shelf life if data does not support full duration.']
  commitment_due_date date [note: 'Regulatory deadline by which stability study results must be submitted to the health authority. Applicable only when stability_commitment_flag is true.']
  container_closure_system varchar [note: 'Detailed specification of the container and closure materials and design used in the stability study. Critical for assessing barrier properties and product protection.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this stability study record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  deviation_count varchar [note: 'Number of protocol deviations or non-conformances recorded during the stability study execution (e.g., missed time points, temperature excursions, testing delays).']
  formulation_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the specific product formulation or recipe being tested. Used to track formulation changes and their impact on stability.']
  ich_climatic_zone varchar [note: 'International Council for Harmonisation (ICH) climatic zone classification for the intended market. Zone I (temperate), Zone II (subtropical/Mediterranean), Zone III (hot/dry), Zone IV (hot/humid). Determines required storage conditions.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this stability study record was last updated. Audit trail for record changes.']
  manufacturing_site varchar [note: 'Identifier or name of the manufacturing facility where the batch being tested was produced. Important for site-specific stability commitments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, observations, or context about the stability study that do not fit in structured fields.']
  out_of_specification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any test results during the study were out of specification, triggering investigation and potential corrective action. True if any OOS results occurred.']
  out_of_trend_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any test results showed unexpected trends or deviations from expected stability behavior, even if within specification. True if any OOT results occurred.']
  packaging_configuration varchar [note: 'Description of the primary and secondary packaging used for the stability samples (e.g., HDPE bottle with child-resistant cap, aluminum tube with flip-top cap). Packaging can significantly impact product stability.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the product type being tested (e.g., oral solid, topical cream, liquid suspension, aerosol). Different product forms have different stability considerations and testing requirements.']
  proposed_shelf_life_months varchar [note: 'Intended or claimed shelf life in months that this stability study is designed to support. Final shelf life determination is based on study results.']
  protocol_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the approved stability study protocol document that defines test methods, sampling schedule, acceptance criteria, and storage conditions. Links to protocol management system or Veeva Vault.']
  regulatory_purpose varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory or business purpose for conducting the stability study. Registration studies support new product applications. Post-approval studies fulfill ongoing commitments. Change-control studies assess impact of manufacturing or formulation changes.']
  regulatory_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or regulatory authority for which this stability study is being conducted (e.g., USA, EU, Japan, Global). Different regions may have different storage condition requirements per ICH climatic zones.']
  report_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the final stability study report was reviewed and approved by quality assurance. Marks formal completion of the study.']
  report_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier for the final stability study report. Links to document management system or Veeva Vault for full study documentation.']
  responsible_laboratory varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the laboratory facility conducting the stability testing. May be internal quality control (QC) lab or external contract testing laboratory.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of individual product units or samples placed on stability for this study. Must be sufficient to support all planned time-point testing.']
  stability_commitment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this study is part of a formal stability commitment made to a regulatory authority. True if this is a committed study that must be completed and reported per regulatory agreement.']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Primary storage condition for the study, expressed as temperature and relative humidity (e.g., 25°C/60%RH for long-term, 30°C/65%RH for intermediate, 40°C/75%RH for accelerated). May include additional conditions such as light exposure or freezing.']
  storage_humidity_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target relative humidity percentage for the stability study storage condition.']
  storage_instruction varchar [note: 'Consumer-facing storage instructions that will appear on product labeling, derived from stability study results (e.g., Store at room temperature, Do not freeze, Protect from light).']
  storage_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the stability study chamber or condition.']
  study_conclusion varchar [note: 'Summary conclusion of the stability study results, including whether the product met all acceptance criteria throughout the study duration and any recommendations for shelf life or storage conditions.']
  study_coordinator varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the person responsible for coordinating and overseeing the stability study execution.']
  study_duration_months varchar [note: 'Planned total duration of the stability study in months. Typical durations are 6 months for accelerated studies, 12-36 months for real-time studies, and up to 60 months for long-term studies.']
  study_end_date date [note: 'Planned or actual completion date of the stability study. For ongoing studies, represents the projected completion date based on protocol duration.']
  study_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the stability study, typically assigned by the quality management system or laboratory information management system (LIMS). Used for external reference and regulatory submissions.']
  study_start_date date [note: 'Date when the stability study was initiated and samples were placed on stability storage. Marks the beginning of the study timeline for time-point calculations.']
  study_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the stability study. Tracks progression from planning through completion or discontinuation.']
  study_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the stability study protocol. Accelerated studies use elevated temperature/humidity to predict shelf life. Real-time studies use recommended storage conditions. Intermediate studies bridge accelerated and real-time. Stress studies test extreme conditions. Photostability studies assess light exposure impact. In-use studies simulate consumer usage conditions.']
  test_parameters varchar [note: 'List of quality attributes and test methods included in the stability protocol (e.g., appearance, pH, assay, impurities, microbial limits, viscosity). Comma-separated or structured list.']
  testing_frequency varchar [note: 'Schedule of time points at which samples are pulled and tested (e.g., 0, 3, 6, 9, 12, 18, 24, 36 months). Defined in the study protocol and varies by study type.']

  Note: 'Master and detail record for product stability studies conducted to validate shelf life and storage conditions per ICH Q1A/Q1B/Q5C guidelines and FDA/EU GMP requirements. Header captures study type (accelerated, real-time, intermediate, stress, photostability), product/formulation, storage conditions (25°C/60%RH, 30°C/65%RH, 40°C/75%RH), study duration, protocol reference, and overall study status. Detail level stores all time-point measurement results including pull date, storage condition, test characteristic, measured value, specification limit, pass/fail status, trend flag, and out-of-trend (OOT) indicator. Feeds shelf-life determination, regulatory submission data packages, and label claim validation in Veeva Vault.'
}

Table quality.stability_result {
  stability_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the stability test result record. Primary key for the stability result entity.']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific production lot or batch being tested for stability.']
  original_result_stability_result_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original stability result record if this is a retest.']
  quality_stability_study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent stability study protocol under which this result was generated.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product being tested in this stability study.']
  analyst_name varchar [note: 'Name of the laboratory analyst who performed the test.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this stability result was reviewed and approved by quality assurance.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or observations recorded by the analyst or reviewer regarding this stability result.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this stability result record was first created in the system.']
  deviation_percentage decimal(10,4) [note: 'Percentage deviation of the measured value from the specification target or initial value.']
  instrument_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the analytical instrument used to perform the test.']
  laboratory_location varchar [note: 'Physical location or site code of the laboratory where the test was performed.']
  measured_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Numeric result obtained from the stability test for this characteristic at this time point.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this stability result record was last modified.']
  out_of_specification_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator that the result is out of specification and requires investigation per GMP requirements.']
  pass_fail_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the measured value meets the specification limits. Pass indicates conformance, fail indicates non-conformance.']
  pull_date date [note: 'Date when the sample was pulled from stability storage for testing at this time point.']
  regulatory_submission_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this result is included in regulatory submission packages to FDA, EMA, or other health authorities.']
  retest_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this result is a retest following an initial out-of-specification result or laboratory investigation.']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the quality professional who reviewed and approved the test result.']
  sap_qm_inspection_lot varchar [note: 'SAP Quality Management inspection lot number associated with this stability test result.']
  shelf_life_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this result impacts the determination of product shelf life or expiration dating.']
  specification_lower_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Minimum acceptable value for this test characteristic as defined in the product specification.']
  specification_target decimal(18,6) [note: 'Target or nominal value for this test characteristic as defined in the product specification.']
  specification_upper_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Maximum acceptable value for this test characteristic as defined in the product specification.']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Environmental condition under which the sample was stored (temperature and relative humidity). Standard ICH conditions include 25°C/60% RH (long-term), 30°C/65% RH (intermediate), and 40°C/75% RH (accelerated).']
  test_characteristic varchar [note: 'Specific quality attribute or parameter being measured (e.g., assay, impurity level, pH, viscosity, color, odor, dissolution rate, microbial count).']
  test_date date [note: 'Date when the laboratory test was performed on the pulled sample.']
  test_interval_number varchar [note: 'Sequential interval number within the stability study protocol (e.g., 0, 1, 3, 6, 9, 12, 18, 24 months).']
  test_method varchar [note: 'Analytical method or procedure used to measure the test characteristic (e.g., HPLC, GC, UV-Vis, titration, Karl Fischer).']
  test_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this stability test result.']
  trend_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this result shows a statistically significant trend toward specification limits that may predict future failure.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the measured value is expressed (e.g., %, mg/g, pH units, cP, CFU/g).']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the associated stability test report or certificate of analysis stored in Veeva Vault quality document management system.']

  Note: 'Time-point measurement results from stability study intervals. Records pull date, storage condition, test characteristic, measured value, specification limit, pass/fail status, and trend flag. Feeds shelf-life determination and regulatory submission data packages in Veeva Vault.'
}

Table quality.gmp_audit {
  gmp_audit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the GMP audit event record. Primary key.']
  audit_checklist_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the audit checklist or protocol used during the audit to ensure consistent evaluation criteria.']
  audit_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the audit program or audit plan under which this audit was conducted (e.g., annual internal audit program, supplier qualification program).']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the CAPA record initiated in response to audit findings.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'System identifier for the lead auditor, linking to employee or external auditor master data.']
  manufacturing_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing facility, contract manufacturer, or laboratory where the audit was conducted.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Actual date when the audit fieldwork was completed.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual date when the audit commenced on-site or remotely.']
  affected_process_areas varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of process areas or departments affected by audit findings (e.g., production, quality control, warehouse, documentation, cleaning validation).']
  audit_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional audit notes, context, or observations not captured in structured fields.']
  audit_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique identifier for the audit event, used for tracking and regulatory reporting.']
  audit_report_date date [note: 'Date when the final audit report was issued to the facility or organization.']
  audit_scope varchar [note: 'Description of the audit scope including processes, systems, and areas covered (e.g., production lines, quality systems, documentation practices, cleaning validation).']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the audit: scheduled (planned but not started), in_progress (fieldwork underway), report_draft (findings being documented), report_issued (final report delivered), closed (all findings resolved), cancelled (audit did not proceed).']
  audit_team_members varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of additional audit team members who participated in the audit.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit based on the auditing party: internal (self-assessment), regulatory (FDA, EU authority), third-party (certification body), supplier (vendor qualification), or customer (buyer audit).']
  capa_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the audit findings require formal CAPA initiation (True) or are informational only (False).']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when all audit findings were resolved and the audit was formally closed.']
  closure_status varchar [note: 'Status of audit closure: open (findings not yet addressed), pending_response (awaiting facility response), under_review (response being evaluated), closed (all findings resolved), escalated (unresolved findings escalated to management or regulatory authority).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit record was first created in the system.']
  critical_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of critical findings that pose immediate risk to product quality, patient safety, or regulatory compliance.']
  follow_up_audit_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a follow-up audit is required to verify implementation of corrective actions (True) or not (False).']
  follow_up_audit_scheduled_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the follow-up audit to verify corrective action effectiveness.']
  gmp_standard_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the GMP standard or regulation against which the audit was conducted (e.g., FDA 21 CFR Part 211, EU GMP Annex 1, ICH Q7).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit record was last updated in the system.']
  lead_auditor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the lead auditor responsible for conducting and reporting the audit.']
  major_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of major findings representing significant GMP deviations requiring corrective action.']
  minor_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of minor findings representing opportunities for improvement without immediate compliance risk.']
  observation_count varchar [note: 'Number of observations noted during the audit that do not constitute non-conformances but warrant attention.']
  overall_rating varchar [note: 'Summary rating assigned to the facility based on audit findings: compliant (no significant issues), acceptable (minor issues), needs_improvement (major issues requiring action), non_compliant (critical GMP violations), critical (immediate regulatory risk).']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory authority conducting the audit (e.g., FDA, EMA, MHRA, Health Canada) for regulatory audits; null for internal or third-party audits.']
  regulatory_reference_number varchar [note: 'Official reference number assigned by the regulatory authority (e.g., FDA 483 number, EMA inspection reference).']
  response_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the audited facility must submit a formal response to audit findings, including corrective and preventive actions (CAPA).']
  response_submitted_date date [note: 'Actual date when the facility submitted its formal response to the audit findings.']
  scheduled_end_date date [note: 'Planned end date for the audit as communicated to the facility.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'Planned start date for the audit as communicated to the facility.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system from which the audit record originated (e.g., SAP QM, Veeva Vault, internal audit management system).']
  total_findings_count varchar [note: 'Total number of findings (observations, deviations, non-conformances) identified during the audit.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the audit report document stored in Veeva Vault for regulatory document management and audit trail.']

  Note: 'GMP audit event records with associated findings for internal and external audits of manufacturing facilities, contract manufacturers, and laboratories. Header captures audit type (internal, regulatory, third-party, supplier), audit scope, facility, lead auditor, audit date range, overall rating, and closure status. Finding detail captures classification (critical, major, minor, observation), finding description, regulatory reference (FDA 483, EU GMP chapter), affected process area, response due date, and linkage to CAPA. Supports regulatory inspection readiness, FDA/EU GMP compliance tracking, and Veeva Vault audit management.'
}

Table quality.audit_finding {
  audit_finding_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the audit finding record. Primary key.']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the CAPA record initiated to address this audit finding.']
  gmp_audit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent GMP audit event during which this finding was raised.']
  logistics_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.logistics_shipment. Business justification: Links audit findings (e.g., temperature excursions) to the specific shipment for CAPA and regulatory reporting.']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific production batch or lot impacted by the finding, if applicable.']
  manufacturing_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing facility or site where the finding was observed.']
  previous_finding_audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the previous audit finding record if this is identified as a repeat finding.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific product or SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) impacted by the finding, if applicable.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Audit findings often relate to supplier‑provided materials; linking enables supplier performance tracking and corrective actions.']
  affected_process_area varchar [note: 'The manufacturing or quality process area where the finding was identified (e.g., Raw Material Receiving, Mixing, Filling, Packaging, Quality Control Laboratory, Warehouse).']
  attachment_count varchar [note: 'Number of supporting documents, photos, or evidence files attached to the finding record (e.g., photos of non-conformance, lab reports, procedure documents).']
  auditor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the auditor or inspection team member who raised the finding.']
  auditor_organization varchar [note: 'Organization or regulatory body the auditor represents (e.g., FDA, Internal QA, Third-Party Certification Body, EU Competent Authority).']
  closure_approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the quality manager or authorized personnel who approved the closure of the finding.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the audit finding was formally closed after successful verification of corrective actions.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional notes, clarifications, or context provided by auditors, quality personnel, or management regarding the finding.']
  corrective_action_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of the corrective actions taken or planned to remediate the finding and prevent recurrence.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit finding record was first created in the quality management system.']
  finding_classification varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the audit finding. Critical: immediate risk to product safety or regulatory compliance; Major: significant deviation from GMP requiring prompt correction; Minor: isolated deviation with low impact; Observation: opportunity for improvement without regulatory breach.']
  finding_date date [note: 'Date when the audit finding was identified and documented during the audit.']
  finding_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the audit finding, including observed deviation, evidence collected, and context of the non-conformance.']
  finding_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the audit finding, typically formatted as audit prefix followed by sequential number (e.g., FDA-000123, INT-002456).']
  finding_source_document varchar [note: 'Reference to the source audit report, inspection form, or document where the finding was originally recorded.']
  finding_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the audit finding in the remediation workflow.']
  finding_title varchar [note: 'Brief summary title of the audit finding for quick identification and reporting.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who last modified the audit finding record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit finding record was last updated or modified.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Citation of the specific regulatory requirement, GMP guideline, or standard that was violated or not met (e.g., 21 CFR 211.100, EU GMP Chapter 4, ISO 22716 Section 5.3).']
  regulatory_report_date date [note: 'Date when the finding was formally reported to the regulatory authority, if applicable.']
  regulatory_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this finding must be reported to regulatory authorities such as FDA (Food and Drug Administration) or EU competent authorities per regulatory requirements.']
  regulatory_response_received_date date [note: 'Date when a formal response or acknowledgment was received from the regulatory authority regarding the reported finding.']
  repeat_finding_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this finding is a repeat observation from a previous audit, suggesting ineffective corrective action or systemic issue.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the organization must submit a formal response or corrective action plan to address the finding.']
  response_submitted_date date [note: 'Date when the formal response or corrective action plan was submitted to the auditor or regulatory authority.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Department or functional area responsible for implementing corrective actions and resolving the finding (e.g., Manufacturing, Quality Assurance, Quality Control (QC), Supply Chain).']
  responsible_person varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual assigned ownership for addressing the finding and implementing corrective actions.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk assessment level assigned to the finding based on potential impact to product quality, patient safety, or regulatory compliance.']
  root_cause_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of the root cause analysis conducted to determine the underlying reason for the finding.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the corrective action effectiveness was verified and the finding was confirmed as resolved.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify the effectiveness of corrective actions (e.g., follow-up audit, document review, process validation, trend analysis).']
  verified_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the quality assurance personnel or auditor who verified the corrective action effectiveness.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who created the audit finding record in the system.']

  Note: 'Individual findings and observations raised during a GMP audit. Stores finding classification (critical, major, minor, observation), finding description, regulatory reference, affected process area, response due date, and linkage to CAPA. Supports regulatory inspection readiness and FDA/EU GMP compliance tracking.'
}

Table quality.supplier_assessment {
  supplier_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for supplier_assessment']
  previous_assessment_supplier_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the prior supplier quality assessment record for tracking assessment history and trends.']
  primary_supplier_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the quality professional who led the supplier assessment.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier being assessed for quality performance.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval decision resulting from the quality assessment, determining the supplier\'s eligibility for business.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the assessment results and supplier status decision were formally approved.']
  approval_valid_from_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the supplier approval status resulting from this assessment.']
  approval_valid_to_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the supplier approval status, after which re-assessment is required.']
  approved_by_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the quality manager who approved the supplier assessment outcome.']
  asl_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the supplier on the Approved Supplier List based on this assessment outcome.']
  assessment_comments varchar [note: 'Additional notes, observations, or context provided by the assessment team regarding the supplier evaluation.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Date when the supplier quality assessment was conducted or completed.']
  assessment_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between required assessments for this supplier based on risk rating and business criticality.']
  assessment_method varchar [note: 'Method or approach used to conduct the supplier quality assessment.']
  assessment_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the supplier quality assessment, typically generated by the quality management system.']
  assessment_report_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier for the detailed supplier quality assessment report stored in the document management system.']
  assessment_scope varchar [note: 'Description of the scope of the assessment including materials, processes, facilities, or systems evaluated.']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the supplier quality assessment process.']
  assessment_team_members varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of team members who participated in the supplier quality assessment.']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Category of supplier quality assessment indicating the trigger or purpose of the evaluation.']
  capa_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the supplier must submit their CAPA response to address identified deficiencies.']
  capa_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier is required to submit a CAPA plan to address assessment findings.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Third-party certification status of the supplier relevant to quality and compliance standards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the supplier quality assessment record was first created in the system.']
  critical_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of critical non-conformances or major deficiencies identified during the assessment.']
  facility_location varchar [note: 'Geographic location or address of the supplier facility that was assessed.']
  gmp_compliance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score evaluating the supplier\'s adherence to Good Manufacturing Practice standards and requirements.']
  improvement_trend varchar [note: 'Trend indicator comparing current assessment results to previous evaluations.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the supplier quality assessment record was last updated.']
  lead_assessor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the lead quality assessor responsible for conducting the evaluation.']
  major_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of major non-conformances identified during the supplier assessment.']
  material_categories_assessed varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of material categories or product types covered in this supplier assessment.']
  minor_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of minor non-conformances or observations identified during the assessment.']
  next_assessment_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic supplier quality assessment.']
  overall_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite numerical score representing the supplier\'s overall quality performance across all evaluation criteria.']
  quality_system_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score assessing the maturity and effectiveness of the supplier\'s quality management system.']
  regulatory_compliance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score evaluating the supplier\'s compliance with applicable regulatory requirements including FDA, EPA, REACH, and other governing body standards.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk classification assigned to the supplier based on assessment findings, indicating the level of quality and compliance risk.']
  score_scale_max decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum possible score on the assessment scale used for this evaluation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system from which this supplier assessment record originated.']

  Note: 'Supplier quality evaluation records scoring raw material and packaging suppliers against GMP, quality system, and regulatory criteria. Captures assessment type (initial qualification, periodic re-evaluation, for-cause), overall score, risk rating, approved/conditional/disqualified status, and assessor details. Feeds approved supplier list (ASL) decisions.'
}

Table quality.product_complaint {
  product_complaint_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the product complaint record. Primary key.']
  action_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_action. Business justification: When a complaint triggers enforcement, a regulatory action is opened; linking enables tracking complaint to action.']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this complaint to a formal CAPA record if one was initiated.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Financial impact of product complaints is allocated to a cost center for cost‑of‑quality reporting.']
  investigation_assigned_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Complaint Investigation Assignment links each complaint to the responsible investigator employee for tracking and regulatory reporting.']
  logistics_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.logistics_shipment. Business justification: Associates customer complaints with the originating shipment for traceability, recall decisions, and service recovery.']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: Customer complaints must be tied to the exact lot batch to assess impact, trigger investigations, and manage recalls.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Brand Complaint Dashboard requires linking complaints to the brand to monitor reputation and adjust campaigns.']
  outbound_order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.outbound_order_line. Business justification: Required for complaint traceability: product complaint must reference the outbound order line that delivered the product, allowing root cause analysis and customer service reporting.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Customer complaints can launch a new R&D project for product improvement; linking captures that initiation relationship.']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.retail_store. Business justification: Store‑level complaint tracking required for quality investigations and performance dashboards; linking complaints to the retail store where purchase occurred is standard in consumer‑goods.']
  shopper_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to consumer.shopper. Business justification: Complaint handling process requires linking each product complaint to the shopper who reported it for traceability and regulatory reporting.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Complaints are investigated per SKU; linking enables root‑cause analysis, corrective actions, and compliance reporting.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Complaints must be tied to the responsible supplier for performance monitoring and regulatory reporting.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the retail or trade account where the product was purchased, if applicable.']
  complainant_address varchar [note: 'Mailing address of the complainant for correspondence and product sample return.']
  complainant_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the complainant location.']
  complainant_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the complainant for follow-up communication.']
  complainant_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual or organization filing the complaint.']
  complainant_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number of the complainant.']
  complaint_category varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the complaint type based on the nature of the quality issue reported.']
  complaint_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the complaint as reported by the complainant, including symptoms, observations, and circumstances.']
  complaint_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique complaint reference number used for tracking and communication with consumers and regulatory bodies.']
  complaint_received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the complaint was first received by the organization.']
  complaint_source varchar [note: 'Origin channel through which the complaint was received.']
  complaint_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the complaint investigation and resolution process.']
  complaint_subcategory varchar [note: 'Detailed sub-classification providing additional granularity to the complaint category.']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Description of corrective actions taken to address the immediate complaint.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the complaint record was first created in the system.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Product expiration or best-before date printed on the package.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'Global Trade Item Number uniquely identifying the product in the supply chain.']
  incident_date date [note: 'Date when the consumer or customer experienced the quality issue or adverse event.']
  investigation_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the investigation was concluded and findings documented.']
  investigation_start_date date [note: 'Date when the formal investigation of the complaint was initiated.']
  investigation_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the internal quality investigation process.']
  lab_analysis_result varchar [note: 'Summary of laboratory test results conducted on the returned product sample.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the complaint record was last updated.']
  manufacturing_date date [note: 'Date when the product batch was manufactured.']
  preventive_action varchar [note: 'Description of preventive actions implemented to prevent recurrence of similar issues.']
  purchase_date date [note: 'Date when the complainant purchased the product.']
  regulatory_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory agency to which the complaint was reported (e.g., FDA, EPA, CPSC).']
  regulatory_case_number varchar [note: 'Case or reference number assigned by the regulatory agency for tracking purposes.']
  regulatory_report_date date [note: 'Date when the complaint was reported to the relevant regulatory authority.']
  regulatory_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this complaint must be reported to regulatory authorities such as FDA, EPA, or CPSC.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date when the complaint was resolved and closed.']
  resolution_outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome or action taken to resolve the complaint with the complainant.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Identified root cause of the quality issue based on investigation findings.']
  sample_received_date date [note: 'Date when the returned product sample was received by the quality assurance team.']
  sample_returned_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the complainant returned a physical product sample for laboratory analysis.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification indicating the potential impact to consumer safety and business reputation.']

  Note: 'Consumer and trade customer product complaint records capturing post-market quality issues. Stores complaint source (consumer, retailer, regulatory), product SKU, batch number, complaint category (foreign body, contamination, labeling, efficacy), severity, investigation status, regulatory reportability flag, and resolution outcome. Integrates with Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud and Veeva Vault.'
}

Table quality.regulatory_hold {
  regulatory_hold_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the regulatory hold event record. Primary key.']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this regulatory hold to a CAPA record initiated to address the root cause.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Regulatory hold financial impact is charged to a cost center for compliance cost tracking.']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: Regulatory holds are placed on specific lot batches; linking ensures hold status propagates to inventory and reporting.']
  manufacturing_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the manufacturing facility or distribution center where the affected product or material is located.']
  related_complaint_product_complaint_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this regulatory hold to a consumer or customer complaint that triggered the investigation.']
  related_recall_product_recall_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this regulatory hold to a product recall event if the hold escalated to a recall.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Regulatory holds are applied to specific SKUs; FK supports hold management, release decisions, and audit trails.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the supplier associated with the affected material or product, if the hold is supplier-related.']
  actual_release_date date [note: 'The actual date when the regulatory hold was lifted and the affected product, batch, or material was released or dispositioned.']
  actual_release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the regulatory hold was lifted and the affected product, batch, or material was released or dispositioned.']
  affected_material_code varchar [note: 'The raw material or component code placed under regulatory hold. May be null if hold applies to finished goods.']
  affected_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The total quantity of product, batch, or material placed under regulatory hold.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this regulatory hold record was first created in the system.']
  expected_release_date date [note: 'The anticipated date when the regulatory hold review and assessment is expected to be completed and a release decision made.']
  financial_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated or actual financial impact of the regulatory hold event, including inventory value, disposal costs, and business disruption.']
  financial_impact_currency varchar [note: 'The currency code for the financial impact amount, following ISO 4217 standard. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|GBP|JPY|CNY|CAD|AUD — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  hold_duration_days varchar [note: 'The total number of calendar days the regulatory hold was in effect, from start to release.']
  hold_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, observations, and context related to the regulatory hold event, investigation, and resolution.']
  hold_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the regulatory hold event, formatted as RH- followed by 8 digits. Used for external communication and tracking.']
  hold_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the primary reason for placing the product under regulatory hold. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: contamination|labeling_error|stability_failure|microbial_excursion|foreign_material|potency_deviation|regulatory_inspection|consumer_complaint|supplier_issue — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  hold_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of the circumstances and findings that led to the regulatory hold decision.']
  hold_scope varchar [note: 'The level at which the regulatory hold is applied, defining the breadth of affected inventory or operations.']
  hold_start_date date [note: 'The date when the regulatory hold was officially placed and became effective.']
  hold_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the regulatory hold was officially placed and became effective.']
  hold_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the regulatory hold event indicating its progression through the review and release process.']
  hold_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the regulatory hold event indicating the nature and origin of the hold decision.']
  initiating_authority varchar [note: 'The organization or entity that initiated or mandated the regulatory hold event. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|CPSC|EPA|internal_qa|internal_regulatory|supplier|customer|third_party_lab — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  initiating_authority_reference varchar [note: 'External reference number or case identifier provided by the initiating regulatory authority or organization.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this regulatory hold record was last updated or modified.']
  quantity_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the affected quantity (EA=Each, CS=Case, KG=Kilogram, LB=Pound, L=Liter, GAL=Gallon, MT=Metric Ton). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|KG|LB|L|GAL|MT — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_notification_date date [note: 'The date when the regulatory authority was formally notified of the hold event.']
  regulatory_notification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this regulatory hold event requires formal notification to external regulatory authorities.']
  release_authorization_name varchar [note: 'The full name of the authorized individual who approved the release or disposition of the regulatory hold.']
  release_authorization_title varchar [note: 'The job title or role of the individual who authorized the release or disposition decision.']
  release_decision varchar [note: 'The final disposition decision made for the product, batch, or material under regulatory hold.']
  release_justification varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation and supporting evidence for the release or disposition decision, including test results and risk assessment findings.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'The assessed risk level of the regulatory hold event based on potential consumer safety impact and regulatory exposure.']
  sap_qm_notification_number varchar [note: 'The SAP QM notification number associated with this regulatory hold event for traceability to the source ERP system.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The originating system or application from which this regulatory hold record was captured.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the regulatory hold documentation stored in Veeva Vault quality document management system.']

  Note: 'Regulatory hold event records placing batches, SKUs, or materials under quarantine pending regulatory review, recall investigation, or safety assessment. Captures hold type (voluntary, regulatory-mandated, precautionary), affected scope, initiating authority (FDA, CPSC, internal QA), hold start/end dates, and release authorization. Critical for CPSC and FDA compliance.'
}

Table quality.specification {
  specification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the quality specification record. Primary key for this entity.']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Each quality specification must satisfy a compliance obligation; linking ensures traceability to the governing regulation.']
  inspection_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP QM inspection plan that uses this specification. Links specification to operational quality control procedures.']
  material_sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material or product this specification applies to. Links to the master material record in SAP MM.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Specifications are developed within an R&D project; linking ties each spec to its originating project for governance.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Quality specifications are defined per SKU to ensure manufacturing compliance and product release criteria.']
  specification_approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  specification_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the person who approved this specification. Links to workforce management system for accountability.']
  superseded_by_specification_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer specification that replaces this one when status is superseded. Maintains specification version lineage.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when this specification was formally approved for use. Part of the specification lifecycle audit trail.']
  coa_inclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether test results for this specification must be included on the Certificate of Analysis provided to customers. Drives CoA generation logic.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this specification record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail for compliance and traceability.']
  critical_quality_attribute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this characteristic is a Critical Quality Attribute that directly impacts product safety, efficacy, or regulatory compliance. CQAs require enhanced controls and documentation.']
  customer_requirement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this specification is driven by specific customer contractual requirements rather than internal or regulatory standards.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date after which this specification is no longer valid. Null indicates an open-ended specification. Used for specification versioning and supersession.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this specification becomes valid and must be used for quality control testing. Supports time-based specification management.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this specification record was last updated. Tracks change history for audit and compliance purposes.']
  lower_specification_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The minimum acceptable value for the test characteristic. Results below this limit indicate non-conformance.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this specification. Used for compliance tracking and specification maintenance planning.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional context, special instructions, or clarifications related to this specification. May include testing conditions, sample preparation requirements, or interpretation guidance.']
  review_frequency_months varchar [note: 'How often this specification must be reviewed and revalidated, expressed in months. Ensures specifications remain current and appropriate.']
  sampling_plan varchar [note: 'Description of the sampling methodology and sample size requirements for this test (e.g., AQL 2.5, n=10, ANSI/ASQ Z1.4). Ensures statistical validity of test results.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system from which this specification record originated (e.g., SAP QM, Veeva Vault, PLM system). Used for data lineage and integration troubleshooting.']
  specification_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the type of quality attribute being specified. Groups related test characteristics for reporting and analysis.']
  specification_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the quality specification, used for external reference and documentation. Unique across the organization.']
  specification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the specification. Only active specifications are used for quality control and Certificate of Analysis (CoA) generation.']
  specification_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the specification based on the material or product stage it applies to. Determines the applicable quality control procedures.']
  stability_indicating_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this test characteristic is used in stability studies to monitor product degradation over time. Critical for shelf-life determination.']
  target_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The ideal or nominal value for the test characteristic. Represents the center point of the acceptable range.']
  test_characteristic varchar [note: 'The specific quality attribute or property being measured or tested (e.g., pH, viscosity, color, weight, purity, microbial count). Defines what is being inspected.']
  test_equipment_required varchar [note: 'Description of specialized equipment or instrumentation needed to perform this test (e.g., HPLC, spectrophotometer, viscometer). Supports resource planning and capability assessment.']
