  test_frequency varchar [note: 'How often this test characteristic must be measured during production or quality control. Drives inspection plan scheduling. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: per_batch|per_lot|hourly|daily|weekly|monthly|on_demand — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  test_method_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the standard test method or procedure used to measure the characteristic (e.g., ASTM D1475, USP 621, internal SOP number). Ensures consistent testing methodology.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the test characteristic is measured (e.g., mg/L, %, pH units, cP, CFU/g). Critical for interpreting specification limits.']
  upper_specification_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The maximum acceptable value for the test characteristic. Results above this limit indicate non-conformance.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the controlled document in Veeva Vault that contains the full specification details and supporting documentation. Ensures document control and regulatory compliance.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the specification to track revisions and changes over time. Follows major.minor versioning convention.']

  Note: 'Master data defining quality specifications for raw materials, packaging, in-process intermediates, and finished goods. Stores specification type, test characteristic, unit of measure, target value, upper/lower specification limits (USL/LSL), test method reference, and effective date range. Serves as the authoritative specification repository linked to inspection plans and CoA generation.'
}

Table quality.lab_test_request {
  lab_test_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the laboratory test request record. Primary key.']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the production lot or batch associated with this test request. Links to inventory.lot_batch.']
  quality_stability_study_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this test request to a formal stability study protocol for shelf-life determination and expiry date validation.']
  requestor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the individual who submitted the laboratory test request.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Lab test requests are issued for a specific SKU to verify formulation and compliance before release.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Lab test requests need supplier identification to assess supplier quality impact; supplier_code is replaced by FK to supplier.']
  actual_turnaround_time_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Measured elapsed time in hours from request submission to result receipt. Used for laboratory performance monitoring and Service Level Agreement (SLA) compliance.']
  assigned_lab_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the internal or external laboratory facility assigned to perform the testing.']
  assigned_lab_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the laboratory facility assigned to perform the testing (internal QC lab or external contract lab).']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification if the laboratory test request was cancelled before completion (e.g., sample compromised, request duplicate, business decision changed).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this laboratory test request record was first created in the quality management system.']
  gmp_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this laboratory test must be performed under Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) conditions with full documentation and traceability for regulatory submission.']
  inspection_lot_number varchar [note: 'SAP QM inspection lot number that triggered this laboratory test request. Links test results back to the quality inspection workflow.']
  lab_assignment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the test request was assigned to a specific laboratory facility and analyst.']
  lab_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the assigned laboratory as internal company facility, external third-party lab, or contract testing organization.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this laboratory test request record was most recently updated.']
  manufacturing_site_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the production facility where the material being tested was manufactured.']
  material_code varchar [note: 'SAP material master code identifying the product, raw material, or packaging component being tested.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Business description of the material being tested for human-readable identification.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the test request determining turnaround time and resource allocation. Critical and regulatory priorities receive expedited processing.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory body requiring this test (e.g., FDA, EPA, EU Commission, Health Canada) if regulatory_requirement_flag is true.']
  regulatory_requirement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this laboratory test is mandated by regulatory authority (FDA, EPA, EU REACH) for product registration, batch release, or ongoing compliance.']
  request_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special instructions, or background information relevant to the laboratory test request.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the laboratory test request, typically formatted as LTR-YYYYNNNN for external reference and tracking.']
  request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the laboratory test request in the workflow from submission through completion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|assigned|in_progress|completed|cancelled|on_hold — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  request_submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the laboratory test request was officially submitted to the laboratory for processing. Marks the start of turnaround time measurement.']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Category of laboratory testing requested: physical properties, chemical composition, microbiological safety, sensory evaluation, stability study, or regulatory compliance testing.']
  requested_completion_date date [note: 'Target date by which laboratory test results must be received to support business decisions (batch release, regulatory submission, etc.).']
  requesting_department varchar [note: 'Name of the business department or functional area that initiated the laboratory test request (e.g., Quality Assurance, Research and Development, Manufacturing, Regulatory Affairs).']
  requestor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who submitted the laboratory test request.']
  required_turnaround_time_hours varchar [note: 'Maximum number of hours allowed from request submission to result receipt based on business priority and operational requirements.']
  result_receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when laboratory test results were received and recorded in the quality management system. Marks the end of turnaround time measurement.']
  sample_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the physical sample submitted for laboratory testing. Links to sample management system.']
  sample_quantity decimal(10,3) [note: 'Quantity of sample material submitted for laboratory testing.']
  sample_quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the sample quantity (e.g., g, kg, mL, L, units).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system from which this laboratory test request record originated (e.g., SAP QM, Siemens Opcenter MES, LIMS).']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Any special requirements for sample handling, storage, or testing conditions (e.g., light-sensitive, temperature-controlled, hazardous material precautions).']
  specification_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the product specification document defining acceptance criteria for the test results.']
  stability_time_point varchar [note: 'Specific time point in the stability study protocol when this sample was pulled for testing (e.g., T0, 3 months, 6 months, 12 months, 24 months).']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Environmental conditions under which the sample was stored prior to testing (e.g., 25°C/60% RH, 40°C/75% RH, refrigerated, frozen).']
  supplier_lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot or batch number assigned by the external supplier for traceability of incoming materials.']
  test_method_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the validated analytical method or standard operating procedure (SOP) to be used for testing (e.g., USP method, ASTM standard, internal SOP number).']
  test_panel_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the predefined set of analytical tests to be performed (e.g., full release panel, stability panel, microbial panel).']
  test_panel_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the test panel including specific analytical methods and parameters to be measured.']
  test_purpose varchar [note: 'Business reason for requesting the laboratory test (e.g., batch release, stability monitoring, complaint investigation, supplier qualification, regulatory submission, method validation).']
  testing_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when laboratory analytical work began on the sample.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated quality document, test protocol, or specification stored in Veeva Vault document management system.']

  Note: 'Laboratory test request records submitted to internal or external QC labs for physical, chemical, microbiological, or sensory testing. Captures requesting department, sample ID, linked inspection lot or stability study, test panel, priority, required turnaround time (TAT), assigned lab, request status, and result receipt date. Supports lab workflow management and bridges QC inspection to specialized analytical testing in SAP QM and Siemens Opcenter.'
}

Table quality.notification {
  notification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the quality notification record. Primary key.']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this quality notification to a formal CAPA record for corrective and preventive actions.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Quality notification financial impact is allocated to a cost center for expense reporting.']
  logistics_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.logistics_shipment. Business justification: Connects quality notifications triggered by shipment issues (e.g., temperature breach) to the specific shipment for corrective actions.']
  notification_closed_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who formally closed the quality notification.']
  notification_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the quality engineer or specialist assigned to investigate and resolve this notification.']
  product_complaint_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this quality notification to an originating customer complaint record, if applicable.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier associated with the quality issue, used when the notification relates to supplier quality performance.']
  affected_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of material or product units affected by the quality issue.']
  affected_quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the affected quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilograms, L for liters).']
  assigned_to_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the person assigned to handle this quality notification.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing batch or lot number of the affected material, enabling traceability and containment actions.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the quality notification was formally closed after all actions were completed and verified.']
  containment_action varchar [note: 'Immediate containment actions taken to prevent further impact or spread of the quality issue while root cause investigation is underway.']
  containment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when containment actions were implemented.']
  created_date date [note: 'Date when the quality notification was initially created in the system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this quality notification record was first created in the lakehouse, representing the audit trail for data lineage.']
  defect_class varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the defect based on its impact on product safety, functionality, and regulatory compliance.']
  defect_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code classifying the type of defect or quality issue for categorization and trend analysis.']
  detected_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the quality issue was first detected or observed, which may differ from when it was formally reported.']
  detection_source varchar [note: 'Origin or source where the quality issue was first identified or detected. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: customer|production|inspection|audit|supplier|warehouse|field — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  disposition_approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the quality authority who approved the material disposition decision.']
  disposition_approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the disposition decision was formally approved.']
  disposition_decision varchar [note: 'Final disposition decision for the affected material or product: use as-is, rework, scrap, return to supplier, or downgrade to alternate use.']
  financial_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated or actual financial impact of the quality issue including costs of rework, scrap, customer credits, and remediation.']
  financial_impact_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the financial impact amount.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this quality notification record was most recently updated in the lakehouse, supporting change tracking and audit requirements.']
  material_code varchar [note: 'SAP material master code identifying the product or raw material affected by this quality notification.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the material or product affected by the quality issue.']
  notification_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the quality issue, defect, or improvement opportunity documented in this notification.']
  notification_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the quality notification, externally visible and used for tracking and reference across systems.']
  notification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the quality notification indicating its position in the resolution workflow.']
  notification_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the quality notification indicating the nature of the issue: customer complaint, internal defect, improvement request, process deviation, audit finding, or supplier quality issue.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or distribution center responsible for addressing this quality notification.']
  plant_name varchar [note: 'Name of the manufacturing plant or facility associated with the quality notification.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the notification based on severity, impact, and urgency of resolution required.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory body to which this quality notification was reported (e.g., FDA, EPA, CPSC).']
  regulatory_report_date date [note: 'Date when the quality issue was formally reported to the relevant regulatory authority.']
  regulatory_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this quality notification must be reported to regulatory authorities such as FDA, EPA, or CPSC.']
  reported_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the quality issue was formally reported, representing the principal business event timestamp for this notification.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Department or functional area responsible for investigating and resolving the quality notification.']
  root_cause_analysis varchar [note: 'Detailed findings from root cause analysis investigation documenting the underlying cause of the quality issue.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source system from which this quality notification record originated (e.g., SAP_QM, VEEVA_VAULT).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this quality notification record in the source system, used for traceability and reconciliation.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this quality notification to supporting documentation stored in Veeva Vault quality document management system.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method or approach used to verify that corrective actions were effective and the quality issue was resolved.']

  Note: 'SAP QM quality notification records used to formally document and route quality defects, deviations, and improvement requests within the organization. Captures notification type (complaint, defect, improvement), priority, affected material/batch, responsible plant, task assignments, and closure details. Native SAP QM entity.'
}

Table quality.control_chart {
  control_chart_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the Statistical Process Control (SPC) control chart record. Primary key for the control chart entity.']
  control_chart_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.specification. Business justification: Link control_chart to the specification that defines the monitored characteristic limits.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual user or quality engineer responsible for maintaining and reviewing this control chart.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production line or manufacturing equipment where this control chart is applied. Links to manufacturing execution system line master data.']
  baseline_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the baseline period used to establish initial control limits.']
  baseline_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the baseline period used to establish initial control limits. Baseline data should represent stable process conditions.']
  baseline_sample_count varchar [note: 'The number of samples or subgroups used in the baseline period to calculate initial control limits. Typically requires 20-25 subgroups minimum for reliable limits.']
  center_line decimal(18,6) [note: 'The center line or process mean for the control chart, representing the target or average value of the monitored characteristic under stable process conditions.']
  characteristic_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code or identifier for the monitored characteristic, typically aligned with SAP QM inspection characteristic master data or quality specification codes.']
  chart_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the control chart, used for external reference and reporting. Typically follows plant or quality system numbering conventions.']
  chart_review_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which this control chart is formally reviewed by quality management. Examples include daily, weekly, monthly, or quarterly reviews.']
  chart_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the control chart. Active charts are in use for real-time monitoring, inactive charts are retired, suspended charts are temporarily paused, under review charts are being evaluated for control limit recalculation.']
  chart_type varchar [note: 'Type of Statistical Process Control chart used for monitoring. X-bar/R for variable data with range, X-bar/S for variable data with standard deviation, p-chart for proportion defective, c-chart for count of defects, u-chart for defects per unit, CUSUM for cumulative sum, EWMA for exponentially weighted moving average. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: x_bar_r|x_bar_s|p_chart|c_chart|u_chart|cusum|ewma — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  control_limit_calculation_date date [note: 'The date when the current control limits were calculated or last recalculated based on process data.']
  control_limit_calculation_method varchar [note: 'The statistical method or formula used to calculate control limits. Examples include 3-sigma method, probability limits, or custom calculation procedures.']
  control_rule_set varchar [note: 'The set of statistical control rules applied to detect out-of-control conditions. Common rule sets include Western Electric rules (1-4), Nelson rules (1-8), or custom rule combinations defined by the quality system.']
  cp_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'The process capability index Cp, measuring the potential capability of the process if it were perfectly centered between specification limits. Does not account for process centering.']
  cpk_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'The process capability index Cpk, measuring how well the process fits within specification limits while accounting for process centering. Values above 1.33 typically indicate capable processes in GMP environments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this control chart record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when this control chart configuration was retired or superseded. Null for currently active charts.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when this control chart configuration became effective and active for process monitoring.']
  gmp_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this control chart is part of GMP-regulated process monitoring and subject to regulatory requirements. True for pharmaceutical, cosmetic, and food products under FDA or EU GMP regulations.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this control chart record was last modified or updated.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'The date when this control chart was last formally reviewed by quality management or process engineering.']
  lower_control_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The lower control limit calculated for this control chart, typically set at 3 standard deviations below the center line. Values below this threshold indicate the process is out of statistical control.']
  lower_specification_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The lower specification limit defined by product quality specifications or regulatory requirements. Represents the minimum acceptable value for the characteristic.']
  material_code varchar [note: 'Material or SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) code for the product being monitored. Links to product master data in ERP systems.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the material or product being monitored by this control chart.']
  monitored_characteristic varchar [note: 'The Critical Quality Attribute (CQA) or process parameter being monitored by this control chart. Examples include fill weight, pH level, viscosity, tablet hardness, dissolution rate, or any measurable quality attribute.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next formal review of this control chart configuration and performance.']
  ocap_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the Out-of-Control Action Plan document or procedure that defines the response actions when control chart violations are detected. Links to quality management system procedures.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant or facility code where the control chart is deployed. Aligns with SAP plant master data or ERP facility codes.']
  ppk_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'The process performance index Ppk, measuring long-term process performance relative to specification limits. Calculated using overall process variation rather than within-subgroup variation.']
  regulatory_requirement_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to specific regulatory requirements or guidance documents that mandate or recommend this control chart. Examples include FDA guidance, ICH guidelines, or pharmacopeial requirements.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'The department or organizational unit responsible for monitoring this control chart and responding to out-of-control signals. Typically Quality Assurance, Production, or Process Engineering.']
  sampling_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which samples are collected for control chart monitoring. Examples include every 15 minutes, hourly, per batch, or per shift.']
  sampling_plan_code varchar [note: 'Code or identifier for the sampling plan used to collect data for this control chart. Links to quality management system sampling procedures.']
  sap_qm_inspection_characteristic varchar [note: 'SAP QM inspection characteristic code that this control chart monitors. Links to SAP QM master data for inspection planning and results recording.']
  subgroup_size varchar [note: 'The number of samples or observations collected in each subgroup for control chart analysis. Typical values range from 2 to 10 for variable control charts.']
  target_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The target or nominal value for the monitored characteristic as defined in product specifications or process design. May differ from the center line if the process is not centered on target.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the monitored characteristic and control limits. Examples include grams, milliliters, pH units, percentage, ppm (parts per million), or dimensionless ratios.']
  upper_control_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The upper control limit calculated for this control chart, typically set at 3 standard deviations above the center line. Values above this threshold indicate the process is out of statistical control.']
  upper_specification_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The upper specification limit defined by product quality specifications or regulatory requirements. Represents the maximum acceptable value for the characteristic.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier in Veeva Vault quality management system linking to the control chart procedure, OCAP, or related quality documentation.']

  Note: 'Statistical Process Control (SPC) control chart records for monitoring critical quality attributes (CQAs) on production lines. Master level stores chart type (X-bar/R, X-bar/S, p-chart, c-chart, CUSUM, EWMA), monitored characteristic, control limits (UCL/LCL/CL), subgroup size, sampling frequency, and out-of-control action plan (OCAP) reference. Event level captures violation signals including rule triggered (Western Electric rules 1-4, Nelson rules), sample point value, detection timestamp, production line, shift, and escalation status. Supports GMP process capability monitoring (Cpk/Ppk), real-time quality intervention, and continuous improvement programs.'
}

Table quality.change_control {
  change_control_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the change control request record. Primary key for the change control entity.']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the CAPA record if this change control request was initiated as part of a corrective or preventive action.']
  change_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_change. Business justification: Regulatory changes often drive change controls; linking records which change control addresses which regulatory amendment.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee or user identifier for the change control request initiator.']
  nonconformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to a nonconformance record if this change control request was triggered by a quality deviation or defect.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Change Control is initiated by an R&D project when product changes are required; linking ensures traceability of project origin.']
  actual_implementation_date date [note: 'Actual date when the change was implemented in production or operations, formatted as yyyy-MM-dd.']
  affected_facilities varchar [note: 'Manufacturing sites, warehouses, or laboratories impacted by the change, identified by facility code or name.']
  affected_markets varchar [note: 'Geographic markets or regulatory jurisdictions where the change will be implemented, using ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or region names.']
  affected_products varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or narrative description of product SKUs, GTINs, or product families impacted by the change control request.']
  approval_workflow_status varchar [note: 'Current stage in the multi-level approval workflow, indicating which functional area or authority is currently reviewing the change control request.']
  change_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the proposed change, including rationale, scope, and expected outcomes. Critical for regulatory review and approval workflow.']
  change_justification varchar [note: 'Business and technical rationale for the proposed change, including benefits, risk mitigation, or compliance drivers.']
  change_priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the change control request, influencing review and implementation timelines.']
  change_request_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the change control request, typically formatted as CR-NNNNNN. Used for external communication and tracking.']
  change_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the change control request in the approval and implementation workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|rejected|on_hold|implemented|closed|cancelled — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  change_title varchar [note: 'Brief descriptive title summarizing the change control request for quick identification and reporting.']
  change_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the change control request by the type of change being proposed: formulation (product recipe), process (manufacturing method), equipment (machinery or tooling), facility (building or infrastructure), specification (quality standards), or quality_system (QMS procedures).']
  closure_comments varchar [note: 'Final remarks or summary notes recorded at the time of change control closure, documenting lessons learned or outstanding items.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the change control request was formally closed after successful implementation and verification, formatted as yyyy-MM-dd.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the change control record was first created in the system, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  effectiveness_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled or actual date for conducting the effectiveness review of the implemented change, formatted as yyyy-MM-dd.']
  effectiveness_review_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the effectiveness review, indicating whether the change achieved its intended objectives.']
  effectiveness_review_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) specifying whether a formal effectiveness review is required after a defined period to assess long-term impact of the change.']
  final_approval_date date [note: 'Date when final approval was granted, authorizing implementation of the change, formatted as yyyy-MM-dd.']
  final_approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the senior authority who provided final approval for the change control request, typically a Quality Director or Plant Manager.']
  initiator_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department or functional area of the change control request initiator.']
  initiator_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who originated the change control request.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the change control record, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  post_change_verification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) specifying whether post-implementation verification activities are required to confirm the change achieved its intended outcome without adverse effects.']
  qa_approval_date date [note: 'Date when Quality Assurance approval was granted, formatted as yyyy-MM-dd.']
  qa_approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the Quality Assurance representative who approved or rejected the change control request.']
  quality_impact_assessment varchar [note: 'Detailed evaluation of the potential impact of the change on product quality attributes, safety, efficacy, and GMP compliance. Documents risk analysis and mitigation strategies.']
  regulatory_approval_date date [note: 'Date when Regulatory Affairs approval was granted, formatted as yyyy-MM-dd.']
  regulatory_approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the Regulatory Affairs representative who approved or rejected the change control request.']
  regulatory_impact_classification varchar [note: 'Assessment of the regulatory significance of the change. Major changes require regulatory authority notification or approval; minor changes may be managed internally per validated change control procedures.']
  request_date date [note: 'Date when the change control request was formally submitted for review, formatted as yyyy-MM-dd.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record from which this change control data originated, typically Veeva Vault QMS.']
  supply_chain_impact_assessment varchar [note: 'Evaluation of the change\'s impact on supply chain operations, including material sourcing, production capacity, lead times, and distribution.']
  target_implementation_date date [note: 'Planned date for implementing the approved change, formatted as yyyy-MM-dd.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Unique document identifier in Veeva Vault QMS system where the change control request and supporting documentation are stored.']
  verification_completion_date date [note: 'Date when post-change verification activities were completed, formatted as yyyy-MM-dd.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Description of the method or procedure used to verify the effectiveness of the implemented change, such as testing, inspection, or performance monitoring.']

  Note: 'Change control request records governing GMP-regulated changes to processes, formulations, equipment, facilities, specifications, and quality systems. Captures change type, change description, impact assessment (quality, regulatory, supply chain), regulatory impact classification, affected products/markets, approval workflow status, implementation date, post-change verification requirements, and effectiveness review. Managed in Veeva Vault QMS. Critical for maintaining validated state per FDA 21 CFR and EU GMP.'
}

Table quality.audit_program {
  audit_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for audit_program']
  parent_audit_program_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on audit_program (parent_audit_program_id)']
  audit_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the audit focus area.']
  audit_criteria varchar [note: 'Specific criteria or checkpoints evaluated during the audit.']
  audit_duration_hours varchar [note: 'Estimated or actual number of hours required to complete the audit.']
  audit_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of non‑conformances or observations identified in the latest audit.']
  audit_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between scheduled executions of the audit program.']
  audit_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to conduct the audit (e.g., internal team, external auditor).']
  audit_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments captured by the audit owner.']
  audit_outcome_template varchar [note: 'Reference to the template used for documenting audit findings.']
  audit_owner varchar [note: 'Name of the internal employee or team responsible for the audit program.']
  audit_report_format varchar [note: 'File format in which the audit report is produced.']
  audit_resources_required varchar [note: 'List of personnel, equipment, or tools needed for the audit.']
  audit_scope varchar [note: 'Textual description of the functional, product, or facility scope covered by the audit.']
  compliance_area varchar [note: 'Regulatory or standards domain the audit program addresses.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Deadline for completing corrective actions arising from the audit.']
  corrective_actions_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the audit generated mandatory corrective actions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit program record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the purpose, scope, and objectives of the audit program.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the audit program expires or is retired (nullable).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the audit program becomes effective.']
  execution_status varchar [note: 'Current execution state of the most recent audit run.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the audit program is required by regulation or policy.']
  last_executed_date date [note: 'Date when the audit program was most recently executed.']
  name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the audit program.']
  next_scheduled_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next execution of the audit program.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable code used to reference the audit program in reports and systems.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Category that defines the nature of the audit program.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Citation of the specific regulation, guideline, or standard that drives the audit program.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department that owns the audit program.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification assigned to the audit program based on impact and likelihood.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the audit program.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the audit program record.']
  version_description varchar [note: 'Narrative describing changes introduced in the current version.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the audit program definition.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for audit_program. Referenced by audit_program_id.'
}

Table quality.audit_checklist {
  audit_checklist_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for audit_checklist']
  parent_audit_checklist_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on audit_checklist (parent_audit_checklist_id)']
  applicable_process varchar [note: 'Business process or operation to which the checklist applies.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit (e.g., internal, external, regulatory, supplier).']
  compliance_area varchar [note: 'Regulatory or standards domain the checklist addresses.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the checklist record was first created.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the audit checklist purpose and scope.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to supporting documentation or SOP (e.g., file name or URL).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the checklist becomes valid.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the checklist is no longer valid (nullable for open‑ended).']
  frequency varchar [note: 'How often the audit checklist should be performed.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the checklist item is mandatory for compliance.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date when the checklist was last reviewed for relevance.']
  name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the audit checklist item.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the checklist item.']
  responsible_role varchar [note: 'Organizational role or function responsible for executing the checklist.']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who performed the last review.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk rating associated with the audit item.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the checklist entry.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the checklist record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the checklist definition.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for audit_checklist. Referenced by audit_checklist_id.'
}

Table quality.laboratory {
  laboratory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for laboratory']
  supervising_laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on laboratory (supervising_laboratory_id)']
  accreditation_body varchar [note: 'Organization that granted the laboratory accreditation (e.g., ISO, FDA).']
  accreditation_expiry date [note: 'Date when the current accreditation expires.']
  accreditation_status varchar [note: 'Current accreditation status of the laboratory.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the laboratory.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, building, etc.).']
  capacity_per_day varchar [note: 'Maximum number of samples the laboratory can process per day.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the laboratory is located.']
  closing_date date [note: 'Date the laboratory ceased operations (null if still active).']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Key compliance framework(s) the laboratory adheres to.']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the laboratory.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the laboratory record was first created in the data lake.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Overall classification level for the laboratory record.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the laboratory\'s capabilities and focus.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email contact for the laboratory.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used in external systems (e.g., SAP QM, Veeva Vault).']
  hazard_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification for hazardous materials handled.']
  lab_code varchar [note: 'External business code used to reference the laboratory in ERP and LIMS systems.']
  lab_type varchar [note: 'Category of analytical capability the laboratory provides.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent internal or external audit.']
  last_audit_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent audit.']
  latitude double [note: 'Geographic latitude of the laboratory location.']
  longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude of the laboratory location.']
  manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the person responsible for laboratory operations.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the laboratory.']
  number_of_equipment varchar [note: 'Total count of major analytical instruments owned by the laboratory.']
  opening_date date [note: 'Date the laboratory began operations.']
  operational_hours varchar [note: 'Standard daily operating hours (e.g., 08:00‑17:00).']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary telephone contact for the laboratory.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code for the laboratory address.']
  quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite quality metric derived from audit results and performance KPIs.']
  region varchar [note: 'Business region where the laboratory operates.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province of the laboratory location.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the laboratory.']
  total_area_sqm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical footprint of the laboratory in square meters.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the laboratory record.']
  waste_disposal_method varchar [note: 'Primary method used for laboratory waste disposal.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for laboratory. Referenced by responsible_lab_id.'
}

Table quality.sample {
  sample_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for sample']
  parent_sample_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on sample (parent_sample_id)']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the finished product from which the sample was taken.']
  analysis_date date [note: 'Date on which the analysis was completed.']
  analysis_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the quality analysis for the sample.']
  analysis_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the analysis result was recorded.']
  analyst_name varchar [note: 'Name of the quality analyst who performed the test.']
  collected_by varchar [note: 'Name of the person who performed the sample collection.']
  collection_date date [note: 'Date on which the sample was physically collected.']
  collection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the sample was collected.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional observations or notes recorded by the analyst.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the sample record was first created in the system.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the sample is considered expired for testing.']
  is_control boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sample is a control specimen used for method verification.']
  is_retest boolean [note: 'True if the sample is a repeat test due to a prior failure or out‑of‑spec result.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the storage location (e.g., freezer, shelf) where the sample is kept.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing lot number associated with the sample.']
  result_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the numeric result.']
  result_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Quantitative measurement obtained from the analysis (e.g., concentration).']
  sample_code varchar [note: 'External code or barcode assigned to the sample for tracking in the quality system.']
  sample_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or description of the sample (e.g., "Batch 12 – Tablet Sample").']
  sample_type varchar [note: 'Category of the sample indicating its purpose in quality testing.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the sample within the quality process.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the storage environment (e.g., "‑20°C freezer, Shelf B3").']
  storage_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature at which the sample is stored, expressed in degrees Celsius.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the sample record.']
  volume_ml decimal(10,3) [note: 'Measured volume of the sample in milliliters.']
  weight_g decimal(10,3) [note: 'Measured weight of the sample in grams.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for sample. Referenced by sample_id.'
}

Table distribution.distribution_facility {
  distribution_facility_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for facility']
  esg_commitment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.esg_commitment. Business justification: Facilities have specific ESG commitments (energy, waste, emissions) that are tracked for performance dashboards and compliance.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Tracks the facility manager employee for each distribution center, required for operational performance metrics and compliance reporting.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the distribution center facility. Organizational contact data classified as confidential business information.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for suite, building, or unit information. Organizational contact data classified as confidential business information.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the distribution center is located. Organizational contact data classified as confidential business information.']
  closed_date date [note: 'Date when the distribution center ceased operations. Null for active facilities. Used for historical analysis and network optimization studies.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary contact email address for the distribution center. Organizational contact data classified as confidential business information.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the distribution center. Organizational contact data classified as confidential business information.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the distribution center is located.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this distribution center record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  cross_dock_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility supports cross-docking operations where inbound goods are directly transferred to outbound shipments with minimal storage time.']
  cycle_count_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Standard frequency in days for cycle counting inventory at this facility. Used for inventory accuracy management and audit compliance.']
  dc_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the distribution center used across operational systems. Externally-known unique code for the facility.']
  dc_name varchar [note: 'Official business name of the distribution center or warehouse facility.']
  dock_doors_inbound varchar [note: 'Number of dock doors designated for inbound receiving operations. Impacts receiving throughput capacity and scheduling.']
  dock_doors_outbound varchar [note: 'Number of dock doors designated for outbound shipping operations. Impacts shipping throughput capacity and carrier scheduling.']
  dsd_hub_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this facility serves as a DSD hub for direct-to-retail delivery operations. DSD hubs bypass traditional distribution channels for faster store replenishment.']
  facility_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the distribution center based on temperature control and operational model. Ambient for room temperature, chilled for refrigerated, frozen for sub-zero, multi-temperature for mixed zones, DSD hub for Direct Store Delivery operations, cross-dock for flow-through distribution.']
  fsc_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility is certified under FSC chain of custody standards for sustainable sourcing. Required for handling FSC-certified paper and wood-based products.']
  gmp_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility is certified under Good Manufacturing Practice standards. Required for handling cosmetics, personal care, and certain consumer goods products.']
  hazmat_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility is certified to handle and store hazardous materials. Required for distribution of products with EPA or OSHA regulated substances.']
  inventory_rotation_method varchar [note: 'Primary inventory rotation method used at this facility. FIFO (First In First Out) for standard goods, FEFO (First Expired First Out) for perishables and date-sensitive products, LIFO (Last In First Out) for specific use cases.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the distribution center in decimal degrees. Used for geospatial analytics, route optimization, and logistics planning.']
  lease_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the facility lease agreement. Null for owned facilities. Used for lease renewal planning and facility strategy.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the distribution center in decimal degrees. Used for geospatial analytics, route optimization, and logistics planning.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this distribution center record was last modified. Audit trail for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  opened_date date [note: 'Date when the distribution center commenced operations. Used for facility age analysis and depreciation calculations.']
  operating_hours_weekday varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for weekdays in format HH:MM-HH:MM. Used for scheduling inbound deliveries, outbound shipments, and workforce planning.']
  operating_hours_weekend varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for weekends in format HH:MM-HH:MM. Used for scheduling weekend operations and carrier coordination.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the distribution center in its lifecycle. Active indicates full operations, inactive for temporarily closed, under construction for new facilities being built, decommissioned for permanently closed, seasonal for facilities operating only during peak periods, maintenance for temporary closure due to upgrades or repairs.']
  osa_target_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target OSA performance percentage for stores served by this distribution center. OSA measures product availability at retail shelf level.']
  otif_target_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target OTIF performance percentage for this distribution center. OTIF measures the percentage of orders delivered on time and in full, a key supply chain KPI.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Classification of facility ownership model. Owned for company-owned facilities, leased for long-term leased properties, third-party logistics for 3PL-operated warehouses.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the distribution center address. Organizational contact data classified as confidential business information.']
  sap_plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA plant code representing this distribution center in the ERP system. Maps to the SAP MM and WM modules for material management and warehouse management.']
  sap_storage_location varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA storage location code within the plant. Represents the primary storage location identifier for inventory transactions in SAP WM module.']
  shifts_per_day varchar [note: 'Number of operational shifts per day at the facility. Used for labor planning, throughput capacity modeling, and operational cost analysis.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or regional administrative division where the facility is located. Organizational contact data classified as confidential business information.']
  storage_capacity_pallet_positions varchar [note: 'Maximum number of pallet positions available for storage. Key metric for inventory capacity planning and space utilization analysis.']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility has temperature-controlled storage zones. True for facilities with chilled or frozen capabilities, false for ambient-only.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the distribution center location. Used for scheduling, shift planning, and cross-facility coordination.']
  total_capacity_sqft decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total warehouse floor space capacity in square feet. Includes all storage, staging, and operational areas within the facility.']
  wms_site_code varchar [note: 'Unique site identifier in the Blue Yonder WMS system. Used for integration and synchronization between the lakehouse and the operational WMS platform.']

  Note: 'Master record for each physical distribution center (DC) or warehouse facility in the CPG network. Captures DC identity, location, type (ambient, chilled, frozen, DSD hub), capacity metrics, WMS integration identifiers (Blue Yonder site codes), SAP plant/storage location mappings, operating hours, and operational status. SSOT for DC facility master data across the distribution domain.'
}

Table distribution.distribution_storage_location {
  distribution_storage_location_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for storage_location']
  distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent distribution center facility where this storage location resides.']
  abc_classification varchar [note: 'Velocity-based classification of the location for slotting optimization: A (high-velocity/fast-moving), B (medium-velocity), C (low-velocity/slow-moving).']
  aisle varchar [note: 'Aisle designation within the warehouse layout for physical navigation and picking route optimization.']
  bay varchar [note: 'Bay or column position within the aisle for precise horizontal location reference.']
  bin_position varchar [note: 'Specific bin or slot position within the level for granular inventory placement and retrieval.']
  blocked_date date [note: 'Date when the storage location was blocked or made unavailable for operations.']
  blocked_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the location is blocked or unavailable, such as maintenance, damage, safety hold, or quality quarantine.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the storage location record was first created in the WMS system.']
  cycle_count_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency for cycle counting inventory at this location to maintain inventory accuracy and compliance.']
  distribution_storage_location_level varchar [note: 'Vertical level or shelf position within the bay for height-based slotting and equipment compatibility.']
  dsd_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the location is designated for DSD operations where products bypass the DC and are delivered directly to retail stores.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the storage location became active and available for warehouse operations.']
  equipment_type_required varchar [note: 'Type of material handling equipment required to access this storage location for put-away and picking operations.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the storage location is scheduled to be decommissioned or removed from active use. Null for indefinite locations.']
  fefo_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the location supports FEFO inventory rotation logic for expiry-sensitive products (pharmaceuticals, food, cosmetics).']
  fifo_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the location supports FIFO inventory rotation logic for age-based stock management.']
  hazmat_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the location is certified and equipped for storing hazardous materials per OSHA and EPA regulations.']
  height_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical height dimension of the storage location in centimeters for vertical clearance and equipment reach validation.']
  last_cycle_count_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent cycle count was performed at this storage location.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the storage location record was most recently updated in the WMS system.']
  length_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical length dimension of the storage location in centimeters for dimensional slotting and equipment compatibility.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the storage location as defined in Blue Yonder WMS. Typically formatted as aisle-bay-level-bin (e.g., A01-B05-L03-P12).']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or description of the storage location for operational reference.']
  location_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the storage location indicating availability for inventory placement and retrieval.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the storage location by operational function: bulk storage, active pick face, reserve inventory, staging area, dock door, or cross-dock zone.']
  mixed_lot_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the location permits storage of multiple lot or batch numbers for the same SKU or requires lot segregation.']
  mixed_sku_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the location permits storage of multiple SKUs simultaneously or requires single-SKU dedication.']
  pick_face_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this location is designated as an active pick face for order fulfillment operations.']
  picking_strategy varchar [note: 'Order fulfillment picking method supported by this location: batch picking, wave picking, zone picking, discrete (single-order), or cluster picking.']
  putaway_strategy varchar [note: 'Algorithm used to assign incoming inventory to this location: directed (system-assigned), random, fixed (dedicated SKU), or dynamic (velocity-based).']
  replenishment_priority varchar [note: 'Priority ranking for automated replenishment from reserve to pick locations, with lower numbers indicating higher priority.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Temperature control classification for the storage location to ensure product integrity and regulatory compliance (ambient, refrigerated 2-8°C, frozen <-18°C, controlled room temperature).']
  volume_capacity_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum volume capacity of the storage location in cubic meters for space utilization and slotting algorithms.']
  weight_capacity_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum weight capacity of the storage location in kilograms for safe load management and slotting optimization.']
  width_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical width dimension of the storage location in centimeters for dimensional slotting and equipment compatibility.']
  zone_code varchar [note: 'Logical zone classification within the distribution center for grouping locations by product category, velocity, or operational workflow (e.g., FAST-PICK, SLOW-MOVE, HAZMAT).']

  Note: 'Granular storage location master within a distribution center — aisles, bays, levels, bin positions, and pick faces as defined in Blue Yonder WMS. Tracks location type (bulk, pick, reserve, staging, dock), zone classification, temperature zone, weight/volume capacity, and FEFO/FIFO eligibility flags. Enables put-away and picking optimization at the slot level.'
}

Table distribution.inbound_receipt {
  inbound_receipt_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inbound receipt transaction. Primary key for this entity.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transportation carrier that delivered the shipment to the distribution center.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Receiving operations incur expenses (labor, equipment) that are allocated to a cost center for expense tracking and budgeting.']
  distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center facility where the goods were received.']
  manufacturing_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.manufacturing_facility. Business justification: Internal transfer receipt: inbound receipts from own plants need the source manufacturing facility to track internal logistics and cost allocation.']
  primary_inbound_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the quality control personnel who performed the inspection, used for accountability and audit trail.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Required for R&D material receipt tracking; links each inbound receipt to the RD project that requested the raw material for formulation trials.']
  receiving_clerk_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse employee who processed the inbound receipt, used for labor tracking and accountability.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier or vendor who shipped the goods, representing the counterparty in this receipt transaction.']
  warehouse_distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific warehouse or storage facility within the distribution center where goods were received.']
  accepted_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of goods accepted into inventory after quality inspection, excluding rejected or damaged items.']
  actual_receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the physical goods arrived and were checked in at the receiving dock, representing the principal business event for this transaction.']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the Advanced Shipping Notice document sent by the supplier prior to shipment arrival, enabling pre-receipt planning and dock scheduling.']
  case_count varchar [note: 'Number of cases or cartons received in this inbound shipment.']
  container_number varchar [note: 'ISO standard container identifier for ocean freight shipments, following the BIC (Bureau International des Containers) format.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this receipt record was first created in the system, representing the audit trail start point.']
  discrepancy_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether any discrepancies (quantity, quality, or documentation) were identified during the receiving process.']
  discrepancy_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional details about any discrepancies identified during the receiving process.']
  discrepancy_reason varchar [note: 'Classification of the type of discrepancy identified during receiving, used for root cause analysis and supplier performance management.']
  dock_door_number varchar [note: 'Physical dock door location where the inbound shipment was unloaded, used for labor planning and dock utilization tracking.']
  expected_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total quantity of goods expected to be received based on the ASN or purchase order, used for discrepancy detection.']
  goods_receipt_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP Material Management (MM) goods receipt document number generated upon posting the receipt to inventory, linking WMS to ERP.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this receipt record was last updated, supporting audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  otif_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether this receipt met both on-time delivery and complete quantity requirements, key supplier performance metric.']
  pallet_count varchar [note: 'Number of pallets received in this inbound shipment, used for dock capacity planning and labor allocation.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating purchase order that authorized this inbound shipment.']
  putaway_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when all received goods were moved from the receiving dock to their designated storage locations, completing the inbound process.']
  quality_inspection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether formal quality inspection was required for this receipt based on product category, supplier risk profile, or regulatory requirements.']
  quality_inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the quality inspection process for this receipt, tracking progression through quality control workflow.']
  receipt_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when all receiving activities (unloading, inspection, put-away) were completed and the receipt was closed in the WMS.']
  receipt_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the inbound receipt, used for tracking and reference across systems and with suppliers.']
  receipt_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inbound receipt transaction, tracking progression from scheduling through completion or exception handling.']
  receipt_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inbound receipt based on the source and nature of the goods being received.']
  received_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Actual total quantity of goods physically received and counted during the receiving process.']
  rejected_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of goods rejected during receiving due to quality issues, damage, or non-conformance to specifications.']
  scheduled_receipt_date date [note: 'Planned date for the inbound shipment to arrive at the distribution center, based on ASN or supplier commitment.']
  seal_intact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the security seal was found intact upon arrival, critical for quality control and loss prevention.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Security seal identifier applied to the trailer or container, verified upon receipt to ensure shipment integrity and prevent tampering.']
  temperature_check_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether temperature verification was required for this receipt due to cold chain or temperature-sensitive product requirements.']
  temperature_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the recorded temperature was within acceptable range per product specifications and cold chain requirements.']
  temperature_reading_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual temperature reading in Celsius recorded during receipt inspection for temperature-sensitive products, critical for cold chain compliance.']
  trailer_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the trailer or truck that delivered the shipment, used for tracking and carrier performance analysis.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for the quantities recorded in this receipt (Each, Case, Pallet, Kilogram, Pound, Liter, Gallon). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PL|EA|KG|LB|LT|GL — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the physical receipt of goods at a DC from suppliers, manufacturing plants, or inter-DC transfers. Records ASN reference, carrier, trailer/container ID, dock door, receipt date/time, received quantity by SKU/lot, temperature check results, and discrepancy flags. Integrates with SAP WM goods receipt and Blue Yonder WMS inbound processing. Drives inventory on-hand updates and FEFO/FIFO lot registration.'
}

Table distribution.inbound_receipt_line {
  inbound_receipt_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inbound receipt line. Primary key for this entity.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User ID of the warehouse associate who physically received and recorded this line. Used for accountability and performance tracking.']
  inbound_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent inbound receipt header transaction. Links this line to the overall receipt event.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Needed to associate each receipt line (specific SKU/lot) with the RD project using it, enabling traceability of trial material consumption.']
  regulatory_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.registration. Business justification: Regulatory registration verification during inbound receipt ensures each received SKU is registered in the jurisdiction, a standard compliance step.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Link receipt line to SKU master for traceability and quality inspection; required by receipt processing and compliance audit reports.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: RECEIVING: When a receipt line is processed, a Stock Position record is created/updated to reflect on‑hand quantity at the DC.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or vendor who shipped the goods. Used for supplier performance tracking and procurement analytics.']
  asn_line_number varchar [note: 'Line number within the ASN document corresponding to this receipt line. Enables automated matching between ASN and physical receipt.']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'Advanced Shipping Notice document number sent by the supplier prior to shipment arrival. Used for pre-receipt planning and variance detection.']
  condition_code varchar [note: 'Quality condition assessment of the received goods. Determines whether product can be put away into available inventory or requires special handling.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this receipt line record was first created in the warehouse management system. Audit trail for data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the unit cost and extended cost. Supports multi-currency procurement operations.']
  damage_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any damage, defects, or quality issues observed during receipt inspection. Used for claims and supplier performance tracking.']
  expected_quantity_cases decimal(15,3) [note: 'Expected quantity in cases as specified in the Advanced Shipping Notice (ASN) or purchase order. Used for variance detection.']
  expected_quantity_eaches decimal(15,3) [note: 'Expected quantity in eaches as specified in the Advanced Shipping Notice (ASN) or purchase order. Used for variance detection.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the product expires and can no longer be sold or used. Critical for FEFO rotation and inventory management in consumer goods.']
  extended_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total cost for this receipt line calculated as unit cost multiplied by received quantity. Used for inventory valuation and financial reconciliation.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'Global Trade Item Number uniquely identifying the product in the supply chain. May be GTIN-8, GTIN-12, GTIN-13, or GTIN-14 format.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this receipt line record was last updated. Audit trail for tracking changes to receipt data.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the inbound receipt. Used for ordering and referencing specific lines in the receipt document.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing lot or batch number assigned by the supplier. Critical for traceability, quality control, and recall management in consumer goods.']
  manufacture_date date [note: 'Date when the product was manufactured by the supplier. Used for shelf-life calculations and FEFO inventory rotation.']
  pallet_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the pallet or handling unit containing this receipt line. Used for put-away and storage location assignment.']
  purchase_order_line_number varchar [note: 'Line number on the purchase order corresponding to this receipt line. Enables three-way matching between PO, receipt, and invoice.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order number authorizing the receipt of these goods. Links the receipt to the original procurement transaction.']
  put_away_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the goods were put away into their assigned storage location. Used for warehouse productivity and cycle time measurement.']
  quality_inspection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this receipt line requires quality inspection before being released to available inventory. True for regulated or high-risk products.']
  quality_inspection_status varchar [note: 'Status of quality inspection for this receipt line. Determines whether goods can be released to available inventory or must remain in quarantine.']
  receipt_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of this receipt line within the warehouse workflow. Tracks progression from initial receipt through put-away completion.']
  received_quantity_cases decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of product received measured in cases. Represents the outer packaging unit typically used for warehouse handling.']
  received_quantity_eaches decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of product received measured in individual units (eaches). Represents the consumer-facing unit count.']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the goods were physically received at the warehouse dock. Critical for OTIF performance measurement and cycle time analytics.']
  sscc varchar [note: '18-digit Serial Shipping Container Code identifying the logistics unit. Global standard for tracking pallets and containers in the supply chain.']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'Warehouse storage location code where the received goods were put away. Identifies the bin, rack, or zone within the distribution center.']
  temperature_at_receipt_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature measurement in Celsius recorded at the time of receipt. Critical for cold chain compliance and product quality verification.']
  unit_cost decimal(15,4) [note: 'Cost per unit for the received goods as specified in the purchase order. Used for inventory valuation and COGS calculation.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Primary unit of measure for the received quantity. Defines the counting and handling unit for warehouse operations.']
  upc varchar [note: 'Universal Product Code barcode identifier for the received item. Standard 12-digit UPC-A format used in North American retail.']
  variance_quantity_cases decimal(15,3) [note: 'Difference between expected and received quantity in cases. Positive values indicate overages, negative values indicate shortages.']
  variance_quantity_eaches decimal(15,3) [note: 'Difference between expected and received quantity in eaches. Positive values indicate overages, negative values indicate shortages.']
  warehouse_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the distribution center or warehouse facility where the receipt occurred. Used for multi-site inventory management.']

  Note: 'Line-level detail for each SKU/lot received within an inbound receipt transaction. Captures SKU code, GTIN, lot number, manufacture date, expiry date, received quantity (cases and eaches), unit of measure, pallet ID, temperature at receipt, and variance from expected ASN quantity. Supports FEFO/FIFO lot registration and discrepancy resolution workflows.'
}

Table distribution.putaway_task {
  putaway_task_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the putaway task record generated by the WMS. Primary key for the putaway task entity.']
  distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center or warehouse where the putaway task is executed.']
  distribution_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the inbound shipment or receipt that triggered this putaway task, linking the task back to the receiving process for traceability.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse associate or operator assigned to execute this putaway task, used for labor tracking and productivity measurement.']
  inventory_replenishment_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the replenishment order that generated this putaway task, applicable when the task type is replenishment from reserve to pick face.']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific lot or batch being moved, critical for traceability, expiration management, and recall readiness.']
  primary_putaway_distribution_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse location from which the goods are being moved (e.g., receiving dock, staging area, reserve storage).']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product SKU being moved in this putaway task.']
  target_location_distribution_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse location to which the goods are being moved (e.g., reserve storage bin, pick face slot, cross-dock staging).']
  confirmation_method varchar [note: 'The method used by the warehouse operator to confirm task completion: barcode scan, RFID tag read, manual entry, voice-directed picking system, or vision-based verification.']
  cube_cubic_feet decimal(12,3) [note: 'The total volume in cubic feet of the goods being moved, used for space utilization analysis and slotting optimization.']
  cycle_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'The elapsed time in minutes from task start to task completion, a key labor productivity KPI used to measure warehouse associate efficiency and identify process bottlenecks.']
  equipment_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material handling equipment (forklift, pallet jack, reach truck) used to execute the putaway task, tracked for equipment utilization and maintenance scheduling.']
