  exception_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating any exception or issue encountered during putaway execution (e.g., damaged goods, location full, quantity mismatch, system error), used for root cause analysis and process improvement.']
  exception_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the exception or issue encountered during putaway execution, providing additional context for operational review and corrective action.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the SKU being moved is classified as hazardous material, requiring special handling, storage segregation, and regulatory compliance per OSHA and EPA standards.']
  movement_strategy varchar [note: 'The inventory rotation or replenishment strategy applied to this putaway task: FEFO (First Expired First Out), FIFO (First In First Out), min/max threshold-based, kanban pull signal, wave-driven batch, zone-based slotting, or ABC classification velocity-based. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FEFO|FIFO|min_max|kanban|wave_driven|zone_based|ABC_classification — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  pallet_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the pallet or handling unit being moved, typically a license plate number (LPN) or SSCC (Serial Shipping Container Code) scanned during putaway execution.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking assigned to the putaway task (typically 1-10, with 1 being highest priority) used by WMS to sequence work assignments and optimize labor allocation.']
  quality_check_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the required quality inspection has been completed and passed, enabling downstream processes to proceed.']
  quality_check_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a quality inspection is required before or after putaway completion, typically for high-value items, temperature-sensitive goods, or regulatory compliance.']
  quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The quantity of the SKU being moved in this putaway task, expressed in the unit of measure specified.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this putaway task record was first created in the source system, used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this putaway task record was last updated in the source system, used for change tracking and data freshness monitoring.']
  source_location_code varchar [note: 'Human-readable code of the source location (e.g., DOCK-A-01, STAGE-B-05) used by warehouse operators for navigation and scanning.']
  system_source varchar [note: 'The source system or module that generated this putaway task record (e.g., BLUE_YONDER_WMS, SAP_EWM, MANHATTAN_WMS), used for data lineage and integration troubleshooting.']
  target_location_code varchar [note: 'Human-readable code of the target location (e.g., A-12-03-B, PICK-C-22) used by warehouse operators for navigation and scanning.']
  task_assigned_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the putaway task was assigned to a warehouse operator, used to measure assignment latency and labor allocation efficiency.']
  task_cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the putaway task was cancelled, applicable when the task is aborted due to inventory discrepancies, system errors, or operational changes.']
  task_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the putaway task was completed (e.g., goods confirmed in target location), marking the end of the task lifecycle and enabling cycle time KPI calculation.']
  task_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the putaway task was created by the WMS, marking the start of the task lifecycle and used for cycle time calculation.']
  task_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the putaway task, typically generated by Blue Yonder WMS for tracking and operator reference.']
  task_started_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the warehouse operator began executing the putaway task (e.g., scanned the source location or pallet), used for labor productivity tracking.']
  task_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the putaway task indicating its execution state within the warehouse workflow.']
  task_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the putaway task based on the movement purpose: inbound putaway (receiving dock to storage), replenishment (reserve to pick face), cross-dock (dock to dock), returns putaway (returns area to storage), or internal transfer.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'The temperature control zone classification for the target storage location: ambient (room temperature), refrigerated (2-8°C), frozen (<0°C), or controlled (specific temperature range), critical for cold chain compliance.']
  travel_distance_feet decimal(10,2) [note: 'The physical distance in feet traveled by the warehouse operator from source to target location, used for slotting optimization and labor efficiency analysis.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the putaway quantity: EA (each), CS (case), PL (pallet), LB (pound), KG (kilogram), L (liter), GAL (gallon). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PL|LB|KG|L|GAL — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  wave_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the wave or batch of tasks to which this putaway task belongs, used for wave-driven replenishment and coordinated task execution.']
  weight_lbs decimal(12,2) [note: 'The total weight in pounds of the goods being moved in this putaway task, used for equipment selection, labor safety, and capacity planning.']
  zone_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse zone where the target location resides, used for labor assignment and zone-based picking strategies.']

  Note: 'Operational task record generated by Blue Yonder WMS directing warehouse associates to move goods within the DC — covering both inbound putaway (staging/dock to storage) and internal replenishment (reserve to pick face). Captures source location, target location, SKU, lot, pallet ID, assigned operator, task creation and completion timestamps, task type (inbound putaway, replenishment, cross-dock), movement strategy (FEFO, min/max, kanban, wave-driven), and priority. Tracks DC labor productivity, putaway cycle time, and pick face availability KPIs.'
}

Table distribution.outbound_order {
  outbound_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the outbound fulfillment order. Primary key for the outbound order entity.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Promotional campaign fulfillment requires outbound orders to be tied to a specific campaign for inventory allocation and performance tracking.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transportation carrier assigned to deliver the outbound order. Used for freight planning, carrier performance tracking, and proof of delivery.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Required for Order Entry Accountability report tracking which sales employee created each outbound order for performance and audit.']
  distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center fulfilling the outbound order. Determines inventory source and warehouse operations responsible for order execution.']
  freight_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.freight_order. Business justification: Freight order creation for every outbound order manages carrier tendering, freight cost tracking, and audit.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Brand‑level financial and sales reporting aggregates order data by brand, so each outbound order must reference its primary brand.']
  order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating sales order document in the ERP system. Links outbound fulfillment to revenue recognition and customer order management.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_order. Business justification: Regulatory traceability: outbound orders must reference the production order that generated the shipped product for recall and compliance reporting.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Needed for revenue recognition and profit‑center reporting; each outbound order\'s sales revenue is attributed to a profit center.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Supports pilot order execution; outbound orders for prototype SKUs are linked to the RD project that owns the launch trial.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the delivery route assigned to the outbound order. Used for DSD (Direct Store Delivery) route optimization and multi-stop delivery planning.']
  ship_to_location_distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the destination location where the order will be delivered. May reference retail store, warehouse, or customer facility.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'FK to product.sku.sku_id — Links distribution outbound orders to product master. Required for product-level fulfillment analytics, category-level DC throughput reporting.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer or retail account placing the outbound order. Links to trade account master for customer details, pricing agreements, and delivery preferences.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Date when the order was actually delivered to the customer destination. Used to calculate OTIF performance and delivery lead time.']
  actual_ship_date date [note: 'Date when the order was actually shipped from the distribution center. Used to measure warehouse execution performance against requested ship date.']
  backorder_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any line items on the order are backordered due to insufficient inventory. True when ordered quantity exceeds available stock.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-issued document number serving as receipt of goods and contract of carriage. Required for freight claims and proof of delivery.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description explaining why the order was cancelled. Examples include customer request, inventory shortage, credit hold, or duplicate order. Used for root cause analysis.']
  cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the order was cancelled. Null for active orders. Used for cancellation rate analysis and order lifecycle reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the outbound order record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and order lifecycle tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the order value. Typically USD for domestic US operations, but may vary for export orders.']
  fill_rate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of ordered quantity that was fulfilled from available inventory. Calculated as (shipped quantity / ordered quantity) * 100. Key metric for inventory availability and customer service.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order contains hazardous materials requiring special handling, labeling, and transportation compliance. True for products regulated under DOT hazmat rules.']
  incoterm varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms defining the division of costs and risks between buyer and seller. Critical for export orders and freight responsibility determination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the outbound order record was last updated. Audit field for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  order_date date [note: 'Date when the outbound order was created or received from the customer. Principal business event timestamp for order lifecycle tracking.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the outbound order. Draft indicates order creation, released means ready for fulfillment, picking/packing/staged represent warehouse execution phases, shipped indicates in-transit, delivered confirms receipt, cancelled indicates order termination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|released|picking|packing|staged|shipped|delivered|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the outbound order based on fulfillment channel and destination. Retail replenishment serves store orders, DSD (Direct Store Delivery) bypasses DC, ecommerce serves online consumers, inter-DC transfer moves inventory between distribution centers, wholesale serves B2B customers, export serves international markets.']
  otif_commitment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this order is subject to formal OTIF performance measurement and customer scorecard reporting. True for orders with contractual delivery commitments.']
  packing_slip_number varchar [note: 'Document number for the packing slip accompanying the shipment. Used for customer receiving and invoice reconciliation.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms agreed with the customer for this order. Examples include Net 30, Net 60, COD (Cash on Delivery), or prepaid. Impacts accounts receivable and cash flow management.']
  pick_ticket_number varchar [note: 'Warehouse document number used to direct picking operations for this order. Generated by WMS when order is released to the warehouse floor.']
  priority_code varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the outbound order for warehouse sequencing and resource allocation. Critical orders receive highest priority, rush orders are expedited, expedited orders have faster processing, standard orders follow normal flow.']
  proof_of_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when delivery was confirmed by the recipient. Captured from carrier POD or customer signature. Used for OTIF measurement and freight audit.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Customer\'s purchase order number provided for order tracking and invoice reconciliation. Required for EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) transactions with retail customers.']
  requested_ship_date date [note: 'Date when the customer or system requests the order to be shipped from the distribution center. Used for warehouse planning and scheduling.']
  required_delivery_date date [note: 'Date by which the order must be delivered to the customer destination. Critical for OTIF (On Time In Full) performance measurement and SLA compliance.']
  service_level varchar [note: 'Delivery speed commitment for the outbound order. Determines carrier selection, freight cost, and OTIF measurement criteria.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Transportation mode used for order delivery. Ground for truck, air for expedited freight, ocean for international, rail for bulk, parcel for small packages, LTL (Less Than Truckload) for partial loads, FTL (Full Truckload) for full loads. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ground|air|ocean|rail|parcel|ltl|ftl — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text instructions for warehouse and logistics teams regarding special handling requirements. May include temperature control, fragile handling, hazmat procedures, or customer-specific delivery instructions.']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order requires temperature-controlled storage and transportation. True for cold chain products requiring refrigeration or freezing.']
  total_order_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total quantity of units across all line items in the outbound order. Used for warehouse capacity planning and OTIF fill rate calculation.']
  total_order_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the outbound order before taxes and freight charges. Used for order prioritization, credit limit checks, and revenue forecasting.']
  total_order_volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total volume of the outbound order in cubic meters. Used for warehouse space planning, truck cube utilization, and pallet configuration.']
  total_order_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total weight of the outbound order in kilograms. Used for freight cost calculation, carrier capacity planning, and vehicle loading optimization.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-provided tracking number for shipment visibility and customer self-service tracking. Used for parcel and LTL shipments.']
  wave_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse wave that includes this order. Wave picking groups orders for efficient batch picking and resource optimization.']

  Note: 'Master outbound fulfillment order record representing a customer or retailer replenishment request to be fulfilled from a DC. Captures order number, order type (retail replenishment, DSD, e-commerce, inter-DC transfer), customer/account reference, requested ship date, required delivery date, priority, OTIF commitment, and order status lifecycle. Sourced from SAP SD and Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud order management.'
}

Table distribution.outbound_order_line {
  outbound_order_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the outbound order line item.']
  distribution_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_storage_location. Business justification: Outbound order line items are stored in a DC location before picking. Adding a FK to distribution_storage_location enables location lookup and normalizes location data.']
  label_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.label_version. Business justification: Label version compliance check before order fulfillment guarantees the label used for the SKU is approved for the destination market.']
  outbound_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent outbound fulfillment order header.']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promotion_event. Business justification: Promotion Attribution Report requires linking each order line to the promotion event that drove the sale; experts expect this FK to attribute revenue to promotions.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: PICKING: Outbound order line pick reduces the corresponding Stock Position quantity, linking order fulfillment to inventory balances.']
  actual_ship_date date [note: 'Actual date the line was shipped from the distribution center.']
  allocated_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity allocated from warehouse inventory to this order line.']
  base_unit_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity converted to base unit of measure for standardized reporting and inventory tracking.']
  confirmed_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity confirmed available for fulfillment after ATP (Available to Promise) check.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the outbound order line record was first created in the system.']
  customer_po_line_number varchar [note: 'Customer\'s purchase order line number for cross-reference and reconciliation.']
  dsd_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line is part of a direct store delivery order bypassing retailer distribution centers.']
  edi_line_reference varchar [note: 'EDI transaction line reference number for electronic order processing and ASN generation.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Product expiration date for FEFO inventory rotation and shelf-life management.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'Global trade item number (GTIN-8, GTIN-12, GTIN-13, or GTIN-14) for the product being shipped.']
  handling_unit_code varchar [note: 'Serial shipping container code (SSCC) or handling unit identifier for the container holding this line item.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line contains hazardous materials requiring special handling and documentation.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the outbound order for ordering and identification.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current fulfillment status of the order line in the warehouse execution lifecycle. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|allocated|picked|packed|shipped|cancelled|short_shipped — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  line_volume_m3 decimal(15,6) [note: 'Total volume of the shipped quantity for this line in cubic meters.']
  line_weight_kg decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total weight of the shipped quantity for this line in kilograms.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing lot or batch number for traceability and quality control.']
  ordered_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of the SKU originally requested by the customer in the order.']
  otif_status varchar [note: 'Line-level OTIF performance status indicating whether the line was delivered on time and in full quantity.']
  pack_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the line item was packed into shipping containers.']
  packed_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity packed into shipping containers and ready for dispatch.']
  pallet_code varchar [note: 'Pallet identifier if the line item was shipped on a palletized load.']
  pick_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the line item was picked from warehouse inventory.']
  pick_zone varchar [note: 'Warehouse pick zone designation for labor management and routing optimization.']
  picked_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity physically picked from warehouse storage locations during fulfillment.']
  requested_ship_date date [note: 'Customer-requested or system-calculated target ship date for this line.']
  serial_numbers varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of serial numbers for serialized inventory items shipped on this line.']
  ship_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the line item was shipped from the distribution center.']
  shipped_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity actually shipped to the customer, may differ from ordered due to short-ship scenarios.']
  short_ship_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line was short-shipped (shipped quantity less than ordered quantity).']
  short_ship_reason_code varchar [note: 'Reason code for short shipment (OOS=Out of Stock, DAMAGE=Damaged Inventory, RECALL=Product Recall, EXPIRED=Expired Product, ALLOCATION=Allocation Constraint).']
  sku_code varchar [note: 'Internal stock keeping unit code identifying the specific product variant being shipped.']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'Specific storage location or bin within the warehouse where the product was picked from.']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line requires temperature-controlled storage and transportation.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for quantities (EA=Each, CS=Case, PL=Pallet, BX=Box, KG=Kilogram, LB=Pound, LT=Liter, GL=Gallon). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PL|BX|KG|LB|LT|GL — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unit_volume_m3 decimal(12,6) [note: 'Volume per unit of the SKU in cubic meters for space utilization and load planning.']
  unit_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Weight per unit of the SKU in kilograms for freight calculation and capacity planning.']
  upc varchar [note: 'Universal product code (UPC-A) for retail scanning and point-of-sale identification.']
  warehouse_location_code varchar [note: 'Distribution center or warehouse location code from which this line was fulfilled.']

  Note: 'Line-level detail for each SKU within an outbound fulfillment order. Records SKU code, GTIN, ordered quantity, confirmed quantity, allocated quantity, picked quantity, shipped quantity, unit of measure, lot number, expiry date, and line-level OTIF status. Enables order fill rate tracking, short-ship identification, and OSA impact analysis at the SKU-customer level.'
}

Table distribution.pick_task {
  pick_task_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the pick task record. Primary key for the pick_task data product.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Pick task labor costs are charged to a cost center; required for labor cost analysis and OTIF performance metrics.']
  destination_location_distribution_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the target location where picked items are staged or packed (e.g., pack station, staging lane, loading dock).']
  distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center or warehouse where the pick task is executed.']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific lot or batch of inventory being picked. Critical for FEFO (First Expired First Out) and FIFO (First In First Out) rotation compliance.']
  outbound_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent outbound order or shipment order that generated this pick task.']
  primary_pick_distribution_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse storage location (bin, shelf, pallet position) from which inventory is picked.']
  primary_pick_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse operator or picker assigned to execute this task.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product SKU being picked or packed in this task.']
  carton_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the carton or shipping container into which items are packed. May be a license plate number (LPN) or SSCC (Serial Shipping Container Code).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the pick task record was first created in the WMS. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  dsd_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this pick task is part of a Direct Store Delivery (DSD) execution workflow (true = DSD, false = standard distribution).']
  exception_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying any exception or issue encountered during task execution (e.g., short pick, damaged goods, location discrepancy). Null if no exception.']
  exception_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the operator or supervisor describing the exception or issue encountered during task execution.']
  gross_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total gross weight of the packed carton or pallet including product and packaging materials, measured in kilograms.']
  gs1_128_label varchar [note: 'The GS1-128 barcode label data applied to the carton or pallet, encoding SSCC, GTIN, lot, expiry, and other supply chain attributes.']
  height_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Height dimension of the packed carton or pallet, measured in centimeters.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the pick task record was last updated in the WMS. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  length_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Length dimension of the packed carton or pallet, measured in centimeters.']
  net_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Net weight of the product content only (excluding packaging), measured in kilograms.']
  otif_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this pick task is subject to OTIF (On Time In Full) performance measurement (true = OTIF tracked, false = not tracked).']
  pack_station_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the packing station where the packing operation is performed. Applicable when task_type includes packing.']
  packaging_material_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the type of packaging material used (e.g., corrugated box, poly bag, shrink wrap). Used for cartonization and sustainability tracking.']
  pallet_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the pallet onto which cartons or items are loaded. Typically an SSCC or internal LPN.']
  pick_accuracy_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the pick was accurate (true = picked quantity matches requested quantity and correct SKU/lot; false = discrepancy detected).']
  pick_list_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable pick list reference number assigned by the WMS for operator identification and tracking.']
  pick_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The quantity of SKU units to be picked for this task, measured in the SKU\'s base unit of measure.']
  picked_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The actual quantity of SKU units picked by the operator. May differ from pick_quantity due to short picks or overages.']
  picking_strategy varchar [note: 'The picking methodology applied: discrete (single order), batch (multiple orders), zone (by warehouse zone), wave (grouped by wave), cluster (multi-order cart picking).']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification of the pick task for sequencing and resource allocation: urgent (immediate), high (expedited), normal (standard), low (backlog).']
  sscc varchar [note: '18-digit GS1 Serial Shipping Container Code uniquely identifying the logistics unit (carton or pallet) for tracking through the supply chain.']
  task_assigned_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the pick task was assigned to an operator by the WMS. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  task_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the pick task was completed and confirmed by the operator or WMS. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  task_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time in seconds from task start to completion. Used for labor productivity analysis and standard time calculation.']
  task_started_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the operator began executing the pick task (scanned start or confirmed task initiation). Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  task_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pick task: pending (awaiting assignment), assigned (allocated to operator), in_progress (actively being executed), completed (finished successfully), cancelled (voided), on_hold (temporarily suspended).']
  task_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator indicating the type of fulfillment task: pick (picking only), pack (packing only), pick_and_pack (combined operation), or replenishment_pick (internal stock movement).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for pick and picked quantities (e.g., EA for each, CS for case, PL for pallet).']
  wave_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the wave batch that groups multiple pick tasks for optimized execution. Supports wave picking strategy.']
  width_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Width dimension of the packed carton or pallet, measured in centimeters.']

  Note: 'Fulfillment task record covering both picking and packing operations within DC outbound execution. For picking: captures pick list reference, source location, SKU, lot, pick quantity, assigned operator, wave/batch reference, pick accuracy flag, and task timestamps. For packing: captures pack station, carton/pallet ID, packed SKUs and quantities, packaging material, gross weight, dimensions, GS1-128/SSCC label, and packer operator. Supports wave picking, batch picking, zone picking, and cartonization strategies in Blue Yonder WMS. Task_type discriminator distinguishes pick vs pack execution steps.'
}

Table distribution.pack_task {
  pack_task_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the packing task record. Primary key.']
  outbound_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the outbound order being packed for dispatch.']
  primary_pack_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse operator who performed the packing task.']
  carrier_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the logistics carrier assigned to transport the packed container.']
  carton_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the shipping carton or container used for packing.']
  cartonization_rule_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the cartonization algorithm or business rule used to determine optimal packing configuration.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the pack task record was first created in the system.']
  destination_facility_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the destination distribution center, retail store, or customer facility for the packed shipment.']
  dsd_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pack task is for Direct Store Delivery channel bypassing distribution center.']
  exception_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying any exception or issue encountered during the packing process.']
  exception_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes describing any exceptions, issues, or special handling instructions for the pack task.']
  gross_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total gross weight of the packed container including product and packaging materials, measured in kilograms.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the packed container contains hazardous materials requiring special handling and labeling.']
  height_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Height dimension of the packed container in centimeters.']
  label_printed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shipping label was printed for the packed container.']
  label_type varchar [note: 'Type of shipping label applied to the packed container.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the pack task record was last updated in the system.']
  length_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Length dimension of the packed container in centimeters.']
  net_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Net weight of the product contents excluding packaging materials, measured in kilograms.']
  otif_target_ship_date date [note: 'Target ship date for OTIF performance measurement and customer service level compliance.']
  pack_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the packing task was completed and sealed for dispatch.']
  pack_duration_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total time in minutes taken to complete the packing task, used for labor productivity analysis.']
  pack_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the packing task was initiated by the operator.']
  pack_station_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical pack station or workstation where packing occurred.']
  pack_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the packing task.']
  pack_type varchar [note: 'Type of packing configuration used for the task.']
  packaging_material_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the type of packaging material used for the carton or pallet.']
  pallet_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the pallet if items were packed onto a pallet for shipment.']
  quality_check_status varchar [note: 'Status of quality verification performed on the packed container before dispatch.']
  sscc varchar [note: 'GS1-128 compliant 18-digit Serial Shipping Container Code applied to the packed unit for supply chain traceability.']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the packed container requires temperature-controlled transportation and storage.']
  total_sku_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct SKUs packed into the container.']
  total_unit_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total quantity of units packed across all SKUs in the container.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-provided tracking number for shipment visibility and proof of delivery.']
  volume_cubic_meters decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total volumetric capacity of the packed container in cubic meters, used for transportation planning.']
  wave_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the wave or batch grouping of orders for coordinated picking and packing.']
  width_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Width dimension of the packed container in centimeters.']

  Note: 'Packing task record capturing the consolidation and packaging of picked items into shipping cartons or pallets for outbound dispatch. Records pack station ID, outbound order reference, carton/pallet ID, packed SKUs and quantities, packaging material used, gross weight, dimensions, label applied (GS1-128 / SSCC), pack completion timestamp, and packer operator ID. Drives cartonization and pallet build compliance.'
}

Table distribution.distribution_shipment {
  distribution_shipment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the distribution shipment record. Primary key for the distribution shipment entity.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: OTIF and delivery KPI dashboards measure shipment performance per marketing campaign, requiring a direct shipment‑to‑campaign link.']
  carbon_offset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.carbon_offset. Business justification: Required for carbon‑offset tracking per shipment to meet ESG reporting and carbon‑neutral logistics commitments.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for shipment cost allocation report; logistics expenses are charged to a cost center, a standard practice in consumer‑goods distribution.']
  intransit_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.intransit_shipment. Business justification: SHIPMENT HAND‑OFF: Distribution shipment creates an In‑Transit Shipment record to track movement from DC to destination.']
  lane_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.lane. Business justification: Costing and transit planning use lane definitions; linking shipment to lane provides distance, rate, and compliance data.']
  loaded_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Needed for Load Execution Log to record which warehouse employee performed the loading of each shipment, supporting OTIF compliance and labor costing.']
  manufacturing_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.manufacturing_facility. Business justification: Shipment origin tracking: linking shipments to the originating manufacturing facility enables performance dashboards and OTIF analysis.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Brand‑level shipment analytics need each shipment to be associated with the brand whose products are being shipped.']
  outbound_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.outbound_order. Business justification: Shipment is a child of an outbound order; each shipment fulfills an order. Adding outbound_order_id to distribution_shipment creates the necessary parent link without creating a bidirectional relation']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promotion_event. Business justification: Shipment Manifest for Promotional Campaigns tracks which shipment fulfills which promotion event, used for logistics planning and compliance reporting.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: OTIF reporting requires tying each shipment to its originating purchase order to measure delivery performance against contractual commitments.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Enables shipment‑level reporting for R&D pilot shipments, required for OTIF and regulatory compliance of trial product deliveries.']
  actual_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the shipment was delivered and received at the destination. Used for final OTIF calculation.']
  actual_departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the shipment departed the DC. Critical for OTIF calculation and carrier performance tracking.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the bill of lading document issued by the carrier. Legal document for freight movement and proof of shipment.']
  carrier_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transportation carrier responsible for moving the shipment from DC to destination.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Business name of the transportation carrier for display and reporting purposes.']
  carrier_service_level varchar [note: 'Service tier selected for this shipment defining speed and handling requirements.']
  carton_count varchar [note: 'Total number of cartons or cases included in the shipment. Used for piece-level tracking and receiving verification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the shipment record was first created in the WMS. Audit trail for shipment lifecycle tracking.']
  destination_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line of the shipment destination. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  destination_city varchar [note: 'City name of the shipment destination address.']
  destination_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier of the destination location (store number, DC code, customer account number) where goods are being delivered.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the shipment destination for customs and routing purposes.']
  destination_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the destination location for display and confirmation purposes.']
  destination_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the shipment destination. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  destination_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province code of the shipment destination address.']
  destination_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shipment destination entity type for routing and handling purposes.']
  dock_door_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the loading dock door at the source DC where the shipment was staged and loaded.']
  estimated_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Current estimated delivery date and time based on real-time tracking and carrier updates. Updated dynamically during transit.']
  freight_charge_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total freight cost charged for this shipment in the transaction currency. Used for logistics cost analysis and freight audit.']
  freight_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the freight charge amount.']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Terms defining which party is responsible for freight payment and at what point ownership transfers. Critical for cost allocation.']
  goods_issue_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP SD goods issue document number linking the physical shipment to the ERP inventory transaction. Critical for inventory accuracy.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this shipment contains hazardous materials requiring special handling and documentation per DOT regulations.']
  in_full_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the shipment was delivered complete with all ordered quantities. Component of OTIF calculation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the shipment record was last updated. Audit trail for change tracking and data freshness verification.']
  line_item_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct SKU line items included in the shipment. Used for picking complexity and receiving planning.']
  load_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the load plan that consolidated multiple orders or shipments into this trailer. Used for load optimization tracking.']
  on_time_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the shipment was delivered within the committed delivery window. Component of OTIF calculation.']
  otif_status varchar [note: 'Calculated OTIF performance status indicating whether the shipment was delivered on time and complete per customer commitment. Key supply chain KPI.']
  pallet_count varchar [note: 'Total number of pallets included in the shipment. Used for handling unit tracking and dock labor planning.']
  pro_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-assigned progressive number used for tracking and tracing the shipment in the carriers system.']
  scheduled_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned date for the shipment to arrive at the destination. Used for customer commitment and OTIF measurement.']
  scheduled_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time for the shipment to arrive at the destination. Precision timestamp for delivery window commitments.']
  scheduled_departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time for the shipment to depart from the DC dock. Precision timestamp for appointment scheduling and carrier coordination.']
  scheduled_ship_date date [note: 'Planned date for the shipment to depart from the distribution center. Used for OTIF performance measurement baseline.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the security seal applied to the trailer or container to ensure shipment integrity and prevent tampering.']
  shipment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the shipment assigned by the WMS. Used for tracking and reference across systems and with carriers.']
  shipment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the shipment in the outbound fulfillment workflow. Tracks progression from planning through final delivery. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planned|staged|loading|loaded|departed|in_transit|delivered|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  shipment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shipment based on delivery method and business purpose. DSD indicates Direct Store Delivery channel.']
  source_dc_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the originating distribution center from which goods are being shipped.']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this shipment requires temperature-controlled transportation for product integrity.']
  total_units varchar [note: 'Total quantity of individual sellable units (eaches) included in the shipment across all SKUs.']
  total_volume_cubic_meters decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total cube or volume of the shipment in cubic meters. Critical for trailer utilization and dimensional weight calculations.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total gross weight of the shipment in kilograms including product and packaging. Used for freight rating and load planning.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-provided tracking identifier for real-time shipment visibility and customer self-service tracking.']
  trailer_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the trailer or container unit used to transport the shipment. Critical for load tracking and yard management.']
  wave_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse picking wave that generated the orders included in this shipment. Links shipment to WMS wave planning.']

  Note: 'Outbound shipment record representing the physical dispatch of goods from a DC to a customer, retailer, or downstream DC. Captures shipment number, carrier, trailer/container ID, seal number, dock door, scheduled and actual departure datetime, destination, total weight, total cube, pallet count, carton count, and OTIF status. Integrates with Blue Yonder WMS load planning and SAP SD goods issue. SSOT for DC-level outbound shipment execution distinct from the logistics domain\'s carrier-level shipment tracking.'
}

Table distribution.inventory_position {
  inventory_position_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for inventory_position']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Inventory compliance reporting links each inventory position to its applicable regulatory obligation to track deadlines and status.']
  distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center where the inventory is physically located.']
  distribution_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_storage_location. Business justification: Inventory position records on‑hand quantities at a specific DC location. Linking to distribution_storage_location provides the authoritative location details and removes the generic storage_location_i']
  inventory_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Supports Inventory Ownership Dashboard, assigning a responsible employee for each DC inventory position for accountability and cycle‑count oversight.']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific lot or batch of the SKU. Critical for traceability, quality control, and FEFO inventory rotation.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Provides real‑time visibility of prototype SKU inventory tied to the RD project, essential for launch readiness dashboards.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product SKU for which inventory position is tracked.']
  actual_weight decimal(18,3) [note: 'The actual measured weight of the inventory for catch weight items. Used for billing and compliance when item weight varies.']
  catch_weight_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SKU is a catch weight item requiring actual weight capture at transaction time (true) or standard weight (false).']
  cost_per_unit decimal(18,4) [note: 'The unit cost of the inventory lot. Used for inventory valuation and cost of goods sold calculations. Business confidential financial data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this inventory position record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for inventory valuation (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  days_on_hand varchar [note: 'The number of days the inventory has been on hand since receipt. Calculated as current date minus receipt date. Used for aging analysis.']
  days_to_expiry varchar [note: 'The number of days remaining until the inventory lot expires. Calculated as expiry date minus current date. Critical for FEFO rotation and markdown decisions.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the product lot expires and can no longer be sold or distributed. Critical for FEFO inventory management and regulatory compliance.']
  inventory_condition varchar [note: 'The physical condition classification of the inventory. Used for disposition decisions and channel restrictions.']
  inventory_status varchar [note: 'The current operational status of the inventory position. Determines availability for picking and order fulfillment.']
  last_cycle_count_date date [note: 'The date when this inventory position was last physically counted during a cycle count operation. Used to schedule next count and assess inventory accuracy.']
  last_movement_date date [note: 'The date when inventory was last moved into or out of this storage location. Used for slow-moving inventory identification.']
  last_movement_type varchar [note: 'The type of the last inventory movement transaction that affected this position (receipt, putaway, pick, replenishment, transfer, adjustment, return). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: receipt|putaway|pick|replenishment|transfer|adjustment|return — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this inventory position record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data freshness assessment.']
  license_plate_number varchar [note: 'The warehouse management system license plate number assigned to the handling unit. Used for tracking and automated material handling.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'The alphanumeric lot or batch number assigned during manufacturing. Used for traceability and recall management.']
  manufacture_date date [note: 'The date on which the product lot was manufactured. Used for shelf-life calculations and FEFO rotation.']
  owner_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the ownership model of the inventory (owned by company, consignment from supplier, customer-owned for returns, vendor-managed inventory).']
  pallet_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the pallet or handling unit on which the inventory is stored. Used for warehouse automation and tracking.']
  pick_face_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inventory position is in a primary pick face location (true) or reserve storage (false). Pick face locations are optimized for order picking efficiency.']
  putaway_date date [note: 'The date when the inventory was put away into this storage location after receiving. Used for aging analysis and FEFO compliance.']
  quantity_allocated decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of on-hand inventory that has been allocated to outbound orders or reservations but not yet picked. Reduces available-to-pick quantity.']
  quantity_available decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity available for new order allocation and picking. Calculated as quantity_on_hand minus quantity_allocated minus quantity_quarantine minus quantity_hold.']
  quantity_damaged decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of inventory identified as damaged and not suitable for sale or distribution. Typically awaiting disposal or return to supplier.']
  quantity_hold decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of inventory placed on hold due to quality issues, customer complaints, or pending investigation. Not available for picking.']
  quantity_on_hand decimal(18,3) [note: 'The total physical quantity of the SKU-lot currently present in the storage location. Measured in the SKU\'s base unit of measure.']
  quantity_quarantine decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of inventory placed in quarantine status pending quality inspection or regulatory clearance. Not available for picking.']
  quantity_reserved decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of inventory reserved for specific customers, channels, or promotional programs. Subset of allocated quantity with additional business constraints.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'The date when the inventory lot was received into the distribution center. Used for inventory aging and supplier performance tracking.']
  replenishment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inventory position requires replenishment from reserve to pick face (true) or not (false). Triggers automated replenishment tasks.']
  snapshot_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this inventory position snapshot was captured. Used for point-in-time inventory reporting and trend analysis.']
  storage_zone varchar [note: 'The logical zone within the distribution center where the inventory is stored (e.g., ambient, refrigerated, frozen, hazmat, high-velocity, reserve).']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'The temperature control zone classification for the storage location. Critical for cold chain compliance and product quality.']
  total_inventory_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total financial value of the inventory position calculated as quantity_on_hand multiplied by cost_per_unit. Used for balance sheet reporting. Business confidential financial data.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The base unit of measure in which inventory quantities are tracked (Each, Case, Pallet, Pound, Kilogram, Liter, Gallon). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PL|LB|KG|L|GAL — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  weight_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for actual weight (Pound, Kilogram, Ounce, Gram). Applicable for catch weight items.']

  Note: 'Current on-hand inventory position at the DC-location-SKU-lot level within distribution center walls. Captures storage location, SKU, lot number, manufacture and expiry dates, quantity on hand, allocated quantity, available-to-pick (ATP) quantity, quarantine quantity, inventory status (available, hold, damaged, expired), and last cycle count date. This is the operational working inventory view for DC execution — distinct from the inventory domain\'s network-wide planning position which aggregates across all nodes.'
}

Table distribution.distribution_cycle_count {
  distribution_cycle_count_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the cycle count event record. Primary key for the distribution cycle count entity.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supervisor or manager who approved the cycle count adjustment.']
  distribution_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center where the cycle count was performed.']
  inventory_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific storage location (bin, rack, aisle) within the DC where the count was performed.']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific lot or batch being counted, critical for FEFO/FIFO inventory rotation and traceability.']
  primary_distribution_counter_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse associate who performed the physical count.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product SKU being counted in this cycle count line.']
  abc_classification varchar [note: 'ABC inventory classification of the SKU at the time of count: A (high value/velocity), B (medium), C (low), D (obsolete/slow-moving).']
  adjustment_approved_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the inventory adjustment has been approved by a supervisor or manager.']
  adjustment_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an inventory adjustment is required based on the variance exceeding tolerance thresholds.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the cycle count adjustment was approved by a supervisor or manager.']
  count_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the physical counting activity was completed.']
  count_method varchar [note: 'The method used to perform the physical count: manual (paper-based), RF scanner (handheld device), voice picking (voice-directed), or automated (RFID/sensor).']
  count_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the cycle count event, typically generated by the WMS. Format: CC-YYYYMMDD-NNNN.']
  count_reason_code varchar [note: 'The business reason triggering this cycle count: routine (scheduled ABC cycle), discrepancy (prior variance), audit (compliance), quality (QC hold), recall (product recall), transfer (location move).']
  count_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the physical counting activity began.']
  count_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cycle count event.']
  count_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the cycle count event: scheduled (routine ABC cycle), ad-hoc (unplanned), exception (triggered by discrepancy), blind (counter does not see system quantity), or targeted (specific SKU/lot focus).']
  counted_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The actual physical quantity counted by the warehouse associate during the cycle count event.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this cycle count record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the inventory value variance amount.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The expiration or best-before date of the lot/batch being counted, critical for FEFO inventory rotation.']
  inventory_value_variance_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The financial impact of the variance, calculated as variance_quantity multiplied by the standard cost of the SKU. Expressed in the DC\'s functional currency.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this cycle count record was last updated in the system.']
  recount_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number indicating which recount iteration this represents (1 for first recount, 2 for second, etc.). Null if no recount performed.']
  recount_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a recount is required due to significant variance or quality control procedures.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'The date on which the cycle count was scheduled to be performed.']
  stock_status varchar [note: 'The inventory status of the counted stock: available (unrestricted), hold (QC hold), quarantine (quality review), damaged, expired, or reserved (allocated to orders).']
  system_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The quantity of the SKU/lot recorded in the WMS at the time of the cycle count.']
  tolerance_threshold_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The acceptable variance percentage threshold. Variances within this percentage may not require adjustment.']
  tolerance_threshold_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The acceptable variance quantity threshold defined for this SKU/location combination. Variances within this threshold may not require adjustment.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which quantities are expressed: EA (each), CS (case), PL (pallet), LB (pound), KG (kilogram), L (liter), GAL (gallon). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PL|LB|KG|L|GAL — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  variance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context or explanation for the variance observed during the cycle count.']
  variance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The variance expressed as a percentage of the system quantity, calculated as (variance_quantity / system_quantity) * 100.']
  variance_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The difference between counted quantity and system quantity (counted minus system). Positive indicates overage, negative indicates shortage.']
  variance_reason_code varchar [note: 'Root cause classification for the variance: picking error, receiving error, damage, theft, system error, location error, expiry, or other. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: picking_error|receiving_error|damage|theft|system_error|location_error|expiry|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  wms_transaction_code varchar [note: 'The unique transaction identifier generated by the WMS for this cycle count event, used for audit trail and system reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Cycle count event record capturing scheduled or ad-hoc physical inventory counts at DC locations, including line-level detail for each SKU/lot counted with system vs. actual quantities and variance calculations.'
}

Table distribution.distribution_dsd_route {
  distribution_dsd_route_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the DSD route record. Primary key.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: DSD route planning assigns a carrier to each route; the carrier_id FK enables carrier performance and cost analysis.']
  distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center or depot from which this DSD route originates. Links to the facility master.']
  lane_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.lane. Business justification: DSD route aligns with a transport lane to calculate distance, fuel surcharge, and service level expectations.']
  primary_distribution_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the driver or sales representative permanently or primarily assigned to this route. May be null for unassigned or pool routes.']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales or service territory to which this route is assigned. Used for sales force alignment and performance tracking.']
  average_order_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Historical average order value per stop on this route in local currency. Used for revenue forecasting and route profitability analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this route record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for monetary values associated with this route (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this route configuration was discontinued or superseded. Null for currently active routes. Used for historical route analysis.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this route configuration became active and operational. Used for historical route analysis and change tracking.']
  estimated_drive_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Estimated total driving time for the route in minutes, excluding stop service time. Used for driver shift planning and OTIF performance targets.']
  estimated_service_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Estimated total time spent at customer stops for unloading, merchandising, and order processing in minutes. Combined with drive time for total route duration.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this route record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit trail.']
  last_optimization_date date [note: 'Date when this route was last reviewed and optimized for efficiency. Used to identify routes due for re-evaluation.']
  merchandising_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this route includes merchandising activities (shelf stocking, display setup, POG compliance) in addition to delivery. True if merchandising is part of the service.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or route-specific considerations (e.g., \'Avoid highway during rush hour\', \'Customer prefers morning delivery\').']
  number_of_stops varchar [note: 'Total count of retail store or customer locations included in this route. Used for route capacity planning and driver workload estimation.']
  osa_target_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target OSA percentage for stores on this route, representing the expected in-stock rate at point of sale. Used for DSD execution quality and replenishment effectiveness tracking.']
  otif_target_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target OTIF performance percentage for this route, representing the expected rate of deliveries completed on time and in full. Used for SLA management and driver performance evaluation.']
  planned_departure_time timestamp [note: 'Standard departure time from the DC for this route in HH:MM format (24-hour clock). Used for driver scheduling and DC loading dock coordination.']
  planned_return_time timestamp [note: 'Expected return time to the DC for this route in HH:MM format (24-hour clock). Used for driver shift planning and vehicle turnaround scheduling.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking of this route relative to other routes in the territory or DC, with lower numbers indicating higher priority. Used for resource allocation during capacity constraints.']
  requires_cold_chain boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this route requires temperature-controlled (refrigerated or frozen) transportation for product integrity. True if cold chain is required.']
  requires_hazmat_certification boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the driver assigned to this route must hold hazmat certification due to regulated products (e.g., aerosols, flammables). True if certification is required.']
  route_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the DSD route, used for operational reference and scheduling. Typically alphanumeric code assigned by logistics planning.']
  route_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or description of the DSD route, often including geographic or customer identifiers (e.g., \'Downtown Metro Route A\', \'North Region Convenience Stores\').']
  route_optimization_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated efficiency score for this route based on distance, time, stops, and revenue, typically ranging from 0-100. Higher scores indicate more optimized routes. Used for continuous improvement initiatives.']
  route_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the DSD route. Active routes are in regular service; inactive routes are permanently discontinued; suspended routes are temporarily paused; under_review routes are being optimized; seasonal_closed routes are off-season.']
  route_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the DSD route based on service model: standard (regular scheduled delivery), express (same-day or expedited), bulk (high-volume customers), emergency (unplanned replenishment), seasonal (temporary routes for peak demand).']
  service_day_pattern varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of days of the week this route is scheduled to run (e.g., \'Mon,Wed,Fri\' for a three-day-per-week route). Supports recurring schedule planning.']
  start_location_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the route start point (typically the DC or depot). Used for route optimization and GPS tracking.']
  start_location_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the route start point (typically the DC or depot). Used for route optimization and GPS tracking.']
  total_distance_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total driving distance for the complete route in kilometers, from DC departure through all stops and return. Used for fuel cost estimation and vehicle range planning.']
  vehicle_capacity_required_units varchar [note: 'Minimum vehicle cargo capacity required in standard units (cases, pallets, or cubic meters) to fulfill typical route demand. Used for vehicle assignment.']
  vehicle_type_required varchar [note: 'Type of vehicle required to service this route based on cargo volume, weight, and product requirements (e.g., refrigerated for cold chain products).']

  Note: 'Master record for Direct Store Delivery (DSD) routes defining the sequence of retail store stops served by a DSD driver/vehicle from a DC or satellite depot. Captures route code, route name, assigned DC, territory, day-of-week schedule, number of stops, total route distance, estimated drive time, vehicle type requirement, and active status. Supports DSD execution planning and OSA performance tracking by route.'
}

Table distribution.distribution_dsd_delivery {
  distribution_dsd_delivery_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the DSD delivery transaction. Primary key for the distribution_dsd_delivery product.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the DSD driver who executed this delivery. Links to workforce employee master data.']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retail store account where this delivery was executed. Links to the customer.retail_store master data.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the DSD route this delivery is part of. Links to the route master data defining the planned sequence of store visits.']
  actual_arrival_time timestamp [note: 'The actual timestamp when the driver arrived at the retail store location. Used for On Time In Full (OTIF) performance measurement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this delivery record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and record lifecycle tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this delivery transaction.']
  delivery_exception_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating any exception or issue encountered during the delivery attempt. Used for root cause analysis of delivery failures. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: store_closed|access_denied|incomplete_order|damaged_product|wrong_address|weather_delay|traffic_delay|vehicle_breakdown|none — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  delivery_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes captured by the driver regarding delivery conditions, store feedback, or special circumstances encountered during the visit.']
  delivery_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the DSD delivery transaction. Externally visible delivery document number used for tracking and proof of delivery reference.']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the DSD delivery transaction. Tracks progression from scheduled visit through completion or failure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|in_transit|arrived|in_progress|completed|failed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  delivery_value_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total monetary value of products delivered during this visit, calculated at invoice price. Represents gross delivery value before returns and credits.']
  delivery_window_end timestamp [note: 'The end of the agreed delivery time window with the retail store. Part of Service Level Agreement (SLA) for OTIF measurement.']
  delivery_window_start timestamp [note: 'The beginning of the agreed delivery time window with the retail store. Part of Service Level Agreement (SLA) for OTIF measurement.']
  departure_time timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the driver departed from the retail store after completing delivery and merchandising activities.']
  gps_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate captured at delivery location for route verification and geospatial analytics.']
  gps_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate captured at delivery location for route verification and geospatial analytics.']
  in_full_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the complete ordered quantity was delivered without shortages. Component of OTIF performance measurement.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales invoice document generated for this DSD delivery. Links to financial billing and accounts receivable.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this delivery record was last updated in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and change tracking.']
  merchandising_activity_type varchar [note: 'The type of in-store merchandising activity performed during the delivery visit. Supports retail execution and On Shelf Availability (OSA) objectives.']
  merchandising_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The total time in minutes spent on merchandising activities during the store visit. Used for labor productivity analysis.']
  merchandising_performed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether in-store merchandising activities (shelf stocking, rotation, display setup) were performed during this delivery visit.']
  net_delivery_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The net monetary value of the delivery after subtracting return credits from gross delivery value. Represents actual secondary sales value.']
  on_time_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the delivery arrived within the agreed delivery time window. Component of OTIF performance measurement.']
  otif_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this delivery met On Time In Full (OTIF) performance criteria: arrived within delivery window and delivered complete order quantity.']
  pod_capture_method varchar [note: 'The method used to capture proof of delivery from the store recipient. Electronic signature and photo are preferred for audit trail.']
  pod_photo_url varchar [note: 'URL reference to the photo evidence of delivery captured at the store. Links to document management system for audit purposes.']
  pod_signature_image_url varchar [note: 'URL reference to the stored electronic signature image captured as proof of delivery. Links to document management system.']
  recipient_name varchar [note: 'The name of the store employee who received and acknowledged the delivery. Captured for proof of delivery and accountability.']
  return_credit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total monetary value of product returns credited to the store during this visit. Reduces net delivery value.']
  scheduled_arrival_time timestamp [note: 'The planned arrival timestamp at the retail store based on route optimization and store delivery window requirements.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record that originated this delivery transaction. Typically Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud or mobile DSD application.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this delivery record in the source operational system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  temperature_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether temperature-sensitive products were maintained within required cold chain temperature ranges during delivery. Critical for food safety and quality compliance.']
  total_cases_delivered decimal(12,3) [note: 'The total quantity of product cases delivered to the store during this visit. Aggregate measure across all SKUs (Stock Keeping Units) delivered.']
  total_cases_returned decimal(12,3) [note: 'The total quantity of product cases returned from the store during this visit due to damage, expiration, or other quality issues.']
  total_units_delivered decimal(12,3) [note: 'The total quantity of individual product units delivered to the store during this visit. Aggregate measure across all SKUs delivered.']
  total_units_returned decimal(12,3) [note: 'The total quantity of individual product units returned from the store during this visit due to damage, expiration, or other quality issues.']
  vehicle_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the delivery vehicle used for this DSD delivery. Links to fleet asset master data.']
  visit_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the DSD delivery visit to the retail store occurred. Business event date for reporting and analytics.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a DSD driver\'s delivery execution to a specific retail store on a given route visit. Captures route reference, store/account reference, driver ID, vehicle ID, visit date, arrival and departure timestamps, products delivered by SKU and quantity, products returned/credited, in-store merchandising activities performed, POD (proof of delivery) capture method, and delivery status. Integrates with Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud retail execution and TradeEdge secondary sales visibility.'
}

Table distribution.dsd_delivery_line {
  dsd_delivery_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the DSD delivery line item. Primary key for this product.']
  distribution_dsd_delivery_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent DSD delivery header record. Links this line item to the overall store visit and delivery transaction.']
  distribution_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse or distribution center location from which this product was sourced for delivery.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the DSD driver who delivered this line item. Used for driver performance tracking and accountability.']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trade promotion or promotional campaign associated with this delivery line, if applicable.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Tracks DSD pilot deliveries of new formulations, required for consumer test logistics and performance reporting.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the delivery route on which this line was delivered. Used for route optimization and logistics analysis.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product master record for the SKU delivered or returned on this line.']
  cold_chain_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether cold chain temperature requirements were maintained throughout delivery. True if compliant, False if temperature excursion occurred.']
  cost_of_goods_sold decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard cost of goods sold for the delivered quantity. Used for margin analysis and profitability reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this delivery line record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary amounts on this line (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  delivered_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Actual quantity of the SKU delivered to the store during this visit. Used for secondary sales capture and inventory replenishment tracking.']
  delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this specific line item was delivered or processed at the store. Used for OTIF performance tracking and delivery window compliance.']
  discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total discount or promotional allowance applied to this line. Used for trade promotion settlement and margin analysis.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Product expiration or best-before date. Used for FEFO inventory rotation and shelf-life management in stores.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'Global Trade Item Number (GTIN-8, GTIN-12, GTIN-13, or GTIN-14) for the product. Enables cross-system product identification and retail scanning.']
