  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this delivery line record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the delivery document. Used for ordering and referencing specific line items within the delivery.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this delivery line. Values: DELIVERED (fully delivered), PARTIAL (partially delivered), REJECTED (rejected by store), RETURNED (returned to warehouse), CANCELLED (cancelled before delivery).']
  line_total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value for this delivery line (delivered quantity × unit selling price). Represents secondary sales revenue at store level.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing lot or batch number for the delivered product. Critical for traceability, quality control, and recall management.']
  manufacture_date date [note: 'Date the product was manufactured. Used for shelf-life calculation and quality tracking.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net monetary value for this line after discounts and taxes (line_total_amount - discount_amount + tax_amount). Represents final revenue for this line.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this delivery line. May include special handling instructions, store feedback, or exception details.']
  oos_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the store was out of stock for this SKU at the time of delivery. True if OOS condition detected, False otherwise. Critical for OSA metrics.']
  ordered_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of the SKU originally ordered by the store or planned for delivery. Used for fill-rate and OTIF analysis.']
  planogram_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product was placed according to the store planogram. True if compliant, False if deviation occurred.']
  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why the delivery line was rejected by the store. Values: DAMAGED, WRONG_PRODUCT, QUALITY, UNAUTHORIZED, EXPIRED, OTHER.']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why product was returned from the store. Values: DAMAGED (damaged goods), EXPIRED (past expiry date), OVERSTOCKED (excess inventory), QUALITY (quality issue), RECALL (product recall), UNSOLD (slow-moving), OTHER (other reason). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DAMAGED|EXPIRED|OVERSTOCKED|QUALITY|RECALL|UNSOLD|OTHER — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  return_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free-text description of the return reason. Provides additional context beyond the standardized return reason code.']
  returned_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of the SKU returned from the store during this visit. Includes damaged goods, expired products, and unsold inventory.']
  shelf_placement_confirmed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the DSD driver confirmed proper shelf placement and merchandising of the delivered product. True if confirmed, False otherwise.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total tax amount applicable to this delivery line. Calculated based on jurisdiction-specific tax rules.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Temperature control zone required for this product during storage and delivery. Values: AMBIENT (room temperature), CHILLED (refrigerated), FROZEN (frozen storage).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for quantities on this line. Common values: EA (each), CS (case), PK (pack), BX (box), PL (pallet), DZ (dozen), KG (kilogram), LB (pound), LT (liter), GL (gallon). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PK|BX|PL|DZ|KG|LB|LT|GL — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unit_selling_price decimal(15,4) [note: 'Selling price per unit for this SKU at the time of delivery. Used for revenue calculation and pricing analysis.']
  upc varchar [note: '12-digit Universal Product Code for the product. Standard barcode identifier used in North American retail.']
  vehicle_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the delivery vehicle used to transport this line item. Used for fleet management and cold chain tracking.']

  Note: 'Line-level SKU detail for each product delivered or returned during a DSD store visit. Records SKU code, GTIN, lot number, expiry date, ordered quantity, delivered quantity, returned quantity, return reason code, unit selling price, line total value, and shelf placement confirmation. Enables secondary sales capture, OOS identification, and FEFO compliance tracking at the store-SKU level.'
}

Table distribution.otif_event {
  otif_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the OTIF performance measurement event. Primary key for the OTIF event record.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the transportation carrier responsible for delivery. Used for carrier performance scorecarding and accountability analysis.']
  delivery_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the delivery execution record for Direct Store Delivery (DSD) or carrier delivery. Links to the actual delivery event.']
  distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution center that originated the shipment. Used for DC-level OTIF performance accountability and scorecard reporting.']
  distribution_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the outbound shipment being measured for OTIF performance. Links to the shipment transaction that this OTIF event evaluates.']
  responsible_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Enables OTIF Incident Management report to identify the employee responsible for resolving each OTIF event, meeting internal SLA tracking.']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific retail store location receiving the delivery. Applicable for Direct Store Delivery (DSD) and store-level OTIF tracking.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer trade account receiving the shipment. Used for customer-specific OTIF scorecard and retailer compliance reporting.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'The actual date the shipment was delivered to the customer location. Used to calculate on-time performance variance.']
  actual_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise timestamp when delivery was completed and proof of delivery was captured. Used for time-window OTIF compliance.']
  committed_delivery_date date [note: 'The delivery date committed to the customer per the Service Level Agreement (SLA) or purchase order terms. Baseline for on-time performance measurement.']
  committed_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise delivery date and time committed to the customer, including time window for time-sensitive deliveries. Used for precise OTIF measurement when time windows apply.']
  committed_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The total quantity committed to the customer per the sales order or purchase order. Baseline for in-full performance measurement. Measured in order unit of measure.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this OTIF event record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and record creation tracking.']
  delivered_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The actual quantity delivered to the customer. Used to calculate in-full performance and quantity variance. Measured in order unit of measure.']
  delivery_channel varchar [note: 'The logistics channel used for delivery execution. Values include Direct Store Delivery (DSD), Less Than Truckload (LTL) carrier, Full Truckload (FTL) carrier, parcel, customer pickup, or third-party logistics (3PL).']
  delivery_variance_days varchar [note: 'The number of days variance between committed and actual delivery date. Negative values indicate early delivery, positive values indicate late delivery, zero indicates on-time.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the OTIF measurement is under dispute by the customer or internal stakeholders. True indicates active dispute, False indicates accepted measurement.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why the OTIF measurement is disputed. Captures customer or internal objections to the performance assessment.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of the OTIF event record. Tracks progression from initial measurement through dispute resolution and final closure.']
  failure_category varchar [note: 'The primary root cause category for OTIF failures. Used for operational improvement analysis and accountability assignment. Values include DC pick error, inventory shortage, dock delay, carrier issue, customer refused, weather delay, system error, quality hold, damaged product, address issue, or no failure for successful deliveries. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dc_pick_error|inventory_shortage|dock_delay|carrier_issue|customer_refused|weather_delay|system_error|quality_hold|damaged_product|address_issue|no_failure — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  failure_reason_detail varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the root cause for OTIF failure. Free-text field capturing specific circumstances, corrective actions, and accountability notes.']
  in_full_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the full committed quantity was delivered. True indicates complete delivery, False indicates short shipment or over shipment outside tolerance.']
  in_full_tolerance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The acceptable variance percentage per customer SLA or business policy. Deliveries within this tolerance are considered in-full compliant.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this OTIF event record was last updated. Audit trail for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  measurement_date date [note: 'The date when the OTIF performance was measured and recorded. Used for reporting period assignment and trend analysis.']
  on_time_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the shipment was delivered on or before the committed delivery date. True indicates on-time delivery, False indicates late delivery.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'The customer purchase order or sales order number associated with this shipment. Business identifier for traceability to the originating order.']
  otif_score boolean [note: 'Composite OTIF performance indicator. True only when both on_time_flag and in_full_flag are True. Primary KPI for DC operations accountability and retailer scorecard compliance.']
  penalty_currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the retailer penalty amount. Standard currency codes such as USD, EUR, GBP.']
  pod_signature_captured_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an electronic or physical signature was captured at delivery. True indicates signature obtained, False indicates delivery without signature capture.']
  proof_of_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when proof of delivery was captured, typically via electronic signature, barcode scan, or delivery confirmation. Source of truth for actual delivery time.']
  quantity_uom varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for committed and delivered quantities. Common values include cases, pallets, eaches, or other standard units.']
  quantity_variance decimal(18,3) [note: 'The difference between delivered quantity and committed quantity. Negative values indicate short shipment, positive values indicate over shipment.']
  quantity_variance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage variance between delivered and committed quantity, calculated as (delivered - committed) / committed * 100. Used to assess severity of in-full failures.']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'The organizational function or external party accountable for the OTIF failure. Used for performance accountability and continuous improvement initiatives.']
  retailer_penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The financial penalty or chargeback amount assessed by the retailer for OTIF non-compliance per the customer contract or retailer compliance program. Zero for compliant deliveries.']
  shipment_number varchar [note: 'The warehouse management system shipment number or bill of lading number. External business identifier for the shipment.']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'The service level tier committed to the customer. Defines the delivery speed and OTIF performance expectations per the customer agreement.']

  Note: 'OTIF (On Time In Full) performance measurement record for each outbound shipment or DSD delivery against committed service levels. Captures shipment/delivery reference, customer/account, committed vs actual delivery date, on-time flag, committed vs delivered quantity, in-full flag, composite OTIF score, root cause category for failures (DC pick error, inventory shortage, dock delay, carrier issue), and retailer penalty exposure amount. Primary KPI entity for DC operations accountability and retailer scorecard compliance.'
}

Table distribution.dock_appointment {
  dock_appointment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the dock appointment record. Primary key for the dock appointment entity.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the transportation carrier responsible for this appointment. Links to carrier master data for performance tracking and compliance monitoring.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Allows Dock Scheduling System to capture which employee scheduled the appointment, enabling workload planning and audit of appointment creation.']
  distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution center where this dock appointment is scheduled. Links to the facility managing the dock operations.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Receiving appointments are scheduled per purchase order, enabling the dock team to allocate doors and resources for each inbound PO.']
  actual_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the carrier truck checked in at the distribution center gate. Used to calculate on-time arrival performance and carrier compliance.']
  actual_case_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of cases handled during the appointment. Used for variance analysis and operational performance tracking.']
  actual_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when loading or unloading operations were completed and the truck departed. Used to calculate dock turnaround time and throughput metrics.']
  actual_pallet_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of pallets loaded or unloaded during the appointment. Compared against expected count to identify discrepancies.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when loading or unloading operations began at the dock door. Marks the beginning of active dock utilization.']
  actual_weight_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Actual total weight of goods handled in kilograms. Verified through weigh station or scale integration.']
  appointment_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique appointment reference number used for scheduling and tracking. Externally visible identifier shared with carriers and suppliers.']
  appointment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the dock appointment: scheduled (initial booking), confirmed (carrier acknowledged), checked_in (truck arrived at gate), in_progress (loading/unloading active), completed (finished), cancelled (appointment voided), no_show (carrier missed appointment). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|confirmed|checked_in|in_progress|completed|cancelled|no_show — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  appointment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the dock appointment purpose: inbound receipt (supplier delivery), outbound load (customer shipment), inter-DC transfer (internal movement), cross-dock (direct transfer), returns processing, or emergency delivery.']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'Advanced Shipping Notice identifier sent by supplier prior to shipment arrival. Used to pre-plan receiving operations and validate expected contents.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation for why the appointment was cancelled. Captures business context for cancelled or no-show appointments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this dock appointment record was first created in the system. Audit trail for appointment booking lifecycle.']
  cross_dock_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a cross-dock appointment where goods are transferred directly from inbound to outbound without storage. True for cross-dock operations.']
  detention_minutes varchar [note: 'Total time in minutes that the carrier was detained beyond the scheduled appointment window. Used to calculate detention charges and identify operational bottlenecks.']
  dock_door_number varchar [note: 'Physical dock door identifier assigned to this appointment (e.g., D-101, DOOR-A5). Indicates the specific loading/unloading bay at the distribution center.']
  driver_name varchar [note: 'Name of the driver assigned to this appointment. Used for gate check-in verification and security purposes.']
  driver_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number for the driver. Used for real-time communication regarding delays, gate instructions, or operational issues.']
  dsd_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this appointment is part of a Direct Store Delivery operation where goods bypass the DC and go directly to retail stores. True for DSD shipments.']
  expected_case_count varchar [note: 'Anticipated number of cases to be handled during this appointment. Supports detailed capacity and labor planning for case-level operations.']
  expected_pallet_count varchar [note: 'Anticipated number of pallets to be loaded or unloaded during this appointment. Used for dock capacity planning and labor scheduling.']
  expected_weight_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total expected weight of goods in kilograms for this appointment. Used for equipment planning and safety compliance.']
  hazmat_class varchar [note: 'UN hazard classification code for hazardous materials (e.g., 3 for flammable liquids, 8 for corrosives). Required for HAZMAT compliance and safety protocols.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this appointment involves hazardous materials requiring special handling, documentation, and certified dock doors. True for HAZMAT shipments.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this dock appointment record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change to appointment details or status.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and special instructions for this appointment. May include gate directions, special handling requirements, or operational alerts.']
  otif_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the appointment met On Time In Full performance criteria: arrived within scheduled window and delivered expected quantities. True if OTIF compliant.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority classification for this appointment: standard (routine), high (expedited), urgent (same-day), or emergency (critical). Influences dock door assignment and resource allocation.']
  sales_order_number varchar [note: 'Sales order number associated with outbound load appointments. Links the dock appointment to customer orders being shipped.']
  scheduled_arrival_date date [note: 'Planned date for the carrier to arrive at the dock door. Used for day-level appointment planning and capacity management.']
  scheduled_end_time timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the appointment window closes. Carrier should complete loading/unloading by this time to maintain schedule compliance.']
  scheduled_start_time timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the appointment window begins. Carrier is expected to arrive at or after this time.']
  seal_intact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the security seal was intact upon arrival. True if seal is unbroken, false if compromised. Critical for security and quality control.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Security seal identifier applied to the trailer. Verified at check-in to ensure cargo integrity and prevent tampering.']
  shipment_number varchar [note: 'Outbound shipment identifier for load appointments. References the shipment being loaded onto the carrier truck.']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this appointment involves temperature-sensitive goods requiring cold chain management. True for refrigerated or frozen shipments.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Required temperature zone for goods in this appointment: ambient (room temperature), refrigerated (2-8°C), frozen (-18°C), or deep frozen (below -25°C).']
  trailer_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the trailer or container arriving for this appointment. Used for yard management and asset tracking.']

  Note: 'Scheduled dock door appointment record for inbound or outbound truck arrivals at a DC. Captures appointment date/time window, dock door assignment, appointment type (inbound receipt, outbound load, inter-DC transfer), carrier, trailer number, expected SKUs and quantities, appointment status (scheduled, confirmed, arrived, completed, no-show), and actual arrival/departure timestamps. Supports dock scheduling optimization and carrier compliance tracking.'
}

Table distribution.load_plan {
  load_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the outbound load plan record. Primary key.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the carrier responsible for transporting this load.']
  distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center from which this load is being dispatched.']
  primary_loading_supervisor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse supervisor who verified and approved the completed load before dispatch.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Load planning uses purchase order details to allocate trailer space, ensure correct items are loaded, and meet OTIF targets.']
  wave_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the picking wave that generated the orders included in this load plan. Links load planning to upstream warehouse execution.']
  actual_departure_datetime timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the loaded trailer departed the distribution center, used for OTIF performance measurement.']
  carrier_service_level varchar [note: 'Service level agreement tier for this shipment, determining transit time and handling requirements.']
  case_count varchar [note: 'Total number of cases or cartons included in this load plan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this load plan record was first created in the WMS.']
  dock_door_number varchar [note: 'Dock door location at the distribution center where this load is staged and loaded.']
  dsd_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this load is part of a Direct Store Delivery route, bypassing retailer distribution centers.']
  estimated_freight_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated transportation cost for this load based on carrier rates, distance, and service level.']
  exception_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying any exception or deviation from standard load planning process (e.g., overweight, incomplete orders, equipment failure).']
  exception_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes describing any exceptions, special handling instructions, or deviations from the standard load plan.']
  freight_cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated freight cost.']
  hazmat_class varchar [note: 'DOT hazard class for hazardous materials in this load (e.g., Class 3 Flammable Liquids, Class 8 Corrosives).']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this load contains hazardous materials requiring special handling and DOT placarding.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this load plan record was last updated, tracking changes through the planning and execution lifecycle.']
  load_plan_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the load plan, typically generated by the WMS. Used for operational tracking and communication with carriers and warehouse staff.']
  load_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the load plan, tracking progression from planning through dispatch. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|confirmed|in_progress|loaded|sealed|dispatched|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  load_sequence_strategy varchar [note: 'Strategy used to determine the order in which pallets are loaded into the trailer, typically aligned with delivery stop sequence.']
  load_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the load based on shipment mode and delivery strategy. DSD indicates Direct Store Delivery.']
  loading_completion_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time when physical loading of the trailer was completed and verified.']
  loading_start_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time when physical loading of the trailer began at the dock door.']
  order_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct outbound orders consolidated into this load plan.']
  otif_target_delivery_datetime timestamp [note: 'Target delivery date and time committed to the customer, used for OTIF performance measurement.']
  pallet_configuration varchar [note: 'Description of how pallets are arranged and stacked within the trailer, supporting optimal space utilization and load stability.']
  pallet_count varchar [note: 'Total number of pallets included in this load plan.']
  planned_departure_datetime timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time when the loaded trailer is planned to depart the distribution center.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Security seal number applied to the trailer after loading to ensure tamper-evidence during transit.']
  stop_count varchar [note: 'Total number of delivery stops planned for this load, used for multi-stop route optimization.']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this load requires temperature-controlled transportation (refrigerated or frozen).']
  temperature_range_max_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable temperature in Celsius for temperature-controlled loads during transit.']
  temperature_range_min_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable temperature in Celsius for temperature-controlled loads during transit.']
  total_volume_cubic_meters decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total cubic volume of the load in cubic meters, used for trailer utilization analysis and capacity planning.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total gross weight of the load in kilograms, including product, packaging, and pallets. Used for carrier billing and DOT compliance.']
  trailer_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the trailer or vehicle assigned to this load. May be a license plate, fleet number, or container number.']
  trailer_type varchar [note: 'Type of trailer or vehicle used for this load, determining capacity and product compatibility.']
  trailer_utilization_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of trailer capacity utilized by this load, calculated based on weight, volume, or pallet positions.']

  Note: 'Outbound load plan record defining the assignment of outbound orders and shipments to a specific trailer/vehicle for dispatch from a DC. Captures load plan number, DC, carrier, trailer ID, planned departure datetime, total weight, total cube, pallet configuration, stop sequence, and load plan status (draft, confirmed, loaded, dispatched). Integrates with Blue Yonder WMS load planning and transportation management.'
}

Table distribution.returns_receipt {
  returns_receipt_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the returns receipt record. Primary key.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the carrier that transported the returned goods to the distribution center.']
  distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center receiving the returned goods.']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the lot or batch number of the returned goods, critical for traceability and recall management.']
  primary_returns_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the quality assurance employee who performed the condition inspection of the returned goods.']
  product_recall_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_recall. Business justification: Return processing for recalled items requires linking the return receipt to the product recall record for quarantine and reporting.']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promotion_event. Business justification: Promotion Return Analysis links returned items to the originating promotion event to evaluate promotion effectiveness and refund liability.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Return processing matches returned goods to the original purchase order for credit issuance and inventory reconciliation.']
  recall_event_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the recall event if this return is recall-driven, linking to the recall management record.']
  receiving_clerk_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse employee who received and logged the returned goods at the distribution center.']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the retail store location from which the goods were returned, if applicable.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) being returned.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer account or retail account that initiated the return (retailer, DSD customer, or consumer account).']
  case_count varchar [note: 'Number of cases included in the return receipt.']
  condition_assessment varchar [note: 'Quality inspection result indicating the physical and salability condition of the returned goods: resalable, damaged, expired, defective, or contaminated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this return receipt record was first created in the system.']
  credit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary credit amount issued to the customer for the returned goods.']
  credit_memo_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the credit memo issued to the customer account for the returned goods.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the credit amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  destroy_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of returned units that must be destroyed due to damage, contamination, or recall requirements.']
  disposition_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the final disposition decision was executed (resale, destroy, rework, etc.).']
  disposition_decision varchar [note: 'Final decision on how the returned goods will be handled: resale to inventory, destroy, rework/repackage, donate, or return to vendor.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the returned product lot, critical for FEFO (First Expired First Out) inventory rotation and recall management.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'Global Trade Item Number (GTIN) or UPC (Universal Product Code) of the returned product, used for global product identification.']
  inspection_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the quality and condition inspection of returned goods was completed.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this return receipt record was last updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing additional details, exceptions, or special handling instructions for the return receipt.']
  pallet_count varchar [note: 'Number of pallets included in the return receipt.']
  quantity_returned decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total quantity of units returned in this receipt.']
  recall_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the return is part of a product recall event (True) or not (False).']
  receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the returned goods were physically received at the distribution center. Principal business event timestamp.']
  resalable_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of returned units deemed resalable and eligible to be returned to inventory.']
  return_authorization_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known return authorization number issued to approve the return. Also known as RA# or RMA number.']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for the return: damaged goods, expired product, overstock, product recall, defective item, or wrong item shipped.']
  return_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free-text explanation of the return reason, providing additional context beyond the reason code.']
  return_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the return receipt: pending arrival, received at DC, inspected, processed for disposition, closed, or cancelled.']
  return_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the return source: retailer return, DSD (Direct Store Delivery) customer return, consumer return, recall-driven return, or internal transfer.']
  rework_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of returned units that will be reworked, repackaged, or refurbished for resale.']
  scheduled_receipt_date date [note: 'Planned date for the return to arrive at the distribution center, as communicated in the return authorization.']
  temperature_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the returned goods were within acceptable temperature range during transit (True) or not (False).']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the returned goods require temperature-controlled handling (True) or not (False).']
  temperature_reading_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature reading in Celsius recorded upon receipt of temperature-sensitive returned goods.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier tracking number for the return shipment, used to trace the inbound return delivery.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the returned quantity: EA (each), CS (case), PL (pallet), KG (kilogram), LB (pound), L (liter), GAL (gallon). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PL|KG|LB|L|GAL — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  wms_transaction_code varchar [note: 'Transaction identifier from the Blue Yonder WMS system that processed this return receipt.']

  Note: 'Inbound returns processing record capturing goods returned from retailers, DSD customers, or consumers to a DC. Records return authorization number (RA#), originating customer/account, return reason code (damaged, expired, overstock, recall, consumer return), SKU, lot, quantity returned, condition assessment (resalable, destroy, rework), disposition decision, and credit memo reference. Supports reverse logistics, FEFO expiry management, and product recall execution.'
}

Table distribution.distribution_offset_allocation {
  distribution_offset_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the offset_allocation association']
  carbon_offset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to carbon_offset']
  distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution_facility']
  allocation_amount decimal [note: 'Quantity of carbon offset (tCO2e) allocated to the facility']
  allocation_date date [note: 'Date the offset amount was allocated to the facility']

  Note: 'This association captures the allocation of purchased carbon offset credits to distribution facilities. Each record links one distribution_facility to one carbon_offset and records the amount of credit allocated and the allocation date.. Existence Justification: Carbon offsets are purchased centrally and then allocated to individual distribution facilities for ESG reporting. Each offset credit can be split among multiple facilities, and each facility can receive allocations from multiple offset credits over time, requiring a many-to-many association with allocation amount and date.'
}

Table distribution.wave {
  wave_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for wave']
  previous_wave_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on wave (previous_wave_id)']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse where the wave is executed.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Real completion time when the wave finished.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Real start time when picking began.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by planners about the wave.']
  distribution_center_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the distribution center associated with the wave.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Primary equipment used for the wave (e.g., conveyor, robotic).']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the wave is marked as critical for service level commitments.']
  on_time_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the wave completed within the scheduled window.']
  picker_count varchar [note: 'Number of warehouse staff assigned to execute the wave.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority of the wave; lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the wave record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the wave record.']
  scheduled_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned completion time for the wave.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned start time for wave execution.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the wave.']
  total_items varchar [note: 'Sum of all line‑item quantities across orders in the wave.']
  total_orders varchar [note: 'Count of distinct orders included in the wave.']
  total_quantity varchar [note: 'Aggregate quantity of units to be picked in the wave.']
  wave_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the wave used by warehouse staff.']
  wave_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable code assigned to the wave for operational tracking.']
  wave_release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the wave was released to the floor for execution.']
  wave_strategy varchar [note: 'Strategic approach applied to the wave (e.g., zone picking).']
  wave_type varchar [note: 'Category of the wave indicating the operational purpose (e.g., pick, replenish).']

  Note: 'Master reference table for wave. Referenced by wave_id.'
}

Table procurement.supplier {
  supplier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the supplier record. Primary key for the supplier entity.']
  esg_commitment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.esg_commitment. Business justification: Required for ESG compliance reporting: procurement tracks each supplier\'s signed ESG commitment to meet corporate sustainability policies.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the supplier\'s registered business location or headquarters.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for additional location details such as suite, building, or floor number.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality name where the supplier\'s business location is registered.']
  contract_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current master supply agreement or contract with the supplier expires and requires renewal or renegotiation.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code identifying the country where the supplier is legally registered and operates.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the supplier record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code indicating the preferred currency for transactions and invoicing with this supplier.']
  diversity_classification varchar [note: 'Classification indicating if the supplier qualifies as a diverse supplier (e.g., minority-owned, women-owned, veteran-owned, small business).']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Nine-digit unique identifier assigned by Dun & Bradstreet to identify the supplier business entity globally.']
  edi_capable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier has EDI capability for automated exchange of purchase orders, invoices, and shipping notices.']
  edi_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique EDI identifier or interchange ID used for electronic document exchange with the supplier.']
  fsc_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier holds FSC certification for sustainable forestry and paper-based materials sourcing.']
  gln varchar [note: 'GS1 Global Location Number uniquely identifying the supplier\'s legal entity or physical location within the supply chain.']
  gmp_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier is certified for Good Manufacturing Practices, critical for consumer goods quality and safety compliance.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'Standard Incoterms code defining the responsibilities, costs, and risks between buyer and supplier for international shipments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  iso_9001_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier maintains ISO 9001 quality management system certification.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent supplier audit or assessment conducted for quality, compliance, or sustainability verification.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of days required from purchase order placement to goods receipt for this supplier.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the supplier record was last modified or updated in the system.']
  moq_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Minimum order quantity required by the supplier for purchase orders to be accepted.']
  moq_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the minimum order quantity (e.g., EA, KG, LB, L, cases, pallets).']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date when the supplier was officially onboarded and approved for procurement activities.']
  onboarding_status varchar [note: 'Current stage of the supplier onboarding process tracking progress from initial registration through final approval.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Preferred method of payment for settling invoices with the supplier.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms negotiated with the supplier defining the payment schedule and conditions (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30).']
  performance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall performance score for the supplier based on quality, delivery, cost, and service metrics, typically on a 0-100 scale.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the supplier\'s business address used for mail delivery and logistics coordination.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary supplier contact used for procurement communications and order coordination.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary business contact person at the supplier organization responsible for account management.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Telephone number of the primary supplier contact for urgent procurement matters and order inquiries.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk assessment classification for the supplier based on financial stability, geopolitical factors, compliance history, and supply continuity.']
  rspo_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier holds RSPO certification for sustainable palm oil sourcing practices.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or regional administrative division for the supplier\'s business address.']
  supplier_code varchar [note: 'Internal business identifier or code assigned to the supplier for procurement and ERP system reference.']
  supplier_name varchar [note: 'The full legal name of the supplier or vendor organization as registered with governing authorities.']
  supplier_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the supplier relationship indicating whether the supplier is eligible for procurement activities.']
  supplier_tier varchar [note: 'Strategic classification of the supplier indicating their relationship status and procurement priority level with the organization.']
  supplier_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the supplier based on the category of goods or services provided to the business.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued tax identification number for the supplier (e.g., EIN in USA, VAT number in EU) used for tax reporting and compliance.']
  vmi_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier is eligible and capable of participating in Vendor Managed Inventory programs.']

  Note: 'Master record for all suppliers, vendors, and contract manufacturers providing raw materials, packaging, and indirect goods. Captures supplier legal entity details, GS1/DUNS identifiers, tax registration, payment terms, MOQ terms, lead times, incoterms, preferred currency, supplier tier classification (strategic/preferred/approved/conditional), VMI eligibility flag, EDI capability, sustainability certifications (FSC, RSPO), and onboarding status. SSOT for supplier identity across the procurement domain.'
}

Table procurement.supplier_site {
  supplier_site_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the supplier site. Primary key for this entity.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent supplier organization that owns or operates this site.']
  supply_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.network_node. Business justification: VMI programs map each supplier site to a specific distribution node to manage inventory ownership and replenishment.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the supplier site location.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for building, suite, or unit information.']
  audit_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Most recent audit score for this site, typically on a 0-100 scale, reflecting compliance and quality performance.']
  capacity_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the site capacity metric (e.g., units_per_month, pallets, square_meters, kilograms_per_day).']
  certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the primary site certifications expire and require renewal. Used for compliance monitoring and supplier audit scheduling.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the supplier site is located.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code identifying the country where the site is located. Critical for country of origin tracking and regulatory compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this supplier site record was first created in the system.']
  edi_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this site is capable of exchanging purchase orders, invoices, and shipping notices via EDI.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this supplier site was deactivated or ceased operations. Null for currently active sites.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this supplier site became active and available for procurement operations.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email contact for the supplier site.']
  fax_number varchar [note: 'Fax number for the supplier site, if applicable.']
  fsc_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this site holds FSC certification for sustainable forestry and paper sourcing.']
  gln varchar [note: 'GS1 Global Location Number uniquely identifying this physical site location for supply chain traceability and EDI transactions.']
  gmp_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this site is certified for Good Manufacturing Practices, critical for pharmaceutical and food-grade materials.']
  iso_14001_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this site holds ISO 14001 Environmental Management System certification.']
  iso_22716_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this site holds ISO 22716 Good Manufacturing Practices for Cosmetics certification.']
  iso_9001_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this site holds ISO 9001 Quality Management System certification.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent quality or compliance audit conducted at this site.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this supplier site record was last updated.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the supplier site, used for logistics optimization and distance calculations.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard lead time in days from this site to the buyer\'s receiving location, used for procurement planning and MRP calculations.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the supplier site, used for logistics optimization and distance calculations.']
  moq decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum order quantity required by this site for purchase orders.']
  moq_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the minimum order quantity (e.g., units, cases, pallets, kilograms).']
  next_audit_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next quality or compliance audit at this site.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the supplier site.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the supplier site address.']
  preferred_site_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this site is designated as a preferred sourcing location based on performance, cost, or strategic criteria.']
  rspo_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this site is certified by RSPO for sustainable palm oil sourcing.']
  site_capacity decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum production or storage capacity of the site, measured in units relevant to the site type (e.g., units per month for manufacturing, pallets for warehouse).']
  site_code varchar [note: 'Internal or supplier-assigned unique code identifying this site location.']
  site_manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the site manager or primary contact person responsible for this location.']
  site_name varchar [note: 'Business name or designation of the supplier site facility.']
  site_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the supplier site in the procurement system.']
  site_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the site based on its primary business function.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region of the supplier site.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the site location, used for scheduling deliveries and coordinating communications.']
  vmi_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this site participates in a VMI program where the supplier manages inventory levels at the buyer\'s location.']

  Note: 'Physical manufacturing, warehouse, or office locations associated with a supplier. Captures site address, site type (manufacturing/warehouse/office/port), GS1 GLN, country of origin, site capacity, certifications held at site level (ISO 9001, ISO 22716, GMP), site-level lead time, and active status. Supports multi-site supplier relationships and origin traceability for regulatory compliance.'
}

Table procurement.supplier_contract {
  supplier_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the supplier contract record. Primary key.']
  contract_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Contract Management process assigns a responsible employee; linking contract_owner to employee enables ownership tracking and compliance reporting.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Brand Contract Management: contracts are associated with the brand they cover, needed for legal compliance and brand‑level cost analysis.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier party with whom this contract is negotiated.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the contract was formally approved by authorized signatories and became legally binding.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or role who provided final approval for this contract.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract automatically renews at expiry unless explicitly terminated by either party.']
  contract_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URL to the signed contract document stored in document management system (e.g., Veeva Vault, SharePoint).']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the contract, used in procurement systems and supplier communications.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the contract: draft for initial creation, pending approval for review workflow, active for in-force agreements, suspended for temporary holds, expired for naturally ended contracts, terminated for early cancellation, or renewed for extended agreements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|active|suspended|expired|terminated|renewed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contract structure: framework agreement for multi-material commitments, blanket order for recurring purchases, spot purchase for one-time buys, long-term agreement for strategic partnerships, consignment for supplier-owned inventory, or vendor managed inventory (VMI) for supplier-controlled replenishment.']
  contract_value_total decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value committed under this contract across all line items and periods, used for spend tracking and budget allocation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contract record was first created in the procurement system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this contract (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  delivery_terms varchar [note: 'Agreed delivery conditions including lead times, delivery windows, and logistics requirements.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the contract becomes legally binding and pricing terms take effect.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the contract naturally ends unless renewed or terminated earlier. Nullable for open-ended agreements.']
  governing_law varchar [note: 'Legal jurisdiction and governing law under which the contract is interpreted and enforced (e.g., State of Delaware, English Law).']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms defining the responsibilities of buyer and supplier for shipping, insurance, and risk transfer (e.g., FOB, CIF, DDP). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contract record was last updated, used for change tracking and audit purposes.']
  maximum_order_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Maximum quantity allowed per purchase order line under this contract, used to manage supplier capacity and volume commitments.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Minimum quantity that must be ordered per purchase order line to qualify for contract pricing, expressed in base unit of measure.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified this contract record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional contract details, special terms, or internal comments not captured in structured fields.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Agreed payment conditions such as net 30, net 60, or early payment discount terms (e.g., 2/10 net 30).']
  penalty_clause varchar [note: 'Terms defining financial penalties for supplier non-compliance, late delivery, quality failures, or other contractual breaches.']
  price_escalation_formula varchar [note: 'Mathematical formula or index reference used to adjust prices over time, such as CPI-based adjustments or commodity index linkage.']
  pricing_mechanism varchar [note: 'Method by which prices are determined: fixed price for static rates, cost plus for markup on supplier costs, market indexed for commodity-linked pricing, volume tiered for quantity-based discounts, or rebate based for retrospective incentives.']
  purchasing_group varchar [note: 'The buyer or procurement team assigned to manage this contract, responsible for execution and supplier relationship.']
  purchasing_organization varchar [note: 'The organizational unit responsible for negotiating and managing this contract, typically aligned with regional or divisional procurement teams.']
  quality_specification varchar [note: 'Reference to quality standards, specifications, or inspection criteria that supplied materials must meet (e.g., GMP, ISO 9001, internal spec codes).']
  quantity_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Base unit of measure for quantity commitments (e.g., KG, LTR, EA, MT) aligned with material master data.']
  rebate_terms varchar [note: 'Description of volume-based or performance-based rebate conditions, including thresholds, percentages, and settlement periods.']
  renewal_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days prior to expiry that either party must provide notice to prevent auto-renewal or initiate renewal negotiations.']
  sds_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether supplier must provide Safety Data Sheets for hazardous materials covered by this contract, per OSHA and EPA regulations.']
  sustainability_certification varchar [note: 'Required sustainability certifications for materials under this contract, such as FSC (Forest Stewardship Council) for paper/packaging or RSPO (Roundtable on Sustainable Palm Oil) for palm oil derivatives.']
  target_quantity_total decimal(15,3) [note: 'Aggregate quantity commitment across all materials and line items over the contract period, used for volume-based pricing and rebate calculations.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Actual date when the contract was terminated early, either by mutual agreement or due to breach. Null if contract expired naturally or is still active.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for early termination, such as supplier performance issues, strategic sourcing changes, or mutual agreement.']

  Note: 'Procurement contracts and framework agreements negotiated with suppliers for raw materials, packaging, and indirect goods. Captures contract header (number, type, effective/expiry dates, total value, currency, pricing mechanism, rebate terms, penalty clauses, auto-renewal, governing law) and contract line items (material/SKU, agreed unit price, price UoM, target quantity, MOQ, max order quantity, price validity period, escalation formula, line status). Tracks contract lifecycle from draft through active to expired/terminated. Enables granular price and volume commitment tracking at the material level. Owns all line-level detail as embedded attributes.'
}

Table procurement.contract_line {
  contract_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the contract line item. Primary key for this entity.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Contract line items are charged to specific GL accounts for cost tracking; required for contract‑based accounting.']
  manufacturing_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant or distribution center that will receive the material. Used for multi-site procurement and logistics planning.']
  sds_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Safety Data Sheet document for hazardous materials. Required for chemical and hazardous substance procurement to ensure workplace safety and regulatory compliance.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material, raw material, packaging component, or indirect good being procured under this contract line. Links to the product master for specifications and attributes.']
  supplier_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent supplier contract that contains this line item. Links this line to the master agreement governing terms, supplier, and overall contract lifecycle.']
  blocked_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the contract line is blocked or inactive. Provides business context for procurement restrictions (e.g., quality issue, supplier non-compliance, price dispute).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this contract line record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  cumulative_invoiced_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total invoiced value for all purchase orders released against this contract line to date. Used for spend tracking and budget management.']
  cumulative_ordered_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total quantity ordered against this contract line to date. Used for tracking contract utilization and remaining commitment.']
  cumulative_received_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total quantity received against this contract line to date. Used for tracking actual fulfillment and supplier performance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the unit price. Defines the monetary denomination for all pricing on this contract line.']
  incoterm_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms code defining the division of costs and risks between buyer and supplier for this line item. Governs shipping, insurance, and transfer of ownership responsibilities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  incoterm_location varchar [note: 'Named place or port associated with the Incoterm. Specifies the geographic point where cost and risk transfer occurs (e.g., supplier factory, destination port, buyer warehouse).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this contract line record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking and audit trail.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard procurement lead time in days from purchase order placement to expected goods receipt. Used for Material Requirements Planning (MRP) and demand planning calculations.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the parent contract. Used for ordering and referencing specific line items in procurement documents and communications.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contract line. Controls whether purchase orders can be created against this line and tracks the lines operational state.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the material or service being procured. Provides human-readable context for the line item beyond the material code.']
  material_group_code varchar [note: 'Classification code grouping similar materials for procurement analysis and reporting. Enables spend analysis by category (e.g., raw materials, packaging, indirect goods).']
  maximum_order_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Maximum quantity that can be ordered per purchase order release against this contract line. May reflect supplier capacity constraints or buyer inventory limits.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Minimum quantity that must be ordered per purchase order release against this contract line. Enforces supplier-defined minimum batch sizes for economic production or logistics.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing additional terms, special instructions, or contextual information for this contract line. Used for operational guidance and exception handling.']
  order_quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for minimum and maximum order quantities. Defines the measurement basis for order quantity constraints.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the agreed payment terms for this contract line (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30). Defines the payment due date calculation and any early payment discounts.']
  price_escalation_formula varchar [note: 'Formula or reference to index-based pricing adjustment mechanism. Defines how unit price will be adjusted over time based on commodity indices, inflation, or other agreed factors.']
  price_unit varchar [note: 'Quantity of the material to which the unit price applies. For example, if price is per 100 units, this field would be 100. Used in conjunction with unit_price to calculate effective per-unit cost.']
  price_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the price unit. Defines the measurement basis for pricing (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, L for liter, M for meter).']
  purchasing_group_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the procurement team or buyer responsible for managing this contract line. Used for workload distribution and accountability tracking.']
  quality_inspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether incoming goods for this contract line must undergo quality inspection before acceptance. Enforces Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) and quality assurance requirements.']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific storage location within the plant where the material will be received and stored. Used for warehouse management and inventory tracking.']
  sustainability_certification varchar [note: 'Sustainability or ethical sourcing certification required for the material on this contract line. Supports compliance with corporate sustainability commitments and regulatory requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FSC|RSPO|PEFC|Rainforest Alliance|Fair Trade|Organic|None — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  target_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Planned or target quantity of material to be procured over the contract validity period. Used for volume commitment tracking and supplier capacity planning.']
  target_quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the target quantity. Defines how the target volume is measured (e.g., EA, KG, L, M).']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax classification code determining the applicable tax rate and tax jurisdiction for this contract line. Used for tax calculation and compliance reporting.']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Agreed price per unit of measure for the material or service. This is the base price before any discounts, surcharges, or escalations are applied.']
  validity_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the pricing and terms on this contract line expire. Purchase orders can reference this line only on or before this date. Nullable for open-ended agreements.']
  validity_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the pricing and terms on this contract line become effective. Purchase orders can reference this line only on or after this date.']

  Note: 'Individual line items within a supplier contract specifying material, packaging component, or service being procured. Captures material/SKU reference, agreed unit price, price unit of measure, target quantity, MOQ, maximum order quantity, price validity period, price escalation formula, and contract line status. Enables granular price and volume commitment tracking at the material level.'
}

Table procurement.rfq {
  rfq_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the request for quotation record. Primary key.']
  awarded_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier selected and awarded the business based on evaluation criteria. Null if RFQ not yet awarded or cancelled.']
  manufacturing_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant or distribution center where the materials will be delivered. Relevant for logistics planning and supplier proximity evaluation.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: RFQ‑Project Allocation process tracks which R&D project each RFQ supports for ingredient sourcing.']
  rfq_created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the RFQ record. Audit trail for accountability and process compliance.']
  rfq_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the procurement professional responsible for managing this RFQ, coordinating supplier communications, and evaluating responses.']
  rfq_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified the RFQ record. Audit trail for change accountability.']
  rfq_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the business unit, plant, or department that initiated the sourcing request and will consume the procured materials or services.']
  rfq_purchasing_organization_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the procurement organization responsible for executing the sourcing event and negotiating with suppliers.']
  supplier_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the purchase contract or purchase order created as a result of this RFQ. Links sourcing event to execution.']
  supply_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.network_node. Business justification: RFQ evaluation includes the target node to assess lead‑time, capacity, and cost for the intended receipt location.']
  award_date date [note: 'Date when the supplier selection decision was finalized and communicated. Marks the transition from evaluation to contracting phase.']
  award_justification varchar [note: 'Business rationale for the supplier selection decision. Documents evaluation results, scoring, and strategic considerations for audit and compliance purposes.']
  commodity_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the materials or services being sourced (e.g., raw materials, packaging, indirect goods, services). Aligns with procurement category management structure.']
  commodity_subcategory varchar [note: 'Detailed classification within the commodity category (e.g., fragrances, surfactants, bottles, labels, MRO supplies). Enables granular spend analysis and category strategy execution.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the RFQ record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the estimated value and supplier quotations (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY).']
  estimated_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total monetary value of the procurement. Used for budget planning, approval routing, and supplier qualification thresholds.']
  estimated_volume decimal(18,3) [note: 'Anticipated quantity or volume of materials or services to be procured. Used by suppliers to calculate economies of scale and pricing.']
  evaluation_criteria_delivery_weight decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage weight assigned to delivery performance (lead time, OTIF history, flexibility) in the supplier evaluation scorecard.']
  evaluation_criteria_price_weight decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage weight assigned to price in the supplier evaluation scorecard. Part of multi-criteria decision analysis for supplier selection.']
  evaluation_criteria_quality_weight decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage weight assigned to quality factors (certifications, defect rates, compliance) in the supplier evaluation scorecard.']
  evaluation_criteria_sustainability_weight decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage weight assigned to sustainability factors (FSC certification, RSPO compliance, carbon footprint, ethical sourcing) in the supplier evaluation scorecard.']
  incoterm varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms defining the responsibilities of buyer and supplier for transportation, insurance, and risk transfer (e.g., EXW, FCA, DDP, FOB, CIF). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date when the RFQ was officially issued to suppliers. Marks the start of the supplier response period.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the RFQ record was last updated. Audit trail for change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional instructions, special requirements, or internal comments related to the RFQ. Used for context that does not fit structured fields.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms requested from suppliers (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30). Used to evaluate total cost of ownership including cash flow impact.']
  requires_quality_certification boolean [note: 'Indicates whether suppliers must provide quality management system certifications (e.g., ISO 9001, GMP, ISO 22716 for cosmetics).']
  requires_sds boolean [note: 'Indicates whether suppliers must provide Safety Data Sheets for the materials being sourced. Mandatory for chemicals, fragrances, and hazardous substances.']
  requires_sustainability_declaration boolean [note: 'Indicates whether suppliers must provide sustainability certifications or declarations (e.g., FSC for paper/packaging, RSPO for palm oil derivatives, conflict minerals disclosure).']
  rfq_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the RFQ, externally visible and used in supplier communications and procurement documentation.']
  rfq_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the RFQ. Draft indicates preparation phase, issued means sent to suppliers, open indicates active response period, closed means submission deadline passed, awarded indicates supplier selected, cancelled means RFQ withdrawn.']
  rfq_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the RFQ based on procurement strategy and urgency. Standard for routine sourcing, spot buy for one-time purchases, framework for long-term agreements, emergency for urgent needs, global sourcing for multi-region procurement, strategic for high-value category sourcing.']
  submission_deadline timestamp [note: 'Date and time by which suppliers must submit their quotations. No responses accepted after this timestamp.']
  supplier_count_invited varchar [note: 'Number of suppliers invited to participate in this RFQ. Used to assess competitive intensity and sourcing strategy effectiveness.']
  supplier_count_responded varchar [note: 'Number of suppliers who submitted quotations by the deadline. Used to calculate response rate and assess market interest.']
  technical_specification_document_url varchar [note: 'Reference to the detailed technical specification document shared with suppliers. Contains material requirements, quality standards, packaging specifications, and testing protocols.']
  volume_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the estimated volume (e.g., kilograms, liters, each, meters, square meters, cubic meters, tons, pounds, gallons, feet, yards). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: KG|L|EA|M|M2|M3|TON|LB|GAL|FT|YD — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Request for Quotation issued to one or more suppliers for raw materials, packaging, or indirect goods. Captures RFQ header (number, type, issue date, submission deadline, commodity category, estimated volume, evaluation criteria weights, status) and all supplier responses/quotations (responding supplier, quoted unit price, lead time, MOQ, validity period, payment terms, sustainability declarations, response status including submitted/accepted/rejected/awarded). Enables competitive sourcing, multi-supplier bid comparison, and sourcing decision documentation. Owns response-level detail as embedded attributes.'
}

Table procurement.rfq_response {
  rfq_response_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the RFQ response record. Primary key.']
  rfq_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent RFQ for which this response was submitted.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier who submitted this quotation response.']
  award_date date [note: 'Date when the RFQ was awarded to this supplier.']
  awarded_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this RFQ response was awarded the business.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the supplier contact person for this response.']
  contact_person_name varchar [note: 'Name of the supplier contact person who submitted the response.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the supplier contact person for this response.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the RFQ response record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the quoted price.']
  delivery_location varchar [note: 'Specified delivery location or plant code for the quoted materials.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount offered by the supplier on the quoted price.']
  evaluation_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Procurement teams evaluation score for this response based on defined criteria.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'Incoterms code defining the delivery and risk transfer terms. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the RFQ response record was last updated.']
  moq decimal(15,3) [note: 'Minimum order quantity required by the supplier for this quotation.']
  moq_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the minimum order quantity (e.g., KG, L, EA, MT).']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms offered by the supplier (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30).']
  quality_certification varchar [note: 'Quality management certifications held by the supplier (e.g., ISO 9001, GMP, ISO 22716).']
  quoted_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from order placement to delivery as quoted by the supplier.']
  quoted_unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Unit price quoted by the supplier for the requested material or service.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for rejecting the RFQ response if not awarded.']
  response_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments provided by the supplier with the quotation response.']
  response_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the RFQ response, typically assigned by the supplier or procurement system.']
  response_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the RFQ response in the procurement workflow.']
  sample_available_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier can provide a sample for evaluation.']
  sample_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days required to provide a sample if available.']
  sds_provided_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier has provided a Safety Data Sheet with the response.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the supplier submitted the RFQ response.']
  sustainability_certification varchar [note: 'Sustainability certifications declared by the supplier (e.g., FSC, RSPO, ISO 14001).']
  technical_specification_compliance varchar [note: 'Suppliers declared compliance level with the technical specifications in the RFQ.']
  total_quoted_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount quoted for the entire RFQ line item or package.']
  validity_end_date date [note: 'Date until which the quoted price and terms remain valid.']
  validity_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the quoted price and terms become valid.']
  warranty_period_months varchar [note: 'Warranty period offered by the supplier in months.']

  Note: 'Supplier quotation submitted in response to an RFQ. Captures responding supplier, quoted unit price, currency, quoted lead time, MOQ, validity period, payment terms offered, sustainability declarations, sample availability, and response status (submitted/accepted/rejected/awarded). Enables multi-supplier bid comparison and sourcing decision documentation.'
}

Table procurement.purchase_requisition {
  purchase_requisition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the purchase requisition record. Primary key.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the requisition at the current or final approval level.']
  inventory_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific storage location within the plant where materials should be delivered.']
