  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this qualification record was last updated. Audit trail for record changes throughout the qualification lifecycle.']
  major_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of major non-conformances identified during the audit. Major findings represent significant quality system gaps that require corrective action but may allow conditional qualification.']
  minor_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of minor non-conformances or observations identified during the audit. Minor findings represent opportunities for improvement that do not prevent qualification.']
  next_qualification_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic re-qualification or audit. Typically set based on qualification type and risk rating, commonly 1-3 years from current qualification.']
  observation_count varchar [note: 'Number of general observations or recommendations noted during the audit that are not formal non-conformances but represent best practice suggestions.']
  overall_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score representing the overall qualification assessment result, typically on a 0-100 scale. Used for supplier performance benchmarking and comparison.']
  qualification_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and comments from the auditor regarding the qualification assessment, including strengths, concerns, special conditions, or context for the qualification decision.']
  qualification_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the qualification event. Externally visible reference number used in audit documentation and supplier communications.']
  qualification_result varchar [note: 'Overall outcome of the qualification assessment. Qualified indicates full approval, conditional indicates approval with corrective actions required, failed indicates supplier did not meet requirements, pending indicates assessment incomplete, not assessed indicates qualification was cancelled or not performed.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the qualification process. Tracks the workflow state from scheduling through completion.']
  qualification_type varchar [note: 'Type of qualification assessment being performed. Initial for new supplier onboarding, periodic for scheduled re-assessments, for-cause for triggered audits due to quality issues, re-qualification for suppliers returning after suspension, pre-qualification for preliminary assessment, special for ad-hoc evaluations.']
  qualification_valid_from_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the qualification status. Date from which the supplier is considered qualified to supply materials or services.']
  qualification_valid_until_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the qualification status. Date after which the qualification is no longer valid and re-qualification is required. Null for indefinite qualifications pending periodic review.']
  regulatory_compliance_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier demonstrated compliance with applicable regulatory requirements such as FDA, EPA, REACH, or other jurisdiction-specific regulations relevant to the materials or services being qualified.']
  sds_documentation_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier provided complete and compliant Safety Data Sheets for all chemical materials and ingredients. Critical for raw material and packaging suppliers.']
  sustainability_criteria_met_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier met sustainability and responsible sourcing criteria during qualification, including certifications such as FSC, RSPO, or environmental management practices.']

  Note: 'Supplier qualification and audit record documenting the formal assessment of a supplier\'s capability, quality systems, and compliance before onboarding or re-qualification. Captures qualification type (initial/periodic/for-cause), audit date, audit scope, auditor name, audit findings count by severity (critical/major/minor), overall qualification result (qualified/conditional/failed), corrective action required flag, and next qualification due date. Supports GMP, ISO 9001, and regulatory compliance requirements.'
}

Table procurement.procurement_vmi_agreement {
  procurement_vmi_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the VMI agreement record.']
  distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the distribution facility in the VMI agreement']
  procurement_vmi_agreement2_id varchar [note: 'Primary key for the vmi_agreement association']
  responsible_buyer_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier who manages inventory under this VMI agreement.']
  supplier_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific supplier site responsible for VMI replenishment under this agreement.']
  agreement_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name for the VMI agreement, typically indicating the supplier, material category, and managed location.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the VMI agreement, used in procurement documents and supplier communications.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the VMI agreement based on the category of materials managed (raw materials, packaging, indirect goods, MRO, or finished goods).']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the VMI agreement automatically renews at the end of its term unless either party provides termination notice.']
  consignment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether inventory under this VMI agreement is held on consignment, where the supplier retains ownership until consumption by Consumer Goods.']
  contract_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the master procurement contract or purchase agreement that governs the commercial terms underlying this VMI arrangement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this VMI agreement record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code used for pricing and payment under this VMI agreement.']
  edi_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether EDI is used for automated exchange of VMI inventory data, replenishment orders, and consumption reports between Consumer Goods and the supplier.']
  edi_message_types varchar [note: 'The specific EDI message types used for VMI transactions (e.g., 846 Inventory Inquiry, 852 Product Activity Data, 850 Purchase Order).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when the VMI agreement expires or is scheduled to terminate, after which the supplier is no longer responsible for inventory management.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when the VMI agreement becomes active and the supplier begins managing inventory at the specified location.']
  inventory_uom varchar [note: 'The unit of measure used for inventory levels in this VMI agreement (e.g., kilograms, liters, pallets, cases, each).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this VMI agreement record was last updated or modified.']
  managed_location_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the Consumer Goods plant, warehouse, or distribution center where the supplier manages inventory under this VMI agreement.']
  managed_location_name varchar [note: 'Name of the Consumer Goods facility where VMI inventory is managed by the supplier.']
  material_scope varchar [note: 'Description of the materials, SKUs, or product categories covered under this VMI agreement, defining what the supplier is responsible for replenishing.']
  max_inventory_level decimal(18,3) [note: 'The maximum inventory quantity allowed at the managed location, defining the upper limit for supplier replenishment to avoid overstocking.']
  min_inventory_level decimal(18,3) [note: 'The minimum inventory quantity that must be maintained at the managed location, triggering replenishment when stock falls below this threshold.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the VMI agreement, including any unique operational requirements or exceptions.']
  ownership_transfer_trigger varchar [note: 'The event that triggers transfer of inventory ownership from the supplier to Consumer Goods (e.g., goods receipt, consumption, withdrawal, invoice receipt, or time-based).']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'The agreed payment terms for VMI inventory, specifying when payment is due after ownership transfer (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, upon consumption).']
  performance_review_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which Consumer Goods and the supplier conduct formal performance reviews of the VMI agreement against SLA targets.']
  procurement_vmi_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the VMI agreement indicating whether it is in draft, actively managing inventory, suspended, terminated, or expired.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'The number of days advance notice required to terminate or modify the VMI agreement before its renewal date.']
  replenishment_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'The number of days required for the supplier to replenish inventory from order placement to delivery at the managed location.']
  replenishment_model varchar [note: 'The inventory replenishment strategy used by the supplier under this VMI agreement (min/max levels, forecast-driven, consumption-based, kanban, or continuous replenishment).']
  review_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which the supplier reviews inventory levels and triggers replenishment orders under this VMI agreement.']
  sla_fill_rate_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The target fill rate percentage the supplier must achieve under this VMI agreement, representing the percentage of demand that must be met from available inventory.']
  sla_otif_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The target OTIF percentage the supplier must achieve for VMI replenishments, measuring the percentage of deliveries that arrive on time and in full quantity.']
  sla_stockout_tolerance_days varchar [note: 'The maximum number of days per period that stockouts are tolerated under this VMI agreement before SLA penalties apply.']
  supplier_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary supplier contact for VMI operations.']
  supplier_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary supplier contact responsible for VMI operations and replenishment under this agreement.']
  supplier_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary supplier contact for VMI operations and issue escalation.']
  target_inventory_level decimal(18,3) [note: 'The optimal inventory quantity the supplier should maintain at the managed location, balancing availability and carrying costs.']
  termination_date date [note: 'The actual date when the VMI agreement was terminated, if applicable.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'The reason for termination if the VMI agreement status is terminated, providing context for the end of the supplier-managed inventory arrangement.']

  Note: 'Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) agreement defining the terms under which a supplier manages and replenishes inventory of raw materials or packaging at a Consumer Goods plant or warehouse. Captures VMI agreement number, supplier, material scope, managed location, min/max inventory levels, replenishment trigger (min stock/forecast-driven), review frequency, VMI start and end dates, consignment flag, ownership transfer trigger, and VMI performance SLA. Supports lean inventory and supply continuity strategies.'
}

Table procurement.price_condition {
  price_condition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the price condition record. Primary key for the price condition entity.']
  manufacturing_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant or facility where this price condition is applicable.']
  rfq_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the RFQ process that resulted in this price condition. Null for non-RFQ pricing.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material or service for which this price condition applies.']
  supplier_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier contract under which this price condition is established. Null for non-contract pricing.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier providing the material or service under this price condition.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which this price condition was formally approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the person who approved this price condition.']
  approved_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price condition has been formally approved for use in purchase orders (True/False).']
  calculation_type varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the condition value (fixed amount, percentage, quantity-based scale, value-based scale).']
  condition_class varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the condition (price, discount, surcharge, freight, tax) for grouping and reporting purposes.']
  condition_record_number varchar [note: 'The unique business identifier for this price condition record in the source procurement system.']
  condition_sequence varchar [note: 'The order in which this condition is applied within the pricing procedure (lower numbers applied first).']
  condition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price condition (active, inactive, pending approval, expired, superseded by newer condition).']
  condition_type varchar [note: 'The category of pricing element this condition represents (base price, freight, surcharge, discount, tax, rebate, duty, handling, insurance, packaging). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: base_price|freight|surcharge|discount|tax|rebate|duty|handling|insurance|packaging — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  condition_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the condition value (e.g., per kilogram, per piece, per liter, percentage).']
  condition_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The numeric value of the price condition (price per unit, discount percentage, surcharge amount, etc.).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this price condition record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the condition value (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'The Incoterms rule applicable to this price condition, defining delivery and cost responsibilities (e.g., FOB, CIF, DDP).']
  incoterms_location varchar [note: 'The specific location or port associated with the Incoterms rule (e.g., FOB Shanghai, CIF New York).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this price condition record was last updated or modified.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The minimum order quantity required for this price condition to be applicable.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the user who last modified this price condition record.']
  moq_uom varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the minimum order quantity (e.g., EA, KG, L).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding this price condition, including special terms, negotiation context, or usage instructions.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'The payment terms code associated with this price condition (e.g., Net 30, 2/10 Net 30).']
  per_unit_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The quantity basis for the condition value (e.g., price per 100 units, per 1000 kg).']
  per_unit_uom varchar [note: 'The unit of measure corresponding to the per unit quantity (e.g., EA, KG, L).']
  price_determination_rule varchar [note: 'The business rule or formula used to determine the final price when multiple conditions apply (e.g., lowest price, weighted average, last price).']
  price_source varchar [note: 'The origin or method by which this price condition was established (contract, RFQ, supplier catalog, negotiated, spot purchase, market index).']
  pricing_procedure varchar [note: 'The pricing procedure schema that determines the sequence and calculation logic for applying this condition.']
  purchasing_group varchar [note: 'The buyer group or team responsible for managing this price condition and supplier relationship.']
  purchasing_organization varchar [note: 'The organizational unit responsible for procurement activities under this price condition.']
  scale_basis varchar [note: 'The basis for tiered pricing scales (none for flat pricing, quantity, value, weight, volume for scale-based pricing).']
  scale_quantity_from decimal(15,3) [note: 'The minimum quantity threshold for this price scale tier. Null for non-scaled pricing.']
  scale_quantity_to decimal(15,3) [note: 'The maximum quantity threshold for this price scale tier. Null for open-ended tiers.']
  scale_uom varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for scale quantity thresholds (e.g., EA, KG, L).']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'The tax classification code applicable to this price condition for tax calculation purposes.']
  tax_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'The geographic tax jurisdiction applicable to this price condition (country, state, or local tax authority).']
  validity_end_date date [note: 'The date after which this price condition is no longer valid. Null for open-ended conditions.']
  validity_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this price condition becomes effective and can be applied to purchase orders.']

  Note: 'Procurement price conditions and pricing records maintained for materials and services sourced from suppliers, including base prices, surcharges, freight conditions, and discounts. Captures condition type (base price/freight/surcharge/discount/tax), supplier, material, plant, validity start and end dates, condition value, condition unit, scale quantity breaks, and price determination sequence. Enables accurate PO pricing, COGS calculation, and price variance analysis.'
}

Table procurement.delivery_schedule {
  delivery_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the delivery schedule line. Primary key for this entity.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Delivery schedule assigns a carrier to each scheduled delivery, enabling carrier performance tracking and OTIF measurement.']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material (raw material, packaging component, or indirect good) being scheduled for delivery.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent purchase order or scheduling agreement to which this delivery schedule line belongs.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Project Delivery Planning schedules deliveries based on the timeline of the associated R&D project.']
  sds_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.sds. Business justification: Delivery schedules include the SDS ID to ensure carriers handle hazardous materials according to regulations.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier responsible for delivering the scheduled material.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'The actual date when the material was received at the plant, captured from goods receipt transactions.']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'Advanced Shipping Notice number sent by the supplier via EDI or portal, providing advance notification of this delivery.']
  atp_relevant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this schedule line is considered in Available to Promise (ATP) calculations for downstream order promising.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer batch or lot number for the material being delivered, critical for traceability and quality management.']
  blocking_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why goods receipt is blocked for this schedule line, if applicable.']
  confirmation_status varchar [note: 'Status indicating whether the supplier has confirmed, rejected, or revised this schedule line.']
  confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the supplier confirmed this delivery schedule line.']
  confirmed_delivery_date date [note: 'The delivery date confirmed by the supplier, which may differ from the scheduled date based on supplier capacity or logistics constraints.']
  confirmed_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of material confirmed by the supplier for delivery. May differ from scheduled quantity based on supplier capacity or constraints.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this delivery schedule line was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for any monetary values associated with this delivery schedule line.']
  delivery_instruction varchar [note: 'Special handling or delivery instructions for this schedule line (e.g., dock requirements, unloading equipment, temperature control).']
  delivery_note_number varchar [note: 'Supplier-provided delivery note or packing slip number accompanying this shipment.']
  delivery_priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to this delivery schedule line, used for expediting and resource allocation decisions.']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this delivery schedule line indicating progress from scheduling through receipt. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|confirmed|in_transit|partially_received|fully_received|cancelled|delayed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  delivery_tolerance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Acceptable over-delivery or under-delivery tolerance as a percentage of scheduled quantity, per contract terms.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration or best-before date of the material batch being delivered, critical for shelf-life management in consumer goods.']
  goods_receipt_blocked_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether goods receipt is blocked for this schedule line due to quality, compliance, or commercial issues.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'Three-letter Incoterms code defining the responsibilities, costs, and risks between buyer and supplier for this delivery (e.g., FOB, CIF, DDP).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this delivery schedule line was last updated in the system.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between order placement and scheduled delivery for this schedule line.']
  mrp_element_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this schedule line is an active MRP element used in material requirements planning calculations.']
  outstanding_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The remaining quantity yet to be received, calculated as confirmed quantity minus received quantity.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Four-character code identifying the receiving manufacturing plant or distribution center for this delivery.']
  quality_inspection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this delivery requires quality inspection before goods receipt can be completed.']
  received_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The actual quantity of material received against this schedule line through goods receipt transactions.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'The delivery date requested by the purchasing organization or production planning, which may differ from the scheduled date based on MRP or DRP requirements.']
  schedule_line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the purchase order identifying this specific delivery schedule. Used for ordering and referencing individual schedule lines.']
  scheduled_delivery_date date [note: 'The originally planned delivery date for this schedule line as specified in the purchase order or scheduling agreement.']
  scheduled_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of material originally scheduled for delivery on this schedule line as per the purchase order or scheduling agreement.']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'Four-character code identifying the specific storage location within the plant where the material will be received.']
  total_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of this schedule line, calculated as confirmed quantity multiplied by unit price.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Shipment tracking number provided by the carrier for monitoring delivery progress.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'The primary mode of transportation used or planned for delivering this schedule line.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for all quantities on this schedule line (e.g., KG for kilograms, EA for each, L for liters).']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Price per unit of measure for the material on this schedule line, as per the purchase order or contract.']

  Note: 'Scheduled delivery lines (schedule lines) against a purchase order or scheduling agreement specifying planned delivery dates and quantities for raw materials and packaging. Captures schedule line number, planned delivery date, confirmed delivery date, scheduled quantity, confirmed quantity, delivery status (scheduled/confirmed/partially received/complete), and transport mode. Supports MRP, DRP, and production scheduling by providing confirmed inbound supply commitments.'
}

Table procurement.po_confirmation {
  po_confirmation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the purchase order confirmation record. Primary key for the po_confirmation product.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original purchase order that this confirmation acknowledges. Links to the purchase order header.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier providing the confirmation. Links to the supplier master data.']
  buyer_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the buyer approved or rejected the confirmation. Null if approval is not required or still pending.']
  buyer_approval_notes varchar [note: 'Notes or comments entered by the buyer during the approval or rejection process. Captures buyer feedback on variances or special conditions.']
  buyer_approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the confirmation requires explicit buyer approval before proceeding. True when there are significant variances, substitutions, or price changes that need buyer review.']
  buyer_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of buyer approval for confirmations that require review. Tracks whether the buyer has accepted or rejected the supplier\'s confirmed terms.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the logistics carrier or freight forwarder that the supplier will use for shipment. Provided in confirmation for shipment tracking and coordination.']
  confirmation_date date [note: 'Date when the supplier issued the confirmation acknowledgement. Represents the business date of confirmation, not the system receipt timestamp.']
  confirmation_method varchar [note: 'Method or channel through which the supplier submitted the confirmation. EDI 855 is the standard electronic acknowledgement, but portal, email, and manual methods are also tracked.']
  confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Unique confirmation number assigned by the supplier to this order acknowledgement. Used for supplier reference and tracking.']
  confirmation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the supplier confirmation indicating whether the order or line item was accepted, partially accepted, or rejected by the supplier.']
  confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the confirmation was received and recorded in the procurement system. Used for tracking confirmation lead time and system audit.']
  confirmed_delivery_date date [note: 'Delivery date confirmed by the supplier. May differ from requested date due to supplier capacity, lead time constraints, or material availability. Critical for OTIF tracking and supply risk management.']
  confirmed_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity confirmed by the supplier for delivery. May differ from requested quantity in cases of partial acceptance, substitution, or supplier capacity constraints.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this confirmation record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the unit price and total amount. Examples: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, CNY.']
  delivery_date_variance_days varchar [note: 'Number of days difference between confirmed delivery date and requested delivery date. Positive values indicate delay, negative values indicate early delivery commitment.']
  delivery_location_code varchar [note: 'Specific delivery location or dock within the plant where goods should be delivered. May be a warehouse, storage location, or receiving dock identifier.']
  edi_transaction_number varchar [note: 'Unique EDI transaction identifier for confirmations received via EDI 855. Null for non-EDI confirmations. Used for EDI audit and reconciliation.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'Incoterms code defining the delivery terms and transfer of risk between buyer and supplier. Examples: EXW, FOB, CIF, DDP. Confirmed by supplier in acknowledgement.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this confirmation record was last updated. Tracks changes to confirmation status, buyer approval, or other fields throughout the confirmation lifecycle.']
  material_code varchar [note: 'Internal material code or SKU for the item being confirmed. Links to material master data for product identification.']
  otif_risk_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this confirmation represents a risk to OTIF performance. True when confirmed delivery date is later than requested or confirmed quantity is less than requested, signaling potential supply disruption.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the receiving plant or facility for the confirmed delivery. Used for logistics planning and inventory allocation.']
  po_line_item_number varchar [note: 'Line item number within the purchase order that this confirmation applies to. Enables line-level confirmation tracking.']
  promised_ship_date date [note: 'Date when the supplier commits to ship the goods. Used for in-transit tracking and logistics planning.']
  quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the requested and confirmed quantities. Examples include EA (each), KG (kilogram), LB (pound), L (liter), CS (case), PLT (pallet).']
  quantity_variance decimal(18,3) [note: 'Difference between confirmed quantity and requested quantity (confirmed minus requested). Positive values indicate over-confirmation, negative values indicate under-confirmation or rejection.']
  quantity_variance_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage variance between confirmed and requested quantity, calculated as (quantity_variance / requested_quantity) * 100. Used for supplier performance tracking and OTIF analysis.']
  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for partial or full rejection of the purchase order line. Populated when confirmation_status is rejected or partially_accepted. Examples: capacity_constraint, material_unavailable, price_dispute, discontinued_product.']
  rejection_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed text description of the rejection reason provided by the supplier. Provides additional context beyond the standardized rejection reason code.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Original delivery date requested in the purchase order. Used as baseline for on-time delivery tracking.']
  requested_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Original quantity requested in the purchase order for this line item. Used as baseline for variance calculation.']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Additional instructions or notes from the supplier regarding the confirmation, delivery requirements, handling instructions, or other special conditions.']
  substitute_material_code varchar [note: 'Material code of the substitute product proposed by the supplier. Populated only when substitution_flag is True. Requires buyer approval before acceptance.']
  substitution_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier is proposing a substitute material or product in place of the originally requested item. True if substitution is proposed, False otherwise.']
  supplier_material_code varchar [note: 'Supplier\'s material code or part number for the item. Used for cross-reference and supplier catalog mapping.']
  supplier_reference_number varchar [note: 'Supplier\'s internal reference number for this order or line item. May be a sales order number, production order number, or other supplier-side tracking identifier.']
  total_line_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount for this confirmation line, calculated as confirmed_quantity * unit_price. Used for financial reconciliation and invoice matching.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier tracking number or waybill number for the shipment. May be provided at confirmation time for pre-arranged shipments or updated later when goods are shipped.']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Confirmed unit price for the material as acknowledged by the supplier. Should match the purchase order price unless price changes are negotiated.']

  Note: 'Supplier order acknowledgement and confirmation record for a purchase order, capturing the supplier\'s confirmed delivery date, confirmed quantity, any partial acceptance or substitution, and confirmation method (EDI 855/portal/email). Captures confirmation number, confirmation date, confirmed delivery date, confirmed quantity per line, variance from PO quantity, supplier reference number, and confirmation status. Enables OTIF tracking and proactive supply risk management.'
}

Table procurement.procurement_vmi_agreement2 {
  procurement_vmi_agreement2_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for procurement_vmi_agreement2']

  Note: 'Represents a VMI agreement between a supplier and a distribution facility, capturing inventory targets and SLA metrics that are managed as a contract entity.. Existence Justification: Suppliers and distribution facilities enter Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) agreements where each supplier can serve multiple facilities and each facility can work with multiple suppliers. The agreement records inventory targets and service‑level metrics, which are actively created, maintained, and queried by both procurement and distribution teams.'
}

Table procurement.service_entry_sheet_line {
  service_entry_sheet_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for service_entry_sheet_line']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the contract under which the service is provided.']
  parent_service_entry_sheet_line_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on service_entry_sheet_line (parent_service_entry_sheet_line_id)']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the purchase order linked to this service line.']
  service_entry_sheet_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent service entry sheet (header).']
  service_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the service or activity recorded on this line.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier providing the service.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the line was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who approved the service entry line.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the line record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Discount amount applied to this line.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the service line becomes effective.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the service line expires or is no longer valid.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount before taxes and discounts (quantity * unit_price).']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Invoice number associated with the service line.']
  is_vmi boolean [note: 'Indicates if the service is part of a Vendor Managed Inventory program.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequence number of the line within the service entry sheet.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the line.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount after tax and discount.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes for the line.']
  quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Quantity of the service units delivered.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Date the service was received or acknowledged.']
  service_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the service performed.']
  sustainability_certification varchar [note: 'Sustainability certification applicable to the service.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax amount applied to this line.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity.']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Price per unit of the service before taxes and discounts.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the line record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for service_entry_sheet_line. Referenced by service_entry_sheet_line_id.'
}

Table procurement.service_entry_sheet {
  service_entry_sheet_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for service_entry_sheet']
  corrected_service_entry_sheet_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on service_entry_sheet (corrected_service_entry_sheet_id)']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the user who approved the service entry sheet.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the service entry sheet was approved.']
  attachment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether supporting documents are attached to the record.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier contract governing the service.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost center to which the service expense is charged.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the monetary values.']
  due_date date [note: 'Payment due date for the service invoice.']
  expense_account varchar [note: 'General ledger expense account used for posting the service cost.']
  is_sustainable boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the service complies with sustainability policies.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net monetary amount after tax and any discounts.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Negotiated payment terms for the supplier (e.g., Net 30).']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date the service entry sheet was posted to the financial system.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order linked to this service entry.']
  quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Measured quantity of the service (e.g., hours, cubic meters).']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Date the service entry sheet was received by the receiving department.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the service entry sheet record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the service entry sheet record.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or notes about the service entry.']
  service_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the service (e.g., Maintenance, Consulting).']
  service_date date [note: 'Date on which the service was performed.']
  service_entry_sheet_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier or number assigned to the service entry sheet.']
  service_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the service was performed.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Specific type of service performed.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the service entry sheet.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier providing the service.']
  supplier_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the supplier.']
  supplier_performance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score reflecting supplier performance for this service.']
  sustainability_certification varchar [note: 'Certification indicating sustainable sourcing (FSC, RSPO, or None).']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component applied to the service total.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax classification code applicable to the service.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross monetary amount of the service before taxes and discounts.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit used for the quantity (e.g., hour, m3).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who initially created the service entry sheet.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for service_entry_sheet. Referenced by service_entry_sheet_id.'
}

Table procurement.service {
  service_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for service']
  parent_service_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on service (parent_service_id)']
  compliance_document_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the compliance or safety document linked to the service.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the contract governing the service terms.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency for price_per_unit.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the service offering.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the service becomes valid for use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the service ceases to be valid (null if open‑ended).']
  external_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier used in external ERP or supplier systems.']
  is_sustainable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service meets sustainability criteria.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User or system that performed the most recent update.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Typical number of days from order to service delivery.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the service offered.']
  price_per_unit decimal(12,2) [note: 'Base price for one unit of the service.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk classification for the service (e.g., operational, safety).']
  service_code varchar [note: 'External code or SKU used to reference the service in contracts and catalogs.']
  service_level_agreement varchar [note: 'Reference to the SLA document or summary text.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Category of service (e.g., logistics, maintenance).']
  sla_penalty_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty applied if SLA target is missed.']
  sla_target_hours varchar [note: 'Maximum response or resolution time promised in the SLA (hours).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the service.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary supplier providing this service.']
  sustainability_certification varchar [note: 'Environmental certification associated with the service.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit used to price or bill the service.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the service record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system that created the service record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for service. Referenced by service_id.'
}

Table promotion.trade_promotion {
  trade_promotion_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the trade promotion program. Primary key for the trade promotion entity.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product category covered by this trade promotion. Links to the product category master entity.']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Trade promotion approval requires linking to the specific compliance obligation that governs the promoted product.']
  esg_commitment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.esg_commitment. Business justification: Promotion planning aligns each trade promotion with a specific ESG commitment for sustainability KPI reporting and regulatory disclosure.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the brand participating in this trade promotion. Links to the brand master entity.']
  owner_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the business user responsible for planning, executing, and analyzing this trade promotion. Typically a trade marketing manager or category manager. Links to the user or employee master entity.']
  primary_trade_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the business user who approved this trade promotion. Links to the user or employee master entity.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Promotion planning requires linking each trade promotion to its originating R&D project for budget alignment and launch reporting.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: REQUIRED: Supplier‑funded trade promotions need a link for budgeting and compliance; experts expect a sponsor supplier on each promotion.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the retail trade account (customer) participating in this promotion. Links to the trade account master entity.']
  accrual_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total accrued trade spend liability for this promotion, representing funds reserved or committed but not yet paid to the retailer.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the trade promotion was formally approved by authorized business stakeholders and authorized for execution.']
  authorized_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total authorized trade spend budget allocated for this promotion, including all funding mechanisms (off-invoice, scan-back, display allowances, etc.). Expressed in the company\'s reporting currency.']
  baseline_volume_units decimal(15,2) [note: 'Expected sales volume in units without the promotion, used as the baseline for calculating incremental lift and ROI.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Retail channel classification where this promotion is executed. Grocery (supermarkets), mass merchandiser (Walmart/Target), drug (pharmacy chains), convenience (c-stores), club (warehouse clubs), ecommerce (online retail).']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where this promotion is executed.']
  coupon_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this promotion includes manufacturer or retailer coupons (print, digital, or mobile). True if coupons are part of the promotion, false otherwise.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this trade promotion record was first created in the system. Represents the business event time of promotion planning initiation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this promotion record.']
  deduction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total deductions taken by the retailer against invoices for this promotion. Used for reconciliation and settlement tracking.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount offered to the retailer or consumer as part of this promotion. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 15.00 for 15% off).']
  display_type varchar [note: 'Type of in-store merchandising display associated with this promotion. End-cap (end of aisle), free-standing (floor display), shelf-talker (shelf signage), pallet (bulk display), cooler (refrigerated display), none (no special display).']
  end_date date [note: 'The date when the trade promotion concludes and promotional pricing/terms expire at retail.']
  expected_roi_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected return on investment for this promotion, calculated as (incremental profit / trade spend) * 100. Used for pre-event planning and approval.']
  feature_ad_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this promotion includes feature advertising in retailer circulars, flyers, or digital media. True if featured, false otherwise.']
  funding_type varchar [note: 'Mechanism by which trade funds are provided to the retailer. Off-invoice (deducted at purchase), scan-back (reimbursed based on POS sales), bill-back (invoiced after event), lump-sum (fixed payment), accrual (reserved funds).']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic coverage of the trade promotion. National (entire country), regional (multi-state or territory), local (city or metro area), store-specific (individual retail locations).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this trade promotion record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit trail purposes.']
  maximum_purchase_quantity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of units a consumer can purchase at the promotional price. Null if no maximum limit applies.']
  minimum_purchase_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum number of units a consumer must purchase to qualify for the promotional offer. Null if no minimum applies.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional business context, special terms, execution notes, or post-event observations related to this trade promotion.']
  pricing_strategy varchar [note: 'Retailer pricing strategy context for this promotion. Hi-Lo (high-low promotional pricing with deep discounts), EDLP (everyday low price with minimal promotions), hybrid (combination approach).']
  promotion_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the trade promotion program for business user identification and reporting purposes.']
  promotion_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the trade promotion program, typically generated by the TPM system. Format: TPM-YYYYNNNN where YYYY is year and NNNN is sequence.']
  promotion_objective varchar [note: 'Primary business objective driving the trade promotion strategy. Volume growth (increase unit sales), market share gain (capture competitor share), new product launch (trial generation), competitive defense (respond to competitor activity), inventory clearance (reduce excess stock), seasonal event (holiday/event-driven).']
  promotion_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the trade promotion. Planned (in design), approved (authorized for execution), active (currently running), closed (completed with results finalized), cancelled (terminated before completion), on-hold (temporarily suspended).']
  promotion_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the trade promotion mechanism. Temporary price reduction (Hi-Lo), feature ad (retailer circular), display (end-cap/shelf), BOGO (buy-one-get-one), multi-buy (quantity discount), scan-back (post-sale rebate). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: temporary_price_reduction|feature_ad|display|bogo|multi_buy|scan_back|off_invoice|coupon|loyalty_reward|edlp|slotting_allowance|markdown_allowance — promote to reference product]']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Status of financial settlement and reconciliation for trade promotion deductions and accruals. Pending (awaiting settlement), in-progress (reconciliation underway), settled (fully reconciled and paid), disputed (discrepancies under review).']
  start_date date [note: 'The date when the trade promotion becomes active and promotional pricing/terms take effect at retail.']
  target_volume_units decimal(15,2) [note: 'Target sales volume in units expected to be achieved during the promotion period, including baseline and incremental lift.']
  tpm_system_reference_code varchar [note: 'External reference identifier from the source Trade Promotion Management system (e.g., Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud TPM module). Used for system integration and traceability.']

  Note: 'Core master entity for trade promotion programs. Captures the full definition of a trade promotion including name, type (Hi-Lo, EDLP, display, coupon, scan-back, off-invoice), promotional period, owning brand/category, status (planned, active, closed, cancelled), total authorized trade spend budget, promotion objectives, promotion type classification (temporary price reduction, feature ad, display, BOGO, multi-buy, scan-back, coupon, loyalty reward), applicable channels, and integration reference to Salesforce TPM. Serves as the SSOT for all trade promotion definitions and the parent entity for all promotion events, spend allocations, and post-event analyses.'
}

Table promotion.promotion_event {
  promotion_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the promotion event. Primary key for this entity.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Required for integrated ROI reporting: each trade promotion event is executed as part of a marketing campaign, enabling campaign‑level performance analysis.']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.capa. Business justification: Promotion event scheduling must consider any open CAPA for the product; linking ensures corrective actions are addressed before promotional activities.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for promotion spend accounting reports linking each event to the responsible cost center for budgeting and variance analysis.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who approved this promotional event for execution.']
  esg_commitment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.esg_commitment. Business justification: Event‑level sustainability reports require linking each promotion event to the ESG commitment it supports for impact tracking.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Enables profit‑center level reporting of promotion performance and ROI, a standard finance requirement.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Promotion events are scheduled against a specific product launch R&D project to ensure timing and KPI consistency.']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific retail store, distribution center, or ship-to location where the promotional event is executed.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: REQUIRED: Promotion events are often sponsored by a specific supplier; linking enables event‑level spend tracking and audit.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retailer or distributor account participating in this promotional event.']
  trade_promotion_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent trade promotion program under which this event is executed. A single program may have multiple events across different retailers or time windows.']
  accrual_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The financial accrual amount reserved for this promotional event, representing the estimated liability for trade promotion obligations.']
  actual_trade_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The realized trade spend for this promotional event, representing the total investment actually incurred by the manufacturer.']
  actual_volume_units decimal(18,2) [note: 'The realized sales volume in units achieved during the promotional event, used for post-event analysis and lift measurement.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date when this promotional event was approved for execution by the authorized business stakeholder.']
  baseline_volume_units decimal(18,2) [note: 'The expected sales volume in units without promotional activity, used as the baseline for calculating promotional lift.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'The date when this promotional event was cancelled, if applicable.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Textual explanation for why this promotional event was cancelled, if applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this promotional event record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this promotional event record.']
  deduction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total deductions claimed by the retailer against invoices for this promotional event, representing retailer-initiated trade spend recovery.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date when the promotional event concludes execution at retail or distribution level.']
  event_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the promotional event, including objectives, mechanics, and special conditions.']
  event_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the promotional event for business user identification and reporting.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the promotion event, used for external communication and reporting.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the promotional event. Planned: event scheduled but not yet approved; Approved: event approved for execution; In Execution: event currently active; Completed: event finished; Cancelled: event terminated before completion.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the promotional event mechanism. Feature Ad: product featured in retailer circular; Display: in-store display placement; Temporary Price Reduction (TPR): short-term price discount; BOGO: Buy One Get One offer; Scan-Back: post-sale rebate based on scanned sales; Coupon: manufacturer or retailer coupon redemption.']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'The primary source of funding for this promotional event. Manufacturer: fully funded by manufacturer; Retailer: fully funded by retailer; Cooperative: jointly funded by both parties.']
  geography_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the geographic market where the event is executed.']
  gmroi_ratio decimal(8,2) [note: 'The gross margin return on investment for this promotional event, calculated as Gross Margin / Average Inventory Investment, measuring profitability efficiency.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this promotional event record was last modified in the system.']
  planned_trade_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The budgeted trade spend allocation for this promotional event, representing the total investment planned by the manufacturer.']
  planned_volume_units decimal(18,2) [note: 'The forecasted sales volume in units expected during the promotional event, used for planning and ROI estimation.']
  post_event_analysis_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether post-event analysis has been completed for this promotional event, including lift measurement and ROI calculation.']
  post_event_analysis_date date [note: 'The date when post-event analysis was completed for this promotional event.']
  pricing_strategy varchar [note: 'The pricing approach applied during this event. Hi-Lo: high-low promotional pricing with deep temporary discounts; EDLP: Everyday Low Price with minimal promotional variation; Hybrid: combination of both strategies.']
  promotional_lift_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage increase in sales volume attributable to the promotional event, calculated as (Actual Volume - Baseline Volume) / Baseline Volume * 100.']
  rebate_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total rebate amount paid to the retailer for this promotional event, typically calculated post-event based on performance metrics.']
  roi_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The return on investment for this promotional event, calculated as (Incremental Profit - Trade Spend) / Trade Spend * 100, measuring promotional effectiveness.']
  salesforce_tpm_event_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier from Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud Trade Promotion Management system for this event record, used for system integration and data lineage.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'The date when financial settlement for this promotional event was completed and all trade spend obligations were resolved.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'The current status of financial settlement for this promotional event. Pending: settlement not yet initiated; In Progress: settlement calculations underway; Settled: all financial obligations resolved; Disputed: settlement under dispute resolution.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date when the promotional event begins execution at retail or distribution level.']

  Note: 'Represents a discrete promotional execution event tied to a trade promotion program. A single trade promotion may have multiple events across different retailers, geographies, or time windows. Captures event-level details: event type (feature ad, display, temporary price reduction, BOGO, scan-back), event start/end dates, retailer account, ship-to location, participating SKUs, planned vs. actual event status, and event-level trade spend allocation. Integrates with Salesforce TPM event records.'
}

Table promotion.event_sku {
  event_sku_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the promotion event SKU association record. Primary key for this entity.']
  carbon_emission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.carbon_emission. Business justification: Carbon accounting attributes emissions of each promoted SKU during a promotion event for lifecycle and ESG reporting.']
  channel_classification_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution channel (e.g., grocery, mass, club, DTC, e-commerce) where this promotional SKU is offered.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved the promotional pricing for this SKU.']
  label_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.label_version. Business justification: Promotion pricing must reference the latest approved label version for each SKU to meet labeling regulations.']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: REQUIRED: Lot traceability for promotional SKUs ensures compliance and quality tracking of the specific production lot used.']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent promotion event to which this SKU is associated.']
  regulatory_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.registration. Business justification: Compliance team validates each promoted SKU against its registration before launch; direct FK enables automated compliance checks.']
  retailer_funding_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retailer funding agreement or trade contract governing the promotional terms for this SKU.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product SKU participating in this promotion event.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: REQUIRED: Promotion Fulfillment Allocation report needs to map each promoted SKU to the specific inventory stock position supplying it.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retailer or trade account for which this promotional SKU pricing applies.']
  accrual_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Accrued trade spend liability for this promotional SKU. Represents the estimated financial obligation based on planned volume and discount.']
  actual_promotional_volume_cases decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual volume of cases sold during the promotion period for this SKU. Captured post-event from POS or shipment data.']
  actual_promotional_volume_units decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual volume of consumer units sold during the promotion period for this SKU. Used for promotional lift and ROI analysis.']
  baseline_volume_estimate_units decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated volume of units that would have been sold without the promotion. Used to calculate incremental lift. Typically derived from historical sales trends.']
  compliance_check_status varchar [note: 'Status of promotional pricing compliance verification for this SKU. Ensures promotional pricing was executed as agreed with the retailer.']
  compliance_verified_date date [note: 'Date when promotional pricing compliance was verified for this SKU through retail execution audits or POS data validation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this promotion event SKU record was first created in the system.']
  deduction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual deduction amount claimed by the retailer for this promotional SKU. Used for trade spend reconciliation and settlement.']
  display_location_type varchar [note: 'Type of in-store display location where this promotional SKU is merchandised. Impacts visibility and promotional lift. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Shelf|End Cap|Free Standing Display|Pallet|Cooler|Checkout|Secondary Placement — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  feature_type varchar [note: 'Type of promotional feature or advertising support provided for this SKU during the promotion event. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Circular Ad|Digital Ad|In-Store Signage|Shelf Talker|Coupon|Loyalty Offer|None — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  gtin varchar [note: 'Global Trade Item Number uniquely identifying the product SKU in the promotion. Supports EAN-8, EAN-13, UPC-A, UPC-E, and GTIN-14 formats.']
  incremental_lift_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage lift in volume due to the promotion. Calculated as (incremental_lift_volume_units / baseline_volume_estimate_units) * 100.']
  incremental_lift_volume_units decimal(18,2) [note: 'Incremental volume of units sold above baseline due to the promotion. Calculated as actual_promotional_volume_units - baseline_volume_estimate_units.']
  is_featured_sku boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU is a featured hero product in the promotion event. Featured SKUs typically receive premium merchandising and advertising support.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the user who last modified this promotion event SKU record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this promotion event SKU record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this promotional SKU.']
  planned_promotional_volume_cases decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted volume of cases expected to be sold during the promotion period for this SKU. Used for trade spend budgeting and supply planning.']
  planned_promotional_volume_units decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted volume of consumer units expected to be sold during the promotion period for this SKU.']
  price_reduction_depth_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount from regular shelf price to promoted price. Calculated as ((regular_shelf_price - promoted_price) / regular_shelf_price) * 100.']
  pricing_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the promotional pricing for this SKU. Tracks the workflow state from draft through execution to completion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Draft|Pending Approval|Approved|Rejected|Active|Completed|Cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  pricing_approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the promotional pricing for this SKU was approved.']
  promoted_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The temporary promotional price offered to consumers during the promotion event. Expressed in the base currency of the business.']
  promoted_price_type varchar [note: 'Type of promotional pricing mechanism applied to this SKU. Hi-Lo: temporary price reduction; EDLP: everyday low price; Multi-Buy: quantity discount; BOGO: buy-one-get-one; Scan-Back: retailer scanned rebate; Off-Invoice: deduction at invoice; Bill-Back: post-event settlement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Hi-Lo|EDLP|Multi-Buy|BOGO|Scan-Back|Off-Invoice|Bill-Back — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  promotion_effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when the promotional pricing for this SKU expires and regular pricing resumes.']
  promotion_effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when the promotional pricing for this SKU becomes effective at retail.']
  promotional_discount_per_unit decimal(18,4) [note: 'Absolute discount amount per unit (regular_shelf_price - promoted_price). Represents the per-unit trade spend investment.']
  promotional_gmroi decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross margin return on investment for this promotional SKU. Calculated as incremental_gross_margin / total_trade_spend_amount.']
  promotional_roi_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Return on investment for this promotional SKU. Calculated as (incremental_gross_profit - total_trade_spend_amount) / total_trade_spend_amount * 100.']
  rebate_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Rebate amount paid to the retailer for this promotional SKU. Typically settled post-event based on actual sales performance.']
  regular_shelf_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The standard everyday shelf price (RSP/MSRP) for this SKU before promotional discount. Used to calculate price reduction depth.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date when the financial settlement for this promotional SKU was completed with the retailer.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the financial settlement process for this promotional SKU with the retailer.']
  total_trade_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total trade spend investment for this SKU in this promotion event. Calculated as promotional_discount_per_unit * actual_promotional_volume_units.']
  upc varchar [note: 'Universal Product Code for the SKU participating in the promotion event. Standard 12-digit UPC-A format.']

  Note: 'Association entity linking individual SKUs (GTINs/UPCs) to a specific promotion event with full promotional pricing authority. Captures SKU-level promotional details including promoted price, promoted price type (Hi-Lo temporary price reduction, EDLP, multi-buy, BOGO, scan-back, off-invoice, bill-back), price reduction depth (%), regular shelf price (RSP/MSRP), effective date range, planned promotional volume (cases/units), actual promotional volume, baseline volume estimate, incremental lift volume, promotional discount amount per unit, retailer account, channel, and pricing approval status. Serves as the SSOT for SKU-level promotional pricing within trade events — distinct from standard list pricing owned by the product domain. Enables SKU-level trade spend, lift analysis, and promotional price compliance per event.'
}

Table promotion.trade_calendar {
  trade_calendar_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for trade_calendar']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product category this promotional calendar covers. Links to the product category entity.']
  channel_classification_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales or distribution channel this promotional calendar applies to (e.g., retail, wholesale, e-commerce). Links to the channel classification entity.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the brand this promotional calendar is associated with. Links to the brand master entity.']
  parent_calendar_trade_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent promotional calendar if this is a sub-calendar or regional variant. Links to another promotional calendar entity.']
  primary_trade_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the brand manager, category manager, or trade marketing manager who owns and is accountable for this promotional calendar. Links to the employee entity.']
  quaternary_trade_last_modified_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that last modified this promotional calendar record. Links to the employee or system user entity.']
  tertiary_trade_created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created this promotional calendar record. Links to the employee or system user entity.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the retailer or trade account this promotional calendar is scoped to. Links to the trade account entity.']
  actual_event_count varchar [note: 'The actual number of promotional events executed or in-flight within this calendar period.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date when this promotional calendar was formally approved by management or finance.']
  calendar_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code for the promotional calendar used in reporting and system integration (e.g., \'FY24Q1-RET\', \'CAL-2024-BRX\').']
  calendar_name varchar [note: 'Business name of the promotional calendar (e.g., \'FY2024 Q1 Retail Promo Calendar\', \'Annual Brand X Trade Plan\').']
  calendar_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the promotional calendar (draft, submitted, approved, locked, active, closed).']
  closed_date date [note: 'The date when this promotional calendar was closed after all events completed and post-event analysis was finalized.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where this promotional calendar is executed (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this promotional calendar record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the planned trade spend (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when this promotional calendar ends and no further promotional events are planned under this calendar.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when this promotional calendar becomes effective and promotional events can begin execution.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year this promotional calendar covers (e.g., 2024, 2025).']
  is_baseline_calendar boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this is the baseline or master promotional calendar for the fiscal year (true) or a variant/supplemental calendar (false).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this promotional calendar record was last updated or modified.']
  locked_date date [note: 'The date when this promotional calendar was locked to prevent further changes, typically after approval and before execution.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this promotional calendar for internal reference.']
  planned_event_count varchar [note: 'The total number of promotional events planned within this calendar period.']
  planning_cycle varchar [note: 'The planning frequency or cycle for this promotional calendar (annual, semi-annual, quarterly, monthly).']
  pricing_strategy varchar [note: 'The primary pricing strategy employed in this promotional calendar: Hi-Lo (High-Low Pricing Strategy), EDLP (Everyday Low Price), hybrid, promotional, or premium.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region or market code this promotional calendar applies to (e.g., \'NA-EAST\', \'EU-WEST\', \'APAC\').']
  total_planned_trade_spend decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total budgeted trade spend envelope allocated for all promotional events within this calendar, in the base currency.']
  tpm_system_code varchar [note: 'External system identifier from the Trade Promotion Management (TPM) or Trade Promotion Optimization (TPO) system (e.g., Salesforce TPM, TradeEdge).']
  trade_calendar_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description or purpose of this promotional calendar, including strategic objectives and key initiatives.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this promotional calendar, incremented with each major revision or replan cycle.']

  Note: 'Master entity representing the annual or periodic trade promotional calendar for a brand, category, or retailer account. Captures calendar name, fiscal year, planning cycle, owning brand/category manager, retailer account scope, calendar status (draft, approved, locked), total planned trade spend envelope, and number of planned promotion events. Serves as the planning container for all trade promotion events within a given period and account relationship.'
}

Table promotion.trade_spend_allocation {
  trade_spend_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the trade spend allocation record. Primary key for this transactional entity capturing budget allocation to promotion events.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Needed to allocate trade spend per marketing campaign, supporting budgeting, spend efficiency tracking, and campaign‑level spend reconciliation.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who approved this trade spend allocation. Provides audit trail for governance and compliance.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the brand for which trade spend is allocated. Enables brand-level spend tracking and ROI analysis.']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific promotion event or campaign to which this trade spend is allocated. Links to the promotion master record in TPM system.']
  sku_group_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SKU group or product category included in this trade spend allocation. May be null if allocation is at brand level only.']
  supplier_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_contract. Business justification: REQUIRED: Allocation of promotional spend must be tied to the underlying supplier contract to enforce spend limits and compliance.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retailer or distributor account receiving the trade spend funding. Identifies the counterparty in the promotional agreement.']
  accrual_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount accrued for this trade spend allocation in the financial period. Used for matching expenses to the period in which the promotional activity occurs, per accrual accounting principles.']
  actual_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual amount of trade spend executed or paid out. Populated post-event for variance analysis and reconciliation with accounts payable.']
  actual_volume decimal(15,3) [note: 'The actual sales volume achieved during the promotion period. Populated post-event from POS or shipment data for lift analysis and ROI calculation.']
  allocated_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary amount of trade spend allocated to this promotion event, account, brand, or SKU group. Represents the planned or budgeted spend.']
  allocation_date date [note: 'The business date when the trade spend allocation was created or committed. Principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  allocation_number varchar [note: 'Business-readable unique identifier for the trade spend allocation. Used for external communication, audit trails, and reconciliation with finance systems.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the trade spend allocation. Tracks progression from planning through approval, execution, and final settlement with retailer. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|committed|executed|settled|cancelled|rejected — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date when this trade spend allocation was formally approved by the authorized approver. Marks transition from draft to committed status.']
  baseline_volume decimal(15,3) [note: 'The expected sales volume without promotional activity, used as the baseline for measuring promotional lift. Expressed in units or cases.']
  committed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of trade spend formally committed after approval. May differ from allocated amount due to negotiation or budget adjustments.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'The cost center responsible for this trade spend allocation. Enables organizational unit-level spend tracking and accountability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this trade spend allocation record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this allocation record. Supports multi-currency trade spend management for global operations.']
  deduction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount deducted by the retailer from invoice payments as part of this trade spend allocation. Tracks retailer-initiated deductions for reconciliation and dispute resolution.']
