  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory affairs manager or quality director who approved this IFRA compliance record for use in regulatory submissions.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Date when the IFRA compliance assessment was performed for this fragrance ingredient and product combination. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  assessment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the next IFRA compliance reassessment is required. Triggered by IFRA standard updates or product reformulation. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  assessor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory affairs or quality assurance professional who performed the IFRA compliance assessment.']
  cas_number varchar [note: 'Chemical Abstracts Service registry number uniquely identifying the fragrance ingredient chemical substance. Standard format: XXXXXX-XX-X.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or notes from the assessor regarding special considerations, assumptions, or clarifications related to this IFRA compliance assessment.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the fragrance ingredient usage against IFRA standards. Compliant indicates actual usage is within permitted limits; non-compliant requires reformulation; pending review indicates assessment in progress.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this IFRA compliance record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Audit trail field.']
  exemption_justification varchar [note: 'Detailed justification text if an exemption or deviation from IFRA standard is claimed. Must include scientific rationale and risk assessment summary.']
  formulation_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the product formulation for which this IFRA compliance assessment applies. Links to PLM system for formulation change tracking.']
  fragrance_ingredient_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specific fragrance ingredient being assessed for IFRA compliance. May be a chemical name, INCI name, or proprietary fragrance component identifier.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic markets or jurisdictions for which this IFRA compliance assessment applies. Pipe-separated list of ISO 3-letter country codes or regional identifiers (e.g., USA|CAN|GBR|DEU|FRA).']
  ifra_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Certificate number issued by fragrance supplier confirming IFRA compliance of the fragrance compound. Required for audit and regulatory submissions.']
  ifra_product_category varchar [note: 'IFRA product category classification determining usage restrictions. Categories range from 1 (toys) to 12 (air fresheners), with subcategories for leave-on vs rinse-off products. Each category has specific maximum permitted usage levels. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Category 1|Category 2|Category 3|Category 4|Category 5A|Category 5B|Category 5C|Category 5D|Category 6|Category 7A|Category 7B|Category 8|Category 9|Category 10A|Category 10B|Category 11A|Category 11B|Category 12 — 18 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  ifra_standard_version varchar [note: 'Version of the IFRA standard applied for this compliance assessment (e.g., 49th Amendment, 50th Amendment). IFRA publishes amendments periodically with updated usage restrictions.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this IFRA compliance record is currently active and valid. Inactive records represent historical assessments superseded by newer evaluations.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this IFRA compliance record was last updated. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Audit trail field for change tracking.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent periodic review of this IFRA compliance record. Ensures ongoing compliance monitoring as IFRA standards evolve. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  maximum_permitted_usage_level decimal(10,6) [note: 'Maximum permitted concentration of the fragrance ingredient as defined by IFRA standard for the specified product category. Expressed as percentage (e.g., 0.5 = 0.5%). Null if no restriction applies.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this IFRA compliance record. Typically triggered annually or upon IFRA standard amendment. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'High-level product type classification determining IFRA category assignment and usage restrictions. Leave-on products have stricter limits than rinse-off products.']
  qra_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number to the Quantitative Risk Assessment document supporting the safety substantiation of this fragrance ingredient usage. Required for regulatory defense.']
  record_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system from which this IFRA compliance record originated (e.g., Veeva Vault, SAP QM, PLM system). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  restriction_type varchar [note: 'Type of IFRA restriction applied to the ingredient. Prohibited means banned entirely; specification means allowed with purity/quality requirements; restricted means usage level limits apply.']
  risk_assessment_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number to the detailed risk assessment document or safety dossier supporting this IFRA compliance determination. Links to document management system.']
  safety_margin_percentage decimal(10,4) [note: 'Calculated safety margin between actual usage level and maximum permitted level, expressed as percentage. Positive values indicate compliance buffer; negative values indicate non-compliance.']
  supplier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the supplier providing the fragrance ingredient or compound. Critical for traceability and IFRA certificate validation.']
  usage_level_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for usage levels. Typically percentage (%) for most fragrance ingredients, or ppm/mg/kg for trace components.']

  Note: 'IFRA (International Fragrance Association) compliance records for fragrance-containing products. Tracks IFRA standard version, fragrance ingredient, product category (leave-on, rinse-off, fine fragrance), maximum permitted usage level per IFRA standard, actual formulated usage level, compliance status (compliant/non-compliant), assessment date, and assessor. Required for fragrance safety substantiation in personal care and household products.'
}

Table regulatory.surveillance_event {
  surveillance_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for surveillance_event']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Investigation costs from post‑market surveillance are charged to a cost center for cost tracking.']
  investigation_assigned_to_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Post‑market surveillance investigations are assigned to an employee; FK enables investigation tracking.']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific production lot or batch implicated in the surveillance event for traceability and potential recall scope determination.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific product (SKU) implicated in the surveillance event.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing batch or lot number of the affected product for traceability and scope analysis.']
  consumer_complaint_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the consumer complaint or adverse event as reported, capturing symptoms, circumstances, and product usage context.']
  corrective_action_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the corrective action was fully implemented and verified for effectiveness.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the corrective action plan implemented or planned to address the root cause and prevent recurrence.']
  corrective_action_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether corrective action is required based on the investigation findings and risk assessment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the surveillance event record was first created in the system for audit trail purposes.']
  event_occurrence_date date [note: 'Date when the adverse event or product issue actually occurred or was first observed by the consumer or reporter.']
  event_received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the surveillance signal was first received by the organization, marking the start of the regulatory clock for reporting obligations.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the post-market surveillance event investigation and resolution workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: reported|under_investigation|root_cause_identified|corrective_action_pending|corrective_action_completed|closed|escalated_to_recall — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the post-market surveillance signal indicating the nature of the safety or quality concern.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration or best-before date of the affected product, relevant for stability-related events.']
  financial_impact_estimate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated financial impact of the surveillance event including investigation costs, corrective actions, potential recall costs, and liability exposure in USD.']
  geographic_market varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country codes indicating the geographic markets where the affected product was distributed, pipe-separated for multiple countries.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'Global Trade Item Number identifying the product involved in the surveillance event for international traceability.']
  injury_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the injury or adverse health effect reported by the consumer or healthcare provider.']
  injury_reported_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the surveillance event involved a reported consumer injury or adverse health effect.']
  investigation_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the investigation was completed and root cause determination was finalized.']
  investigation_start_date date [note: 'Date when the formal investigation of the surveillance event was initiated.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the surveillance event record was last updated, supporting audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  manufacturing_date date [note: 'Date when the affected product batch was manufactured, used for shelf-life and stability analysis.']
  medical_treatment_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reported injury required medical treatment or healthcare intervention.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified the surveillance event record for accountability and audit purposes.']
  recall_classification varchar [note: 'FDA recall classification (Class I, II, III) or equivalent regulatory classification indicating the level of health hazard.']
  recall_initiated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the surveillance event resulted in a product recall or market withdrawal action.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority to which the surveillance event must be reported based on jurisdiction and product category. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|CPSC|EPA|EU_RAPEX|Health_Canada|TGA_Australia|ANVISA_Brazil|CFDA_China|MHRA_UK|none — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_report_reference_number varchar [note: 'Official reference or tracking number assigned by the regulatory authority upon submission of the surveillance report.']
  regulatory_report_submission_date date [note: 'Date when the mandatory regulatory report was submitted to the applicable authority.']
  regulatory_reporting_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the surveillance event triggers a mandatory regulatory reporting obligation to FDA, CPSC, EPA, or other authorities.']
  reporter_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the reporter for written communication and documentation exchange.']
  reporter_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number of the reporter for follow-up investigation and clarification.']
  reporter_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who reported the surveillance event, required for follow-up and regulatory submissions.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Standardized classification of the root cause for trending and analytics purposes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: manufacturing_defect|formulation_issue|packaging_failure|labeling_error|contamination|stability_failure|consumer_misuse|no_defect_found|under_investigation — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  root_cause_identified varchar [note: 'Documented root cause of the surveillance event as determined through investigation, including manufacturing defect, formulation issue, packaging failure, or misuse.']
  severity_classification varchar [note: 'Risk-based severity rating of the surveillance event based on potential harm to consumers and regulatory impact.']
  signal_source varchar [note: 'Origin channel through which the post-market surveillance signal was received. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: consumer_direct|call_center|retailer|distributor|regulatory_authority|social_media|healthcare_provider|internal_audit — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sku varchar [note: 'Stock Keeping Unit code of the affected product for traceability and inventory correlation.']
  surveillance_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the surveillance event, externally referenced in regulatory submissions and internal tracking systems.']
  units_affected_estimate varchar [note: 'Estimated number of product units potentially affected by the surveillance event, used for recall scope and risk assessment.']

  Note: 'Post-market surveillance records tracking product safety signals, adverse event reports, consumer complaints with safety implications, and regulatory authority inquiries after product launch. Captures signal type, source (consumer complaint, retailer report, regulatory authority, social media), severity classification, affected SKU/batch, investigation status, root cause, corrective action, and regulatory reporting obligation triggered. Supports CPSC and FDA post-market requirements.'
}

Table regulatory.product_recall {
  product_recall_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the product recall event. Primary key for the product recall record.']
  coordinator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Recall coordination is managed by a specific employee; FK supports recall project management.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Recall expenses (logistics, disposal) are allocated to a cost center for budgeting and reporting.']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.jurisdiction. Business justification: Product recalls are managed per jurisdiction; linking provides a normalized jurisdiction reference.']
  logistics_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.logistics_shipment. Business justification: Recall execution must pinpoint shipments containing recalled items; linking product_recall to logistics_shipment supports targeted recall logistics and reporting.']
  affected_channels varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of distribution channels impacted by recall (e.g., Retail, E-commerce, Wholesale, DSD, DTC). Defines which go-to-market channels received affected product.']
  affected_countries varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes where recalled products were distributed (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX, GBR, DEU).']
  affected_gtin_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of GTIN/UPC/EAN codes for recalled products. Enables point-of-sale and supply chain identification of affected items.']
  affected_lot_batch_numbers varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of manufacturing lot or batch numbers included in the recall. Enables traceability to specific production runs.']
  affected_production_date_range_end date [note: 'End date of the production date range for affected products. Used when recall spans multiple production dates.']
  affected_production_date_range_start date [note: 'Start date of the production date range for affected products. Used when recall spans multiple production dates.']
  affected_sku_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of SKU codes for all product variants included in the recall. Links recall to specific product master records.']
  affected_states_provinces varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of state or province codes where recalled products were distributed within countries. Used for regional recalls.']
  authority_notification_date date [note: 'Date when the company notified the relevant regulatory authority of the recall. FDA requires notification within 24 hours of recall initiation.']
  consumer_hotline_number varchar [note: 'Toll-free or dedicated phone number for consumers to call with questions about the recall or to request remedies.']
  consumer_remedy_instructions varchar [note: 'Detailed instructions for consumers on how to return, dispose of, or obtain remedy for recalled products. Includes contact information and process steps.']
  consumer_remedy_type varchar [note: 'Type of corrective action or compensation offered to consumers who purchased recalled products. Defines how consumers are made whole.']
  corrective_action_plan_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) plan implemented to prevent recurrence of the issue that caused the recall.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for financial amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  effectiveness_check_date date [note: 'Date when the recall effectiveness check was conducted to verify that all consignees were notified and appropriate action taken. FDA requires effectiveness checks at specified intervals.']
  effectiveness_check_result varchar [note: 'Result of the recall effectiveness check. Effective: recall objectives met. Partially Effective: some objectives met. Ineffective: recall objectives not met. Pending: check in progress.']
  estimated_financial_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total financial cost of the recall including product value, logistics, disposal, consumer remedies, and administrative costs. Reported in company reporting currency.']
  health_hazard_evaluation varchar [note: 'Assessment of the health risk posed by the recalled product. Describes potential adverse health consequences and likelihood of occurrence. Used to determine recall classification.']
  press_release_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number, URL, or document identifier for the official press release or public announcement of the recall.']
  public_notification_date date [note: 'Date when the recall was publicly announced through press release, media, or regulatory agency posting.']
  quantity_recalled_units decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total number of product units subject to recall. Represents the volume of affected inventory across all distribution levels.']
  quantity_recovered_units decimal(18,3) [note: 'Number of recalled product units successfully recovered and removed from distribution. Used to calculate recall effectiveness.']
  recall_classification varchar [note: 'FDA/CPSC classification of recall severity. Class I: dangerous or defective products that could cause serious health problems or death. Class II: products that might cause temporary health problem or slight threat of serious nature. Class III: products unlikely to cause adverse health reaction but violate FDA labeling or manufacturing regulations. Voluntary: company-initiated without regulatory mandate. Market Withdrawal: minor violation not subject to FDA legal action.']
  recall_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the recall was officially closed after all corrective actions were completed and effectiveness verified. Nullable for ongoing recalls.']
  recall_coordinator_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the recall coordinator for internal and external communications regarding the recall event.']
  recall_coordinator_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the recall coordinator for urgent communications regarding the recall event.']
  recall_effectiveness_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of recalled units successfully recovered (quantity_recovered_units / quantity_recalled_units * 100). FDA requires effectiveness checks to verify recall completion.']
  recall_initiation_date date [note: 'Date when the company officially initiated the recall action. Marks the beginning of the recall event lifecycle.']
  recall_number varchar [note: 'Official recall identification number assigned by the company or regulatory authority. External business identifier for the recall event.']
  recall_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code categorizing the root cause of the recall (e.g., contamination, mislabeling, undeclared allergen, packaging defect, microbial contamination, foreign material).']
  recall_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of the defect, hazard, or violation that triggered the recall. Includes specific product issue, potential health risk, and circumstances of discovery.']
  recall_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic scope of the recall action. Defines the market territories affected by the recall.']
  recall_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the recall event. Tracks progression from initiation through completion or termination.']
  recall_type varchar [note: 'Distribution level at which the recall is executed. Consumer Level: product reached end consumers. Retail Level: product at retail stores. Wholesale Level: product at distribution centers. Manufacturing Level: product not yet distributed.']
  recall_website_url varchar [note: 'URL of the dedicated webpage providing detailed recall information, consumer instructions, and remedy request forms.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this recall record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data governance.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this recall record was last modified. Audit trail for data governance and change tracking.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the government agency or regulatory body overseeing the recall (e.g., FDA, CPSC, EPA, Health Canada, EU Commission). May be multiple authorities for international recalls.']
  regulatory_mandate_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the recall was mandated by a regulatory authority (True) or initiated voluntarily by the company (False).']
  root_cause_analysis_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the formal root cause analysis document or investigation report that identified the underlying cause of the product defect or contamination.']

  Note: 'Product recall and market withdrawal master records initiated voluntarily or mandated by regulatory authorities. Captures recall class (Class I/II/III per FDA/CPSC), recall reason, affected SKUs, affected lot/batch numbers, recall scope (geographic, channel), initiation date, authority notification date, press release reference, quantity recalled, completion date, effectiveness check results, and consumer remedy type. SSOT for recall event management across all jurisdictions and authorities.'
}

Table regulatory.jurisdiction {
  jurisdiction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the regulatory jurisdiction. Primary key.']
  parent_jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to a parent jurisdiction if this jurisdiction is a sub-jurisdiction (e.g., a state within a country, or a country within a regional bloc). Null for top-level jurisdictions.']
  animal_testing_ban_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the jurisdiction prohibits the sale of products tested on animals (True) or allows it (False).']
  cfr_reference varchar [note: 'The specific CFR title and part number applicable to this jurisdiction (e.g., 21 CFR Part 700 for cosmetics). Primarily used for US FDA jurisdictions.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, clarifications, or special instructions related to this jurisdiction.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Official email address for contacting the regulatory authority for this jurisdiction.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Official phone number for contacting the regulatory authority for this jurisdiction.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the primary country of the jurisdiction (e.g., USA, DEU, CHN). For regional jurisdictions, this may represent the lead country or be null.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this jurisdiction record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this jurisdiction definition and its requirements became effective.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which this jurisdiction definition expires or is superseded. Null if currently active with no planned expiration.']
  gmp_compliance_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) certification or compliance is mandatory for products sold in this jurisdiction (True) or not (False).']
  ifra_compliance_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether compliance with IFRA standards for fragrance ingredients is required (True) or not (False).']
  ingredient_declaration_format varchar [note: 'The required format for ingredient listing on labels: INCI (International Nomenclature of Cosmetic Ingredients), CAS (Chemical Abstracts Service number), common name, or proprietary (brand-specific naming).']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the jurisdiction (e.g., US-FDA, EU-REACH, ASEAN-CCD).']
  jurisdiction_name varchar [note: 'Full official name of the regulatory jurisdiction (e.g., United States Food and Drug Administration, European Union REACH).']
  jurisdiction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the jurisdiction record: active (in use), inactive (no longer applicable), pending (awaiting activation), superseded (replaced by newer version).']
  jurisdiction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the jurisdiction scope: national (country-level), regional (multi-country bloc), supranational (international treaty), state/provincial (sub-national), or municipal (city-level).']
  labeling_language_primary varchar [note: 'ISO 639 two- or three-letter code for the primary language required on product labels in this jurisdiction (e.g., en, fr, de, zh).']
  labeling_language_secondary varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 639 language codes for additional languages required or recommended on labels (e.g., fr,nl for Belgium).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this jurisdiction record was last updated or modified.']
  market_entry_requirement_type varchar [note: 'The type of regulatory requirement for market entry: notification (simple filing), registration (formal registration), pre-market approval (full dossier review before sale), post-market surveillance (monitoring after launch), or voluntary (no mandatory requirement).']
  notification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal notification to the regulatory authority is required before product launch (True) or not (False).']
  post_market_surveillance_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether ongoing post-market surveillance (adverse event reporting, product monitoring) is mandatory (True) or not (False).']
  pre_market_approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether pre-market approval (full dossier review and authorization before sale) is required (True) or not (False).']
  product_information_file_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Product Information File (PIF) or dossier must be maintained and made available to authorities (True) or not (False).']
  reach_registration_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether REACH registration for chemical substances is required for this jurisdiction (True) or not (False). Primarily applicable to EU and EEA jurisdictions.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the geographic region or economic bloc (e.g., EU, ASEAN, GCC, MERCOSUR). Used for multi-country jurisdictions.']
  registration_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether formal product registration with the regulatory authority is mandatory (True) or not (False).']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the governing regulatory body or agency responsible for enforcement within this jurisdiction (e.g., FDA, EPA, CPSC, European Commission).']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'The primary legal or regulatory framework governing this jurisdiction (e.g., FDA 21 CFR, EU Cosmetics Regulation 1223/2009, ASEAN Cosmetic Directive, GCC Standardization Organization).']
  responsible_person_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a designated Responsible Person (RP) within the jurisdiction is required for regulatory compliance (True) or not (False). Common in EU jurisdictions.']
  sds_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Safety Data Sheet (SDS) or Material Safety Data Sheet (MSDS) must be provided for products in this jurisdiction (True) or not (False).']
  submission_portal_url varchar [note: 'URL of the online portal or system used for submitting regulatory notifications, registrations, or dossiers to this jurisdiction.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Official website URL of the regulatory authority for this jurisdiction, providing access to regulations, guidance documents, and submission portals.']

  Note: 'Reference master for regulatory jurisdictions defining the geographic and legal scope of regulatory requirements. Captures jurisdiction code, jurisdiction name, country/region, applicable regulatory framework (FDA, EU, ASEAN, GCC), market entry requirement type, language requirements for labeling, and notification vs. registration requirement flag. Used to drive multi-jurisdictional compliance workflows across registrations, labels, and submissions.'
}

Table regulatory.restricted_substance {
  restricted_substance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the restricted substance record. Primary key.']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.jurisdiction. Business justification: Restricted substances are subject to jurisdiction‑specific regulations; linking provides a proper reference and removes the duplicated code.']
  affected_product_categories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of product categories or SKU families in which this substance is restricted or prohibited (e.g., cosmetics, household cleaners, food contact materials, children\'s products).']
  alternative_substance_recommendations varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of CAS numbers or substance names recommended as safer alternatives for formulation substitution.']
  applicable_regulations varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of all regulations, directives, or legal frameworks under which this substance is restricted (e.g., EU REACH, California Prop 65, FDA 21 CFR 700, EPA FIFRA, CPSC regulations).']
  authorization_status varchar [note: 'Current status of regulatory authorization for use of the substance in specific applications or markets.']
  cas_number varchar [note: 'Unique numerical identifier assigned by the Chemical Abstracts Service to every chemical substance described in the open scientific literature.']
  cfr_reference varchar [note: 'Specific CFR title, part, and section citation(s) that govern the use, labeling, or prohibition of this substance in the United States.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special handling instructions, or notes regarding the restricted substance and its regulatory status.']
  concentration_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the maximum permitted concentration: percent by weight, percent by volume, parts per million (ppm), parts per billion (ppb), or milligrams per kilogram.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this restricted substance record was first created in the system.']
  downstream_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether manufacturers and importers must notify downstream users (formulators, distributors, retailers) of the presence of this substance in articles or mixtures.']
  ec_number varchar [note: 'Seven-digit identifier assigned to chemical substances for regulatory purposes within the European Union.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the restriction or regulatory requirement becomes legally enforceable.']
  epa_restricted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the substance is subject to EPA restrictions under TSCA, FIFRA, or other environmental regulations.']
  fda_prohibited_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the substance is explicitly prohibited by the FDA for use in cosmetics, drugs, or food contact materials under CFR Title 21.']
  hazard_classification varchar [note: 'GHS hazard class and category codes assigned to the substance (e.g., Acute Tox. 3, Skin Corr. 1B, Carc. 1A) for safety data sheet and labeling purposes.']
  ifra_prohibition_category varchar [note: 'IFRA product category (1-11) in which the substance is prohibited or restricted, ranging from Category 1 (lip products) to Category 11 (non-skin contact products).']
  ifra_standard_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the applicable IFRA Standard number and amendment that governs the use of this substance in fragrance compounds.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this restricted substance record was last updated or modified.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the restricted substance record was last reviewed for regulatory updates, new restrictions, or changes in applicable law.']
  maximum_permitted_concentration decimal(10,6) [note: 'Maximum allowable concentration of the substance in finished products, expressed as a percentage by weight or volume, as defined by applicable regulations.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the next periodic review of this restricted substance record is scheduled to ensure ongoing compliance with evolving regulations.']
  owning_team varchar [note: 'Name of the internal team or department responsible for maintaining this restricted substance record (e.g., Regulatory Affairs, Product Safety, Quality Assurance).']
  post_market_surveillance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether ongoing post-market surveillance, adverse event reporting, or periodic safety updates are required for products containing this substance.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact for regulatory inquiries and substance compliance questions.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual responsible for regulatory compliance and updates related to this restricted substance.']
  prop_65_listing boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the substance is listed under California Proposition 65 as known to cause cancer, birth defects, or reproductive harm, requiring warning labels for California sales.']
  reach_registration_number varchar [note: 'Unique registration number assigned under EU REACH for substances manufactured or imported in quantities of one tonne or more per year.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the primary regulatory body or agency that issued the restriction (e.g., ECHA, FDA, EPA, CPSC, Health Canada).']
  restriction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the regulatory restriction imposed on the substance: banned (prohibited entirely), concentration_limit (allowed below threshold), disclosure_required (must be declared on label), authorization_required (needs pre-approval), restricted_use (limited applications), conditional_approval (allowed under specific conditions).']
  rspo_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the substance (if palm-derived) meets RSPO certification requirements for sustainable sourcing.']
  sds_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Safety Data Sheet (formerly MSDS) must be maintained and provided to downstream users for this substance.']
  substance_name varchar [note: 'Common or chemical name of the restricted substance as recognized in regulatory filings and industry practice.']
  sunset_date date [note: 'Date after which the use of the substance is prohibited unless a specific authorization has been granted, applicable primarily under REACH authorization provisions.']
  svhc_status boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the substance is identified as a Substance of Very High Concern under REACH, triggering additional authorization and notification obligations.']
  tonnage_band varchar [note: 'Annual production or import volume range in metric tonnes per year, used for REACH registration tier determination.']

  Note: 'Master record for all chemicals, ingredients, and materials subject to prohibition, restriction, authorization, or disclosure under any applicable regulation worldwide. Captures substance name, CAS number, EC number, restriction type (banned, concentration limit, disclosure required, authorization required), applicable regulation(s), REACH registration number, tonnage band, SVHC status, authorization status, maximum permitted concentration, effective date, downstream user notification obligations, and affected product categories. SSOT for all regulated substance data including EU REACH, California Prop 65, FDA prohibited ingredients, EPA restricted chemicals, and FIFRA substances.'
}

Table regulatory.regulatory_claim {
  regulatory_claim_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the regulatory claim record. Primary key.']
  compliance_reviewer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Claims are reviewed by a designated employee; FK supports claim review audit and reporting.']
  product_brand_id varchar [note: 'FK to product.product_brand']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product to which this claim applies. Links to the product master record.']
  adverse_event_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether adverse events related to the claim must be reported to regulatory authorities. True if reporting is required, False otherwise.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the regulatory authority approved the claim for use. Null if not yet approved. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority approval status: not submitted (claim not yet filed), submitted (application filed), under review (being evaluated), approved (authorized for use), conditionally approved (approved with restrictions), denied (rejected), or appeal pending (denial being contested). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not_submitted|submitted|under_review|approved|conditionally_approved|denied|appeal_pending — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  approved_markets varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of market codes or regions where the claim is approved for use (e.g., USA, EU, LATAM, APAC). Enables market-specific claim deployment.']
  certification_number varchar [note: 'Unique certification or registration number issued by the third-party certifying body. Null if no certification applies.']
  cfr_reference varchar [note: 'Specific CFR citation that governs the claim (e.g., 21 CFR 101.54 for health claims, 16 CFR Part 260 for environmental claims). Provides regulatory traceability.']
  claim_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the regulatory claim, formatted as RC- followed by 8 digits. Used for external reference and tracking.']
  claim_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of the claim application: product-specific (applies to one SKU), brand-level (applies to all products under a brand), category-level (applies to a product category), or ingredient-specific (applies to products containing a specific ingredient).']
  claim_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the regulatory claim: draft (being prepared), pending review (submitted for approval), approved (authorized for use), rejected (denied by authority), expired (approval period ended), or withdrawn (voluntarily removed).']
  claim_text varchar [note: 'Full text of the regulatory claim as it appears or will appear on product labels, packaging, or marketing materials. Must match approved wording exactly.']
  claim_type varchar [note: 'Category of regulatory claim: structure/function claims (e.g., strengthens hair), environmental claims (biodegradable, recyclable), organic/natural certifications, efficacy claims (clinically proven), safety claims, nutritional claims, or hypoallergenic claims. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: structure_function|environmental|organic_natural|efficacy|safety|nutritional|hypoallergenic — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, context, or special instructions related to the claim. May include reviewer comments, historical context, or implementation guidance.']
  compliance_review_date date [note: 'Date when the internal compliance review was completed. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  compliance_review_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of internal FTC/FDA compliance review: compliant (meets all requirements), non-compliant (violates regulations), requires modification (needs changes before use), pending review (under evaluation), or not reviewed (not yet assessed).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the regulatory claim record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Supports audit trail and data lineage.']
  disclaimer_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a disclaimer statement must accompany the claim on labels or marketing materials. True if disclaimer is required, False otherwise.']
  disclaimer_text varchar [note: 'Full text of the required disclaimer that must accompany the claim. Null if no disclaimer is required. Must be displayed in accordance with regulatory guidelines.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the approved claim becomes valid for use on products and marketing materials. May differ from approval date due to implementation lead time. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  eu_regulation_reference varchar [note: 'Specific EU regulation or directive that governs the claim (e.g., Regulation 1223/2009 for cosmetics, Regulation 1924/2006 for nutrition and health claims). Null if not applicable to EU markets.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the claim approval expires and can no longer be used unless renewed. Null for claims with indefinite approval. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  ifra_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the claim complies with IFRA standards for fragrance ingredients. True if compliant, False if not applicable or non-compliant. Relevant for cosmetics and personal care products.']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the jurisdiction where the claim is approved or pending (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR, DEU, FRA, BRA, CHN, AUS). Supports multi-jurisdictional claim tracking.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code for the claim text (e.g., en for English, es for Spanish, fr for French). Supports multi-language claim management.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the regulatory claim record was last updated. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.']
  owning_team varchar [note: 'Name of the internal team or department responsible for managing the claim (e.g., Regulatory Affairs, Product Development, Marketing). Supports accountability and workflow routing.']
  post_market_surveillance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether post-market surveillance or ongoing monitoring is required for the claim. True if surveillance is mandated, False otherwise.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact person for the claim. Used for notifications and inquiries.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary contact person responsible for the claim. Typically a regulatory affairs specialist or product manager.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'High-level category of the product associated with the claim: cosmetics, personal care, household products, food, dietary supplements, over-the-counter (OTC) drugs, or medical devices. Determines applicable regulatory framework. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cosmetics|personal_care|household|food|dietary_supplement|otc_drug|medical_device — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory authority that reviewed or approved the claim: FDA (U.S. Food and Drug Administration), EPA (Environmental Protection Agency), CPSC (Consumer Product Safety Commission), EU Commission, EFSA (European Food Safety Authority), USDA (U.S. Department of Agriculture), FTC (Federal Trade Commission), Health Canada, TGA (Therapeutic Goods Administration - Australia), ANVISA (Brazil), or CFDA (China Food and Drug Administration). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EPA|CPSC|EU_COMMISSION|EFSA|USDA|FTC|HEALTH_CANADA|TGA|ANVISA|CFDA — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  renewal_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between required renewals for the claim. Null if no renewal is required. Common values: 12, 24, 36, 60.']
  renewal_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the claim requires periodic renewal with the regulatory authority. True if renewal is required, False if approval is indefinite.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Stock Keeping Unit identifier for the specific product variant to which the claim applies. Enables SKU-level claim tracking.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date when the claim was submitted to the regulatory authority for review. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  substantiation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to substantiate the claim: clinical study (human trials), laboratory test (in vitro), consumer perception study, literature review, expert opinion, historical use documentation, or third-party certification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clinical_study|laboratory_test|consumer_perception|literature_review|expert_opinion|historical_use|third_party_certification — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  supporting_study_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or citation for the study, test report, or documentation that substantiates the claim. May include internal study IDs, published journal citations, or third-party certification numbers.']
  third_party_certification varchar [note: 'Name of third-party certification body or seal that validates the claim (e.g., USDA Organic, Leaping Bunny, EcoCert, NSF, Rainforest Alliance). Null if no third-party certification applies.']
  usage_restriction varchar [note: 'Any restrictions or conditions on the use of the claim, such as required disclaimers, context limitations, or co-claim requirements. Free text field for detailed restriction documentation.']

  Note: 'Master record of approved and pending regulatory claims for products including structure/function claims, environmental claims (biodegradable, recyclable), organic/natural certifications, and efficacy claims. Captures claim text, claim type, substantiation method, supporting study references, regulatory authority approval status, approved markets, claim expiry date, and FTC/FDA compliance review outcome. Prevents unapproved claims from appearing on labels or marketing materials.'
}

Table regulatory.action {
  action_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the regulatory action record. Primary key.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Costs of corrective actions are charged to a cost center for internal cost control.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Penalties or fees from regulatory actions are posted to a GL account for financial compliance.']
  manufacturing_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing facility, warehouse, or distribution center that is the subject of this regulatory action. Links to facility master data. Nullable when action is product-specific rather than facility-specific.']
  regulatory_action_responsible_party_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Regulatory actions assign a responsible employee for follow‑up; FK enables tracking of accountability.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific product affected by this regulatory action. Links to the product master data. Nullable when action applies to facility or process rather than specific product.']
  action_date date [note: 'Official date the regulatory authority issued or published the enforcement action. This is the business event timestamp representing when the action became effective.']
  action_number varchar [note: 'Official reference number assigned by the regulatory authority to this enforcement action (e.g., warning letter number, import alert number, consent decree docket number).']
  action_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the regulatory action. Tracks progression from initial receipt through company response, corrective action implementation, and final resolution or closure by the authority. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: received|under_review|response_submitted|corrective_action_in_progress|resolved|closed|appealed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  action_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the regulatory enforcement action received. Warning letters address violations; import alerts restrict product entry; consent decrees are legal settlements; Form 483 observations document inspection findings; recalls remove products from market; injunctions halt operations; seizures confiscate products; penalties impose fines; prosecutions pursue criminal charges. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: warning_letter|import_alert|consent_decree|form_483|mandatory_recall|voluntary_recall|injunction|seizure|civil_penalty|criminal_prosecution — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  affected_facility_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of facility names or codes affected by this regulatory action when multiple sites are involved. Used for multi-site enforcement actions or consent decrees covering multiple locations.']
  affected_product_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of product names, SKUs (Stock Keeping Units), or GTINs (Global Trade Item Numbers) affected by this regulatory action when multiple products are involved. Used when action spans multiple products or product families.']
  audit_completion_date date [note: 'Date the mandated external audit was completed and final audit report submitted to the regulatory authority. Null if no external audit is required or if audit is still in progress.']
  board_notification_date date [note: 'Date the Board of Directors was formally notified of this regulatory action. Required for material enforcement actions that could impact financial statements, stock price, or corporate reputation. Null if board notification was not required.']
  citation_reference varchar [note: 'Specific legal or regulatory code sections cited in the enforcement action (e.g., 21 CFR 211.110 for GMP violations, 40 CFR 261 for hazardous waste, 16 CFR Part 1500 for CPSC). Provides the legal basis for the action.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for internal notes, strategic considerations, lessons learned, or additional context about this regulatory action. May include legal strategy notes, root cause analysis summary, or cross-functional coordination details. Confidential internal use only.']
  corrective_action_plan_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the formal Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) plan submitted to the regulatory authority in response to this action. Links to the CAPA management system where detailed remediation steps, timelines, and verification evidence are documented.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory action record was first created in the system. Follows ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Audit trail for data lineage.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this regulatory action has been escalated to executive leadership (CEO, COO, General Counsel) due to severity, financial impact, or reputational risk. True for actions requiring board-level visibility; False for routine enforcement handled at operational level.']
  external_audit_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the regulatory action mandates an independent third-party audit or inspection to verify corrective actions. True when authority requires external verification; False when internal verification is sufficient.']
  external_auditor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the independent third-party auditing firm or consultant engaged to verify corrective actions as required by the regulatory authority. Null if no external audit is required.']
  financial_penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary fine or civil penalty assessed by the regulatory authority as part of the enforcement action. Expressed in the currency specified in penalty_currency_code. Null if no financial penalty was imposed.']
  insurance_claim_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number for any insurance claim filed to recover costs associated with this regulatory action (e.g., recall expenses, legal fees, penalties). Links to the insurance claims management system. Null if no claim was filed.']
  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'The regulatory body or government agency that issued the enforcement action. FDA (Food and Drug Administration) for food/drug/cosmetics; EPA (Environmental Protection Agency) for environmental violations; CPSC (Consumer Product Safety Commission) for product safety; EU REACH for chemical compliance; FTC (Federal Trade Commission) for advertising; OSHA for workplace safety; state agencies for local enforcement; international authorities for foreign jurisdictions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EPA|CPSC|EU_REACH|FTC|OSHA|STATE_AGENCY|INTERNATIONAL_AUTHORITY — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Geographic or regulatory jurisdiction where the action was issued. Uses ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes (e.g., USA, DEU, GBR) with optional subdivision codes for state/province level enforcement (e.g., USA-CA for California, USA-NY for New York).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory action record was last updated in the system. Follows ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Audit trail for change tracking.']
  legal_counsel_assigned varchar [note: 'Name of the internal or external legal counsel assigned to advise on this regulatory action. Critical for consent decrees, injunctions, civil penalties, and criminal prosecutions where legal strategy is required.']
  penalty_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the financial penalty amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Null if no financial penalty was imposed.']
  previous_action_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the earlier regulatory action that cited the same or related violations. Used to track repeat violations and enforcement escalation patterns. Null if this is a first-time violation.']
  public_disclosure_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the regulatory action has been publicly disclosed by the authority (e.g., published on FDA warning letters database, EPA enforcement actions website). True if publicly available; False if confidential or under seal.']
  public_disclosure_url varchar [note: 'Web address where the regulatory action is publicly published by the issuing authority. Typically links to FDA warning letters database, EPA enforcement actions, CPSC recall notices, or equivalent public registries. Null if not publicly disclosed.']
  recall_class varchar [note: 'FDA recall classification indicating the health hazard level. Class I: dangerous or defective products that could cause serious health problems or death; Class II: products that might cause temporary health problem or slight threat of serious nature; Class III: products unlikely to cause adverse health reaction but violate FDA regulations. Null if no recall is involved.']
  recall_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the regulatory action mandates a product recall. True for mandatory recalls ordered by the authority; False for actions that do not require recall or for voluntary recalls initiated by the company.']
  received_date date [note: 'Date the company officially received notification of the regulatory action. May differ from action_date due to transmission delays or publication lag.']
  repeat_violation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this regulatory action cites violations that were previously identified in earlier inspections or enforcement actions. True for repeat violations, which typically result in escalated enforcement and higher penalties; False for first-time violations.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date the regulatory authority officially closed or resolved the enforcement action, indicating the company has satisfactorily addressed all cited violations and corrective actions have been verified.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the company must submit a formal response to the regulatory authority. Typically 15-30 days from receipt depending on action type and jurisdiction. Missing this deadline can escalate enforcement.']
  response_submitted_date date [note: 'Date the company submitted its formal response to the regulatory authority addressing the violations or observations cited in the action.']
  responsible_party_email varchar [note: 'Corporate email address of the internal executive or department head accountable for this regulatory action. Used for escalation and executive reporting.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Internal risk assessment score (0-100 scale) evaluating the potential business impact of this regulatory action. Considers factors such as financial exposure, reputational damage, operational disruption, and likelihood of escalation. Higher scores indicate greater risk requiring executive attention.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Regulatory assessment of the seriousness of the violation. Critical: immediate health/safety risk requiring urgent action; Major: significant compliance failure with potential harm; Minor: procedural non-compliance with low risk; Observation: noted deficiency not yet a violation but requiring attention.']
  violation_category varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the regulatory violation cited in the action. GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice) violations relate to production standards; labeling violations involve packaging claims or ingredient declarations; safety violations concern product hazards; environmental violations address EPA compliance; advertising violations involve FTC rules; ingredient violations relate to prohibited or undeclared substances; quality system violations concern QMS (Quality Management System) deficiencies; record-keeping violations involve documentation failures; reporting violations concern mandatory disclosure failures. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: gmp_violation|labeling_violation|safety_violation|environmental_violation|advertising_violation|ingredient_violation|quality_system_violation|record_keeping_violation|reporting_violation — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Records of regulatory authority actions received by the company including warning letters, import alerts, consent decrees, enforcement actions, Form 483 observations, and mandatory recall orders. Captures action type, issuing authority, action date, affected products/facilities, required response deadline, company response submitted date, corrective action plan reference, and resolution status. Critical for executive and legal visibility into regulatory risk.'
}

Table regulatory.cpsc_filing {
  cpsc_filing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the CPSC filing record. Primary key.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product involved in the incident or subject of the filing. Links to product master data.']
  submitted_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the person who submitted the filing. Links to HR system for accountability.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional notes, internal comments, or context regarding the filing, CPSC interactions, or corrective actions.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the corrective action plan, including consumer notification strategy, remedy process, and timeline.']
  corrective_action_type varchar [note: 'Type of corrective action proposed or implemented to address the product hazard. May include multiple remedies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: recall|repair|replacement|refund|warning|product_modification|none_proposed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cpsc_case_number varchar [note: 'Official case number assigned by CPSC upon receipt and acceptance of the filing. Used for all subsequent correspondence and tracking.']
  cpsc_response_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of CPSC response, including any requirements, recommendations, or determinations made by the agency.']
  cpsc_response_received_date date [note: 'Date CPSC provided official response to the filing, including acceptance, request for additional information, or determination.']
  cpsc_response_type varchar [note: 'Type of response received from CPSC regarding the filing. Indicates regulatory action required or determination made.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the CPSC filing record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  defect_description varchar [note: 'Technical description of the product defect or failure mode that creates the hazard. Includes root cause analysis when known.']
  fatality_count varchar [note: 'Number of deaths associated with the product hazard. Triggers immediate CPSC notification and investigation.']
  filing_date date [note: 'Date the filing was officially submitted to CPSC. Must be within 24 hours of knowledge of substantial product hazard per Section 15(b) requirements.']
  filing_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique filing number assigned by CPSC or internally generated for tracking. Format: CPSC-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the CPSC filing. Tracks progression from draft through submission, CPSC review, and final disposition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|accepted|rejected|closed|withdrawn — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  filing_type varchar [note: 'Type of CPSC filing. Section 15(b) reports are mandatory substantial product hazard reports; incident reports capture consumer complaints; SaferProducts.gov submissions are public database entries; voluntary corrective actions and recall notifications document remediation efforts.']
  gtin varchar [note: '14-digit GTIN of the product involved. Global standard for product identification across supply chain.']
  hazard_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the product hazard, defect, or safety issue. Explains how the product can cause harm to consumers.']
  hazard_type varchar [note: 'Primary type of hazard posed by the product defect. Categorizes the nature of risk to consumers. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fire|burn|laceration|choking|chemical_exposure|electrical_shock|fall|entrapment|suffocation|poisoning|other — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  incident_count varchar [note: 'Total number of reported incidents involving the product hazard, including those without injury.']
  incident_date date [note: 'Date the product incident, injury, or hazard event occurred or was first reported by consumer.']
  injury_count varchar [note: 'Number of reported injuries associated with the product hazard. Includes both confirmed and alleged injuries.']
  injury_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of injuries sustained by consumers, including medical treatment required. May include multiple incident summaries.']
  injury_severity varchar [note: 'Highest severity level of injuries reported. Used to assess risk level and prioritize regulatory response.']
  knowledge_date date [note: 'Date the company first obtained information reasonably supporting the conclusion that a product contains a defect which could create a substantial product hazard. Triggers 24-hour reporting obligation under Section 15(b).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the CPSC filing record was last updated. Audit trail for tracking changes throughout the filing lifecycle.']
  legal_review_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether legal counsel has reviewed and approved the filing before submission. Required for Section 15(b) reports.']
  legal_reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the legal counsel who reviewed the filing.']
  lot_batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing lot or batch number of the affected product units. Essential for determining scope of hazard and recall.']
  manufacturing_date_range_end date [note: 'End date of the manufacturing period for affected product units.']
  manufacturing_date_range_start date [note: 'Start date of the manufacturing period for affected product units.']
  population_at_risk varchar [note: 'Description of consumer population particularly vulnerable to the hazard (e.g., children under 3, elderly, pregnant women). Used for targeted warnings and recalls.']
  product_model_number varchar [note: 'Specific model or SKU number of the product involved in the incident. Critical for identifying affected units.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Commercial name of the product involved in the CPSC filing as known to consumers.']
  recall_announced_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a public recall has been announced in conjunction with this filing. True if recall press release issued.']
  recall_announcement_date date [note: 'Date the product recall was publicly announced via CPSC press release and website posting.']
  regulatory_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the regulatory contact for official CPSC correspondence.']
  regulatory_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary regulatory affairs contact responsible for managing this filing and CPSC communications.']
  saferproducts_report_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned to the report in the SaferProducts.gov public database if the filing resulted in a public posting.']
  submitted_by_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the submitter for CPSC correspondence and follow-up communications.']
  submitted_by_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who submitted the filing to CPSC on behalf of the company.']
  supporting_document_count varchar [note: 'Number of supporting documents attached to the filing (test reports, photos, consumer complaints, medical records, etc.).']
  units_affected varchar [note: 'Estimated number of product units in commerce that are subject to the reported hazard. Used to assess population at risk.']
  units_sold varchar [note: 'Total number of units of the affected product sold to consumers. Helps determine recall scope and consumer notification requirements.']
  upc varchar [note: '12-digit UPC barcode of the product involved in the filing. Used for retail identification and tracking.']
  vault_reference_code varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the document management system (Veeva Vault) where all filing documentation is stored.']

  Note: 'CPSC (Consumer Product Safety Commission) filing records for mandatory incident reports, Section 15(b) substantial product hazard reports, and SaferProducts.gov submissions. Captures filing type, filing date, CPSC case number, product involved, hazard description, injury/incident details, population at risk, corrective action proposed, and CPSC response received. Tracks compliance with CPSA reporting obligations.'
}

Table regulatory.epa_registration {
  epa_registration_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the EPA registration record. Primary key for this product.']
  manufacturing_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal facility master record for the manufacturing site producing this registered product. Links EPA registration to internal facility management.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal product master record for the registered product. Links this EPA registration to the company\'s product catalog.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory submission record that resulted in this EPA registration. Links to the submission dossier and supporting documentation.']
  active_ingredient_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of active ingredient(s) in the registered product. Each active ingredient is the chemical substance responsible for the pesticidal or antimicrobial effect. Format: Chemical Name (CAS Number); Chemical Name (CAS Number).']
  active_ingredient_percentage varchar [note: 'Percentage concentration of each active ingredient in the formulation. Corresponds to the active_ingredient_list. Format: percentage values separated by semicolons matching the ingredient list order.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'Date when the EPA registration was cancelled, either voluntarily by the registrant or involuntarily by EPA. Nullable for active registrations.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the EPA registration was cancelled. Includes voluntary cancellation reasons (e.g., product discontinuation, business decision) or involuntary reasons (e.g., safety concerns, data deficiencies, regulatory non-compliance).']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special conditions related to this EPA registration. May include internal notes on registration strategy, EPA correspondence summaries, or special handling instructions.']
  company_number varchar [note: 'Five-digit EPA company number assigned to the registrant. This is the first component of the EPA registration number and identifies the company holding the registration.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this EPA registration record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  data_call_in_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the registrant must submit data or studies in response to an EPA data call-in. Failure to meet this deadline may result in registration cancellation.']
  data_call_in_requirement varchar [note: 'Description of any outstanding data call-in requirements issued by EPA. Data call-ins require registrants to submit additional studies or data to support continued registration. Includes requirement reference number and due date.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the EPA registration becomes legally effective and the product may be manufactured, distributed, and sold under this registration.']
  epa_establishment_number varchar [note: 'EPA establishment number identifying the facility where the product is manufactured. Format: Company Number-State Code-Facility Number (e.g., 12345-CA-001). Required on product labels.']
  epa_registration_number varchar [note: 'Official EPA registration number assigned under FIFRA. Format: Company Number-Product Number-Supplemental Number (e.g., 12345-67 or 12345-67-890). This is the externally-known unique identifier for the registered pesticide or antimicrobial product.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the EPA registration expires and is no longer valid unless renewed. Nullable for registrations without a fixed expiration (subject to periodic review).']
  formulation_type varchar [note: 'Physical form and delivery method of the registered product. Determines packaging, application method, and handling requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: liquid|aerosol|wipe|powder|granular|tablet|gel|paste|ready_to_use|concentrate — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  label_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the current label version was approved by EPA. Critical for compliance tracking and ensuring only approved labels are used.']
  label_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the EPA-approved product label. The label must be approved before the product can be marketed. Approved = label accepted by EPA, Pending Review = submitted awaiting EPA review, Conditionally Approved = approved with minor changes required, Rejected = label not acceptable, Revision Required = EPA requested changes.']
  label_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the currently approved EPA label. Used to track label revisions and ensure correct label is in use for manufacturing and distribution.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this EPA registration record was last updated in the system. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  last_renewal_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent renewal action for this EPA registration. Used to track renewal history and calculate next renewal due date.']
  next_renewal_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the next renewal application must be submitted to EPA to maintain continuous registration status. Critical for compliance planning.']
  product_brand_name varchar [note: 'Commercial brand name under which the product is marketed to consumers. May differ from the registered product name.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'EPA product classification category based on intended use and target pest. Antimicrobial = broad-spectrum microbial control, Disinfectant = kills bacteria/viruses on surfaces, Sanitizer = reduces microbial load, Insecticide = controls insects, Rodenticide = controls rodents, Fungicide = controls fungi, Herbicide = controls weeds. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: antimicrobial|disinfectant|sanitizer|insecticide|rodenticide|fungicide|herbicide — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Official product name as registered with EPA. This is the name that appears on the approved label and in EPA databases.']
  registrant_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the company or entity holding the EPA registration. This is the party legally responsible for the registered product.']
  registration_date date [note: 'Date when the EPA registration was officially granted or approved by the Environmental Protection Agency. This is the principal business event timestamp for this registration record.']
  registration_review_case_number varchar [note: 'EPA case number for the periodic registration review process. EPA reviews all pesticide registrations on a 15-year cycle to ensure they continue to meet safety standards.']
  registration_review_decision_date date [note: 'Date when EPA issued the most recent registration review decision for this product. Used to track compliance with periodic review requirements.']
  registration_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the EPA registration. Active = currently valid and marketable, Pending = application submitted awaiting EPA decision, Approved = EPA approved but not yet effective, Suspended = temporarily not valid, Cancelled = permanently terminated, Expired = validity period ended, Withdrawn = applicant withdrew before approval. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|pending|approved|suspended|cancelled|expired|withdrawn — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  registration_type varchar [note: 'Type of EPA registration action. New = initial registration, Amendment = modification to existing registration, Renewal = periodic renewal, Reregistration = compliance with updated data requirements, Cancellation = voluntary or involuntary termination, Conditional = approved pending additional data, Emergency Exemption = Section 18 emergency use. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new|amendment|renewal|reregistration|cancellation|conditional|emergency_exemption — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary regulatory affairs contact for this EPA registration. Used for internal coordination and EPA correspondence tracking.']
  regulatory_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary regulatory affairs contact responsible for managing this EPA registration. Internal point of contact for registration maintenance and EPA correspondence.']
  reregistration_eligibility_decision varchar [note: 'EPA determination of whether the product is eligible for reregistration under updated safety and environmental standards. Eligible = meets current standards, Ineligible = does not meet standards and requires reformulation or cancellation, Pending = under EPA review, Not Applicable = not subject to reregistration.']
  signal_word varchar [note: 'Required signal word on the product label indicating relative acute toxicity. Danger = highest toxicity (Category I), Warning = moderate toxicity (Category II), Caution = lower toxicity (Category III), None = minimal toxicity (Category IV).']
  target_pest_list varchar [note: 'List of target pests, pathogens, or organisms that the product is registered to control (e.g., bacteria, viruses, mold, ants, cockroaches). Defines the approved efficacy claims.']
  use_classification varchar [note: 'EPA classification determining who may purchase and apply the product. General Use = available to general public, Restricted Use = requires certified applicator due to potential hazards to environment or applicator.']
  use_site_description varchar [note: 'Description of approved use sites and application locations for the registered product (e.g., household surfaces, food contact surfaces, hospitals, schools, outdoor residential). Defines where the product may legally be applied.']
  vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the EPA registration documents stored in the regulatory document vault system (e.g., Veeva Vault). Links to approved labels, safety data sheets, and registration correspondence.']

  Note: 'EPA pesticide and antimicrobial product registration records under FIFRA (Federal Insecticide, Fungicide, and Rodenticide Act). Captures EPA registration number, product name, active ingredient(s), registration type (new, amendment, renewal, cancellation), registration date, expiry date, use classification (general/restricted), label approval status, and data call-in requirements. Applicable to household disinfectants, insecticides, and antimicrobial cleaning products.'
}

Table regulatory.change {
  change_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the regulatory change record.']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.jurisdiction. Business justification: Regulatory changes are tied to a specific jurisdiction; linking enables consistent jurisdiction lookup and removes the duplicated code column.']
  regulatory_change_responsible_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Regulatory change implementation is owned by a specific employee; linking records ownership for change management.']
  superseded_change_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on change (superseded_change_id)']
  affected_product_category varchar [note: 'Product category or categories impacted by the regulatory change.']
  affected_product_scope varchar [note: 'Scope description of the products impacted (e.g., all SKUs in a brand, specific formulations).']
