  change_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the regulatory change.']
  change_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the regulatory change, including scope and rationale.']
  change_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned by the regulatory authority or internal tracking system.']
  change_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the change (regulatory authority, industry body, court decision).']
  change_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the regulatory change record.']
  change_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the change (new regulation, amendment, repeal, guidance update).']
  compliance_risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk rating associated with non‑compliance to the change.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the regulatory change record was first created.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the regulatory change becomes effective for the business.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the regulatory change ceases to be applicable, if applicable.']
  impact_assessment_date date [note: 'Date the impact assessment was completed or recorded.']
  impact_assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the impact assessment process.']
  impact_severity varchar [note: 'Severity level of the regulatory impact on the business.']
  implementation_end_date date [note: 'Date when implementation activities were completed.']
  implementation_start_date date [note: 'Date when implementation activities began.']
  implementation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the implementation of required actions.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether compliance with the change is mandatory (true) or optional (false).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the regulatory change record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments regarding the change.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the change for planning and resource allocation.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Citation or reference number of the regulation (e.g., CFR section, EU directive).']
  required_action_deadline date [note: 'Latest date by which required compliance actions must be taken.']
  source_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory authority issuing the change (e.g., FDA, EPA).']
  source_body varchar [note: 'Governing body or standard organization responsible for the change.']
  title varchar [note: 'Brief title summarizing the regulatory change.']
  url varchar [note: 'Link to the official document or announcement describing the change.']

  Note: 'Tracks new, amended, or repealed regulations and standards that may impact the company\'s products or operations. Captures change source (regulatory authority, industry body), change type (new regulation, amendment, repeal, guidance update), affected product categories, affected jurisdictions, impact assessment status, required action deadline, responsible regulatory affairs owner, and implementation status. Drives proactive regulatory intelligence and ensures timely compliance adaptation.'
}

Table workforce.employee {
  employee_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the employee record. Primary key for the employee entity.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee ID of the direct manager or supervisor to whom this employee reports. Used for organizational hierarchy and reporting structure.']
  business_unit varchar [note: 'High-level organizational division or business unit to which the employee belongs (e.g., Manufacturing, Sales, R&D, Supply Chain).']
  cba_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or name of the collective bargaining agreement that governs the employee\'s terms and conditions, if applicable. Null for non-union employees.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled end date of the current employment contract. Null for permanent or at-will contracts without a defined end date.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the current employment contract or agreement.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Type of employment contract governing the employee\'s terms: permanent (indefinite), fixed-term (specific end date), at-will (terminable by either party), or project-based (tied to specific project completion).']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost center to which the employee\'s compensation and expenses are allocated. Used for budgeting and financial reporting.']
  department varchar [note: 'Specific department within the business unit where the employee works (e.g., Quality Control, Brand Marketing, Procurement).']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary corporate email address assigned to the employee for business communication.']
  emergency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the person to be contacted in case of an emergency involving the employee.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the emergency contact person.']
  emergency_contact_relationship varchar [note: 'Relationship of the emergency contact to the employee (e.g., spouse, parent, sibling, friend).']
  employment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the employee: active (working), inactive (not currently working), leave (on approved leave), terminated (employment ended), suspended (temporarily not working), or probation (trial period).']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the employee\'s working arrangement: full-time, part-time, contract, seasonal, temporary, or intern.']
  flsa_classification varchar [note: 'Classification under the Fair Labor Standards Act indicating whether the employee is exempt or non-exempt from overtime pay requirements.']
  hire_date date [note: 'Date the employee was originally hired by the organization. Used for tenure calculations, benefits eligibility, and anniversary tracking.']
  job_code varchar [note: 'Standardized internal code representing the job classification or position type. Used for compensation benchmarking, workforce planning, and reporting.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Official job title or position name assigned to the employee (e.g., Manufacturing Supervisor, Sales Representative, Quality Analyst).']
  legal_first_name varchar [note: 'Employee\'s legal first name as it appears on government-issued identification and payroll records.']
  legal_last_name varchar [note: 'Employee\'s legal last name (surname/family name) as it appears on government-issued identification and payroll records.']
  legal_middle_name varchar [note: 'Employee\'s legal middle name or initial as it appears on government-issued identification.']
  national_id_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued national identification number (e.g., Social Security Number in USA, National Insurance Number in UK). Used for payroll, tax reporting, and legal compliance.']
  notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days of advance notice required for voluntary resignation or termination as specified in the employment contract.']
  pay_grade varchar [note: 'Compensation grade or band assigned to the employee\'s position. Used for salary administration and compensation planning.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the employee (mobile or desk phone).']
  plant_site_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the physical manufacturing plant, distribution center, office location, or field territory where the employee is primarily assigned.']
  preferred_name varchar [note: 'Name the employee prefers to be called in day-to-day business interactions, which may differ from legal name.']
  probation_end_date date [note: 'Date when the employee\'s probationary period ends. Null if probation has been completed or does not apply.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this employee record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this employee record was last modified in the system.']
  shift_pattern varchar [note: 'Standard shift schedule or rotation pattern assigned to the employee (e.g., day shift, night shift, rotating 3-shift, 4-on-4-off). Particularly relevant for manufacturing and distribution center workers.']
  standard_working_hours_per_week decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contractual number of hours the employee is expected to work per week (e.g., 40.00 for full-time, 20.00 for part-time).']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the employee\'s employment ended. Null for active employees. Used for offboarding, final payroll, and compliance reporting.']
  termination_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for employment termination (e.g., voluntary resignation, involuntary termination, retirement, end of contract). Null for active employees.']
  union_membership_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is a member of a labor union or covered by a collective bargaining agreement.']
  union_name varchar [note: 'Name of the labor union to which the employee belongs, if applicable. Null for non-union employees.']
  work_location_city varchar [note: 'City where the employee\'s primary work location is situated.']
  work_location_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the country where the employee primarily works (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU).']
  work_location_state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region where the employee\'s primary work location is situated.']
  workday_worker_code varchar [note: 'External system identifier from Workday HCM. Used for integration and reconciliation with the source system of record.']

  Note: 'Core master record for every worker across manufacturing plants, commercial offices, distribution centers, and field sales teams. Stores legal name, employee ID, national ID, employment type (full-time, part-time, contract, seasonal), hire date, termination date, employment status, job title, job code, cost center, business unit, plant/site assignment, manager reference, pay grade, FLSA classification, union membership flag, Workday HCM worker ID, contract terms (type, notice period, probation, working hours, shift pattern, CBA reference), emergency contact, and employment action history (hire, promotion, transfer, termination, LOA with effective dates and reason codes). SSOT for all workforce identity, employment lifecycle, and worker demographic data integrated from Workday HCM.'
}

Table workforce.org_unit {
  org_unit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the organizational unit. Primary key for the org_unit data product.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost center associated with this organizational unit for financial tracking and labor cost allocation.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who is the responsible manager or leader of this organizational unit.']
  parent_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent organizational unit in the hierarchy. Enables multi-level org hierarchy traversal for reporting and analytics. Null for top-level units.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Annual budget allocation for this organizational unit in the reporting currency. Used for financial planning and variance analysis.']
  budget_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the budget amount. Supports multi-currency financial consolidation.']
  business_unit_code varchar [note: 'Business unit or division code for management reporting and performance analysis. Supports segment reporting and profitability analysis.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Legal entity or company code to which this organizational unit belongs. Used for statutory reporting and financial consolidation.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where this organizational unit is domiciled. Used for regulatory compliance and labor law adherence.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that created this organizational unit record. Supports audit trail and accountability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this organizational unit record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  current_headcount varchar [note: 'Current number of employees assigned to this organizational unit. Updated periodically for workforce analytics and reporting.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this organizational unit ceased or will cease to be active. Null for currently active units. Enables point-in-time org structure reconstruction.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this organizational unit became or will become active and operational. Supports temporal hierarchy analysis and historical reporting.']
  external_code varchar [note: 'External system identifier or legacy code for this organizational unit. Used for integration with third-party systems and data migration tracking.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code for posting labor costs and expenses associated with this organizational unit. Supports financial consolidation and reporting.']
  headcount_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum planned headcount capacity for this organizational unit. Used in workforce planning and S&OP labor capacity analysis.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level of this organizational unit in the enterprise hierarchy. Level 1 is top-level, increasing numbers indicate deeper nesting. Supports hierarchy traversal and reporting.']
  is_commercial_unit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this organizational unit is a commercial or sales unit. Used to segment commercial workforce for sales force automation and trade promotion planning.']
  is_manufacturing_unit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this organizational unit is a manufacturing or production unit. Used to segment manufacturing workforce from commercial operations.']
  is_support_function boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this organizational unit is a corporate support function (HR, Finance, IT, Legal). Used for overhead allocation and indirect labor analysis.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified this organizational unit record. Supports change management and compliance.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this organizational unit record was last updated. Supports change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Physical location or site code where this organizational unit operates. Used for facility management and workforce planning.']
  org_unit_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the organizational unit. Used in reporting, financial systems, and operational processes. Must be unique within the enterprise.']
  org_unit_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the organizational units purpose, responsibilities, and scope within the enterprise.']
  org_unit_name varchar [note: 'Full business name of the organizational unit as it appears in corporate communications and reporting.']
  org_unit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the organizational unit. Active units are operational and can have employees assigned. Inactive or closed units are historical.']
  org_unit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the organizational unit indicating its role in the enterprise hierarchy. Supports segmentation for headcount reporting and S&OP workforce planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: department|division|business_unit|plant|region|team|cost_center|function|subsidiary|branch — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant or production facility code for plant-level organizational units. Supports MES integration and production workforce planning.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region code for regional organizational units. Supports geographic segmentation in sales and operations planning.']
  safety_classification varchar [note: 'OSHA safety risk classification for this organizational unit based on work environment and hazard exposure. Used for safety incident tracking and compliance reporting.']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or short name of the organizational unit for display in constrained UI contexts and reports.']
  union_representation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employees in this organizational unit are represented by a labor union. Used for labor compliance and collective bargaining agreement tracking.']

  Note: 'Organizational hierarchy unit representing departments, divisions, business units, cost centers, and plant-level teams within the consumer goods enterprise. Stores org unit code, name, org unit type (department, division, plant, region, team), parent org unit reference, effective date range, cost center code, GL account mapping, and responsible manager. Supports multi-level org hierarchy traversal for headcount reporting, labor cost allocation, and S&OP workforce planning.'
}

Table workforce.position {
  position_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the approved headcount position within the organizational structure. Primary key.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost center that bears the financial responsibility for this position\'s compensation and benefits.']
  job_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the standardized job profile that defines the role responsibilities, competencies, and requirements for this position.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary work location (plant, site, office, distribution center) where this position is based.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the organizational unit (department, division, or business unit) to which this position belongs.']
  requisition_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the active recruitment requisition for this position if it is currently vacant and being recruited for.']
  supervisor_position_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the position that supervises this position, establishing the reporting hierarchy within the organizational structure.']
  budgeted_headcount decimal(5,2) [note: 'The approved headcount allocation for this position in the annual workforce budget. May differ from FTE for shared or split positions.']
  compensation_grade varchar [note: 'The compensation grade or pay band assigned to this position, defining the salary range and compensation structure.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this position record was first created in the system.']
  critical_position_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this position is designated as business-critical, requiring succession planning and priority filling due to its impact on operations.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the salary range (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when this position was or will be eliminated or made inactive. Null for positions with no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when this position became active and available for assignment within the organizational structure.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the position based on working hours commitment (full-time, part-time, casual, or on-call).']
  flsa_classification varchar [note: 'Classification under the U.S. Fair Labor Standards Act indicating whether the position is exempt or non-exempt from overtime pay requirements.']
  fte_allocation decimal(5,4) [note: 'The full-time equivalent allocation for this position expressed as a decimal (1.0000 = full-time, 0.5000 = half-time). Used for workforce planning and labor budget calculations.']
  job_family varchar [note: 'Broad occupational category or job family to which this position belongs (e.g., Manufacturing Operations, Supply Chain, Sales, Quality Assurance, R&D).']
  job_level varchar [note: 'Hierarchical level or grade of the position within the organization\'s job architecture (e.g., entry-level, mid-level, senior, executive).']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'The user or system identifier of the person who last modified this position record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this position record was last updated or modified.']
  max_salary_range decimal(15,2) [note: 'The maximum annual salary for this position based on its compensation grade and market data.']
  min_salary_range decimal(15,2) [note: 'The minimum annual salary for this position based on its compensation grade and market data.']
  minimum_education_level varchar [note: 'The minimum educational qualification required for this position (e.g., High School Diploma, Bachelor\'s Degree, Master\'s Degree).']
  minimum_experience_years varchar [note: 'The minimum number of years of relevant work experience required for this position.']
  occupancy_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the position is currently filled by an employee, vacant and available for recruitment, or partially filled (for positions with multiple incumbents or job-sharing arrangements).']
  position_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the position, typically a human-readable alphanumeric code used across HR systems and organizational charts.']
  position_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the position indicating whether it is active and available for filling, frozen due to budget constraints, eliminated, proposed for future creation, or pending approval.']
  position_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the position based on employment duration and nature (permanent full-time, temporary, seasonal for peak production, contract, or internship).']
  purpose varchar [note: 'A brief statement describing the primary purpose and key responsibilities of this position within the organization.']
  remote_work_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this position is eligible for remote or hybrid work arrangements.']
  safety_sensitive_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this position is classified as safety-sensitive under OSHA regulations, requiring additional safety training, certifications, and compliance monitoring.']
  title varchar [note: 'Official title of the position as it appears in organizational documentation and job postings.']
  travel_requirement_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The expected percentage of time this position requires business travel, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 25.00 for 25% travel).']
  union_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this position is eligible for union membership or covered by collective bargaining agreements.']
  work_shift varchar [note: 'The primary work shift assigned to this position, particularly relevant for manufacturing and distribution operations.']

  Note: 'Approved headcount position within the organizational structure representing a budgeted role slot. Stores position code, position title, job profile, org unit, plant/site, position type (permanent, temporary, seasonal), FTE allocation, budgeted headcount, filled/vacant status, effective date, and requisition linkage. Enables workforce planning, vacancy tracking, and labor budget management aligned with Workday HCM Position Management.'
}

Table workforce.job_profile {
  job_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the job profile. Primary key. Serves as the reference template for position creation, talent acquisition, compensation management, and performance evaluation across the CPG enterprise.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Default cost center for labor expense allocation for positions created from this profile. Used for financial planning and budget management.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supervisory organization or department to which positions created from this profile typically report. Links to organizational hierarchy.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when this job profile was formally approved for use in the organization. Required for SOX compliance and internal controls.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the HR business partner or executive who approved this job profile for use. Supports governance and accountability.']
  career_path varchar [note: 'Typical progression trajectory for employees in this role, including potential next-level positions and lateral moves. Supports talent development and retention strategies.']
  competency_profile varchar [note: 'Structured list of technical, functional, and behavioral competencies required for success in the role. Used for talent assessment, development planning, and succession management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this job profile record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this job profile version was superseded or retired. Null for currently active profiles. Supports historical analysis and compliance auditing.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this job profile version became active and available for use in position creation and talent management processes.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Standard employment arrangement for positions created from this profile. Determines benefits eligibility, work schedule expectations, and labor cost classification.']
  flsa_classification varchar [note: 'Federal classification determining overtime eligibility and wage requirements under U.S. labor law. Exempt roles are salaried and not eligible for overtime; non-exempt roles are eligible for overtime pay.']
  gmp_training_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the role requires completion of GMP training due to involvement in manufacturing, quality control, or product handling activities. Critical for FDA and ISO 22716 compliance.']
  incentive_type varchar [note: 'Type of variable compensation plan applicable to this job profile. Determines incentive calculation methodology and payout frequency. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: none|annual_bonus|quarterly_bonus|sales_commission|profit_sharing|long_term_incentive|stock_options — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  job_description varchar [note: 'Comprehensive narrative describing the purpose, responsibilities, and scope of the role. Used for recruitment, performance management, and organizational design.']
  job_family varchar [note: 'Broad occupational category grouping similar roles across the organization. Aligns with core business processes in consumer goods manufacturing and distribution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: manufacturing|quality|supply_chain|sales|marketing|finance|procurement|research_development|regulatory|human_resources|information_technology|legal|customer_service — 13 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  job_level varchar [note: 'Hierarchical level of the role within the organizational structure. Determines scope of responsibility, decision-making authority, and career progression path. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: entry|intermediate|senior|lead|manager|senior_manager|director|senior_director|vice_president|senior_vice_president|executive — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  job_profile_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code identifying the job profile template. Used for integration with Workday HCM and cross-system reference.']
  job_profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the job profile. Active profiles are available for position creation; inactive or obsolete profiles are retained for historical reference only.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Official job title as it appears in organizational documentation, offer letters, and employee records. Human-readable label for the role.']
  key_responsibilities varchar [note: 'Structured list of primary duties and accountabilities for the role. Defines expected outcomes and performance standards.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this job profile record was most recently updated. Supports change tracking and version control.']
  management_level varchar [note: 'Classification indicating whether the role has direct reports and the span of management responsibility. Critical for organizational design and succession planning.']
  merit_increase_guideline_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard annual merit increase percentage for satisfactory performance. Used for compensation planning and budget forecasting.']
  pay_grade varchar [note: 'Compensation band code defining the salary range and incentive structure for this job profile. Used for internal equity analysis and market benchmarking.']
  physical_demand_level varchar [note: 'Classification of physical exertion required for the role based on U.S. Department of Labor standards. Used for ADA compliance, workplace accommodations, and safety planning.']
  remote_work_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether positions created from this profile are eligible for remote or hybrid work arrangements. Reflects operational requirements and organizational policy.']
  required_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of professional certifications, licenses, or credentials required for the role. May include industry-specific certifications such as Six Sigma, PMP, CPA, or regulatory certifications.']
  required_education_level varchar [note: 'Minimum educational qualification required for candidates to be considered for positions created from this profile. Used for talent acquisition screening.']
  required_years_experience varchar [note: 'Minimum number of years of relevant professional experience required for the role. Used for candidate qualification and internal mobility eligibility.']
  safety_sensitive_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the role is classified as safety-sensitive, requiring adherence to OSHA standards, drug testing, and enhanced safety protocols. Applies to manufacturing, warehouse, and equipment operation roles.']
  salary_range_maximum decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum annual base salary in USD for positions created from this profile. Represents ceiling for exceptional performers or long-tenured employees.']
  salary_range_midpoint decimal(15,2) [note: 'Midpoint annual base salary in USD representing market-competitive compensation for fully competent performance in the role. Target for experienced incumbents.']
  salary_range_minimum decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum annual base salary in USD for positions created from this profile. Represents entry point for new hires or internal transfers into the role.']
  target_bonus_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target annual incentive bonus as a percentage of base salary for on-target performance. Used for total compensation calculations and offer letter generation.']
  travel_requirement_percentage varchar [note: 'Expected percentage of work time requiring business travel. Used for candidate expectations, expense budgeting, and work-life balance considerations.']

  Note: 'Standardized job profile defining the role template, competency requirements, compensation structure, and eligibility criteria for a class of positions across the CPG enterprise. Stores job profile code, job family, job level, FLSA classification, management level, required certifications, GMP training requirements, physical demand level, compensation plan details (pay grade, salary range min/mid/max, target bonus percentage, merit guidelines, incentive type), and job description. Used as the reference template for position creation, talent acquisition, compensation management, and performance evaluation.'
}

Table workforce.payroll_record {
  payroll_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payroll record. Primary key for this entity.']
  payroll_run_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the payroll processing batch or run that generated this record. Groups all payroll records processed together.']
  primary_payroll_approved_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved this payroll record for payment. Required for SOX compliance and audit trail.']
  base_salary_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The base salary or hourly wage amount earned during this pay period, before any additional compensation or deductions.']
  bonus_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Discretionary or performance-based bonus payments included in this payroll period. May include annual bonuses, spot awards, or incentive payments.']
  commission_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sales commission earnings for this pay period. Applicable to sales force and commercial roles with variable compensation plans.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost center to which this payroll expense is allocated. Used for departmental expense tracking and financial reporting.']
  federal_tax_withheld_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Federal income tax withheld from gross pay based on employee W-4 elections and IRS withholding tables.']
  garnishment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Court-ordered or legally mandated wage garnishments deducted from gross pay. Includes child support, tax levies, creditor garnishments, and student loan garnishments.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code for posting this payroll expense. Links payroll to financial accounting system.']
  gross_pay_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross compensation before any deductions. Sum of base salary, overtime, shift differential, bonus, commission, and other earnings.']
  health_insurance_deduction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Employee contribution toward health insurance premiums deducted from gross pay. May include medical, dental, and vision coverage.']
  local_tax_withheld_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Local or municipal income tax withheld from gross pay where applicable. Includes city, county, or school district taxes.']
  medicare_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Employee portion of Medicare tax (FICA) withheld from gross pay. Calculated at statutory rate with no wage base limit.']
  net_pay_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final take-home pay amount disbursed to the employee after all deductions. The actual amount paid to the employee.']
  other_benefit_deduction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total of all other voluntary benefit deductions including life insurance, disability insurance, FSA, HSA, dependent care, and other employee-elected benefits.']
  overtime_hours_worked decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total overtime hours worked during this pay period. Includes time-and-a-half and double-time hours per FLSA regulations.']
  overtime_pay_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total overtime compensation earned during this pay period. Includes time-and-a-half and double-time calculations per FLSA regulations.']
  pay_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this payroll record. Supports multi-country payroll operations.']
  pay_date date [note: 'The date on which the net pay is disbursed to the employee. The actual payment date for this payroll record.']
  pay_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which the employee is paid. Determines the cadence of payroll processing for this employee.']
  pay_period_end_date date [note: 'The last date of the pay period covered by this payroll record. Defines the end of the work period for which compensation is calculated.']
  pay_period_start_date date [note: 'The first date of the pay period covered by this payroll record. Defines the beginning of the work period for which compensation is calculated.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which net pay is disbursed to the employee. Defines the payment instrument used for this payroll payment.']
  payroll_approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payroll record was approved for payment. Captures the approval event in the payroll workflow.']
  payroll_processed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payroll record was calculated and processed by the payroll system. Audit timestamp for payroll processing.']
  payroll_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of this payroll record. Tracks the payroll record through calculation, approval, payment, and potential reversal workflows.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payroll record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payroll record was last modified. Audit field for tracking changes and data lineage.']
  regular_hours_worked decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total regular work hours during this pay period, excluding overtime. Used for hourly employee pay calculations.']
  retirement_contribution_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Employee pre-tax or Roth contribution to retirement savings plan (401k, 403b, etc.) deducted from gross pay.']
  shift_differential_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Additional compensation for working non-standard shifts such as night, weekend, or holiday shifts. Common in manufacturing operations.']
  social_security_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Employee portion of Social Security tax (FICA) withheld from gross pay. Calculated at statutory rate up to annual wage base limit.']
  state_tax_withheld_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'State income tax withheld from gross pay based on employee state tax elections and state withholding requirements.']
  total_deductions_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all deductions from gross pay including taxes, benefits, and garnishments. Gross pay minus total deductions equals net pay.']
  work_location_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the physical work location or facility where the employee worked during this pay period. Important for multi-site operations and tax jurisdiction determination.']
  year_to_date_federal_tax decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative federal income tax withheld from the beginning of the calendar year through this pay period. Used for tax reporting and W-2 preparation.']
  year_to_date_gross_pay decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative gross pay for the employee from the beginning of the calendar year through this pay period. Used for tax reporting and W-2 preparation.']
  year_to_date_medicare_tax decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative Medicare tax withheld from the beginning of the calendar year through this pay period. Used for W-2 preparation.']
  year_to_date_social_security_tax decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative Social Security tax withheld from the beginning of the calendar year through this pay period. Used for W-2 preparation and wage base limit tracking.']
  year_to_date_state_tax decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative state income tax withheld from the beginning of the calendar year through this pay period. Used for state tax reporting.']

  Note: 'Periodic payroll processing record capturing gross-to-net pay calculation results for each employee pay period. Stores pay period start/end dates, pay frequency (weekly, bi-weekly, semi-monthly, monthly), gross pay, base salary, overtime pay, shift differential, bonus, commission, total deductions, net pay, tax withholdings (federal, state, local), benefit deductions, garnishments, pay currency, and payroll run status. Sourced from Workday HCM Payroll module.'
}

Table workforce.benefit_enrollment {
  benefit_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the benefit enrollment record. Primary key for the benefit enrollment entity.']
  primary_benefit_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the employee who enrolled in the benefit plan. Links to the employee master record in Workday HCM.']
  annual_election_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total annual dollar amount elected by the employee for this benefit plan. Applicable to FSA (Flexible Spending Account), HSA (Health Savings Account), and retirement contribution elections.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this benefit enrollment requires HR or management approval before becoming effective. True for enrollments that exceed standard limits or require special authorization.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status for benefit enrollments requiring authorization. Indicates whether approval is not required, pending review, approved, or rejected by the approving authority.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the benefit enrollment was approved by the authorized approver. Used for audit trail and compliance documentation.']
  beneficiary_designation varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the designated beneficiary for life insurance and retirement benefit plans. Indicates who will receive benefits in the event of employee death.']
  benefit_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Unique code identifying the insurance carrier or benefit provider administering this plan (e.g., Aetna, Blue Cross, Fidelity). Used for carrier file feeds and reconciliation.']
  benefit_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the benefit type. Used for grouping and reporting benefit elections across different plan types. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: health|dental|vision|life_insurance|disability|retirement|fsa|hsa|supplemental|wellness — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  benefit_plan_code varchar [note: 'Unique code identifying the specific benefit plan (e.g., health, dental, vision, life insurance, 401k). Standardized plan identifier used across HR systems.']
  carrier_member_number varchar [note: 'Unique member identifier assigned by the benefit carrier to the enrolled employee. Used on insurance cards and for claims adjudication.']
  carrier_policy_number varchar [note: 'Policy or contract number assigned by the benefit carrier for this specific enrollment. Used for claims processing and carrier communication.']
  cobra_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this benefit enrollment is eligible for COBRA continuation coverage upon qualifying event (termination, reduction in hours). True if plan is subject to COBRA regulations.']
  cobra_qualifying_event_date date [note: 'Date of the qualifying event that triggered COBRA eligibility (e.g., termination date, reduction in hours date). Used to calculate COBRA election and coverage periods.']
  contribution_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for employee and employer contribution amounts. Supports multi-country workforce with localized benefit plans.']
  contribution_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which employee and employer contributions are deducted and remitted for this benefit enrollment. Aligns with payroll processing schedule.']
  coverage_end_date date [note: 'Last date of active benefit coverage under this enrollment. Populated when enrollment is terminated, employee separates, or coverage is cancelled. Null for active ongoing enrollments.']
  coverage_start_date date [note: 'First date of active benefit coverage under this enrollment. May differ from enrollment effective date based on plan waiting periods or carrier processing timelines.']
  coverage_tier varchar [note: 'Level of coverage elected by the employee indicating who is covered under the benefit plan (employee only, employee plus spouse, employee plus children, or full family coverage).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this benefit enrollment record was first created in the system. Represents the initial enrollment submission or data load timestamp.']
  deduction_code varchar [note: 'Payroll deduction code used to process employee contributions for this benefit enrollment. Maps to payroll system deduction configuration.']
  dependent_count varchar [note: 'Number of eligible dependents covered under this benefit enrollment. Used for coverage tier validation and premium calculation.']
  employee_contribution_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Dollar amount contributed by the employee per pay period for this benefit plan. Deducted from employee paycheck on a pre-tax or post-tax basis depending on plan type.']
  employer_contribution_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Dollar amount contributed by the employer per pay period for this benefit plan. Represents company subsidy or match for employee benefit coverage.']
  enrollment_confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Unique confirmation number generated upon successful benefit enrollment submission. Provided to employee as proof of enrollment and used for tracking and audit purposes.']
  enrollment_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the benefit enrollment becomes effective and coverage begins. Must align with plan rules and qualifying event timelines per regulatory requirements.']
  enrollment_event_type varchar [note: 'Type of event that triggered the benefit enrollment or change. Includes new hire enrollment, annual open enrollment period, qualifying life events (marriage, birth, adoption), or other triggering events per IRS regulations.']
  enrollment_source varchar [note: 'Channel or system through which the benefit enrollment was submitted. Indicates whether enrollment was completed via employee self-service portal, HR administrator, benefits vendor portal, or other method.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the benefit enrollment. Indicates whether the enrollment is active, pending approval, cancelled, terminated, suspended, or waived by the employee.']
  evidence_of_insurability_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee must provide evidence of insurability (medical underwriting) for this benefit enrollment. Typically required for life insurance or disability coverage above guaranteed issue amounts.']
  evidence_of_insurability_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the evidence of insurability review process. Indicates whether medical underwriting is not required, pending carrier review, approved, denied, or incomplete awaiting additional information.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this benefit enrollment record was last updated. Tracks any changes to enrollment details, status updates, or data corrections.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this benefit enrollment. Used by HR administrators to document exceptions, special circumstances, or processing details.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the source system from which this benefit enrollment record originated (e.g., WORKDAY_HCM, BENEFITS_PORTAL, CARRIER_FEED). Used for data lineage and integration tracking.']
  tax_treatment varchar [note: 'Tax treatment classification for employee contributions indicating whether deductions are taken on a pre-tax basis (Section 125), post-tax basis, Roth basis, or fully employer-paid with no employee contribution.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for termination of the benefit enrollment. Common reasons include employee separation, plan cancellation, coverage change, loss of eligibility, or voluntary termination by employee.']
  termination_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the benefit enrollment was terminated in the system. Used for audit trail and to track when coverage cessation was processed.']
  waiver_date date [note: 'Date when the employee formally waived or declined benefit coverage. Used for compliance tracking and audit trails.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided by employee for waiving or declining benefit coverage. Common reasons include coverage through spouse, alternative coverage, or cost considerations. Required for compliance documentation.']

  Note: 'Employee benefit enrollment record capturing active benefit plan elections across health, dental, vision, life insurance, disability, retirement (401k/pension), FSA, HSA, and supplemental benefits. Stores enrollment effective date, benefit plan code, coverage tier (employee only, employee+spouse, family), employee contribution amount, employer contribution amount, coverage start/end dates, enrollment event type (new hire, open enrollment, life event), and benefit carrier code.'
}

Table workforce.time_entry {
  time_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the time entry record. Primary key for individual time, attendance, and absence events.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee ID of the manager or supervisor who approved this time entry. Used for audit trail and approval workflow tracking.']
  payroll_period_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the payroll period to which this time entry belongs. Used to group time entries for payroll processing and financial period closing.']
  primary_time_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the employee who submitted this time entry. Links to employee master data in Workday HCM.']
  absence_end_date date [note: 'The last date of the absence period for leave requests. Used to calculate total absence duration and expected return-to-work date.']
  absence_start_date date [note: 'The first date of the absence period for leave requests. Used for multi-day absence tracking and leave balance calculations.']
  absence_type varchar [note: 'Specific type of absence when entry_type is absence or leave. Critical for leave compliance tracking, FMLA reporting, and workforce availability planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sick|vacation|fmla|parental|bereavement|jury_duty|military|unpaid|other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the time entry or absence request. Tracks workflow state from submission through manager approval to payroll processing.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact timestamp when this time entry was approved by the manager. Critical for payroll cutoff and audit compliance.']
  break_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of unpaid breaks taken during this shift in minutes. Deducted from total hours for payroll calculations and labor law compliance.']
  clock_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact timestamp when the employee clocked in or started their shift. Captured from time clock systems, mobile apps, or manual entry.']
  clock_out_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact timestamp when the employee clocked out or ended their shift. Used to calculate total hours worked.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or notes about this time entry. May include explanations for late entries, absence reasons, or special circumstances.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost center code to which this time entry\'s labor costs should be allocated. Used for financial reporting and departmental cost tracking.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the organizational department where the employee worked during this time entry. Used for departmental labor cost tracking and workforce analytics.']
  entry_date date [note: 'The calendar date for which this time entry applies. Represents the business date of the work performed or absence recorded.']
  entry_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the time entry indicating whether it represents hours worked, an absence request, approved leave, or other time-off category. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: worked|absence|leave|holiday|training|jury_duty|bereavement — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  fmla_case_number varchar [note: 'Unique case identifier for FMLA leave tracking when this absence is part of an approved FMLA claim. Used for regulatory reporting and leave balance management.']
  fmla_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this absence qualifies for FMLA protection. Critical for regulatory compliance and leave tracking under federal labor law.']
  holiday_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this time entry falls on a company-recognized holiday. Used for holiday pay calculations and premium pay eligibility.']
  incident_case_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the safety incident case number if this absence is related to a workplace injury or illness. Links to OSHA incident records.']
  labor_category_code varchar [note: 'Classification code for the type of labor performed during this time entry. Used for labor cost analysis, union reporting, and job costing in manufacturing.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this time entry record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit compliance.']
  meal_break_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Duration of meal break taken during this shift in minutes. Tracked separately for compliance with state labor laws requiring meal periods.']
  osha_recordable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this absence is related to an OSHA recordable injury or illness. Critical for OSHA 300 log reporting and workplace safety compliance.']
  overtime_hours_1_5x decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total number of overtime hours worked at time-and-a-half pay rate. Typically applies to hours beyond 40 per week or 8 per day depending on jurisdiction.']
  overtime_hours_2x decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total number of overtime hours worked at double-time pay rate. Typically applies to holidays, seventh consecutive day, or hours beyond daily thresholds.']
  paid_time_off_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of PTO hours used for this time entry when employee takes paid leave. Deducted from employee\'s PTO balance and included in payroll.']
  payroll_processed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this time entry has been processed in payroll. Prevents duplicate processing and enables payroll reconciliation.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the manufacturing plant or facility where the employee worked during this time entry. Critical for OTIF workforce planning and labor cost allocation.']
  production_line_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific production line or work center assignment for this time entry. Used for manufacturing labor cost tracking and efficiency analysis.']
  regular_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total number of regular hours worked during this time entry period, excluding overtime. Used for standard payroll calculations and labor cost tracking.']
  return_to_work_date date [note: 'The date the employee is expected to return to work following an absence. Used for workforce planning and OSHA injury/illness return-to-work tracking.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the work shift for this time entry. Used for shift differential pay calculations and workforce planning in manufacturing operations.']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this time entry was originally submitted by the employee or supervisor. Used for audit trail and late submission tracking.']
  time_entry_source varchar [note: 'The system or method through which this time entry was captured. Used for data quality tracking and audit purposes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: time_clock|manual|mobile_app|web_portal|biometric|badge_swipe|supervisor_entry|import — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  union_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the labor union affiliation for this employee\'s time entry. Used for union reporting, collective bargaining compliance, and labor relations.']
  unpaid_time_off_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of unpaid hours for this time entry when employee takes unpaid leave. Excluded from payroll calculations but tracked for attendance and compliance.']
  weekend_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this time entry falls on a weekend day. Used for weekend shift differential pay and scheduling analytics.']
  work_order_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing work order or production order number to which this time entry\'s labor hours should be allocated. Used for job costing and production variance analysis.']

  Note: 'Individual time, attendance, and absence record capturing hours worked, leave requests, and absence events for each employee per shift or day. Stores entry date, clock-in/clock-out timestamps, regular hours, overtime hours (OT1.5x, OT2x), shift type (day, evening, night, weekend), plant/line assignment, entry type (worked, absence request, leave), absence type (sick, vacation, FMLA, parental, bereavement, jury duty, military), requested start/end dates, approval status, approver, FMLA eligibility flag, time entry source (time clock, manual, mobile), and return-to-work date. Critical for manufacturing labor cost tracking, OTIF workforce planning, leave compliance, and OSHA reporting.'
}

Table workforce.recruiting_requisition {
  recruiting_requisition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for recruiting_requisition']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost center that will bear the financial burden of the new hire\'s compensation and benefits.']
  job_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job profile that defines the standard job template, competencies, and requirements for this requisition.']
  primary_recruiting_business_unit_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the business unit or division where the position will be located and the employee will report.']
  primary_recruiting_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who is the hiring manager responsible for the position and final hiring decision.']
  tertiary_recruiting_replacement_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee being replaced, applicable for backfill and replacement requisitions.']
  approved_headcount varchar [note: 'The number of positions approved by finance and leadership for hiring under this requisition.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the requisition record was first created in the system.']
  education_level_required varchar [note: 'The minimum education level required for the position, ranging from high school to advanced degrees or professional certifications. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: high_school|associate|bachelor|master|doctorate|professional_certification|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'The type of employment arrangement for the position, defining whether it is full-time, part-time, temporary, contract-based, internship, or seasonal.']
  evergreen_requisition boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is an evergreen requisition that remains open continuously to build a talent pipeline for high-turnover or high-demand roles.']
  internal_posting_only boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the requisition is restricted to internal candidates only before being opened to external applicants.']
  job_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the role, responsibilities, qualifications, and requirements for the position.']
  job_posting_title varchar [note: 'The external-facing job title used in job postings and advertisements, which may differ from the internal job title for marketing purposes.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'The official job title for the position being recruited, as it will appear in job postings and offer letters.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the requisition record was last updated or modified.']
  minimum_years_experience varchar [note: 'The minimum number of years of relevant work experience required for candidates applying to this position.']
  number_of_openings varchar [note: 'The total number of positions to be filled under this requisition, supporting bulk hiring scenarios.']
  position_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the position master record that defines the role being filled.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'The urgency level assigned to this requisition, influencing recruiter focus and resource allocation.']
  reason_for_opening varchar [note: 'The business reason or trigger event that created the need for this requisition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: resignation|termination|retirement|promotion|transfer|new_position|business_growth|seasonal_demand — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  remote_work_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the position is eligible for remote or hybrid work arrangements.']
  requisition_approved_date date [note: 'The date when the requisition received final approval from leadership and finance to proceed with hiring.']
  requisition_closed_date date [note: 'The date when the requisition was officially closed, either after being filled or cancelled.']
  requisition_created_date date [note: 'The date when the requisition was initially created in the system by the hiring manager or recruiter.']
  requisition_filled_date date [note: 'The date when the requisition was marked as filled after a candidate accepted an offer.']
  requisition_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the job requisition, externally visible and used for tracking and communication across hiring stakeholders.']
  requisition_opened_date date [note: 'The date when the requisition was officially opened and made available for candidate sourcing and applications.']
  requisition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the requisition indicating its progression through the approval and hiring workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|open|on_hold|filled|cancelled|closed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  requisition_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the requisition indicating whether it is for new headcount expansion, backfill of a vacated position, temporary staffing, contractor engagement, internship, or seasonal workforce. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new_headcount|backfill|replacement|temporary|contractor|intern|seasonal — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  salary_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the salary range (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  salary_range_maximum decimal(15,2) [note: 'The maximum annual salary or hourly wage budgeted for this position, defining the upper limit for offer negotiations.']
  salary_range_minimum decimal(15,2) [note: 'The minimum annual salary or hourly wage budgeted for this position, used for candidate expectations and offer negotiations.']
  sourcing_channels varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of sourcing channels and job boards where this requisition will be posted (e.g., LinkedIn, Indeed, internal referrals, campus recruiting).']
  target_hire_date date [note: 'The desired date by which the hiring manager expects the position to be filled and the candidate to start.']
  target_start_date date [note: 'The planned first day of work for the new hire, used for onboarding and workforce planning.']
  time_to_fill_days varchar [note: 'The number of calendar days from requisition opening to requisition filled, used as a key performance indicator (KPI) for recruiting efficiency.']
  work_location_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary work location or facility where the employee will be based.']

  Note: 'Talent acquisition job requisition record initiating the hiring process for a new or backfill position. Stores requisition number, position reference, job title, job profile, hiring manager, recruiter assigned, requisition type (new headcount, backfill, temporary), target hire date, approved headcount, requisition status (draft, approved, open, on-hold, filled, cancelled), sourcing channels, and time-to-fill tracking dates. Sourced from Workday HCM Recruiting module.'
}

Table workforce.job_application {
  job_application_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the job application record. Primary key for the job application entity.']
  applicant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the applicant (candidate) who submitted this application. Links to the candidate master record.']
  primary_job_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the recruiter responsible for managing this application. Links to the employee master record for the assigned recruiter.']
  recruiting_requisition_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job requisition for which this application was submitted. Links the candidate to the specific open position.']
  tertiary_job_referral_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who referred this candidate, if the application source is employee referral. Used for referral bonus tracking.']
  application_date date [note: 'Date when the candidate submitted the application for the job requisition. Key timestamp for recruiting funnel analysis.']
  application_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable unique application number assigned to this job application for tracking and reference purposes.']
  application_source varchar [note: 'Channel through which the candidate discovered and applied for the position. Used for source effectiveness analysis and recruiting ROI tracking.']
  application_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage of the application in the recruiting workflow. Tracks candidate progression through the hiring funnel from initial application to final disposition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: applied|phone_screen|hiring_manager_review|interview|offer|background_check|hired|rejected|withdrawn — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  background_check_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the background check process was completed and results were received.']
  background_check_status varchar [note: 'Status of the pre-employment background check process. Indicates whether the background verification has been completed and the outcome.']
  cover_letter_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or storage path to the candidate\'s cover letter document if provided.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the job application record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  disability_status varchar [note: 'Voluntary self-identification of disability status for compliance with equal opportunity and affirmative action reporting.']
  diversity_self_identification varchar [note: 'Voluntary self-identification information provided by the candidate for diversity reporting purposes. Stored separately for compliance with equal opportunity regulations.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address of the candidate used for communication throughout the recruiting process.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'First name of the candidate submitting the application. Part of candidate identity within the recruiting lifecycle.']
  hire_date date [note: 'Actual date when the candidate was hired and became an employee. Represents the successful completion of the recruiting lifecycle.']
  internal_candidate_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the applicant is an internal employee applying for a different position. True for internal candidates, False for external candidates.']
  interview_date date [note: 'Date when the formal interview (on-site or virtual) was conducted with the candidate.']
  interview_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite numerical score from the formal interview process. Aggregates feedback from multiple interviewers to assess candidate suitability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the job application record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change to any field in the record.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Last name of the candidate submitting the application. Part of candidate identity within the recruiting lifecycle.']
  offer_acceptance_date date [note: 'Date when the candidate formally accepted the job offer. Marks the transition to pre-hire onboarding activities.']
  offer_date date [note: 'Date when a formal job offer was extended to the candidate.']
  offer_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the job offer extended to the candidate. Tracks whether the offer is awaiting response, accepted, declined, or withdrawn by the company.']
  offered_salary decimal(15,2) [note: 'Annual salary amount offered to the candidate in the job offer. Used for compensation analysis and offer acceptance tracking.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the candidate. Used for scheduling interviews and communication.']
  phone_screen_date date [note: 'Date when the initial phone screening interview was conducted with the candidate.']
  phone_screen_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score assigned during the phone screening interview. Used to evaluate candidate fit and progression decisions.']
  rejection_date date [note: 'Date when the application was rejected and the candidate was notified of the decision.']
  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the primary reason for rejecting the application. Used for rejection pattern analysis and process improvement.']
  rejection_reason_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context for the rejection decision. Supplements the rejection reason code with specific details.']
  resume_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or storage path to the candidate\'s resume document. Links to document management system for retrieval.']
  stage_change_date date [note: 'Date when the application last moved to its current stage. Used to calculate time-in-stage metrics and identify bottlenecks.']
  time_in_stage_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the application has been in its current stage. Used to identify stalled applications and measure recruiting velocity.']
  time_to_hire_days varchar [note: 'Total number of days from application submission to hire date. Key performance indicator (KPI) for recruiting efficiency.']
  veteran_status varchar [note: 'Voluntary self-identification of veteran status for compliance with federal contractor reporting requirements.']

  Note: 'Individual job application and candidate record linking an applicant to a specific job requisition. Stores candidate profile data (name, contact, source, resume reference, internal/external flag), application date, application source channel, application stage (applied, phone screen, hiring manager review, interview, offer, background check, hired, rejected), stage change dates, interview scores, offer details (offer date, offered salary, offer status), rejection reason code, and time-in-stage metrics. SSOT for candidate identity within the recruiting lifecycle. Enables talent pipeline tracking, recruiting funnel analysis, and diversity reporting.'
}

Table workforce.performance_review {
  performance_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the performance review record.']
  primary_performance_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee being reviewed.']
  reviewer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the manager or reviewer conducting the evaluation.']
  calibration_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the calibration step across reviewers.']
  cascade_level varchar [note: 'Organizational level at which the goal is cascaded.']
  compensation_change_flag boolean [note: 'True if any compensation component was adjusted as a result of the review.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Classification indicating the sensitivity of the review data.']
  corporate_kpi_alignment varchar [note: 'Identifier of the corporate KPI that the goal supports.']
  employee_self_assessment_flag boolean [note: 'True if the employee completed a self‑assessment component.']
  goal_actual_achievement decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual measured result for the goal metric.']
  goal_category varchar [note: 'Category that classifies the nature of the goal.']
  goal_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the goal.']
  goal_target_metric varchar [note: 'Metric or target value the employee is expected to achieve.']
  goal_title varchar [note: 'Title or short description of an individual performance goal.']
  goal_weight decimal(5,2) [note: 'Weight of the goal expressed as a percentage of the overall rating.']
  is_finalized boolean [note: 'True when the review has been finalized and no further edits are allowed.']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'True when the record is locked for compliance or audit purposes.']
  manager_comments varchar [note: 'Free‑text feedback provided by the manager.']
  merit_increase_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the salary increase.']
  merit_increase_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of salary increase awarded based on the review outcome.']
  overall_rating decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score representing the employee\'s overall performance.']
  pip_flag boolean [note: 'True if a Performance Improvement Plan was created as a result of the review.']
  promotion_recommendation_flag boolean [note: 'True if the reviewer recommends the employee for promotion.']
  rating_scale varchar [note: 'Defines the rating scale used for the overall rating.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the review record was initially created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the review record.']
  review_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the review was marked complete.']
  review_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the review, often used in reporting and audit trails.']
  review_period_end date [note: 'End date of the performance review period.']
  review_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the performance review period.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the review record.']
  review_submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the final submission of the review.']
  review_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the type of review cycle.']
  reviewer_comments varchar [note: 'Additional narrative comments from the reviewer.']

  Note: 'Annual or mid-year employee performance review record capturing formal evaluation outcomes and associated goal achievement. Stores review period, review type (annual, mid-year, probation, project), overall performance rating, rating scale, manager comments, employee self-assessment flag, calibration status, PIP flag, promotion recommendation flag, review completion date, and goal details (goal title, category, weight, target metric, actual achievement, status, corporate KPI alignment, cascade level). Supports MBO/OKR frameworks, merit increase decisions, and talent calibration. Sourced from Workday HCM Performance module.'
}

Table workforce.training_record {
  training_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the training record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who completed the training.']
  training_course_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.training_course. Business justification: Normalize training records by referencing the master training_course table; remove duplicated course identifiers.']
  certifying_body varchar [note: 'Organization that issued the training certification.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date the employee completed the training.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date the training certification expires, if applicable.']
  pass_fail_status varchar [note: 'Result of the training assessment.']
  recertification_due_date date [note: 'Date by which recertification must be completed, if required.']
  recertification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the training must be renewed periodically.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the training record was first created.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the training record.']
  score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score achieved in the training (0‑100).']
  training_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of hours credited for the training.']
  training_location varchar [note: 'Physical or virtual location where the training was delivered.']
  training_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the training record.']
  training_type varchar [note: 'Category of the training (e.g., GMP, safety/OSHA, regulatory). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: gmp|safety|regulatory|product_knowledge|leadership|compliance|onboarding — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Employee training and learning completion record tracking mandatory and elective training activities. Stores training course code, course name, training type (GMP, safety/OSHA, regulatory, product knowledge, leadership, compliance, onboarding), delivery method (classroom, e-learning, OJT, external), completion date, expiry date, pass/fail status, score, training hours, certifying body, and recertification required flag. Critical for GMP compliance, OSHA requirements, and FDA audit readiness in CPG manufacturing.'
}

Table workforce.training_course {
  training_course_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the training course record.']
  accreditation_body varchar [note: 'External organization that accredits the course, if any.']
  applicable_job_profiles varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of job profile codes for which the course is relevant.']
  assessment_method varchar [note: 'Primary method used to evaluate learner performance.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the course currently meets all applicable regulatory requirements.']
  cost decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary cost to the organization for delivering the course (e.g., vendor fees, material costs).']
  course_code varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the course, e.g., a catalog or LMS code.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the course cost.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Mode through which the course is delivered to learners.']
  duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total instructional time required to complete the course, expressed in hours.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the course is retired or no longer offered (nullable).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the course becomes available for enrollment.']
  is_enrollment_open boolean [note: 'Indicates whether new enrollments are currently accepted for the course.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether completion of the course is required for the associated job profile(s).']
  language varchar [note: 'Primary language in which the course is delivered.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the course content was last reviewed for accuracy and compliance.']
  last_reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or role that performed the most recent review.']
  learning_objectives varchar [note: 'Bullet‑point list of the competencies or knowledge the learner will acquire.']
  max_participants varchar [note: 'Maximum number of learners allowed to enroll in a single offering of the course.']
  owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the department or individual responsible for the course content and maintenance.']
  passing_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum score a learner must achieve to pass the course, expressed as a percentage.']
  prerequisite_course_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of course codes that must be completed before enrolling.']
  provides_certification boolean [note: 'True if successful completion awards a formal certification.']
  recertification_interval_months varchar [note: 'Number of months after which the learner must retake or renew the certification.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the course record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the course record.']
  regulatory_requirement_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific OSHA, FDA CFR, GMP, or other regulatory standard the course satisfies.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the employee groups or roles the course is intended for.']
  title varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the training course.']
